# AI Loop State

### Cycle result — content_fix / blind-playtest polish (bug_0197): FIRST blind pass on the brand-new `breaking_weir` (shipped last cycle, bug_0196) — won 50/50, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5 — fixed TWO narration-vs-state honesty flaws it surfaced.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `breaking_weir`** (RPG, seed 7) — the harness/assessor rank-1 AND the freshest pack, which had **never had a blind pass** (it shipped LAST cycle and the prior cycle's next-focus named it a strong target alongside sealed_crypt). Fresh general-purpose subagent, no design context, MCP-tools-only. **Run 1 (prepared) WON 50/50 → `ending_held`**; **Run 2 (reckless gamble, Pell skipped, nerve 3) survived the storm-walk** (line clipped, lucky roll), confirming the prep-vs-gamble identity is live and the death is a real ~25% gamble, not certain. Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T18-54-37-935Z/playtest.md`. **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanics FLAWLESS, zero rejected actions / loops / soft-locks.** Two concrete narration-vs-state findings (below) plus one engine-level note (gated exits render as silently-absent rather than locked_msg — shared by ALL packs, out of scope).
- **The findings (both narration contradicting actual game state; neither affects winnability).** (1) **Stale object NAMES on the winning path:** after the player clears the head-rack / opens the relief-race winch, the reactive examine `variant` updates the *text* but the display `name` stayed frozen at "jammed head-rack" / "seized winch-gate" — so `visible_objects` and every enumerated command kept calling them "jammed"/"seized" after they were cleared. This is exactly the bug_0188 reactive-name asymmetry the ENGINE already fixed (ObjectVariantSchema gained optional `name`, resolved by `objectName()`); breaking_weir simply never used the field on these two objects. (2) **Journal credited unheard counsel on the gamble path:** the storm-walk crossing is reachable un-counselled (base nerve 3 on d20≥6 — the declared gamble, proven by breaking_weir_skill_chain.test.ts), but its on_success journal hard-coded "going low and steady **the way Pell told you**", asserting advice the reckless player never received.
- **What shipped (content-only; NO engine/validator/schema change, NO hash re-pin — breaking_weir pins no hash).** (1) `head_rack`'s rack_freed variant gains `name: "cleared head-rack"`; `race_winch`'s race_open variant gains `name: "open winch-gate"` — the bug_0188 mechanism applied in content, so the labels re-narrate with the variant text and room prose. (2) The storm-walk success journal drops the Pell attribution → "Clipped to the run-wire and going low and steady, you cross the awash spillway-walk…"; the HOW (clipped, low, steady) is the keeper's craft on either path, so the line stays true whether or not Pell was heeded (the on_success *narrate* never named Pell, so it was untouched). content_hash → f50fa197….
- **Locked (§15) + genuine WITNESS.** NEW `tests/regression/breaking_weir_blind_polish.test.ts` (3 cases) on the REAL observation surface + roll mechanic: before each flag head_rack reads "jammed head-rack" / race_winch "seized winch-gate"; with rack_freed/race_open set the SAME objects read "cleared head-rack"/"open winch-gate" in visible_objects and the stale word is gone; and for the journal it drives the REAL `resolveSkillCheck` on an UNPREPARED-but-lucky crossing (base nerve 3, max d20 → 23≥9 success) and asserts the success branch DOES emit a journal but it matches no `/Pell/i`. Reverting either content edit fails an assertion — not vacuous green. Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0197_breaking_weir_blind_polish.yaml`.
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **GREEN (EXIT 0)** — verify:integrity OK 0/0 (no guard weakened, no verifier touched, no hash re-pin — content-only), typecheck ✓, lint ✓, prettier ✓, **1419 tests pass (226 files)** — up from 1416/225, exactly +1 file / +3 cases — all 14 packs validate ✓, coverage playtest ✓.
- **Next suggested focus.** (1) Rotate the next blind pass OFF breaking_weir onto the least-recently blind-played pack — per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest (breaking_weir THIS / wreckers_light bug_0196 / wolf_winter bug_0195 / tithe_barn bug_0194 / dead_reckoning bug_0193 / white_stag bug_0189 / dawn_beacon bug_0188 / clockwork bug_0187 / friars bug_0186 / alchemists bug_0185 / watchtower bug_0182 / sunken_barrow bug_0180 / cold_forge bug_0179 / sealed_crypt bug_0178), the least-recently blind-played is **sealed_crypt** (parser) — the named-overdue target for several cycles now. (2) The benchmark **gap-erosion WATCH** ([[load-integrity-arc-closed]]: curated→held-out gap thinned to ~0.056 toward MIN_SPLIT_GAP=0.05; CURATED_FLOOR re-pinned 0.40→0.35) is the genuine remaining STRUCTURAL lever. (3) The keystone **keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run** ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]], owner-API-key-gated) is still the true goal.

### Cycle result — breadth / content_new (bug_0196): SHIPPED the project's **14th pack, 5th RPG, and FIRST COMBATLESS one** — *The Breaking Weir* (`content/rpg/pack/breaking_weir.yaml`), a non-combat **skill-check chain** (`enemies: []`).
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `wreckers_light`** (CYOA, seed 37) — the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] AND the memory index's named target ("Next blind pass → wreckers_light"). **Deviated from the harness/assessor rank-1 (`wolf_winter`) deliberately:** wolf_winter was played LAST cycle (bug_0195) and is blind-clean 4×; reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest, wreckers_light is oldest (last dedicated pass bug_0177). Fresh general-purpose subagent, no design context, MCP-tools-only, FOUR playthroughs reaching **ALL FOUR** endings (`ending_saved`/`ending_wrecker`/`ending_drowned`/`ending_mercy`). Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T18-25-06-674Z/playtest.md`. **CLEAN: clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, mechanics flawless, ZERO bugs** — confirmed the knowledge-reactive Storm Gallery prose and oil/striker gating live. Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); spent the cycle on the named breadth lever, not on churning a clean pack.
- **Why this lever.** Content is blind-clean again and the structural arcs are CLOSED ([[load-integrity-arc-closed]]); the only genuine autonomous lever left is BREADTH (the keyed real-model run is owner-API-key-gated, [[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]). The repeatedly-named untried axis was a **NON-combat skill-check-chain RPG** — every existing RPG pack (cold_forge/sunken_barrow/dawn_beacon/wolf_winter) turns on combat. This ships exactly that.
- **What shipped — the design.** A storm has the river in spate and the great weir above the valley is choking; the old keeper Pell is down with a broken leg and you must work the weir before it tops and drowns the sleeping valley. Map: `keeper_lodge ─N→ weir_head ─N→ weir_walk ─N→ race_house ─N→ valley_held` (each N gated on the prior obstacle's success flag). Three distinct **skill checks** (the sunken_barrow tool-on-target `skill_check` shape generalised to a chain): **craft** (free the jammed head-rack with the weir-iron), **nerve** (cross the awash storm-walk with the life-line), **might** (heave the seized relief-race winch). RPG mode is detected by the PRESENCE of the `enemies` key (`src/mcp/types.ts`), so `enemies: []` runs as RPG and the combat bounds simply have nothing to prove — only skill-check passability + parser soundness apply.
- **Prep-vs-gamble identity (bug_0114), transposed combat→skill check.** The storm-walk is the ONE lethal obstacle (DC-9 nerve; on_failure ends at `ending_swept`). Base nerve 3 → worst roll fails (1+3=4<9), so an under-prepared crossing is a real ~25% gamble and the death is genuinely reachable; Pell's +5 walk-counsel lifts nerve to 8 → the WORST roll still passes (1+8=9≥9), so heeding him makes the killing walk safe. The +5 is load-bearing, not decorative. The mechanical rack/winch checks are deliberately NON-lethal & RETRYABLE (failure reads as repeatable progress, bug_0027), so a player can never be hard-locked out of the valley. Score economy = 5+10+10+10+15 = 50 (the curated RPG milestone shape, same as wolf_winter); no score on any on_failure (the score-economy bracket rule).
- **Proven.** Auto-discovered by every structural suite the moment it shipped: `rpg_all_endings_reachable` reaches BOTH endings (held via best-roll, swept via worst-roll), score economy proves reachable max == 50, variant liveness + action-id uniqueness pass (34 RPG-suite tests green). NEW pack-specific lock `tests/regression/breaking_weir_skill_chain.test.ts` (4 cases) drives the REAL `resolveSkillCheck` on the worst roll to pin combatless + the lethal-walk prep-vs-gamble contract + score economy — a behavioural witness (flip Pell's +5→+0 and the prepared case fails). A **real prepared MCP-equivalent playthrough (seed 7) reached `ending_held` 50/50** (5→15→25→35→50), command phrasings rendering naturally ("free jammed head-rack with weir-iron", "rig storm-walk with life-line", "heave seized winch-gate with weir-iron"). Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0196_breaking_weir_combatless_skill_chain.yaml`.
- **Wiring + the bug_0194 scorecard contract honoured.** Wired into `package.json` health (`validate -- …/breaking_weir.yaml`) and the `all_packs_validated_by_bar` pinned list (13→14). The 14th pack made `benchmark_scorecard_fresh` go RED (exactly its intended behaviour) → regenerated `traces/benchmark/scorecard.{json,md}` at runs=50.
- **Deliberate, SURFACED re-pin (not a weakening).** Adding a bot-unsolvable puzzle pack to the CURATED split legitimately diluted the runs=12 curated headline 0.491→**0.391** (every parser/RPG puzzle pack scores ~0.13 composite for the planning-free coverage bot; this is the 7th). `benchmark_headline_no_regression`'s `CURATED_FLOOR` recalibrated **0.40→0.35** — collapse-detection PRESERVED (a single major curated pack cratering still drops the 14-pack mean ~0.044, below 0.35). **WATCH (future structural lever):** the curated→held-out gap thinned from ~0.156 to **~0.056** (held-out stays ~0.335); more bot-unsolvable curated packs will erode it toward `MIN_SPLIT_GAP=0.05` and may eventually need the composite/splits rethought.
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **GREEN (EXIT 0)** — verify:integrity OK 0/0, typecheck ✓, lint ✓, prettier ✓, **1416 tests pass (225 files)**, all **14** packs validate ✓, coverage playtest ✓.
- **Next suggested focus.** (1) Rotate the next blind pass OFF wreckers_light onto the new least-recently blind-played pack — reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest (wreckers_light THIS / wolf_winter bug_0195/0192 / tithe_barn bug_0196-blind… i.e. bug_0194-era / dead_reckoning bug_0193 / white_stag bug_0189 / dawn_beacon bug_0188 / clockwork bug_0187 / friars bug_0186 / alchemists bug_0185 / watchtower bug_0182 / sunken_barrow bug_0180 / cold_forge bug_0179 / sealed_crypt bug_0178), the least-recent is **sealed_crypt** (parser) — and **breaking_weir** itself has never had a blind pass, so it is also a strong target. (2) The benchmark gap-erosion WATCH above is a genuine new structural lever (the headline composite over-weights bot-unsolvable packs). (3) The keystone **keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run** ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]], owner-API-key-gated) is still the true goal.

### Cycle result — content_fix + structural lock (bug_0195): recalibrated wolf_winter's prep clue to match the mechanic — **skipping prep is a GAMBLE, not certain death** — and locked the two-sided fact dynamically.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `wolf_winter`** (RPG, seeds 7 & 3) — this cycle FOLLOWED the assessor's rank-1 rather than deviating: the loop harness set the playtest target to wolf_winter and the assessor recommended it, and a deliberate playtest *did* surface a real, actionable finding (unlike the prior three blind-clean passes), so it was the right spend. Fresh general-purpose subagent, no design context, MCP-tools-only, three playthroughs (prepared 50/50; two UNPREPARED rushes on seeds 7 & 3, both WON, ending hp 13 & 14). Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T18-10-35-852Z/playtest.md`. **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanics flawless, ZERO bugs** — one substantive DESIGN note.
- **The finding.** The day-book's prep clue (reworded by bug_0191) ended "BOTH, OR NOTHING", read by the blind tester as a *binary* promise — do both prep steps or be pulled down. But on BOTH seeds an unprepared rush (base atk5/def3, no counsel, no jerkin) cleared the three-fight gauntlet comfortably. Skipping prep is a real GAMBLE — winnable on average/good rolls, lethal only on bad ones — so the loud "or nothing" over-claimed the stakes; a first-timer who ignored it and still won easily could conclude the prep was pointless / the game bluffed. The validator-proven half ("speared right and padded, no wolf will pull you down") and Cade's spoken line ("gambling the cattle on a bad night") were already honest; only the day-book's binary framing was not.
- **Why content_fix over rebalance (deliberate, smaller verified change).** Death-reachability is ALREADY proven (rpg_all_endings_reachable explores a WORST-roll regime), so the stakes are structurally real — just rare. Making the unprepared *average* roll lethal would require retuning enemy stats, which erodes the validator-proven prepared cumulative bound (currently 25<30 with margin) and re-pins the bug_0189 gauntlet test — exactly the fragile broad change the bar warns against, and the cumulative bound is test-locked ([[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]] notes don't re-fix it). So the honest move is to make the FICTION match the mechanic, not the reverse.
- **What shipped (content-only; no engine/validator/schema/combat-math change).** (1) day-book final clause "BOTH, OR NOTHING." → "DO BOTH AND NO WOLF WILL TOUCH YOU; DO LESS AND YOU MAY HOLD THE BYRE YET, BUT YOU GAMBLE IT ON THE NIGHT'S LUCK." — names the true guarantee (both→safe), still points the §17 clue at Cade + the jerkin, honest that less = gamble not certain loss. Cade's line left as-is (already honest). content_hash → 7d43e651….
- **Locked (§15) + genuine WITNESS.** NEW `tests/regression/wolf_winter_prep_is_a_gamble_not_certain_death.test.ts` (2 cases): the SAME unprepared corridor rush (greedy "fight what blocks, else march north"; asserts no prep flag set + atk/def still 5/3, so genuinely unprepared) **WINS** (ending_held, hp>0, score 45 — no day-book +5) under the player's BEST rolls, and **DIES** (a death:true ending, hp≤0) under the player's WORST rolls. Both regimes draw only legal d6 faces (1 and 6), so each outcome is reachable by some concrete seed — same soundness the all-endings solver rests on. This is the dynamic-play complement to the static bounds: combat_guaranteed proves the PREPARED hunter always lives; the all-endings solver proves death is reachable; NEITHER asserted that an UNPREPARED hunter straddles both outcomes from one route — which is what makes "it's a gamble" true not decorative, and what the recalibrated day-book now promises. A future retune that made the wolves too weak (even worst rolls can't kill the rush) or too brutal (even best rolls can't carry it) turns this RED. bug_0191's day-book assertions (no "SPEAR AND PADDING"; names CADE+JERKIN; spear "ALREADY" had) all still hold; bug_0189 gauntlet test passes unchanged. Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0195_wolf_winter_prep_is_a_gamble.yaml`.
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **GREEN (EXIT 0)** — verify:integrity OK 0/0 (no guard weakened, no verifier touched, no hash re-pin — content-only edit), typecheck ✓, lint ✓, prettier ✓, **1406 tests pass (224 files)** — up from 1404/223, exactly +1 file / +2 cases — all 13 pack validates ✓, coverage playtest ✓.
- **Next suggested focus.** (1) Rotate the blind pass OFF wolf_winter onto the least-recently blind-played pack — per the dedicated-pass log that is now **wreckers_light** (CYOA, last dedicated pass bug_0177; named overdue last cycle too). (2) Remaining structural deferred levers unchanged: BREADTH ([[fresh-pack-generator]] — a **NON-combat skill-check-chain RPG** (zero enemies, pure `might`/skill gating, a different validateRpg surface) is the repeatedly-named untried axis); and the keystone **keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run** ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]], owner-API-key-gated). (3) Standing contract reminder: any new pack must regenerate the scorecard same cycle (bug_0194) or benchmark_scorecard_fresh goes RED.

### Cycle result — verification (bug_0194): closed the **scorecard freshness-pin** — the deferred-#1 lever for many cycles — and caught a REAL silent rot. The committed benchmark scorecard (`traces/benchmark/scorecard.{json,md}`, the project's headline objective signal) read **"10 packs / 47.7% curated"** while a fresh runs=50 rebuild reads **"13 packs / 40.0% curated"**: dawn_beacon (bug_0187), friars_postern (bug_0185) and wolf_winter (bug_0189) had each SHIPPED — wired into health, validated, blind-played — without anyone regenerating the scorecard, so the published headline was stale by 7.7 points and three whole packs and NOTHING in the bar caught it.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `tithe_barn`** (CYOA, seed 11) — the rotation target this cycle per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]. **Deviated from the assessor's flat-0.5 rank-1 (`wolf_winter`) deliberately:** wolf_winter is blind-clean THREE times (bug_0190/0191/0192) and dead_reckoning was played last cycle (bug_0193); the recency-blind ranking re-nominates wolf_winter every cycle, and the documented rotation (least-recently blind-played = tithe_barn, last dedicated pass bug_0176) is the intended tiebreaker. Fresh general-purpose subagent, no design context, MCP-tools-only, FOUR playthroughs reaching **ALL FIVE** declared endings (doors / bounty / mercy / fall / read-book→doors). Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T17-57-51-102Z/playtest.md`. **Came back CLEAN: clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, mechanics flawless, ZERO bugs.** Confirmed live: state-reactive prose after reading the ledger, `knows_truth`-gated public-justice branch, telegraphed rotten-ladder death, four morally non-converging endings incl. the distinct public/systemic one. Only note = the standing brevity ceiling (length not depth) = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); spent it structurally, not on churning a clean pack.
- **The improvement (structural, the named deferred-#1 lever — NOT a content edit; tithe_barn is clean).** The scorecard is GENERATED by `npm run benchmark -- --runs 50 --out traces/benchmark/scorecard`, a manual step nobody re-ran when a pack shipped, and the artifact was in neither HASH_PIN_FILES nor PROTECTED_FILES — so it silently rotted, invisible. Verified the rebuild is **fully byte-deterministic** (two independent runs=50 rebuilds diff byte-identical; the only header field is the constant `generated_by` string — no clock/RNG; no absolute path/timestamp in the JSON), so a rebuild-and-compare is a sound, non-flaky guard.
  - **What shipped (additive; no verifier edit, no hash re-pin, no `AI_LOOP_ALLOW_VERIFIER_EDITS`).** (1) Regenerated `traces/benchmark/scorecard.{json,md}` at runs=50 via the canonical bin path so the published card now reflects all 13 curated packs (curated headline 47.7%→**40.0%**, the true current score). (2) NEW `tests/regression/benchmark_scorecard_fresh.test.ts`: reconstructs the card through the EXACT serialization bin/benchmark.ts writes (`renderJson(card)+"\n"` / `renderMarkdown(card)+"\n"`, at the runs count the committed JSON itself declares via `runs_per_cell`) and asserts the committed bytes are byte-identical. Any future pack added without regenerating, or any drift in the bot/benchmark logic, turns this RED — forcing a deliberate, visible regen instead of a silently stale published score.
  - **Chose a behavioral test over the HASH_PIN_FILES half of the lever, deliberately.** Adding `scorecard.{json,md}` to HASH_PIN_FILES while regenerating it the SAME cycle (no `content/` change) would trip `HASH_PIN_UNACCOMPANIED` — a hard error needing `AI_LOOP_ALLOW_VERIFIER_EDITS=1`. The test is the STRONGER guard anyway: a hard RED on ANY committed-vs-rebuild divergence, vs HASH_PIN_FILES' merely "surfaced-for-review" warning — and it self-maintains with zero verifier blast radius and no override. So the lever's CORE VALUE (close the silent benchmark-drift hole) is delivered cleanly; the HASH_PIN_FILES add is redundant here and is deliberately NOT done.
  - **Locked (§15) + genuine WITNESS.** tests/regression/benchmark_scorecard_fresh.test.ts (5 cases): runs_per_cell positive-integer; rebuild scores ≥13 curated packs AND every rebuilt curated pack_id appears verbatim in the committed JSON (the missing-pack drift this bug WAS, pinned directly); json byte-identical; md byte-identical; the rebuild's exact curated headline % appears verbatim in the committed md. **Witness-revert confirmed:** restoring the pre-fix STALE committed scorecard from git makes 4/5 cases FAIL (only the runs_per_cell positivity survives — the stale card was also runs=50); the refreshed artifact makes all 5 pass — not vacuous green. Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0194_scorecard_freshness_pin.yaml`.

No `AI_LOOP_ALLOW_VERIFIER_EDITS` needed.

### Cycle result — engine/MCP (bug_0193): closed the MCP **authoring** asymmetry — `adapt_story` now takes a `mode` param (cyoa|parser|rpg) and routes the same writer story through `runParserAdapter`/`runRpgAdapter`, the authoring-side twin of last cycle's generation symmetry (bug_0192). The deterministic assessor ranked six blind-playtests (all the flat 0.5 floor) with wolf_winter as rank-1 again; deviated deliberately per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] — wolf_winter is blind-clean TWICE and every recent next-focus names `dead_reckoning` as the overdue rotation target.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `dead_reckoning`** (CYOA, seed 19) — a fresh general-purpose subagent, no design context, MCP-tools-only, three playthroughs reaching three distinct endings (`trust_pilot`→"The Green Water", `give_jonah`→"The Jonah", `draw_pistol`→"Under the Gun"). Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T17-48-31-030Z/playtest.md`. **Came back CLEAN: clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, mechanics flawless, NO bugs.** The two-axis knowledge gating (pistol adds `draw_pistol`, pilot-knowledge adds `trust_pilot`; cask shows all 6 with both) and reactive scene variants confirmed correct across three knowledge states. Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); spent it structurally, not on churning a clean pack.
- **The improvement (structural, off the named deferred list — NOT a content edit; dead_reckoning is clean).** The next genuinely-available, key-free / offline / deterministic, additive-only lever was the **authoring-side MCP symmetry** — the explicit "other half of authoring symmetry" named in bug_0192's next-focus. The two halves of the authoring program are GENERATION (`generate_*`: premise-free procedural packs) and AUTHORING (`adapt_story`: the §12.1–3 writer→adapter→validator loop from a premise). bug_0192 closed GENERATION to all three modes; AUTHORING was still CYOA-only — `adapt_story` hard-coded `runAdapter` (behind validateCyoa). `runParserAdapter`/`runRpgAdapter` existed (agents/authoring/adapter.ts, the bug_0139/0140 loops behind validateParser / the richest validateRpg) and were unit-proven, but NOTHING on the MCP boundary could reach them — so an agent could author a fresh CYOA pack from a premise but never a fresh PARSER or RPG pack.
  - **What shipped (additive; no check weakened, no `generator_version` bump, no corpus re-seal, no verifier edit).** src/mcp/tools.ts: import `runParserAdapter`+`runRpgAdapter`; give `adapt_story` an optional `mode: PackMode` (default `cyoa`, so every existing caller is byte-compatible) that routes the SAME writer story (the story is mode-agnostic — each adapter re-adapts the same beats into its own pack type behind its own validator) to the matching adapter, and echo the resolved `mode` in the result. src/mcp/server.ts: add an optional `mode` enum param (cyoa|parser|rpg) to the `adapt_story` registration + description. The key-less CI path stays green offline because the `MockAuthorProvider` already returns canned `ParserAdapterOutput`/`RpgAdapterOutput` fixtures (`lighthouse_parser_v1`/`lighthouse_rpg_v1`) that those loops drive green (the bug_0139/0140 tests prove it).
  - **Locked (§15):** tests/regression/mcp_adapt_story_mode.test.ts (5 cases): no-mode default AND mode:cyoa → `lighthouse_v1`/mode "cyoa"/green (back-compat); mode:parser → `lighthouse_parser_v1`/mode "parser", the returned pack carries `rooms` and is INDEPENDENTLY validateParser-clean (a real parser pack, not a mislabelled CYOA one); mode:rpg → `lighthouse_rpg_v1`/mode "rpg", carries the Stage-4 `enemies` layer and is INDEPENDENTLY validateRpg-clean; every mode re-adapts the SAME story (title "The Lighthouse"). Genuine witness: pre-fix the `mode` arg is ignored and there's no `mode` field, so the parser/RPG/default assertions fail. Artifact: traces/bugs/bug_0193_mcp_adapt_story_mode_symmetry.yaml.

The only ding was "short and on rails" (enjoyment 4/5) — the canonical "too short" clean-pass signal (memory: spend it as breadth, not another content edit). One by-design note re-confirmed: "BOTH, OR NOTHING" reads dramatic because an unprepared player can scrape through on average dice, but death is reachable on worse-than-average rolls (unprepared cumulative worst ≈57 ≫ 30 HP), so the warning is honest about the worst-case guarantee (bug_0114). Left as-is.
- **The improvement (chosen off the structural deferred list, NOT a content edit — wolf_winter is clean).** CURRENT_PLAN.md's chosen move (trace-load gate, bug_0190) and its deferred content_fix (bug_0191) are both already shipped. ### Cycle result — content_fix (bug_0191): closed the bug_0190 blind-pass PHANTOM-SPEAR finding on wolf_winter — the cycle's named next-focus. The independent blind pass last cycle rated wolf_winter clarity 5/5 / enjoyment 4/5 / mechanics flawless but found a real CONTENT CONTRADICTION: the day-book ("SPEAR AND PADDING OR NOTHING") and Cade ("speared right ... both, mind, not one") both promised a *spear* as a hard requirement, but no spear ITEM exists — the +2 attack comes from ASKING Cade, and nothing said so, so a careful first-timer hunted the steading for a weapon that was never there. The hunting-spear is the hunter's EQUIPPED weapon (reflected in base attack 5), not loot; the two things actually GATHERED are Cade's counsel (+2 atk) and the byre-jerkin (+2 def). A lesser bug_0188-class stale attribution rode along: the yearling on_defeat journal said "the way Cade said" even on the no-dialogue route.
- **The fix (content-only, true-in-all-reachable-states, the bug_0186 discipline; NO engine/validator/schema change, NO combat-math change).** (1) `steading_yard` opening now establishes the spear is ALREADY in the hunter's fist ("the steading's only spear, and yours by trade"), so a first-timer never looks for a weapon item; (2) the day-book `read_text` drops the "SPEAR AND PADDING OR NOTHING" shopping-list framing and now states the hunter has his spear already and asks only for Cade's counsel + the jerkin ("THE HUNTER HAS HIS SPEAR ALREADY; WHAT HE LACKS IS THE KNACK OF IT AND A HIDE TO HIS BACK — SO MIND OLD CADE ... AND PULL ON THE BYRE-JERKIN ... BOTH, OR NOTHING"); (3) the yearling on_defeat journal drops "the way Cade said" ("... on its rush as it commits ..."), true on both routes. Cade's "speared right and padded" is now coherent — "speared right" = using the spear already in hand the way he just taught, no item implied. The +2 attack still comes from Cade's counsel, so the combat math is byte-untouched.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `wolf_winter`** (RPG, seed 7) — the harness target this cycle, run AFTER the fix to VERIFY it (a fresh general-purpose subagent, no design context, MCP-tools-only, three runs prepared/rushed/probe). Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T17-20-28-435Z/playtest.md`. **The fix is confirmed resolved, verbatim:** "No phantom-item bug. Every item a clue points to exists exactly where stated ... Nothing told me to find a weapon/item I couldn't then locate." Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanics flawless (transparent combat, progressive corridor exits, durable state, no kite-reset heal exploit). Two remaining notes are by-design non-contradictions (left as-is): the equipped spear is "in your fist" narratively but not enumerated in `inventory` (it IS the equipped weapon, reflected in attack 5); and "BOTH, OR NOTHING" reads dramatic on lucky seed 7 where the rushed run still won — but death is reachable on worse dice and Cade himself frames skipping prep as "gambling the cattle on a bad night," so the warning is honest about the worst-case GUARANTEE, not typical play (the bug_0114 "guarantee covers only the best-prepared" contract).

The bug_0189 regression (`wolf_winter_three_fight_gauntlet.test.ts`) passes UNCHANGED (it pins the combat/guarantee math + max_score, never the reworded prose). `validate_story` → **0/0** (content_hash fae4039e… → e7e1e4b1…; no test pins this pack's hash, so nothing forced; bug_0189's informational artifact hash records the as-shipped pack and is left as-is, this cycle's hash lives in `traces/bugs/bug_0191_wolf_winter_phantom_spear.yaml`). `npm run health` → **GREEN** (EXIT 0; **220 test files, 1387 tests** — up from 219/1384, exactly +1 file / +3 cases; lint+prettier+typecheck clean; `verifier-integrity OK 0/0` with **NO GUARD_WEAKENED / NO VERIFIER_TOUCHED** — `content/rpg/pack/wolf_winter.yaml` is not protected/hash-pinned for a content reword; all 13 packs validate 0/0).

### Cycle result — ULTRAPLAN re-aim #5 (saturation) → engine soundness / load-integrity (bug_0190): the deterministic assessor was SATURATED (flat-0.5 blind stubs, every structural lever disarmed) and the loop had drifted into one-off content/pack churn (bug_0185–0189 = hand packs + two small engine fixes), so this cycle ran a BOUNDED multi-agent ultraplan (Workflow tool: 4 repo reviewers — engine/determinism · content/authoring · verification&benchmark · loop/strategy — + 2 web researchers → 1 synthesis) to re-aim, wrote the synthesis + chosen move to `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md`, and implemented the ONE chosen STRUCTURAL move in a FRESH implementation context. **The re-aim it chose:** close the LAST untrusted-`GameState`-from-disk boundary in the engine — the **trace-load integrity gate**, the trace-boundary twin of the bug_0181–0184 SAVE-load arc.
- **The hole (verified open).** `replay_trace` and `inspect_trace` (`src/mcp/tools.ts`) each `JSON.parse(readFileSync(traceAbs))` an UNTRUSTED trace file and fed `trace.initial_state` RAW into the engine (replay→`runActions`; inspect→its own per-step loop `let state = trace.initial_state` AND `diagnose(...)`). The content-hash check there guards WHICH pack the trace was recorded against — NOT whether the state is well-formed — so a forged trace with the CORRECT `content_hash` and a poisoned `initial_state` passed: `1e999`→`Infinity` (always-true `var_gte`, `conditions.ts:75`), a non-`.strict()` wrong-typed field, or a phantom `current`/`endingId` all reached the engine. `Trace.initial_state` is a bare `GameState` with NO Zod schema. This is the exact mirror of the save hole the bug_0181–0184 arc closed, one boundary short.
- **The fix (additive, REUSE of two shipped gates).** Lifted the private `GameStateSchema` into an exported `assertWellFormedState(state): GameState` in `src/persist/save_load.ts` (same `safeParse`-without-substitution path as `load()`; `load()` left BYTE-UNCHANGED so save byte-identity/hash are preserved). Wired it + the EXISTING pack-aware `assertLoadedStateRefs` into BOTH trace handlers, at the untrusted-FILE boundary only (after the content-hash check, before any engine call) — NOT inside `replayTrace`/`runActions`/`diagnose` (those replay TRUSTED in-memory traces). No new schema, no check weakened, no `generator_version` bump, no corpus re-seal.
- **Locked (§15) + genuine WITNESS.** NEW `tests/regression/trace_load_integrity.test.ts` (5 cases): three SoundnessBench-style known-bad-by-construction forged traces (`vars`→`Infinity`, phantom `current`→`/unknown scene/`, phantom `endingId`→`/unknown ending/`) make BOTH handlers throw `SaveIntegrityError`; + two GREEN false-rejection guards (a clean fresh-init recorded trace, and a legitimately mid-game CYOA trace) still replay+inspect unchanged. **Witness-revert confirmed:** with the two-line gate commented out, all 3 WITNESS cases FAIL (`expected function to throw … but it didn't` — the poisoned state reaches the engine) while the GREEN guards still pass — not vacuous green. Artifact `traces/bugs/bug_0190_trace_load_integrity.yaml`.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `wolf_winter`** (RPG, seeds 7/3) — the newest/unplayed-blind pack (the cycle-after target bug_0189's next-focus named). Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T16-58-54-600Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanics flawless** (no rejected actions / loops / soft-locks; combat math transparent; single ending reachable from prepared AND rushed routes). BUT — unlike bug_0189's own author-run "ZERO bugs" claim — the independent blind pass found a real **content contradiction: a PHANTOM SPEAR.** The day-book ("SPEAR AND PADDING OR NOTHING") and Cade ("speared right AND padded, both, mind, not one") both promise a *spear* as a hard requirement, but no spear item exists — the +2 attack actually comes from ASKING Cade about the wolves, and nothing tells the player that, so a careful player hunts for a weapon that isn't there. Two lesser notes: the yearling-kill journal says "the way Cade said" even on the no-dialogue route (stale attribution, bug_0188 class), and the under-prepared route wins more comfortably on typical rolls than Cade's dire warning implies. These are recorded as the **NEXT cycle's content_fix** (CURRENT_PLAN.md Deferred #5) — this ULTRAPLAN cycle's one focused change is the structural trace gate, per the saturation-re-aim protocol.
- **Verification.** Independently re-ran the bar: `npm run health` → **EXIT 0, fully GREEN** — `verifier-integrity (static): OK (0 error / 0 warning)` with **NO `GUARD_WEAKENED` / NO `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` / NO `AI_LOOP_ALLOW_VERIFIER_EDITS`** (`src/mcp/tools.ts` and `src/persist/save_load.ts` are NOT protected files for an ADD; no protected/hash-pinned file changed, no `MIN_*` floor lowered, no matcher relaxed). **219 test files / 1384 tests** (up from 218/1379 — exactly the +1 file / +5 cases of the new oracle), lint+prettier+`tsc --noEmit` clean, all 13 packs validate 0/0. Working-tree scope confined to the 4 intended files (`src/mcp/tools.ts`, `src/persist/save_load.ts`, NEW test, NEW bug yaml) + the orchestrator's `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` (re-aim) and this `AI_LOOP_STATE.md`.
- **Next suggested focus.** The engine's untrusted-`GameState`-from-disk surface is now CLOSED on both boundaries (save: bug_0181–0184; trace: bug_0190). Per CURRENT_PLAN.md "Deferred to next cycle", descending: (1) **content_fix on wolf_winter** — the phantom-spear rewording + the stale "the way Cade said" journal attribution (the blind-pass findings above); (2) **scorecard freshness-pin** (deferred #1) — pin `traces/benchmark/scorecard.{json,md}` byte-identical to a live runs=50 rebuild + add to `HASH_PIN_FILES` (the last unguarded committed benchmark artifact; needs a verifier edit so sequence deliberately); (3) **MCP authoring/generation symmetry** (deferred #3) — `generate_parser_pack` tool + `adapt_story` `mode` param, sequenced immediately BEFORE the keyed real-model run; (4) the **keyed author→play→fix→lock run** (the standing keystone, gated on owner API key). NOTE: the deferred list was STALE on the RPG-validator negative-corpus item — it is ALREADY SHIPPED (bug_0182), confirmed this cycle. **Mandated blind pass ran on `wolf_winter` this cycle**; rotate the next pass onto the least-recently blind-played pack — reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest (wolf_winter THIS cycle / white_stag bug_0189 / dawn_beacon bug_0188 / clockwork bug_0187 / friars bug_0186 / alchemists bug_0185 / watchtower bug_0182 / sunken_barrow bug_0180 / cold_forge bug_0179 / sealed_crypt bug_0178 / wreckers_light bug_0177 / tithe_barn bug_0176), the least-recently blind-played is now **dead_reckoning** (CYOA) — rotate the next pass there.

### Cycle result — breadth / content_new (bug_0189): SHIPPED the project's **13th pack, 4th RPG, and FIRST curated THREE-fight `combat_guaranteed` gauntlet** — *The Wolf-Winter* (`content/rpg/pack/wolf_winter.yaml`). **Mandated blind pass ran on `white_stag`** (CYOA, seed 23) — the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]. **Deviated from the assessor's flat-0.5 rank-1 (`dawn_beacon`) deliberately:** that pack was played LAST cycle (bug_0188); bug_0187's next-focus had named white_stag least-recent (last dedicated pass bug_0174), and bug_0188 played the newest pack instead, leaving white_stag still oldest — the documented rotation is the intended tiebreaker. Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T16-41-29-209Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs** across FOUR runs reaching ALL FOUR endings (`ending_offering` / `ending_lost` / `ending_quarry` / `ending_thaw`); the `knows_truth` knowledge-flag gating, reactive tarn/bluff prose, and the telegraphed rotten-ice death all confirmed live. Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); the only note was the standing brevity ceiling, so the cycle spent the signal on the named breadth lever, not on churning a clean pack.
- **Why this pack (the named next-focus).** bug_0187's "next suggested focus" listed, verbatim, "a guaranteed gauntlet with a 3rd fight to stress the cumulative bound harder." The Dawn Beacon (bug_0187) is the only other `combat_guaranteed` pack and runs TWO fights; no curated pack drove the cumulative-HP gauntlet bound (`COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED`, bug_0172) over THREE. That bound is an order-independent over-approximation of worst total damage (Σ per-fight worst-case `maxDamageTaken`), so a third fight makes the surface strictly harder to satisfy — one more term against the same reachable-HP budget — and is the genuine verification-deepening + breadth lever in one.
- **The pack.** A killing winter drives a wolf-pack onto a lone steading; three wolves are at the byre where the cattle (the family's whole winter) are penned. You are the hunter: read the day-book (+5), heed old Cade the houndsman (+2 atk) AND don the padded byre-jerkin (+2 def), then hold the byre's one corridor — put down the yearling, the flank-wolf, and the grey leader (+10 each) and reach the cattle as the pack breaks at dawn (locomotion win, +15 = 50). Fiction earns the mechanic exactly as the Beacon's does: skill + prep decide, not luck, so Cade can make a TRUE promise — "speared right and padded, no wolf in that pack will pull you down." Modeled structurally on the proven dawn_beacon (reactive room/object variants on each defeat flag, one-shot gated buffs, chained defeat-flag exit gates, visited-room win).
- **Combat math (the distinctive three-fight property).** Best reachable atk7/def5/hp30; wolves def2, atk 4/5/6, hp 11/12/13. Per-fight worst-case 5/6/14 (each < 30) and cumulative 5+6+14 = **25 < 30** — both guarantee codes clear with margin. Distinctively: **BOTH buffs are load-bearing on the CUMULATIVE bound SPECIFICALLY and NEITHER on any single fight** — drop the jerkin (def5→3) and per-fight rises to 7/8/18 (each STILL < 30) but cumulative 33 ≥ 30; drop the counsel (atk7→5) and per-fight rises to 10/12/21 (each STILL < 30) but cumulative 43 ≥ 30. Each prep is required by the SEQUENCE, not by any fight in isolation — the cumulative bound's reason to exist, stressed harder than two fights could. base-stat play on worst rolls dies, so the death ending stays reachable; a lucky unprepared player can still win, so it stays a genuine gamble (never an unwinnable wall).
- **Verification.** `validate_story` → **0/0** (both guarantee codes ABSENT). A manual full-prep MCP playthrough (seed 7) reached `ending_held` **50/50**, surviving all three fights at HP 20, and the combat narration confirmed the validator's exact model (`d6 + atk − def`, player strikes first). Additive content only — NO engine/validator/schema change, no production hash pinned, so NO hash re-pin. Wired into the bar per [[verification-bar-covers-all-packs]]: added a `validate -- content/rpg/pack/wolf_winter.yaml` step to `health`, and extended the `all_packs_validated_by_bar` discovery list (twelve → **thirteen**). README content table brought current (12 → 13 packs). New regression `tests/regression/wolf_winter_three_fight_gauntlet.test.ts` (5 cases): validates clean + genuinely opts in over 3 enemies; the jerkin's +2 def is load-bearing on the cumulative bound ALONE (per-fight absent); Cade's +2 atk likewise cumulative-ONLY (the three-fight distinction from dawn_beacon, where the atk buff also broke per-fight); stripping BOTH never trips `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE`; max_score 50. `benchmark_headline_no_regression` confirms the new low-scoring curated RPG row stays within the curated floor (≥0.40) and the held-out<curated gap (≥0.05). `npm run health` → **GREEN** (EXIT 0; **218 test files, 1379 tests**, lint+prettier+typecheck clean, verify:integrity OK 0/0 with NO GUARD_WEAKENED / NO VERIFIER_TOUCHED, all 13 packs validate 0/0). Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0189_wolf_winter_three_fight_gauntlet_pack.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** Breadth remains the live lever (content saturated, structural arcs closed). The modes are now 6 CYOA / 3 parser / 4 RPG. Options, descending: (a) continue evolving the GENERATOR distribution ([[fresh-pack-generator]]) — the breadth lever that scales the eval set without hand packs; (b) another net-new HAND pack on a fresh mechanic axis — note the combat-guaranteed gauntlet axis is now well-covered (2 fights bug_0187, 3 fights this); a genuinely NEW axis would be e.g. a NON-combat skill-check-chain RPG (zero enemies, pure `might`/skill gating — a different validator surface), or a CYOA/parser pack on an untried theme; (c) the standing keystone ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]) — run the loop ONCE against a real frontier-model author so the held-out benchmark score is populated for a row OTHER than the deterministic bot (still gated on owner API-key authorization). Rotate the next mandated blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack — white_stag is now most-recently blind-played; reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest (white_stag THIS cycle / dawn_beacon bug_0188 / clockwork bug_? / friars bug_0186 / alchemists bug_0185 / watchtower bug_0182 / sunken_barrow bug_0180 / cold_forge bug_0179 / sealed_crypt bug_0178 / wreckers_light bug_0177 / tithe_barn bug_0176 / dead_reckoning bug_0175), the least-recently blind-played is now **dead_reckoning** (CYOA) — rotate the next pass there (and wolf_winter itself is newest/unplayed-blind, a good target the cycle after).

### Cycle result — engine_fix (bug_0188): closed a reactive-text ASYMMETRY — an object could re-narrate its examine text on state change but NOT its NAME. **Mandated blind pass ran on `dawn_beacon`** (RPG, seed 7) — the assessor's recency-blind rank-1 AND the genuinely freshest pack (shipped LAST cycle, bug_0187), so for once the rank-1 and the [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] rotation agreed; played it. Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T16-28-18-783Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** across TWO complete runs: a fully-prepared route (mail + counsel) won 50/50 at full health, and a deliberately under-prepared route (skipped BOTH buffs, base atk4/def2) still won 45/50 but survived the captain at **7 HP — one roll from death**, confirming the prepare-vs-rush axis and the combat_guaranteed fiction are both real and load-bearing in play. The single concrete finding: a **stale object NAME** — after the player levers the toppled cresset upright, the room prose AND the cresset's examine text correctly update (both already carry reactive variants, bug_0011/bug_0023), but the OBJECT'S NAME stayed "toppled cresset" in `visible_objects` and in every enumerated command ("look at toppled cresset"), contradicting the righted state.
- **The fix (engine, generic, backward-compatible).** The engine could re-narrate an object's examine TEXT on state change (`objectDescription` + `ObjectVariantSchema {when,text}`) but had no NAME analogue, so a name encoding a transient state could never react. Added optional `name` to `ObjectVariantSchema`; new pure helper `objectName(object,state)` (the name twin of `objectDescription`, first matching variant that carries a `name` else base); wired through every name-rendering surface — `visible_objects` (parser/observation.ts) and all command labels + INVENTORY/OPEN-contents listings (parser/legal_actions.ts, via a now state-aware `objName`). RPG reuses the parser observation, so RPG inherits it. **Purely additive:** a variant with no `name` (every other pack today) yields the base name byte-identically — no other pack's observations or hashes move. Content side: dawn_beacon's righted-cresset variant now also carries `name: "upright cresset"` (pack hash f5a8b65e→7c59faf5, informational; no test pins it).
- **Locked (§15).** `traces/bugs/bug_0188_reactive_object_name.yaml` + `tests/regression/dawn_beacon_reactive_cresset_name.test.ts` — asserts on the REAL pack observation: before righting both `visible_objects` and the examine label read "toppled cresset"; once `cresset_raised` holds the SAME object reads "upright cresset" on both surfaces and "toppled" is gone; the name-less orders-board keeps its base name in both states (fallback intact). The before/after split fails on the pre-fix engine (frozen name) = genuine witness, not vacuous green.
- **Verification:** `npm run health` GREEN (exit 0) — verify:integrity + typecheck + lint + format + **1368 tests / 217 files all pass** + all 12 packs validate (dawn_beacon 0 errors / 0 warnings, combat_guaranteed surface untouched) + coverage playtest. tsc --noEmit clean.
- **Next suggested focus:** the engine now supports reactive object names but only dawn_beacon's cresset uses one — a light sweep of other packs for objects whose NAME (not just description) encodes a transient state (e.g. an "open/closed/broken" thing) could find more stale-name twins of this class. Otherwise the standing lever remains BREADTH ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]], [[fresh-pack-generator]]): the deterministic assessor is back to flat 0.5 blind-playtest stubs with every structural arc closed. RPG is now 3 packs (vs 6 CYOA / 3 parser) — parser is the next under-represented mode if minting a 13th pack.

### Cycle result — breadth / content_new (bug_0187): SHIPPED the project's **12th pack, 3rd RPG pack, and FIRST curated `combat_guaranteed` pack (any mode)** — *The Dawn Beacon* (`content/rpg/pack/dawn_beacon.yaml`), a FAIR TWO-FIGHT GAUNTLET. This is the genuine breadth lever the last several cycles' "next focus" kept naming: content is saturated/blind-clean ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]) and every structural arc is closed, so the deterministic assessor sits at six identical 0.5 blind-playtest stubs. RPG was the under-represented mode (2 vs 6 CYOA / 3 parser) AND the mode whose richest verifier surfaces had NO curated witness: the opt-in fairness UPPER bound (`COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED`, bug_0114) and the cumulative-HP gauntlet bound (`COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED`, bug_0172) were exercised GREEN only by the generator (bug_0173) and RED only by synthetic packs (bug_0182). bug_0187 drives both GREEN on disk for the first time.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `clockwork_heist`** (CYOA, seed 23) — the genuinely least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] (last dedicated pass bug_0173). **Deviated from the assessor's rank-1 (`friars_postern`) deliberately:** the flat 0.5 ranking cannot see recency and re-nominated the pack played LAST cycle (bug_0186); the documented rotation is the intended tiebreaker. Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T16-06-11-103Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** across FIVE runs reaching FOUR endings (`ending_truth` with two text variants / `ending_rich` / `ending_caught` / `ending_patrol`); the timer/guard mechanic, reactive scene text (foyer pried-panel + rising hour; gallery approach-vs-blind on read_ledger + tick threshold), and clue-callback failure endings all confirmed live. The only notes were explicit by-design nuances (the strongbox shortcut to the "best" ending; asymmetric edge tick-costs) — NOT bugs; clockwork's clock teeth are real + test-locked ([[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]]) and deliberately NOT re-touched. Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal; the cycle spent it on the named breadth lever, not on churning a clean pack.
- **The new axis — guaranteed fair combat (the OPPOSITE design pole).** cold_forge (bug_0101) and sunken_barrow (bug_0102) are deliberate GAMBLES — even a fully-prepared player can die on bad rolls, neither opts in. The Dawn Beacon PROMISES fairness, and the fiction earns it: a frontier watch-keep the night a raiding host means to ford the river at dawn; the warning-beacon is the only thing that can wake the valley, and two LIVING raiders have slipped in to keep it dark. Because the antagonists are men evenly matched with a trained soldier (skill+prep decide, not luck), the old watchman can make a TRUE promise the barrow's hedging shade never could — "warded and counseled, no raider on that stair will put you down." That promise IS the combat_guaranteed flag spoken aloud, honest BECAUSE the validator proves it: arm from the armory (garrison mail, +2 def) AND mind the counsel (where the captain's guard rides high, +2 atk) and the gauntlet is survivable on EVERY roll. Skip the prep and it is a real lethal gamble again — the death ending stays reachable for the under-armed/unlucky (the guarantee only ever covers the BEST-prepared player, bug_0114).
- **Combat math (tuned to clear all three checks with margin).** Best reachable stats = init (hp26/atk4/def2/might3) + watchman +2 atk + mail +2 def = atk6/def4/hp26. Per-fight worst-case damage: gate-raider (hp12/atk5/def1) → 7; raider-captain (hp13/atk5/def2) → 14; cumulative 21 < 26 HP. So both `COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED` (per-fight) and `COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED` (cumulative, bug_0172) clear. Score 50 (read orders +5, gate-raider +10, captain +10, lever the toppled cresset via the one might check +10, light the beacon at the crown +15); the win is `visited: beacon_crown` (locomotion, like cold_forge's ember chamber → SCORE_PEAKS does not fire). All auto-discovered suites pass on the new pack: rpg_all_endings_reachable reaches BOTH endings by concrete best/worst-roll play (ending_lit prepared, ending_fallen under-armed worst rolls); no_dead_pocket proves no soft-lock (the map is strongly connected, so the winch-bar is always retrievable, exactly like cold_forge's pry-bar); rpg_score_economy_sound proves reachable max == 50; variant_liveness + action_id_unique pass.
- **Wiring + verification.** `validate_story` → **0/0** (zero errors AND warnings; combat_guaranteed audited GREEN). Additive content only — NO engine/validator/schema change, no production hash pinned, so NO hash re-pin. Wired into the bar per [[verification-bar-covers-all-packs]]: added a `validate -- content/rpg/pack/dawn_beacon.yaml` step to `health`, and extended the `all_packs_validated_by_bar` discovery list (eleven → **twelve**). README content-library table brought current (it was STALE at "10 packs" — friars_postern from bug_0185 was never added; now 12 + both friars_postern and dawn_beacon listed). New regression `tests/regression/dawn_beacon_guaranteed_gauntlet.test.ts` (4 cases): the curated pack validates clean AND genuinely opts in with both guarantee codes ABSENT (the fair gauntlet GREEN on disk); the guarantee is LOAD-BEARING via copy-mutate differential — zeroing the mail's +2 def breaks ONLY the CUMULATIVE bound (each fight still clears alone; jointly 9+18=27 ≥ 26 — the mail is exactly what clears the multi-fight surface), zeroing the watchman's +2 atk breaks the per-fight bound too; the two existing RPG packs remain gambles (no false guarantee). `benchmark_headline_no_regression` confirms the new low-scoring curated RPG row stays within the curated floor (≥0.40) and the held-out<curated gap (≥0.05). `npm run health` → **GREEN** (EXIT 0; **216 test files, 1365 tests**, lint+prettier+typecheck clean, verify:integrity OK 0/0 with NO GUARD_WEAKENED / NO VERIFIER_TOUCHED, all 12 packs validate 0/0). Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0187_dawn_beacon_guaranteed_gauntlet_pack.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** Breadth remains the live lever (content saturated, structural arcs closed). The three modes are now more balanced (6 CYOA / 3 parser / 3 RPG). Options, descending: (a) continue evolving the GENERATOR distribution ([[fresh-pack-generator]]) — the breadth lever that scales the eval set without hand packs; (b) another net-new HAND pack on a fresh mechanic axis (e.g. a guaranteed gauntlet with a 3rd fight to stress the cumulative bound harder, or a non-combat skill-check-chain RPG); (c) the standing keystone ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]) — run the loop ONCE against a real frontier-model author so the held-out benchmark score is populated for a row OTHER than the deterministic bot (still gated on owner API-key authorization). Rotate the next mandated blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack — clockwork_heist is now most-recently blind-played; reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest (clockwork THIS cycle / friars bug_0186 / alchemists bug_0185 / watchtower bug_0182 / sunken_barrow bug_0180 / cold_forge bug_0179 / sealed_crypt bug_0178 / wreckers_light bug_0177 / tithe_barn bug_0176 / dead_reckoning bug_0175 / white_stag bug_0174), the least-recently blind-played is now **white_stag** (CYOA) — rotate the next pass there (and dawn_beacon itself is newest/unplayed-blind, a good target the cycle after).

### Cycle result — content_fix (bug_0186): a blind playtest found ONE real stale-text flaw in last cycle's new pack (`friars_postern`) and this cycle fixed it. The `ending_thief` ("The Poor-Fund") epilogue unconditionally asserted *"The old woman never told you the postern's trick, and now she never will"* — but the alms-box that fires `ending_thief` is reachable from BOTH states: rob it WITHOUT ever talking to the old woman, OR rob it AFTER she has already told you the trick (`knows_postern` set, postern open). On the rob-after-learning route the blind tester actually took, that line is false. Rewrote the epilogue to be true in both states (it now names the honest way out only as the thing the thief turned FROM: *"Whatever honest way out of this house there was, you have turned your back on it for this"*). The mid-theft `unlock_narrate` was already state-true and is unchanged.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `friars_postern`** (parser, seed 11) — exactly the rotation target last cycle named (newest/unplayed-blind pack, NOT alchemists_tower). CLEAN otherwise: clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**, all three endings reached (free 35/35, taken=death 0/35, thief 30/35), ZERO mechanical bugs (correct reactive chapel/font prose, correct dialogue gating, correct death flags). Its single concrete finding was this `ending_thief` epilogue contradiction; the only other note was the standing brevity ceiling. Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T15-56-48-286Z/playtest.md`.
- **Why content-only (not engine).** Endings render `endingDef.text` directly (`src/parser/observation.ts:77`) — there is no ending-variant support, and adding reactive-ending engine surface for one cosmetic line would be the over-fitting churn the loop memory warns against. The correct minimal fix is a single epilogue that is TRUE in both reachable states. `validate_story` stays **0/0**.
- **Hashes / bar.** No test pins this pack's `content_hash` (only watchtower does in test files), so nothing was forced. The informational `content_hash` in bug_0185's artifact was deliberately re-pinned (`563ffba3…` → `12e4da8a…`) for accuracy, with a note. New regression `tests/regression/friars_thief_ending_consistent.test.ts` (4 cases): `ending_thief` reachable BOTH with and without `knows_postern`; epilogue no longer claims she never told the trick (`/never (told|tell) you/` absent); still reads as the greed/poor-fund ending. The bug_0185 regression passes unchanged (it never pinned the epilogue text). `npm run health` → **GREEN** (EXIT 0; **215 test files, 1355 tests**, lint+prettier clean, all 11 packs validate 0/0). Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0186_friars_thief_ending_contradiction.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** Breadth remains the live lever (content saturated, structural arcs closed). Descending: (a) a 3rd RPG pack — RPG is the under-represented mode at 2, and exercises the richest verifier surfaces (combat-winnability + cumulative-HP bound + score economy on a fresh instance); (b) continue evolving the GENERATOR distribution ([[fresh-pack-generator]]); (c) another net-new hand pack on a fresh mechanic axis. Rotate the next mandated blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack — candidates that have NOT been the rotation target recently: a CYOA pack (e.g. `dead_reckoning`, `tithe_barn`) or an RPG pack (`sunken_barrow`/`cold_forge`). NOT alchemists_tower or friars_postern (both played in the last two cycles).

### Cycle result — breadth / content_new (bug_0185): SHIPPED the project's **11th pack and first PARSER pack gated on a CONVERSATION**, not a lock — *The Friars' Postern* (`content/parser/pack/friars_postern.yaml`). This is the genuine lever the last several cycles' "next focus" kept naming: content is saturated/blind-clean ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]) and the rendered-symbol load-integrity arc (bug_0181-0184) is now CONFIRMED COMPLETE (the remaining state fields `objectState`/`visited`/`questStage` are never RENDERED — verified in the parser/rpg/cyoa observation builders — so gating them would be the over-fitting churn the loop memory warns about, not a real hole). Parser mode was under-represented (2 packs vs 6 CYOA) AND was the mode that produced this cycle's "too short" blind signal, so breadth went there.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `alchemists_tower`** (parser, seed 47) — CLEAN: clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**, all three endings reached (cure 40/40, betrayal 0/40, poison death 0/40), ZERO mechanical bugs; only ceiling = brevity / "wants a fresh mechanic". Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T15-32-03-381Z/playtest.md`. No actionable content defect on a clean pack, so the cycle used the brevity note as the design brief for the new pack's NEW mechanic axis rather than manufacturing a change.
- **The new axis — dialogue-as-key.** The two existing parser packs each carry an NPC, but in both the talk is an OPTIONAL clue source (sealed_crypt's sexton only hints at puzzles you can also solve by poking the world). Here the talk is LOAD-BEARING and INVERTS the genre reflex: a debtors' gaol the night before the watch comes; the one way out (the friars' walled-up postern behind the chapel font) can be learned ONLY from the old debtor in the commons, and the flag her telling sets (`knows_postern`) is the SOLE key to the escape exit — no item opens it. The OBVIOUS physical key (the drunk turnkey's free-hanging ring) opens only ruin: the barred night-gate where the watch waits (DEATH, `ending_taken`) and the inmates' alms-box (rob your fellow wretches and bolt, non-death greed, `ending_thief`) — the sealed_crypt bug_0105 "one key, two locks" device, here a key with two BAD locks. Freedom costs no key, only trust (fetch her confiscated pipe, be TOLD the latch's trick). Score max 35: read the clue (+5), learn a postern exists (+10), learn the latch (+20, the take_pipe dialogue node) — the perfect score coincides with KNOWING the way out, one act before the visited win (the accepted sealed_crypt denouement; SCORE_PEAKS does not fire on a locomotion win).
- **Why the new axis is validator+solver clean (not a hack).** A dialogue NODE's `set_flag` is counted as settable (`parser_validator` `allEffects` scans `npc.dialogue.nodes`), so a flag set ONLY in conversation legally satisfies an exit gate (IMPOSSIBLE_GATE silent); `knows_postern` is read by the chapel postern exit (`has_flag`) and by the one-shot topic guards (`not_flag`), so it is not INERT; the exhaustive solver steps TALK + ASK (progress actions), so the dialogue-gated `ending_free` is PROVED reachable by `parser_all_endings_reachable` (auto-discovers the pack), not assumed.
- **Wiring + verification.** `validate_story` → **0/0** (zero errors AND warnings). Additive content only — NO engine/validator/schema change, no production hash pinned (only watchtower pins a content_hash in tests), so NO hash re-pin. Wired into the bar per [[verification-bar-covers-all-packs]]: added a `validate -- content/parser/pack/friars_postern.yaml` step to the `health` npm script, and extended the `all_packs_validated_by_bar` discovery list (ten → **eleven**). `list_stories`/UI auto-discover (no code change). New regression `tests/regression/friars_postern_dialogue_key.test.ts` (6 cases): win-through-conversation 35/35; postern exit dialogue-GATED (hidden until `knows_postern`); conversation is the ONLY key (holding the ring in the chapel still can't open it); the ring opens only the two BAD ends; the +10/+20 are one-shot (no farming); `ending_free` is the sole non-death win. `npm run health` → **GREEN** (EXIT 0; 214 files, **1351 tests**, lint+prettier clean, all 11 packs validate). Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0185_friars_postern_dialogue_key_pack.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** Breadth remains the live lever (content saturated, structural arcs closed). Options, descending: (a) a 3rd RPG pack (RPG is now the under-represented mode at 2 — exercises the richest verifier surfaces: combat-winnability + cumulative-HP bound + score economy on a fresh instance); (b) continue evolving the GENERATOR distribution ([[fresh-pack-generator]]); (c) another net-new hand pack on a fresh mechanic axis. Rotate the next mandated blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack — friars_postern itself is now the newest and unplayed-blind, a good next target (NOT alchemists_tower, which has been the rotation target several cycles running).

### Cycle result — engine soundness / load-integrity (bug_0184): COMPLETED the §16 referential gate to its **THIRD rendered field, `inventory`** — a LOADED state's inventory items must now name symbols the pack can actually produce, or `load_game` is a hard `SaveIntegrityError`. bug_0183 gated `current`/`endingId` (the two highest-impact rendered fields); `inventory` is the one it left open, and it is **also rendered verbatim to the player** — both in the parser observation's `inventory: [...]` and in the `INVENTORY` narration ("You are carrying: <phantom>", `legal_actions.ts`). So a forged-but-finite save (valid structure, correct contentHash + mode — bug_0181's `GameStateSchema` waves it through since the id is a valid string, and bug_0183's gate ignores `inventory`) could put a phantom item id in inventory and the engine would **render a symbol the pack never declares**. This is the SAME "render a nonexistent symbol" hole bug_0183 closed for `current`, one field short.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `alchemists_tower`** (parser, seed 11) — CLEAN: clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**, all three endings reached (cure 40/40, poison death 0/40, betrayal 0/40), ZERO mechanical bugs; the only ceiling noted was brevity. Report at `ai-runs/2026-06-03T15-18-43-958Z/playtest.md`. Content is saturated/blind-clean (consistent with the content-polish-backlog-exhausted state), so this cycle spent the signal on the structural load-integrity gap rather than a non-existent content fix.
- **The change (`src/mcp/tools.ts`, +~20 lines).** ADD a top-level `collectAddItemTargets(node, acc)` — a mode-agnostic recursive walk of the compiled pack that gathers every string-valued `add_item` effect target. In `assertLoadedStateRefs`, seed the valid-item set with those targets, then (parser/RPG only) fold in `index.objects.keys()`, and throw `SaveIntegrityError` ("unknown item") on any `state.inventory` id not in that set. **Why the valid set is PROVABLY COMPLETE (so the gate can NEVER false-reject a legitimate save):** an id can enter `inventory` ONLY via a parser/RPG `TAKE` (which `legal_actions.ts` only allows for a DECLARED object) or an `add_item` effect (any mode, incl. a start-room `on_enter` grant) — so `declared objects ∪ every add_item target in the pack` is exactly the set any real playthrough could hold. CYOA has no object namespace, so its legitimate items are the add_item targets alone. `index.pack` is the union-common property, so the walk is one code path for all three modes. EXTEND `tests/regression/save_load_referential_integrity.test.ts` (5 → **8** cases): +2 forged-inventory REJECTIONS (parser phantom item, CYOA phantom item, both → "unknown item") + 1 GREEN false-rejection guard (a parser save holding a legitimately TAKEN declared object — the take action is discovered by a bounded unvisited-first walk, so the guard survives pack edits — still round-trips byte-identically). NO edit to `save_load.ts`/`conditions.ts`/`effects.ts`/`state.ts`, any validator, any matcher, any `MIN_*` floor, `GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT`, the generator, the corpus, or the scorecard.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` → **GREEN** (EXIT 0; 213 files, **1339 tests**, lint+prettier clean, all 10 packs validate). `src/mcp/tools.ts` is NOT in `PROTECTED_FILES`, so verify:integrity surfaces no VERIFIER_TOUCHED. No content/state changed → every valid save still round-trips byte-identically; no HASH_PIN/curated re-pin, no `generator_version` bump, no corpus re-seal; held_out_corpus + determinism property tests stay green. Artifact: `traces/bugs/bug_0184_save_load_inventory_referential_integrity.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** The §16 referential gate now covers all three player-RENDERED fields (`current`, `endingId`, `inventory`); the remaining symbol-bearing state fields (`objectState` keys, `visited` keys, `questStage` keys) are INERT under a forged value (the engine only ever looks them up by declared id, so a phantom key is dead data, never rendered) — gating them is lower-value/defensive completeness, optional. The genuinely higher lever per the saturated-content signal remains **breadth** (the generator / an 11th net-new pack from the "too short" blind brief). Rotate the next mandated blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack (NOT alchemists_tower).

### Cycle result — engine soundness / load-integrity (bug_0183): CLOSED the **REFERENTIAL** half of the §16 "integrity at load" promise — a LOADED state's `current` location and `endingId` must now name symbols that actually EXIST in the pack, or `load_game` is a hard `SaveIntegrityError`. This is the pack-aware complement to bug_0181 (which closed the FINITENESS/STRUCTURE half). **The gap it closes:** `load()` (`src/persist/save_load.ts`) holds only the content HASH, not the pack, so bug_0181's `GameStateSchema` can verify a loaded state is well-formed and finite but NOT whether its symbols are real. A forged-but-finite save (valid structure, correct contentHash + mode) could set `state.current` to a phantom room/scene (the engine then renders the WHOLE observation/action loop from a location that does not exist) or `state.endingId` to a fabricated ending — both are valid STRINGS, so bug_0181's gate passes them, and `src/mcp/tools.ts` `load_game` fed `bundle.state` straight into `startSession` → engine with no referential check. The layering is deliberate: `load()` (no pack) owns finiteness; the pack-aware tools.ts layer (which builds the mode index) owns reference.
- **The change (`src/mcp/tools.ts`, +~40 lines).** ADD a top-level `assertLoadedStateRefs(mode, index, state)` and CALL it inside `startSession` AFTER `const st = state ?? initStateFor(...)`, guarded `if (state !== undefined)` — so only the untrusted LOAD-path state is validated and a fresh init state (new_game) is byte-unchanged. CYOA valid locations = `scenes.keys()` UNION `terminalIds`, valid endings = `terminalIds` (a CYOA game ends via goto+end_game, so an ENDED save's `current`/`endingId` is a TERMINAL id, NOT a scene — the fold is load-bearing). Parser/RPG valid locations = `rooms.keys()`, valid endings = `pack.endings` ids (end_game keeps the player in a room). Throw `SaveIntegrityError` on an unknown `current` or a non-null unknown `endingId`. Imports added: `SaveIntegrityError` (persist/save_load), `type CyoaIndex` (cyoa/runner), `type ParserIndex` (parser/model). This is the SoundnessBench (arXiv:2412.03154) REJECTION-DIRECTION oracle carried from finiteness (bug_0181) to REFERENCE. NEW `tests/regression/save_load_referential_integrity.test.ts` (+5 cases): 3 forged-reference REJECTIONS (CYOA phantom `current` scene → "unknown scene"; parser phantom `current` room → "unknown room"; parser fabricated `endingId` → "unknown ending") + 2 GREEN false-rejection guards (a clean mid-game CYOA save round-trips; an ENDED CYOA save with `current`=`endingId`=`ending_escape`, a terminal id, still loads — the case the `terminalIds` fold exists for). NO edit to `save_load.ts`/`conditions.ts`/`effects.ts`/`state.ts`, any validator, any matcher, any `MIN_*` floor, `GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT`, the generator, the corpus, or the scorecard.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `alchemists_tower` (parser, seeds 73/99/7)** — the assessor's rank-1 target. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T15-02-42-691Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** across three runs reaching all three declared endings — `ending_cured` (the cure, WIN 40/40) / `ending_poisoned` (drink the black phial, death) / `ending_betrayal` (crack the silver casket, the non-death greed fork). The two-step brew (steep→pour), the brass→strongbox→iron→cellar key chain, the reactive room/object prose, and the score gates (5/10/20/5=40) all confirmed live; the only notes were by-design (no confirmation on the Sierra-genre betrayal fork; the well-warned narrative poison). Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the cycle's lever was the documented structural soundness slice, not churning a clean pack.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **213 test files, 1336 tests pass** (up from 212/1331; +1 file, +5 cases). `verify:integrity` clean — `src/mcp/tools.ts` is NOT in `PROTECTED_FILES`, so **NO `VERIFIER_TOUCHED`, NO `GUARD_WEAKENED`, NO `PROTECTED_DELETED`**. No `MIN_*`/floor change, no matcher relaxed, no hash re-pin, net test count ROSE. No content/state changed, so every valid save still round-trips byte-identically — 10 packs validate 0/0; held-out corpus/generator/scorecard UNTOUCHED (no `generator_version` bump, no re-seal). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0183_save_load_referential_integrity.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** Both halves of the §16 load boundary are now sealed — FINITENESS/STRUCTURE inside `load()` (bug_0181) and REFERENCE at the pack-aware `startSession` chokepoint (this). The untrusted load→replay→play path that the contamination-free benchmark rests on is now construction-rejection-tested in both directions. Remaining lower-value deferred lever: freshness-pin `traces/benchmark/scorecard.{md,json}` (NON-load-bearing per bug_0178, low value). The standing keystone ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]) remains highest-value: run the loop ONCE against a real frontier-model author so the held-out benchmark score is populated for a row OTHER than the deterministic bot — still gated on owner API-key authorization. Otherwise BREADTH ([[fresh-pack-generator]] / a net-new pack; the blind pass's standing brevity note — "wanted more of it" — is the brief). ### Cycle result — verification-depth / RPG validator soundness (bug_0182): added a **SoundnessBench-style NEGATIVE CORPUS for `validateRpg`** — bug_0181's explicitly-named deferred lever (2) ("a SoundnessBench-style negative corpus of deliberately-UNSOUND minted RPG packs `validateRpg` must reject, the generator-boundary twin of [bug_0181's] load-boundary gate"). **The gap it closes:** `validateRpg` (`src/validate/rpg_validator.ts`) emits ten Stage-4 error codes, but an audit this cycle found SIX had NO rejection-direction witness anywhere in the suite — only COMBAT_UNWINNABLE / COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED / COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED / ENEMY_DEATH_ENDING_UNDECLARED were pinned red-going. The unwitnessed six: **MISSING_STAT, BAD_HP, ENEMY_ROOM_MISSING, ENEMY_DEATH_NOT_DEATH, SKILL_CHECK_IMPOSSIBLE, and END_GAME_UNDECLARED** (the RPG-only `on_defeat`/skill-check branch path the parser validator never scans — distinct from the parser-level END_GAME_UNDECLARED). The whole suite leaned on the ACCEPTANCE direction plus the four combat codes, so a regression silently breaking one of the six (a dropped `findings.push`, an inverted guard like `else if (!ending.death)`, a `?? 0` that swallows a missing stat) would pass every existing test GREEN — the present-but-untested-checker surface (SoundnessBench arXiv:2412.03154; the single-checker blind spot arXiv:2510.14253, [[verifier-assertion-guard]]).
- **The change (NEW `tests/regression/rpg_validator_negative_corpus.test.ts`, +8 cases).** The GREEN base is the canonical sound pack `generateRpgPack(0)` (pinned green by `rpg_generator.test.ts`), which carries every structure the defects need (two enemies with `on_defeat`, a `might` skill_check, `vars_init {hp,attack,defense,might}`, a declared non-death `ending_victory`). Each case `structuredClone`s the base and applies EXACTLY ONE defect (the bug_0118/0179 copy-mutate discipline) → asserts `validateRpg` rejects with the targeted code: delete `vars_init.defense` (MISSING_STAT); `hp=0` (BAD_HP); `enemy.room="no_such_room"` (ENEMY_ROOM_MISSING); `enemy.death_ending`→the non-death ending (ENEMY_DEATH_NOT_DEATH); `skill_check.difficulty=100` (SKILL_CHECK_IMPOSSIBLE); push `{end_game:"no_such_ending"}` onto `enemy.on_defeat` (END_GAME_UNDECLARED, RPG-only path). Plus a **differential anchor** (green base validates clean AND carries none of the six codes — proves each code is absent until its mutation introduces it) and a non-degeneracy test (every mutation flips the clean base to ≥1 error). **Teeth verified out of band:** inverting the ENEMY_DEATH_NOT_DEATH guard (`else if (!ending.death)`→`else if (false && …)`) turned the corpus RED (exactly that case + the non-degeneracy case), then the validator was restored byte-identically (git diff clean) — the SoundnessBench rejection-direction oracle at the Stage-4 generator/validator boundary.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `watchtower_road`** (CYOA, seed 29) — the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] (last dedicated pass bug_0172). **Deviated from the assessor's rank-1 (`alchemists_tower`) deliberately:** that pack was the prior cycle's dedicated pass (bug_0181, seed 7, clean 5/5); the deterministic assessor's flat 0.5 ranking cannot see recency, so the documented rotation is the intended tiebreaker. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T14-49-19-857Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** across three runs reaching `ending_truth` (justice) / `ending_captured` (jailed) / `ending_escape` (walk away) by genuinely different routes; state-gating (`show_letter`/`show_papers` correctly ABSENT without the sealed letter), one-shot self-removing actions, reactive hermit re-entry, and the two redundant evidence sources (cellar ledger vs hermit-broken letter) all confirmed live. The only notes were by-design (elided north-road traversal after the beacon; the dramatically-effective letter-to-the-named-sergeant fork). Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the cycle's lever was the documented structural slice, not churning a clean pack.
