### Cycle result — engine soundness / load-integrity (bug_0181): CLOSED the §16 "integrity at load" hole — `load()` (`src/persist/save_load.ts`) now STRUCTURALLY + FINITENESS validates `bundle.state` before returning it to the engine, REJECTING (never coercing) any malformed or non-finite save. This de-bundles cleanly off the bug_0179/0180 one-pack curated-combat CHURN onto the last genuinely-open engine soundness hole the ultraplan re-aim (cycle #4) identified. **The gap it closes:** `load()` checked only `version`/`contentHash`/`mode`, then cast the untrusted payload straight in (`const bundle = parsed as SaveBundle; … return bundle;`) — the contentHash check guarded WHICH pack, nothing guarded WHETHER the state is well-formed. The effects-layer `guardFinite` (`effects.ts`) only fires on effect APPLICATION during play, never on a load-injected value, so a forged save bypassed every finite-guard: `JSON.parse('{"…":1e999}')` → `Infinity`, which flows into `conditions.ts:75` `var_gte` `(state.vars[name] ?? 0) >= value` and makes EVERY `var_gte` gate always-true (NaN makes every `var_*` always-false). The MCP `load_game` (`src/mcp/tools.ts`) feeds an arbitrary save string's `bundle.state` straight into `startSession` → engine, so this was the single unguarded entry point into the otherwise-pure deterministic engine.
- **The change (`src/persist/save_load.ts`, +61 lines).** ADD a module-level `GameStateSchema` (zod, `.strict()`, mirroring `GameState`/`ObjectRuntime` field-for-field) — the load-bearing gates being `vars: z.record(z.number().finite())` and finite `seed`/`step` (rejects `Infinity`/`-Infinity`/`NaN`); plus a single `safeParse`/throw guard inside `load()` AFTER the byte-unchanged version/contentHash/mode checks and BEFORE `return bundle`, throwing `SaveIntegrityError`. Validates WITHOUT substituting `parsedState.data`, so every valid engine state still round-trips byte-identically (validation runs before hashing — no hash moves, no `HASH_PIN`/curated re-pin). This is the SoundnessBench (arXiv:2412.03154) REJECTION-DIRECTION oracle for the load boundary and closes the ASL (arXiv:2510.14253) "present-but-incomplete checker fed only well-behaved input" surface. NEW `tests/regression/save_integrity_adversarial.test.ts` (+10 cases): 8 forged-save REJECTIONS (the `vars.hp=1e999`→`Infinity` WITNESS citing `conditions.ts:75`, a NaN var, a `-Infinity` var, `seed=Infinity`, a wrong-type flag, missing `current`, malformed `objectState`, an unknown `objectState` key) + 2 GREEN round-trips (clean micro with `hashState` unchanged, AND a rich finite state with populated vars/flags/inventory/objectState/journal/questStage incl. a negative var). Also fixed one stale doc ref the ultraplan introduced in `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` (`src/demo/micro.js` → the real `.ts` source) so the assessor doc-staleness radar stays disarmed. NO edit to `conditions.ts`/`effects.ts`/`state.ts`, any matcher, any `MIN_*` floor, `GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT`, the generator, the corpus, or the scorecard.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on `alchemists_tower` (parser, seed 7)** — the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] (rotation note from bug_0180 named it next). Clean, **clarity 5/5**, no fix indicated. Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the cycle's lever was the documented structural soundness slice, not churning a clean pack.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **211 test files, 1323 tests pass** (up from 210/1322 with the prior cycle's pre-existing doc-staleness failure now fixed; +1 file, +10 cases net). `verify:integrity` static OK 0/0; drift `--against HEAD` = **OK 0 error(s), 1 warning(s): a single non-blocking `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` for `src/persist/save_load.ts`** — a STRENGTHENING, **NO `GUARD_WEAKENED`, NO `PROTECTED_DELETED`**, no `MIN_*`/floor change, no matcher relaxed, no hash re-pin, net test count ROSE. No curated content touched (10 packs validate 0/0); held-out corpus/generator/scorecard UNTOUCHED (no `generator_version` bump, no re-seal). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0181_save_load_integrity_gate.yaml`.

Per the ultraplan's Deferred list: (1) freshness-pin the committed `traces/benchmark/scorecard.{md,json}` (the last unguarded committed benchmark artifact, mirroring bug_0176's manifest guard); (2) a SoundnessBench-style negative corpus of deliberately-UNSOUND minted RPG packs `validateRpg` must reject (the generator-boundary twin of this load-boundary gate). The standing keystone ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]) remains highest-value: run the loop ONCE against a real frontier-model author so the held-out benchmark score is populated for a row OTHER than the deterministic bot — still gated on owner API-key authorization. ### Cycle result — verification-depth / curated combat (bug_0180): PINNED **the barrow's HEADLINE claim** — that The Sunken Barrow's reaver-shade ward (+3 defense, bug_0113) is MECHANICALLY load-bearing, so "preparation decides the wight fight" (bug_0102) — with a **SAME-STEP (PRNG-clean)** concrete-play witness. This is the exact slice the previous cycle (bug_0179) named as the obvious next structural lever: the barrow's parallel to The Cold Forge's founder's-plate witness, bringing the two curated RPG packs to verification parity. **The gap it closes:** the barrow's death→victory flip was already pinned by concrete play, but ONLY via `rpg_barrow_wight_prep_matters` comparing the UNDER-ARMED route to the WARDED route — which take DIFFERENT numbers of steps (the warded route detours west + runs the shade dialogue). The engine PRNG is (seed, step)-keyed (`src/rpg/combat.ts`), so the two routes draw DIFFERENT fight rolls — the flip is real but RNG-confounded (apples-to-oranges, the precise weakness bug_0179 fixed for cold_forge). The only existing SAME-STEP isolation (`rpg_barrow_ward_reliable_survival` case 3) covers the MARGINAL +1 (def4→def5), never the FULL +3 ward (def2→def5) the headline rests on. So a regression that re-softened the wight or weakened the ward's `inc_var: defense` could shift the route-comparison's outcome via the route's different rolls and still read plausibly green.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sunken_barrow (RPG, seed 3)** — the assessor's rank-1 target AND the rotation's least-recently-played pack (last dedicated pass bug_0170). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T14-16-58-318Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** across two playthroughs — the "right way" (warded → ending_victory 50/50) and the doom fork (skip the shade, beat the wight on good rolls, then prise the sarcophagus → ending_woken, score frozen 25/50, death). The tester ran the warded-vs-unwarded comparison directly and noted "the unwarded wight fight is winnable [on this seed], so the warding is a strong-but-optional advantage" — i.e. exactly the load-bearing-ness this cycle then locked. Reactive room text, exposed combat math (the +3 ward visibly cut incoming 7→5/hit), gated exits, the retryable might-check slab, and both scored endings all confirmed live. Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); the only note was the standing brevity/wall-of-text-monologue observation (the shade's counsel is carefully honesty-tuned across bug_0119/0132 — trimming it risks reintroducing the over-promise, so left alone). Per the loop doc the lever was the named structural slice, not churning the clean pack.
- **The change (NEW `tests/regression/rpg_barrow_ward_load_bearing.test.ts`, +4 cases).** (1) the pack still validates green; (2) FAITHFUL LINKAGE — asking the shade's wight topic raises defense 2→5 (+3) and sets heard_warding by real play; (3) THE FULL WARD FLIPS A LETHAL FIGHT — at **seed 2**, from the identical under-armed pre-fight state (def2, full HP, AT the guard crypt — a fixed step), the UNWARDED copy is killed (ending_fallen) while the WARDED copy (def 5) — same step, same step-keyed d6 rolls, ONLY the +3 defense differs — survives (wight_slain, hp>0); (4) THE CUSHION IS REAL — across seeds where both win (1,3,5,6,7,13,16,19) the warded player ends with STRICTLY MORE HP. **Soundness:** two copies of ONE under-armed pre-fight state at the IDENTICAL step (the bug_0113-case-3 / bug_0179 copy-mutate pattern), differing only in defense; per-round wight damage `max(1, d6+5-playerDef)` is monotone DECREASING in defense, so the def-5 copy can only take ≤ damage per round — never a lucky-roll artefact. Probe over seeds 1-40 (committed PRNG, throwaway): the ward FLIPS death→life at seeds 2/8/9/10/11/12/14/15 (among others) and ALWAYS leaves strictly more HP on both-win seeds — consistent with bug_0102/0113's "decisive but not guaranteed" (no combat_guaranteed; ~12% of warded seeds still lose by design, recoverable via save §8.7).
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **210 test files, 1313 tests pass** (up from 209/1309; +1 file, +4 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK 0/0; **NO GUARD_WEAKENED, NO VERIFIER_TOUCHED** — no protected/source/engine/validator/schema/content file touched (sunken_barrow.yaml byte-unchanged, content_hash f24f792c… unchanged); the cycle adds a regression test + a bug artifact only. No `MIN_*`/floor change, no matcher relaxed, no hash re-pin, net test count ROSE. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0180_sunken_barrow_ward_load_bearing.yaml`.

That curated-combat verification slice is closed. ### Cycle result — verification-depth / curated combat (bug_0179): PINNED the **third, previously-unguarded tier of bug_0101's "preparation decides the fight" claim** — that The Cold Forge's founder's plate (+2 defense) is MECHANICALLY load-bearing, not cosmetic — with a concrete-play regression witness. bug_0101 retuned the slag sentinel (hp18/atk7) so three preparation tiers matter (under-armed atk4/def2 "lethal more often than not"; spirit +2 attack atk6/def2 "the decisive lever ~45%→~90%"; founder's plate on top atk6/def4 "near-certain win with a comfortable HP cushion"). Two tiers were already pinned by concrete play (cold_forge_sentinel_prep_matters, bug_0101: under-armed→ending_fallen / +2-attack→ending_victory, both seed 1). The PLATE tier had NO mechanical oracle — it was verified only by a throwaway 40-seed probe (`__probe.ts`, long deleted) and is otherwise pinned only for its ROOM-TEXT nudge (cold_forge_unworn_plate_nudge, bug_0118). So a regression that re-softened the sentinel, dropped the plate's `inc_var: defense`, or made the plate cosmetic again (the EXACT bug bug_0101 was created to kill — "preparation was cosmetic") would pass every existing check green: the best-case winnability validator only errors if even the LUCKIEST run loses (bug_0113 semantics), and all-endings-reachable only proves the death ending is reachable *somehow*. This is the curated-pack counterpart to the generator's load-bearing-buff witness (rpg_generator_cumulative_survival, bug_0174, Phase B/C), and it operationalizes this cycle's mandated blind-playtest theme directly.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on cold_forge (RPG, seed 7)** — the assessor's rank-1 target. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T14-01-18-745Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** across two playthroughs (fully-prepared and deliberately under-prepared), both reaching ending_victory 50/50; the spirit's one-shot +2-attack counsel, the founder's plate, the milestone scoring (sentinel 15 / grate 15 / heart 20), the retryable might check, and the gated exits all confirmed live. The only note was the standing design observation — "preparation shifts the odds heavily but isn't strictly required … the death path is luck-dependent for the careless" — which is BY DESIGN (cold_forge is deliberately a gamble, NOT combat_guaranteed) and is exactly the load-bearing-ness this cycle then locked with a test. Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per the loop doc the lever was the named structural slice, not churning the clean pack.
- **The change (NEW `tests/regression/cold_forge_plate_load_bearing.test.ts`, +4 cases).** (1) the pack still validates green; (2) FAITHFUL LINKAGE — donning the plate raises defense 2→4 by real play (sets plate_donned + inc_var defense); (3) THE PLATE FLIPS A LETHAL FIGHT — at **seed 4**, from the identical +2-attack pre-fight state (atk6/def2, full HP, fixed step), the UNPLATED copy is killed (ending_fallen) while the PLATED copy (def 4) — same step, same step-keyed d6 rolls, ONLY the +2 defense differs — survives (sentinel_stilled, hp>0); (4) THE CUSHION IS REAL — across seeds where both win (0,1,2,3,7) the plated player ends with STRICTLY MORE HP. **Soundness:** the engine PRNG is (seed, step)-keyed, and the plate route's side-detour shifts the step, so playing the two routes would draw different rolls — apples-to-oranges. So the test fights two copies of ONE pre-fight state at the IDENTICAL step (the bug_0118-case-5 copy-mutate pattern), differing only in defense; per-round damage `max(1, d6+7-def)` is monotone DECREASING in defense, so the def-4 copy can only take ≤ damage per round — never a lucky-roll artefact. Probe over seeds 0–50 (committed PRNG): plate ALWAYS ends with strictly more HP (+2–4 cushion) and FLIPS death→life at seeds 4/22/26/32/36/44/45/48/50; both die only at 25/40 — consistent with "near-certain", not guaranteed.

No `MIN_*`/floor change, no matcher relaxed, no hash re-pin, net test count ROSE. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0179_cold_forge_plate_load_bearing.yaml`.

The sibling curated RPG pack **sunken_barrow** has the analogous "preparation is a real gamble" design (bug_0102) and the barrow ward (bug_0113/0119) — a parallel load-bearing-buff witness there is the obvious next slice if a structural lever is wanted. Otherwise the standing keystone ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]) remains highest-value and unchanged: run the loop ONCE against a real frontier-model author so the held-out benchmark score is populated for a row OTHER than the deterministic bot — still gated on owner API-key authorization. ### Cycle result — verification-depth / benchmark (bug_0178): GUARDED the **benchmark headline scores against silent regression AND inflation** — bug_0177's explicitly-named next-focus lever ("surfacing the headline … so a score regression becomes a [guarded] candidate"). bug_0177 shipped the per-split headline (`summary.score`, curated 49.1% / held-out 33.5% @runs=12) but pinned it for SELF-CONSISTENCY only (score == an independent re-mean of the rows; curated > 0). That guard is BLIND to the two failure modes that actually matter for a benchmark, both of which leave it GREEN: (1) **COLLAPSE** — an engine/validator/generator/content regression that breaks the deterministic bot's progress so the headline craters toward ~0; a cratered number still "equals the mean of the rows" and is still > 0. (2) **INFLATION of the held-out split** — the contamination-free corpus drifting into packs the greedy no-planning bot trivially completes (a generator "deepening" that drops gating), so the held-out score climbs toward/past the curated baseline, DESTROYING the "distinct lower number" property bug_0177 states is "exactly the contamination-free signal". This is the score-level twin of the DGM "swap in a degraded eval distribution" launder the verifier-integrity guard fights at the hash level ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). Realised as the ENFORCED verification bar (a red-going test), the stronger reading of "the automated verification is the bar" — not the assessor's advisory ranking (which would also add a heavy per-cycle full-scorecard build). KEY-FREE, deterministic, PURELY ADDITIVE (`src/afk/benchmark.ts` byte-unchanged — the guard reads the existing summary).
- **The change (NEW `tests/regression/benchmark_headline_no_regression.test.ts`, +4 cases).** Builds the scorecard at the documented runs=12 reading and asserts WIDE bands that catch a real regression, not benign drift — each mapped to a concrete failure the self-consistency test can't see: curated FLOOR only (>= 0.40; the bot improving must never fail, so no ceiling); held-out two-sided BAND ([0.25, 0.45] — collapse below, difficulty-destroyed above); a run-count-ROBUST relative invariant `held_out.score < curated.score` with a MATERIAL gap (>= 0.05; the curated→held-out gap holds ~15pts across runs 8/12/50), so held-out drifting up toward curated fires even within its absolute band; plus the two-split shape (curated before held-out) and per-split non-vacuity. The scorecard is deterministic at a fixed run count (confirmed: two builds @12 byte-identical), so the bands are reproducible, not flaky.
- **Also refreshed the long-stale committed sample scorecard** (`traces/benchmark/scorecard.json` + `.md`), frozen since **bug_0142 (~35 cycles)**: it carried 27 curated-only rows with NO held-out split (added bug_0165) and NO headline (added bug_0177) — actively misrepresenting what `npm run benchmark` reports today. Regenerated via the sanctioned `npm run benchmark -- --out` at its existing runs=50 default → 66 rows (10 curated + 12 held-out packs × 3 cells) + the `## Headline` table (curated 47.7%, held-out 31.7% @runs=50). NON-load-bearing (no test reads it; only `bin/benchmark.ts` writes it), so ZERO verification risk — a surfaced deterministic refresh, not a re-pinned check.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sealed_crypt (parser, seed 7)** — the assessor's rank-1 target. ### Cycle result — benchmark / true-goal (bug_0177): rolled the benchmark's ~30 per-pack rows up into **one headline score per split** (`src/afk/benchmark.ts`). The successor-benchmark thesis (ULTRAPLAN 2026-06-02, restated in the file docstring) names "a single, comparable NUMBER per (pack, agent)" as the PRECONDITION for a benchmark and calls the held-out rows "the contamination-free signal the benchmark ultimately reports" — but `buildScorecard` only ever emitted per-row cells, so the figure the thesis says it reports had to be averaged out of the table BY HAND and the held-out headline was never stated. This closes that gap: a deterministic, purely-additive per-split aggregate. KEY-FREE; `BenchmarkRow` is byte-unchanged so every existing row pin holds.
- **The change.** New `BenchmarkSummary` type + `Scorecard.summary[]`: one entry per split (curated before held-out), each aggregating ONLY the PRIMARY baseline cell (`coverage` / graph **shown** — `random` is graph-agnostic noise, `hidden` is the auxiliary spatial axis, so neither is the figure a model is primarily compared against; `PRIMARY_CELL` names it). `score` = mean over the split's packs of each pack's own equal-weight mean of (completion_rate, ending_coverage, scene_coverage); `mean_turns_to_end` is deliberately EXCLUDED (un-normalised efficiency axis, not a 0–1 progress fraction). `renderMarkdown` now prints a `## Headline` table (**curated 49.1%, held-out 33.5%** at runs=12 — the held-out being a DISTINCT, lower number is exactly the contamination-free signal made legible) above the renamed `## Per-pack rows` table.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on wreckers_light (CYOA, seed 13)** — the assessor's rank-1 target. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T13-33-08-730Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** across FOUR runs reaching all four declared endings (`ending_saved` relight the lamp / `ending_wrecker` the false low light / `ending_mercy` tend the dying keeper / `ending_drowned` the elected descent) and both `knows_truth` states; reactive variant ordering confirmed live (mantel reads bare after taking the striker, cask reads dipped after filling, the false-light action appears only with oil+striker), action-gating and the journal gate sound. "No bugs, no friction worth fixing — among the strongest packs I've playtested." Blind-clean on a clean pack = the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per the loop doc the cycle's lever was the named true goal (the benchmark), not churning a clean pack.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **207 test files, 1301 tests pass** (up from 206/1295; +1 file, +6 cases via `tests/regression/benchmark_headline_score.test.ts`). That guard pins: one summary per present split (curated→held-out, primary-cell-tagged); `score` == an INDEPENDENTLY-recomputed mean over the split's primary-cell rows of each row's mean fraction (anti-vacuity oracle, recomputed WITHOUT the production path) plus the three component means; score ∈ [0,1] and curated > 0 (genuinely played, not stubbed); the score EXCLUDES the auxiliary random/hidden cells (must not equal the all-cell mean when they differ); the `## Headline` table carries each split's bold percent score; a custom cell set without the primary cell yields an empty summary while the per-row table stays authoritative. No hash re-pin, no test disabled/weakened, no check routed around — the two markdown row-count assertions (`tests/unit/benchmark.test.ts`, `benchmark_held_out_split.test.ts`) were TIGHTENED (scoped to `## Per-pack rows` so the new summary table is not miscounted), not loosened. `verify:integrity` static OK 0/0; no validator/engine/schema/generator/corpus change; no curated content touched (10 packs validate 0/0). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0177_benchmark_headline_score.yaml` + the new regression test.
- **Next focus.** Keystone unchanged ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]): run the loop ONCE against a real frontier-model author so the held-out score is populated for a row OTHER than the deterministic bot — the first genuinely contamination-free datapoint the headline now exists to report. Lighter structural levers: a held-out-vs-curated DELTA line, and surfacing the headline in `src/afk/assessor.ts` so a score regression becomes a ranked candidate.

### Cycle result — verifier-integrity / trust-tightening (bug_0176): brought the **sealed held-out corpus manifest (`corpus/manifest.json`) under the verifier-integrity guard's `PROTECTED_FILES`** — the standing trust-tightening lever (c) deferred across bug_0172/0173/0174. The manifest is the OUTPUT of `bin/seal-corpus.ts` (protected since bug_0167) and the committed pin the contamination-free benchmark rests on (bug_0163/0165): each entry fixes a generated pack `content_hash` + `generator_version` for a frozen seed window, defining the held-out split `src/afk/benchmark.ts` scores against. The generators that MINT it were already guarded, but the manifest itself was the LAST load-bearing committed verification artifact OUTSIDE the guard — so a manual re-seal that rewrites these hashes WITHOUT a generator change (the launder that swaps in a degraded eval distribution and re-pins to make `held_out_corpus_sealed` go green) would slip past the drift check unseen. KEY-FREE, deterministic, purely additive (one `PROTECTED_FILES` entry + a regression test + a bug artifact; the manifest itself byte-unchanged — no re-seal).
- **The change (`scripts/verify-integrity.ts`).** Added `"corpus/manifest.json"` to `PROTECTED_FILES`. Adding an entry is pure TIGHTENING (never a GUARD_WEAKENED): deleting the manifest is now a hard `PROTECTED_DELETED` error, re-sealing it surfaces a non-blocking `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` for review (a deliberate deepen cycle behind a `generator_version` bump, which also re-seals, stays legal — just visibly), and dropping it back out is itself the DGM "edit-the-checker" launder (`GUARD_WEAKENED`). The protecting comment is kept free of apostrophes/quotes/brackets so `parseGuardConstants` still extracts the full literal — locked by the live-constants round-trip in `tests/unit/verifier_integrity.test.ts` (which re-parses the live file text and asserts it `toEqual` the imported list).

"No fixes needed." - **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **206 test files, 1295 tests pass** (up from 205/1289; +1 file, +6 cases via `tests/regression/sealed_corpus_manifest_protected.test.ts`, which exercises the REAL pure detectors on the REAL `PROTECTED_FILES`: membership + on-disk existence + no `PROTECTED_MISSING`, DELETION→`PROTECTED_DELETED` hard error, RE-SEAL→`VERIFIER_TOUCHED` warning-not-block, removal→`GUARD_WEAKENED`; mirrors bug_0167). `verify:integrity` static OK 0/0; drift vs HEAD OK with exactly 1 EXPECTED `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` warning (`scripts/verify-integrity.ts` is itself protected) — **NO GUARD_WEAKENED**, no `PROTECTED_DELETED`, no `MIN_*`/`GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT` floor change, no matcher relaxed, net test count ROSE. ### Cycle result — content_fix / breadth (bug_0175): the Chekhov pistol FIRES — promoted the captain's cased pistol from an inert, described-but-dead object to a real, fully-telegraphed **FIFTH act** at the cask, `ending_irons` ("Under the Gun" — rule the boat by lethal force). This is the ONE concrete finding of this cycle's mandated blind pass (dead_reckoning, seed 7): the pistol is named in the chest prose (base AND the knows_course variant) but had no affordance — "a curious player may reach for it," exactly the "clue that points nowhere" the protocol's WATCH-FOR list flags. Rather than manufacture an unrelated change to a clean pack, the cycle gives the existing named object an affordance, which ALSO presses the standing breadth lever (branching, not length) one notch on the project's first two-axis pack ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]).
- **The change (`content/cyoa/pack/dead_reckoning.yaml`, content-only).** New `take_pistol` choice at the chest sets `has_pistol` (a THIRD optional take, INDEPENDENT and order-free vs the two truths `knows_course`/`knows_pilot`); taking it unlocks a fifth telegraphed cask act `draw_pistol` (gated on `has_pistol`) → new `ending_irons`. This is the investigate-to-unlock SYMMETRY of `trust_pilot` (absent until you take the pistol, present after — a payoff reserved for the player who opened the chest). `ending_irons` is a thematically DISTINCT fifth stance — survival bought with FEAR, never converging on holdfast's peace-by-fairness or adrift's greed — and fires a `knows_course` reactive variant (a TRUE course enforced at pistol-point vs a blind gamble held the same way), matching the pack's paired-epilogue discipline. New `has_pistol` chest text variants (both-flags FIRST so a single-flag variant never shadows it — cyoa_variant_shadowing) keep the "cased pistol" prose honest after the gun is taken (no stale dangling object).
- **Mandated blind pass ran on dead_reckoning (CYOA, seed 7)** — the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] (last dedicated pass bug_0165) and the assessor's rank-1 target. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T13-12-02-110Z/playtest.md`. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** across 5 sessions / all four prior endings (landfall/jonah/holdfast both-variants/adrift); flags set once and persisted, no loops/dead-ends, determinism held. Genuine four-stance moral fork, "clean, polished, thematically sharp." The ONE concrete finding (the inert pistol) became this cycle's fix; the soft intro-stakes-vs-ration-success note is deliberate and self-narrated, left as authored.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **205 test files, 1289 tests pass** (up from 205/1285; +4 cases in `dead_reckoning_branching.test.ts`). The bug_0121 exhaustive CYOA solver auto-discovers the pack and now reaches all FIVE declared endings (`ending_irons` included) with none undeclared and no state-cap-out; bug_0145 variant-liveness confirms the new chest + ending_irons reactive variants are each the first match in some reachable state; cyoa_inert_flag passes (`has_pistol` is read by the draw_pistol gate + chest variants — NOT inert); cyoa_variant_shadowing passes. Validator 0/0 on all 10 packs. NO engine/validator/schema/generator/corpus change; this pack pins NO test hash, so NO hash re-pinned (the pack's own content_hash moved bdab6d27…→7b2ed14d by the deliberate content edit — a new pack revision, not a weakened check); no protected file touched, no GUARD_WEAKENED. ### Cycle result — verification-depth (bug_0174): added a **VALIDATOR-INDEPENDENT concrete-play SECOND WITNESS** to the RPG generator's cumulative-survival `combat_guaranteed` promise — bug_0173's explicitly-named next-focus lever (a) ("an optional validator-INDEPENDENT exhaustive **cumulative-survival cross-check** … prove the fully-buffed best/worst bracket survives both fights by concrete play, a second witness beside the validator's arithmetic"). Until now the v3 gauntlet's guarantee (a prepared player atk6/def4/hp20 survives BOTH keepers cumulatively) was proven only ONE way — ARITHMETICALLY, by `validateRpg`'s `COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED` upper bound (bug_0172) — and EVERY test of it (`tests/unit/rpg_generator.test.ts`, `rpg_generator_guaranteed_gauntlet.test.ts`) leans on that SAME oracle, so a subtle error in the validator's combat math would pass an unsound guarantee AND be rubber-stamped by every test trusting it transitively (the single-checker blind spot the verifier-soundness literature names; arXiv 2510.14253, [[verifier-assertion-guard]]). The new witness corroborates the guarantee by CONCRETE PLAY through the production engine, never importing the validator. KEY-FREE, deterministic, PURELY ADDITIVE (a new regression test + a bug artifact — no source/validator/engine/schema/generator/corpus/protected-file change, no hash re-pin).
- **The change (NEW `tests/regression/rpg_generator_cumulative_survival.test.ts`, 12 seeds / all 5 themes).** It plays the generated gauntlet via the shared `exhaustiveEndingsMulti` solver under the player's WORST combat rolls (own strike d6=1, damage taken d6=6 — real legal faces), staged to match the validator's ACTUAL claim (prepare fully, THEN fight — a guaranteed-sufficient STRATEGY, not a property of any play that merely ends buffed): **Phase A** reaches a READY state by exploring everything EXCEPT ATTACK — fully buffed (best reachable atk/def derived BY PLAY as the max over the combat-free region, never hardcoded) and pristine (HP full, no keeper engaged); **Phase B** from READY exhaustively plays every worst-roll continuation — NO death ending reachable (the independent counterpart to `COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED`) AND some continuation fells BOTH keepers while still standing (hp>0, warden flag set) so it is non-vacuous; **Phase C** from the START (combat allowed) confirms BOTH keeper deaths stay reachable under worst rolls by under-prepared play, so the guarantee is earned by preparation, not by toothless enemies. The WORST regime runs ALONE (never unioned with BEST), so survival is never reached via a lucky roll.
- **The "verify, don't assume" payoff.** The FIRST draft defined "prepared" as best stats AT THE TERMINAL and asserted ALL such terminals survive — it FAILED on every seed (`ending_fallen_guardian`), correctly exposing that the claim is too strong: a player can don the ward, grind the FIRST keeper at low attack (max(1,1+4−4)=1 dmg/strike ⇒ 6 rounds ⇒ ~15 damage taken), survive, THEN take the attack buff and die to the warden — ending atk6/def4 but never "prepared for the fight it lost". The validator's bound credits BEST stats in EACH fight, so that ordering is correctly OUTSIDE the promise; the corrected witness stages preparation BEFORE combat (Phase A pristine ready state). **Teeth verified out of band:** a throwaway run mutating the warden to hp30/atk20 made Phase B surface a prepared death (`preparedDeaths.length > 0`), confirming the NEGATIVE assertion is non-vacuous; the scratch was deleted.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **white_stag** (CYOA, seed 7 — this cycle's assessor rank-1 target AND the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], last dedicated pass bug_0164) came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical/design bugs across FOUR routes to the declared endings** (`ending_offering` the informed kneel / `ending_lost` the rotten-ice drowning / `ending_quarry` reached TWICE with knowledge-aware divergent text — ignorant dramatic-irony kill vs knowing tragic-culpability kill; `lower_bow` the plain spare present/reachable). The tester confirmed the `knows_truth` knowledge-flag gating, state-reactive tarn/bluff prose, idempotent set_flag/add_journal on re-entry, and the well-telegraphed rotten-ice death all live; zero findings, "production-quality". A blind-clean pass on a clean pack is the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop pivots to structural work, and the validator-independent cross-check was bug_0173's documented standing lever (a). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T12-55-04-511Z/playtest.md`.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on white_stag (CYOA, seed 7).** Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T12-55-04-511Z/playtest.md`. 5/5 clarity, 5/5 enjoyment, ZERO bugs, four routes/endings reached (incl. the shared-ending divergent-text quarry) — blind-clean; no content defect, so the cycle's lever was the structural verification-depth slice.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **205 test files, 1285 tests pass** (up from 204/1273; +1 file, +12 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK 0/0; **NO GUARD_WEAKENED, NO VERIFIER_TOUCHED** (no protected/source file touched — the cycle adds only a regression test + a bug artifact), no `MIN_*`/`GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT` floor change, no matcher relaxed, net test count ROSE. No curated content touched (10 packs validate 0/0); no curated hash re-pin (sunken_barrow `f24f792c…` + cold_forge `8590fefd…` unchanged); no corpus re-seal. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0174_rpg_generator_cumulative_survival.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** The RPG cumulative-survival guarantee now has TWO independent oracles (validator arithmetic + concrete worst-roll play), so a divergence surfaces as a test failure rather than a silently-trusted unsound guarantee — the generator-soundness arc is now defense-in-depth. Remaining named levers: (b) the held-out vs curated **DELTA** summary metric in `renderMarkdown` (stale since bug_0165; still load-bearing only once a keyed non-bot agent row exists — rejected as information-free key-free in bug_0172's ultraplan, so deprioritized); (c) optional trust tightening — add `corpus/manifest.json` to `PROTECTED_FILES`. The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains highest-value overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]). ### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0173): RE-TUNED the **RPG generator's two-fight gauntlet to SOUNDLY set `meta.combat_guaranteed: true`** — the explicit next-focus bug_0172 named ("re-tune the RPG generator so its two-fight gauntlet SOUNDLY sets `meta.combat_guaranteed: true` ... making every RPG mint exercise the new cumulative upper bound as a GREEN case"). bug_0171 deepened the generator to a two-fight gauntlet but left it an UNDECLARED gamble — each fight winnable on best stats, but the then per-fight guarantee bound couldn't see cumulative HP drain, so the flag stayed UNSET. bug_0172 closed that validator blind spot (`COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED`, cumulative-HP-aware) but its hardest check ran ONLY against frozen witness packs, never the per-cycle MOVING distribution. v3 re-tunes the two enemies so a FULLY-PREPARED descent (the OPTIONAL spirit +2 attack + cell ward +2 defense ⇒ best reachable atk6/def4, hp20) survives BOTH fights on EVERY roll AND cumulatively, sets the flag, and bumps `generator_version` 2→3 + re-seals — so the validator's hardest check is now a per-mint GREEN obligation, not a frozen target (the never-frozen-target property the contamination-free benchmark thesis rests on; [[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, deterministic.
- **The change (`src/gen/rpg_generator.ts`, v2→v3).** `RPG_GENERATOR_VERSION` 2→3; `meta.combat_guaranteed: true` added; foe (gallery sentinel) hp 12→6 / attack 5→1 / defense 2→4; warden (span guardian) hp 18→9 / attack 7→2 / defense 2→4. The high enemy defense is the hinge: a PREPARED player (atk6/def4) takes worst-case 3 (foe) + 8 (warden) = **11 < 20 HP cumulatively** → the gauntlet guarantee passes GREEN; an UNDER-prepared player (atk4/def2) who skips the buffs faces a real, lethal gamble (foe worst ≤25, warden worst ≤48), so **both death endings stay reachable** and the 3-ending census holds. Preparation is now a guaranteed-sufficient strategy, not a hope — the player-experience contract every RPG blind playtest asks for. No engine/validator/schema/score-economy/room/ending change. Corpus re-sealed (the 4 held-out RPG packs genrpg_0..3 → generator_version 3 + new hashes; cyoa/parser entries byte-unchanged); the generator edit surfaces an EXPECTED non-blocking **VERIFIER_TOUCHED** (PROTECTED_FILES, bug_0167), the sanctioned deepen workflow, NOT a GUARD_WEAKENED.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **clockwork_heist** (CYOA, seed 7 — this cycle's assessor rank-1 target AND the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], last dedicated pass bug_0163) came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs across FOUR routes to all declared endings** (`ending_truth` via the crawlspace hidden letter / `ending_truth` again via the vault displayed letter with DIFFERENT reactive prose / `Rich` the clean pick-the-lock-and-grab-gold heist / `Caught` the force-the-door alarm). The tester confirmed item-gated lockpicks, state-reactive foyer/gallery prose, the force-vs-finesse fair choice, and the letter-vs-gold moral fork all live. Its lone note — the watchman/`ticks` clock is atmospheric, not a hard time-gate — is the KNOWN "cosmetic clock" false-positive magnet ([[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]]: clockwork's timing teeth are real + test-locked, do NOT re-fix), explicitly not-a-bug. A blind-clean pass on a clean pack is the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop pivots to structural work, and the RPG-generator guarantee re-tune was bug_0172's documented next slice. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T12-36-58-503Z/playtest.md`.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on clockwork_heist (CYOA, seed 7).** Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T12-36-58-503Z/playtest.md`. 5/5 clarity, 4/5 enjoyment, ZERO bugs, four routes/endings reached — blind-clean; the one note (atmospheric clock) is the known cosmetic-clock false positive, rated not-a-bug, so no content defect and the cycle's lever was the structural generator guarantee re-tune.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** NEW `tests/regression/rpg_generator_guaranteed_gauntlet.test.ts` (+12 cases) pins, across 12 seeds: (1) every mint sets `combat_guaranteed === true`; (2) validateRpg emits NONE of COMBAT_UNWINNABLE / COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED / COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED on the real pack (GREEN); (3) NON-VACUITY — bumping ONLY the span guardian's attack to 7 (each fight still passes ALONE at 18<20) makes the JOINT 3+18=21≥20 trip COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED and NOT the per-fight codes, proving the green is the cumulative bound actually holding; (4) the promise is CONDITIONAL ON PREPARATION — the best/worst-roll bracket still reaches all 3 endings. `tests/unit/rpg_generator.test.ts` (24 seeds, validateRpg-clean + 3-ending census) and `tests/regression/rpg_generator_two_fight_gauntlet.test.ts` (the v2 SHAPE guard; version pin relaxed 2→`>=2` since the two-fight structure is preserved across the v3 stat re-tune) stay green. `tests/unit/mcp_generate_rpg_pack.test.ts` needed NO edit (room 7 / enemy 2 / ending 3 / max_score 40-80 / vars_init unchanged). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0173_rpg_generator_guaranteed_gauntlet.yaml`.

No curated content touched (10 packs validate 0/0); no curated hash re-pin (sunken_barrow `f24f792c…` + cold_forge `8590fefd…` unchanged); `tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts` zero diff (curated packs are single-enemy and don't opt in). The corpus hash change is a deliberate, surfaced re-seal behind a version bump; re-seal is deterministic (a second seal produced no further diff).
- **Next suggested focus.** The RPG generator now exercises the FULL combat-validator depth as a per-mint GREEN obligation — cumulative gauntlet guarantee included — so all three generators are deepened AND their hardest checks are live moving targets. Remaining named levers: (a) an optional validator-INDEPENDENT exhaustive **cumulative-survival cross-check** in `tests/regression/support/exhaustive_endings.ts` (prove the fully-buffed best/worst bracket survives both fights by concrete play, a second witness beside the validator's arithmetic); (b) the held-out vs curated **DELTA** summary metric in `renderMarkdown` (stale since bug_0165; load-bearing only once a keyed non-bot agent row exists); (c) optional trust tightening — add `corpus/manifest.json` to `PROTECTED_FILES`. ### Cycle result — ULTRAPLAN re-aim #3 / validator-soundness (bug_0172): CLOSED the **cumulative-HP soundness blind spot in `validateRpg`'s opt-in combat guarantee** — the genuinely-next-harder VALIDATOR-deepening check bug_0171 explicitly named. This was a SATURATION-triggered ULTRAPLAN cycle: the deterministic assessor sits at its 0.5 floor by construction and the content backlog is blind-saturated, so per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop re-aimed via a bounded local ultraplan (4 repo reviewers — engine/determinism · content/authoring · verification&benchmark · loop/strategy — + 2 web researchers → 1 synthesis), grounded in `docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md` + `docs/ROADMAP.md` and verified against the live tree. **All 6 reviewers + both researchers converged on this lever and REJECTED the held-out-vs-curated DELTA metric (lever a)** as information-free key-free (the only offline agent is the deterministic bot — 0% on parser/RPG, byte-identical hidden==shown CYOA rows — so a delta would measure generator difficulty, not contamination; literature 2410.09247/LiveBench/AntiLeakBench: a held-out delta is undefined without a capable contamination-exposed agent, which is gated on the owner API key). The synthesis + chosen move were written to `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md`; a FRESH implementation subagent read ONLY that doc + the files it named.
- **The blind spot (real, quantified).** `validateRpg` (`src/validate/rpg_validator.ts`) proved combat winnability PER-FIGHT, each vs the player's FULL reachable HP: a lower `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE` bound (best-case rolls) and an opt-in upper `COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED` bound (worst-case rolls, only when `meta.combat_guaranteed`). Neither threaded HP CUMULATIVELY across sequential fights, so two fights that each clear the upper bound alone could still kill a best-prepared player JOINTLY. The just-shipped two-fight gauntlet (bug_0171) relied on this gap staying open — it deliberately does NOT set `combat_guaranteed`, its docstring noting two "guaranteed" fights "could still drain a best-prepared player on worst cumulative rolls." The reward-hacking/verifier literature (arXiv 2510.14253 (this repo cites it), EvilGenie 2511.21654, BALROG ICLR'25, SoundnessBench) names a present-but-incomplete checker as the canonical exploit surface and verifier SOUNDNESS as the load-bearing property for a procedurally-fresh benchmark — exactly this repo's state. Witness (live `max(1, d6+atk−def)` math): player hp20/atk6/def4, two enemies each hp13/atk5/def2 → per fight worst `maxDamageTaken = max(1,6+5−4)*(ceil(13/max(1,1+6−2))−1) = 7*(3−1) = 14 < 20` (per-fight clean) yet cumulative `14+14 = 28 ≥ 20` (the guarantee is false across the gauntlet).
- **The change (additive, `src/validate/rpg_validator.ts`).** A new `cumulativeWorstDamage` accumulator sums each enemy's EXISTING worst-case `maxDamageTaken` (only when `combat_guaranteed`); after the per-enemy loop, a NEW error `COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED` (where `["meta:combat_guaranteed"]`) fires when the running total `>= playerHp`, even though every individual fight clears the per-fight bound. **CRITICAL direction:** the cumulative term goes on the UPPER/guarantee bound ONLY, NOT the lower `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE` bound — that bound is a route-EXISTENCE proof and summing it would be UNSOUND (it would forbid a legitimate gamble gauntlet a lucky player can clear, over-flagging deliberate cold_forge/sunken_barrow design). A do-not-tighten code comment locks this: the sum is an order-independent over-approximation that can only REFUSE an unsafe guarantee, never falsely grant one. Per-fight `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE` (lower) and `COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED` (upper) findings left byte-for-byte UNCHANGED. No `combat.ts`/generator/`generator_version`/corpus/scorecard change.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on watchtower_road (CYOA, seed 7).** Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T12-10-21-693Z/playtest.md`. The least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] (last dedicated pass bug_0162). Came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs across THREE routes to ALL THREE declared endings** (`ending_truth` the east/beacon route / `ending_captured` the west/no-papers force-through / `ending_escape` the letter→hermit→checkpoint confrontation). Confirmed live: deterministic seed-7 hashes, item-gated checkpoint papers / hermit letter / cellar lantern, spent-option retirement + adaptive hermit text, the twice-paying Chekhov letter. Its two notes (a no-downside beacon framing; a slightly generic shared "slip away" escape-ending line on the converging routes) were rated not-a-bug scope/narrative observations — no content defect, so the cycle's lever stayed the structural validator-soundness slice. The generic-escape-line note is carried as an optional future content polish brief.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** NEW cases in `tests/regression/rpg_combat_guaranteed_optin.test.ts` (a `bug_0172` describe block, +5 cases) pin: the WITNESS (two-fight guaranteed pack passes per-fight 14<20 but trips `COMBAT_GAUNTLET_NOT_GUARANTEED` at 28≥20); a genuinely-fair two-fight gauntlet (hp50/atk6/def12) trips NEITHER; single-fight MONOTONICITY (cumulative == single term, so the new code fires iff the per-fight code fires — curated single-enemy packs unaffected); and the `>=` boundary (sum == HP fires, sum == HP−1 clean), mirroring bug_0114's boundary discipline. All existing bug_0114 tests unchanged. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0172_rpg_cumulative_combat_winnability.yaml`.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **203 test files, 1261 tests pass** (up from 203/1256; +5 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK 0/0; drift vs HEAD OK with exactly 1 expected `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` warning (rpg_validator.ts is a PROTECTED file) — **NO GUARD_WEAKENED**, no `MIN_*`/`GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT` floor change, no matcher relaxed, net test count ROSE. `tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts` (a HASH_PIN_FILE) has ZERO diff — curated `cold_forge`/`sunken_barrow` are single-enemy so the sum equals the existing single term, provably unaffected (no hash re-pin). Committed corpus + generator UNTOUCHED (no `generator_version` bump, no re-seal); the generator does not set `combat_guaranteed`, so no minted pack's verdict changes; `held_out_corpus_sealed.test.ts` stays green.
- **Next suggested focus.** Per `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` "Deferred to next cycle": re-tune the RPG generator so its two-fight gauntlet SOUNDLY sets `meta.combat_guaranteed: true` (behind a `generator_version` bump + corpus re-seal), making every RPG mint exercise the new cumulative upper bound as a GREEN case — turning the validator's hardest check into a per-mint obligation rather than a frozen target (optionally paired with a validator-independent exhaustive cumulative-survival cross-check in `tests/regression/support/exhaustive_endings.ts`). Also still open: the held-out-vs-curated DELTA metric + scorecard refresh (stale since bug_0165; load-bearing only once a keyed agent row exists); optional trust tightening (add `corpus/manifest.json` to `PROTECTED_FILES`). ### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0171): DEEPENED the **RPG generator to a two-fight gauntlet** — the explicit next-focus named by BOTH bug_0168 and bug_0169 (parser → depth-2 chain; CYOA → two-axis 2×2 fork; the RPG generator was left the shallowest), and the exact deepening bug_0170 carried as a brief from the sunken_barrow §6 blind note ("a deeper RPG vertical slice — more than one combat + one skill check"). v1 emitted a SINGLE gallery fight + one skill check + a THREE-award economy across SIX rooms. v2 grows a SECOND combat tier across SEVEN rooms: entry → hall → gallery[**LESSER sentinel** "foe"] → **span**[**GREATER guardian** "warden"] → hearth[lever check] → vault[relic], + the optional ward cell. Each combat room's east exit gates on that enemy's own defeat flag (FOE_DOWN / WARDEN_DOWN), so the descent runs fight → fight → lever; the spirit's one-shot +2 attack and the cell ward's one-shot +2 defense now serve BOTH fights. The economy is now **FOUR awards** (sentinel/guardian/lever/relic), max_score = their exact sum (each 10/15/20 → [40,80]). Two **DISTINCT death endings** (`ending_fallen_sentinel` / `ending_fallen_guardian`) + `ending_victory` = a **3-ending census**. This completes the generator-deepening arc across all three modes; every RPG mint now forces `validateRpg` to prove TWO independent fights winnable + a four-term economy tight, and the exhaustive best/worst bracket to survive both fights CUMULATIVELY (the proof v1's single fight never stressed) to a 3-ending census — a strictly harder fresh distribution every cycle ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, deterministic, behind a `generator_version` bump (1→2) + re-seal.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **alchemists_tower** (parser, seeds 77/88/99 — this cycle's assessor rank-1 target) came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs across THREE routes to ALL THREE declared endings** (`ending_cured` 40/40 / `ending_poisoned` death / `ending_betrayal` the non-death greed fork), with the adaptive room/examine prose, exact 5+10+20+5=40 score economy, key typing, and the dynamic casket warning all confirmed live. Its only notes were brevity and an optional polish suggestion (one more red-herring / a casket-resist reward), neither an actionable content defect. A blind-clean pass on a clean pack is the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop pivots to structural work — the documented standing lever was the RPG-generator deepening. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T11-48-54-507Z/playtest.md`.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on alchemists_tower (parser, seeds 77/88/99).** Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T11-48-54-507Z/playtest.md`. 5/5 clarity, 4/5 enjoyment, ZERO bugs, all 3 endings reached — blind-clean; the two notes were rated not-actionable scope observations, so no content defect, and the cycle's lever was the structural generator-deepening slice.
- **The change (`src/gen/rpg_generator.ts`, version 1→2).** The `Theme` type gained `wardenName`/`wardenDesc` + `spanName` for all 6 themes; the inscription clue + the spirit's counsel rewritten to signpost two keepers. `generateRpgPack` restructured: a new `span` room holds the guardian between gallery and hearth; the gallery foe is the LESSER sentinel (hp12 atk5 def2), the span warden the GREATER guardian (cold_forge's bug_0101-proven hp18 atk7 def2). Each fight is winnable on the player's BEST reachable stats independently (validator checks each vs full HP crediting both buffs: atk 4+2, def 2+2) AND the best-line CUMULATIVE HP survives both (20→~18→~14). Deliberately **NOT `meta.combat_guaranteed`**: that flag is single-fight-sound only (the validator checks each enemy against full HP and cannot see cumulative drain), so two "guaranteed" fights could still fell a best-prepared player on worst cumulative rolls — both fights stay real gambles, so both deaths are reachable. No engine/validator/schema change (multiple enemies + distinct death endings are existing RPG features).
- **Corpus re-sealed (`npm run corpus:seal`).** The 4 held-out RPG packs (genrpg_0..3) re-stamped to generator_version 2 with new content hashes; cyoa/parser entries byte-unchanged (rpg-only change). The sanctioned deepen workflow: the generator edit surfaces a non-blocking **VERIFIER_TOUCHED** drift warning (src/gen/rpg_generator.ts is in PROTECTED_FILES, bug_0167), and the version bump + re-seal keep `held_out_corpus_sealed.test.ts`'s version-match + re-mint-determinism green. Re-seal is deterministic (a second seal produced a byte-identical tree).
- **Held to the shipped bar.** NEW `tests/regression/rpg_generator_two_fight_gauntlet.test.ts` (+15 cases) locks the two-fight SHAPE so a future flattening back to one fight fails loudly even though it would still pass the generic bar: two distinct enemies in sequence (gallery sentinel → span guardian), distinct defeat flags + distinct death endings, each combat-room east exit gated on its enemy's defeat, escalating HP, four-award economy; and BEHAVIORALLY (exhaustive best/worst bracket) BOTH the FIRST fight gating the whole descent (never striking the sentinel ⇒ NO ending at all, since combat is player-initiated) AND the SECOND fight being load-bearing (beating only the sentinel ⇒ victory AND the guardian's death unreachable, while the sentinel's own death stays reachable — non-vacuous). `tests/unit/rpg_generator.test.ts` updated (reached.size 2→3; a new two-fight-structure case; gate-load-bearing now asserts BOTH east gates). `tests/unit/mcp_generate_rpg_pack.test.ts` counts updated (room 6→7, enemy 1→2, ending 2→3, max_score 30-60→40-80). - **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **203 test files, 1256 tests pass** (up from 202/1240; +1 file, +16 cases). The 24-seed `rpg_generator.test.ts` is green (every v2 pack schema-valid, validateRpg-clean ZERO findings — combat winnability + score economy + soft-lock all pass, exhaustively solvable to all 3 endings, none undeclared, no cap-out). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0), **NO GUARD_WEAKENED**, no assertion-count regression. - **Next suggested focus.** All three generators are now deepened (parser depth-2 / CYOA two-axis 2×2 / RPG two-fight gauntlet) AND guarded at the source (bug_0167). The generator-deepening arc is COMPLETE — further depth has diminishing returns vs the soundness risk (e.g. a THIRD fight or `combat_guaranteed` multi-fight runs into the validator's single-fight cumulative-HP blind spot, which would be a VALIDATOR-deepening cycle: make the combat-winnability proof cumulative-HP-aware across a gauntlet, the genuinely next-harder check). Other open levers: (a) the held-out vs curated **DELTA** summary metric in `renderMarkdown` once a capable (non-bot) agent row exists; (b) make `validateRpg`'s combat bound CUMULATIVE across multiple fights (currently per-fight vs full HP — sound for the gambles this generator emits, but it would let an unsound multi-fight `combat_guaranteed` pack through). The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains highest-value overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]). ### Cycle result — content_fix (bug_0170): SIGNPOSTED the optional shade in **The Sunken Barrow**'s Entry Hall, so the prepared path is discoverable and skipping it is an INFORMED gamble (not a blind one). The Entry Hall is the pack's real fork — west to the optional reaver's shade (whose **+3 ward**, bug_0113, is the decisive survival lever) and north into the under-armed, **~43%-lethal** wight (bug_0102). The old prose narrated the west breach as a bare "cramped side cell" with no cue anything was there, so a first-timer could walk straight past the prepared path and die to RNG with **no signpost the counsel ever existed** — the exact [[verifier-assertion-guard]]/bug_0027/0029 **anti-gotcha** failure mode this pack fights everywhere (a player loses a winnable game for want of a telegraph), here landing on the wight rather than the slab. Fix = pure CONTENT: the west-breach prose now telegraphs the shade IN SCENE ("a low breach gapes west into a cramped side cell, and from its dark a cold breath drifts, prickling the neck — something lingers in there, watchful"), **mirrored into the has_item:iron_bar variant** so both renderings agree, reading true throughout (the shade never leaves the cell). The shade stays OPTIONAL and bug_0102's intended lethal under-armed gamble is UNTOUCHED — this only signposts the choice, it does not soften the fight. No flag/var/item/exit/quest-gating/score/reachable-ending change.
- **Why this slice.** The mandated blind pass on **sunken_barrow** (rpg, this cycle's assessor rank-1 target; seed 42 warded victory + seed 7 unwarded doom) came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs, BOTH reachable endings hit** (`ending_victory` 50/50 warded / `ending_woken` 25/50 doom). Its ONE actionable §5 finding was a design gap, not a bug: the swingy unwarded fight (won at exactly 2 HP on seed 7) has no in-room cue drawing a first-timer to the load-bearing shade. That is a genuine [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] honesty-discipline defect (signposting a winnable-but-lethal choice), so it earned a focused content_fix rather than the usual pivot-to-structural — distinct from a "too short" note. The §6 brevity/replay note stays deferred as a future net-new-pack brief. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T11-37-52-305Z/playtest.md`.

Carry the deferred sunken_barrow §6 brevity/replay note as a brief for a future net-new pack (a deeper RPG vertical slice — more than one combat + one skill check).

### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0169): DEEPENED the **CYOA generator to a two-axis (2×2-knowledge) moral fork** — bug_0168's explicitly-named next-focus (a) structural lever (the CYOA generator was "the shallowest"). v1 emitted a SINGLE-axis fork: one investigation set one `truth` flag and one "best" ending gated on it (one gate, one reactive variant, no shadowing stack to order). v2 grows the SECOND knowledge axis the hand-authored frontier pack **dead_reckoning** has: TWO independent investigations, in either order, each setting its own flag — a SITUATIONAL truth (`knows_way`: the crisis is engineered and relief is at hand) and a PERSONAL truth (`knows_ally`: the maligned figure is the one who can mend it) — forming a 2×2 of knowledge over a finale of three-or-four telegraphed acts: HOLD the line (reframed by knows_way), empower the ALLY (the surest act, **GATED on knows_ally**; reframed by knows_way), the DARK sanctioned act (reframed by knows_ally — ignorant vs knowing), and (seed-chosen) GREED (reframed by knows_way). Knowledge does not add routes; it REFRAMES them via reactive endingText variants (the white_stag paired-epilogue device), so the hub now carries a **three-variant first-match-wins stack** (both flags, then each alone) that MUST be ordered most-specific-first or the validator's `UNREACHABLE_VARIANT` shadowing check fires. Every mint now forces `validateCyoa` to prove a two-flag obtainability, a real load-bearing gate, AND a correctly-ordered shadowing-clean variant stack — a strictly harder fresh distribution every cycle (the never-frozen-target property the contamination-free benchmark thesis rests on; [[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, deterministic, behind a `generator_version` bump (1→2) + re-seal.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **cold_forge** (rpg, seed 61 — this cycle's assessor rank-1 target AND the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], last dedicated pass bug_0159) came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs across TWO routes to BOTH declared endings** (`ending_victory` the full-prep 50/50 win / `ending_fallen` the under-armed death on round two, verified from the SAME seed). The tester confirmed reactive room text, transparent combat math, one-shot dialogue, determinism, and the back-loaded 50/50 economy all live. Its only notes were design observations rated not-a-bug (score stays 0 through prep; the founder-looting moral hook stays cosmetic; the examine-only hammer is deliberate restraint). A blind-clean pass on a clean pack is the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop pivots to structural work, and the CYOA-generator deepening was bug_0168's documented next slice. (Thematically convergent: the playtest's lone narrative note was an UNSPENT moral axis, and the deepening adds exactly a second moral/knowledge axis.) Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T11-19-32-362Z/playtest.md`.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on cold_forge (rpg, seed 61).** Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T11-19-32-362Z/playtest.md`. 5/5 clarity, 4/5 enjoyment, ZERO bugs, both endings reached — blind-clean; the three notes were rated not-a-bug, so no content defect, and the cycle's lever was the structural generator-deepening slice.
- **The change (`src/gen/cyoa_generator.ts`, version 1→2).** The `Theme` type was rewritten for two `Truth` axes (`way`/`ally`) + four `Act`s (hold/best/dark/greed, each with a `known` reframe) across all 6 themes. `generateCyoaPack` restructured: scenes hub + `clue_way` + `clue_ally` (scene_count 2→3); the hub holds both not_flag-gated investigations + the finale acts (`best` gated `has_flag: knows_ally`); the optional fourth GREED act is the seed-chosen variety knob (the old 2-vs-3-stance knob), and both 3-act and 4-act mints keep BOTH flags read (no INERT_FLAG). The hub's three reactive variants are ordered both-flags → way → ally (most-specific-first); each ending carries one reactive variant keyed on its reframing axis. ending_count 3 or 4. No engine/schema change (the reactive variants + flag gates are existing CYOA features).
- **Corpus re-sealed (`npm run corpus:seal`).** The 4 held-out CYOA packs (gen_0..3) re-stamped to generator_version 2 with new content hashes; rpg/parser entries byte-unchanged (cyoa-only change). The sanctioned deepen workflow: the generator edit surfaces a non-blocking **VERIFIER_TOUCHED** drift warning (the generator program is in PROTECTED_FILES, bug_0167), and the version bump + re-seal keep `held_out_corpus_sealed.test.ts`'s version-match + re-mint-determinism green. Re-seal is deterministic (a second seal produced no further diff).
- **Held to the shipped bar.** NEW `tests/regression/cyoa_generator_two_axis_fork.test.ts` (+15 cases) locks the two-axis SHAPE so a future collapse back to one axis/one gate fails loudly even though it would still pass the generic bar: two distinct investigation scenes each setting their own flag; the `best` act gated on the PERSONAL axis only; the hub's three-variant reframe stack ordered most-specific-first; the 2×2 ending reframing keyed on the right axis; and BEHAVIORALLY (exhaustive solver) BOTH the load-bearingness (no knows_ally → ending_best unreachable, other acts still reach) AND the independence (no knows_way → ending_best STILL reachable, so the axes are distinct gates). `tests/unit/cyoa_generator.test.ts`'s gate case + `tests/unit/mcp_generate_pack.test.ts` (scene_count, the live play-surface gate, the ungated-act case) updated for the new shape. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0169_cyoa_generator_two_axis_fork.yaml`.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **201 test files, 1237 tests pass** (up from 200/1222; +1 file, +15 cases). The 24-seed `cyoa_generator.test.ts` is green (every v2 pack schema-valid, validator-clean ZERO findings, exhaustively solvable — every declared ending reached, none undeclared, no cap-out). `benchmark_held_out_split.test.ts` green (the deterministic bot still reaches a declared ending on the held-out CYOA split). Drift vs HEAD: 0 errors, 1 expected VERIFIER_TOUCHED warning (cyoa_generator.ts edited — the surfaced deepen workflow), **NO GUARD_WEAKENED**, no assertion-count regression. No engine/validator/schema/assessor behavior change; no curated content touched (10 packs validate 0/0); no curated hash re-pin (sunken_barrow `7791fc14…` + cold_forge `8590fefd…` unchanged). The corpus hash change is a deliberate, surfaced re-seal behind a version bump, not a weakened check.
- **Next suggested focus.** All three generators are now deepened (CYOA two-axis 2×2 / parser depth-2 chain / RPG still the shallowest). The remaining generator-deepening lever (bug_0167 (a)): the **RPG generator** is now the simplest — it could grow **multiple enemies** or a `combat_guaranteed` fairness variant so each mint stresses MORE of the RPG combat/score validator (safe-but-visible: a generator edit surfaces VERIFIER_TOUCHED, behind a `generator_version` bump + re-seal). Also still open: (b) the held-out vs curated **DELTA** summary metric in `renderMarkdown` once a capable (non-bot) agent row exists. The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains highest-value overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]). ### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0168): DEEPENED the **PARSER generator to a two-tier (depth-2) obtainability chain** — bug_0167's explicitly-named next-focus (a) structural lever. v1 emitted a depth-1 spine (a key in an UNLOCKED hub coffer opened the gate); v2 grows a SECOND lock tier: an entrance coffer (unlocked) holds a LESSER key that opens a LOCKED hub strongbox, and only the GREAT key cased inside it opens the gate (win) and the hazard (the death fork — the SAME key, the sealed_crypt iron-key discipline). So the goal's obtainability chain now runs key→lock→key→lock across two rooms. This is high-value because parser owns the **STRICTEST validator in the suite** (`src/validate/parser_validator.ts`: the obtainability fixpoint, soft-lock detection, SCORE economy) — a generator that only ever emits a trivial depth-1 shape exercises a shrinking slice of that reasoning. Every mint now forces the validator to prove a depth-2, cross-room obtainability chain AND a three-milestone score economy, holding the parser bar against a harder fresh distribution every cycle (the never-frozen-target property the contamination-free benchmark thesis rests on; [[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, deterministic, behind a `generator_version` bump + re-seal.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **sealed_crypt** (parser, seed 13 — this cycle's assessor rank-1 target AND the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], last dedicated pass bug_0158) came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs across THREE routes to ALL THREE declared endings** (`ending_victory` 35/35 the gate route / `ending_plunder` rob the founder's coffin, non-death / `ending_doom` break the lead-sealed plague tomb, death). The tester confirmed context-sensitive action surfacing, reactive room text, determinism (identical state_hash across runs), and the exact 35/35 economy all live. Its two notes were cosmetic and NOT actionable (terminal observations list held keys — expected; the puzzle is short/linear — a scope choice), both rated not-a-bug. A blind-clean pass on a clean pack is the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop pivots to structural work, and the parser-generator deepening was bug_0167's documented next slice. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T10-57-24-904Z/playtest.md`.

Score economy moved from read(+5)+gate(+10)=15 to **three one-shot +5 milestones** (read clue / unlock strongbox / unlock gate) = 15, so SCORE_UNREACHABLE/no-farm is exercised at the new tier too. max_score stays 15; both endings stay reachable. The engine's first-class UNLOCK is generic (fires `unlock_effects` for any locked object incl. a container), so the locked strongbox needs no engine change.

This is the sanctioned deepen workflow bug_0167 documented: the generator edit surfaces a non-blocking **VERIFIER_TOUCHED** drift warning (the generator program is in PROTECTED_FILES), and the version bump + re-seal keep `held_out_corpus_sealed.test.ts`'s version-match + re-mint-determinism green. ### Cycle result — trust-boundary hardening (bug_0167): placed the **GENERATOR PROGRAM under the verifier-integrity guard** — the source files that mint the eval distribution are now in `PROTECTED_FILES` (`scripts/verify-integrity.ts`), so a SILENT weakening of a generator trips the integrity guard, not just the downstream output gates. This is bug_0166's / bug_0164's named deferred trust-boundary item (c). The generators (`src/gen/{cyoa,rpg,parser}_generator.ts`) + the seal CLI (`bin/seal-corpus.ts`) became first-class verification assets when the loop wired them into the per-cycle assessor mint-and-check levers (CYOA bug_0158 / RPG bug_0162 / parser bug_0166) and the committed held-out corpus (bug_0163/bug_0165) — they ARE the moving target the contamination-free benchmark thesis rests on — yet they sat OUTSIDE the guard, so a quiet degradation (e.g. narrowing a generator so the validators only ever see trivial packs) would pass the OUTPUT gates while hollowing out the eval signal. Guarding the SOURCE closes that gap. KEY-FREE, deterministic, additive — a pure TIGHTENING of the guard surface.

The tester confirmed the inventory-gated `hang_false_light` (absent empty-handed), the knowledge-gated gallery/lantern prose (generic pre-journal, names the Mourning Star post-`knows_truth`), and the reactive mantel/cask/chest text all live. Its lone note — the gallery `climb_to_wreck` death is unsignposted — was NOT actionable: that label was already renamed in a prior cycle to foreshadow its own lethality (`ai-runs/2026-06-02T01-45-25-898Z`) and the gallery prose already shows the "sickening drop"; the tester explicitly rated it not-a-bug. - **The change (additive, `scripts/verify-integrity.ts`).** Added `src/gen/cyoa_generator.ts`, `src/gen/rpg_generator.ts`, `src/gen/parser_generator.ts`, and `bin/seal-corpus.ts` to `PROTECTED_FILES`. Adding entries is pure TIGHTENING (never a `GUARD_WEAKENED`): a DELETION of any of them is now a hard `PROTECTED_DELETED` error; an EDIT (e.g. a deliberate `deepen` cycle behind a `generator_version` bump) surfaces a non-blocking `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` warning for review (so deepen cycles stay legal, just visible); and dropping one back OUT of `PROTECTED_FILES` is itself the DGM "edit-the-checker" launder (`GUARD_WEAKENED`). **Gotcha fixed in-cycle:** the explanatory comment placed inside the array literal first broke the pure `parseGuardConstants` parser — its `'…'` alternation matched apostrophes in the prose (`distribution's … bug_0164's`) as a phantom string entry, failing the `verifier_integrity.test.ts` round-trip. Reworded the comment apostrophe/quote/bracket-free with a `NOTE:` warning to future editors.

### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0166): shipped the **PARSER generator MINT-AND-CHECK lever** — completing the generator trilogy on the assessor side. Every cycle the deterministic assessor now ALSO mints a fresh window of never-seen procedural **parser** packs and asserts the production `validateParser` bar holds on them. This is the **bug_0158/bug_0162 analogue one mode over** and the last open leg: the CYOA generator went core (bug_0156) → MCP (bug_0157) → assessor mint-and-check (bug_0158); the RPG generator went core (bug_0159) → MCP (bug_0160) → assessor mint-and-check (bug_0162); the parser generator went core+MCP (bug_0164) → sealed corpus (bug_0163) → scorecard (bug_0165), and this lands its mint-and-check half. It is high-value because parser owns the **STRICTEST validator in the suite** (`src/validate/parser_validator.ts`, ~1200 lines: the obtainability fixpoint, soft-lock detection, WIN_FIRES_AT_START stability, SCORE_UNREACHABLE economy) — which until now ran against GENERATED parser content ONLY via the static 24-seed unit test and the 4-seed sealed corpus, never against the per-cycle MOVING window the CYOA and RPG levers already give. The never-frozen-target property now holds for **all THREE modes**, the memorisable-target condition the frozen-verifier literature warns against ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253) closed on the strictest verifier. KEY-FREE, deterministic, no outbound call, additive.

### Cycle result — engine/benchmark (bug_0165): wired the **HELD-OUT CORPUS into the BENCHMARK SCORECARD** — the thesis bridge from a sealed corpus to a *reportable contamination-free number*. The objective scorecard (`src/afk/benchmark.ts`, the repo's ULTRAPLAN successor goal: one comparable number per (pack, agent) so real frontier-model rows slot in beside the deterministic-bot baseline) played ONLY `list_stories()` — the ten authored disk packs under `content/*/pack` — so the contamination-free **held-out split** (the procedural corpus sealed under `corpus/` by bug_0163, the *entire credibility anchor* of the benchmark thesis) never appeared in the scorecard at all. The scorecard reported only curated rows a frontier model could plausibly have seen, and NONE of the uncontaminated signal. `buildScorecard` now ALSO scores every sealed corpus pack through the **identical** bot/cells, tags each row `held_out`, and renders a `Split` column — so the same table reports the curated baseline and the held-out signal side by side, directly comparable. This lands the **#1 deferred slice** bug_0163/bug_0164 both named ("wire `corpus/` into the benchmark scorecard — the thesis bridge, sensible now that the corpus is real"). KEY-FREE, deterministic (no clock/RNG/network), additive.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **dead_reckoning** (CYOA, seed 7 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack: last dedicated pass bug_0155, OLDER than every other pack) came back **blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs across FOUR routes to ALL FOUR declared endings** (ending_landfall full-knowledge / ending_holdfast ignorant ration / ending_jonah the knowing tragedy / ending_adrift greed). The tester confirmed the two-axis 2×2-knowledge design holds under blind play (the Cask scene's three narration variants; the `trust_pilot` option gated on `knows_pilot`) and called it "clean, polished, the strongest-written pack I've blind-tested." Lone nit: the cased pistol is non-interactable atmosphere — intentional restraint. A blind-clean pass on a clean pack is the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop pivots to breadth/structural work. **Rotation correction:** bug_0164's next-focus mis-named watchtower_road as least-recent, but its own dedicated-pass log list shows dead_reckoning at bug_0155 is the oldest — the deterministic assessor independently corrected the rotation. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T10-26-28-335Z/playtest.md`.
- **The change (additive, `src/afk/benchmark.ts`).** (1) `BenchmarkRow` gains `held_out: boolean` (true for a corpus pack no external agent/training set has seen, false for a curated authored pack — per-row so both splits live in one comparable table). (2) The per-pack cell loop is refactored into a shared `scorePack(storyPath, heldOut)` closure (identical `run_playtest` path; the only difference is the tag); the curated loop calls `scorePack(s.path, false)` as before, and a NEW held-out loop reads `corpus/manifest.json` (its authoritative membership) and scores `corpus/<mode>/<pack_id>.yaml` via `scorePack(..., true)`. An absent/un-sealed corpus (`existsSync` false) simply yields no held-out rows — the scorecard stays valid. (3) The deterministic row sort gains `held_out` as its FIRST key (curated block then held-out block); `renderMarkdown` adds a `Split` column (curated | held-out) after `Graph` plus an explanatory note — the existing `"| Pack | Mode | Strategy | Graph |"` header substring is preserved, so the prior `benchmark.test.ts` header assertion still holds. Observed shape mirrors the curated baseline honestly: the bot fully solves generated CYOA (4/4 endings, 100% completion) but not the generated parser/RPG spatial packs (0% completion, partial scene coverage) — exactly as on the curated packs of each mode. NO content/engine/validator/schema/generator change.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on dead_reckoning (CYOA, seed 7).** Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T10-26-28-335Z/playtest.md`. 5/5 clarity, 5/5 enjoyment, ZERO bugs, 4/4 endings reached — blind-clean; no content defect, so the cycle's lever was the structural benchmark slice.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** NEW `tests/regression/benchmark_held_out_split.test.ts` (+7 cases): every `corpus/manifest.json` entry is scored once per cell tagged `held_out` (held row count == membership × cells); the split spans cyoa+parser+rpg; it is genuinely PLAYED not enumerated (every held row ran the full run count over a real pack with `scenes_total>0`, and at least one reaches a declared ending — the bot solves generated CYOA); held rows are well-formed fractions; the curated split is NOT polluted (no corpus pack_id appears as curated); determinism preserved (re-run byte-identical); the `Split` column renders both labels and the table row count equals `card.rows.length + 1`. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0165_benchmark_held_out_split.yaml`. The existing `tests/unit/benchmark.test.ts` passes unchanged — its CYOA-no-op and spatial-drop assertions now ALSO cover the held-out rows of each mode (held-out CYOA hidden==shown; held-out parser/RPG hidden<=shown).
