### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0164): shipped the **PARSER PACK GENERATOR** — completing the generator trilogy across all three game modes (CYOA bug_0156 + RPG bug_0159 + **parser now**) and extending the held-out corpus (bug_0163) to the parser mode. The parser owns the **strictest validator in the suite** (`src/validate/parser_validator.ts`, ~1200 lines: soft-locks, an obtainability fixpoint, quest-item loss, dialogue termination, score economy, win-fires-at-start, dead reactive content) yet it was still exercised only against the **two** hand-authored packs (sealed_crypt, alchemists_tower) — the frozen/memorisable-target condition the reward-hacking literature warns against ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). `generateParserPack(seed)` now mints fresh, never-seen, validator-clean, exhaustively-solvable parser packs on demand, so the parser bar too is held against a moving target every cycle. KEY-FREE, deterministic (mulberry32, no Date/RNG, §8.5), additive. This lands bug_0163's named next-focus item **(c)**.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **white_stag** (CYOA, seed 3) came back blind-clean — clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **5/5**, ZERO bugs across 4 playthroughs to 3 distinct endings, confirming the knowledge×action two-axis reactivity, the gated `lay_offering`, and the telegraphed ice death; the tester's verdict was "shippable as-is, no fixes needed." A blind-clean pass on a clean pack is the saturation signal ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop pivots to breadth/structural work. The parser generator was the open mode in the generator trilogy and the lowest-hanging high-leverage slice now that persistence exists. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T10-10-18-913Z/playtest.md`.
- **The change (additive, key-free, deterministic).** (1) NEW `src/gen/parser_generator.ts`: `generateParserPack(seed)` emits a `ParserPack` of the signature shape, theme-skinned by seed (6 themes) over a FIXED skeleton — a three-room spine entrance→hub→goal whose two NON-win rooms are strongly connected (no quest-item soft-lock), the goal gated behind a flag-locked exit; a readable CLUE (+5, one-shot) and an NPC (ungated `bye` ⇒ dialogue always terminates) as two in-world clue sources (§17); a KEY in an openable coffer; a first-class UNLOCK gate (sets the gate flag the goal exit reads, awards +10) → the canonical win on reaching the goal; and a telegraphed DEATH fork — the SAME key opens a plainly-warned hazard that `end_game`s (the failure pole, the sealed_crypt bound_tomb discipline, never an ambush). Exact economy: read +5 + unlock +10 = `max_score` 15. Self-checks via `ParserPackSchema.parse`. `export const PARSER_GENERATOR_VERSION = 1`. (2) `bin/seal-corpus.ts`: add `PARSER_SEEDS [0,1,2,3]`, mint → `validateParser` (THROW on any finding) → write `corpus/parser/<id>.yaml` + manifest entry; mode union widened. (3) Re-sealed `corpus/` — NEW `corpus/parser/genpar_{0..3}_v1.yaml`, manifest now **12 entries** (4 cyoa + 4 rpg + 4 parser), still under the top-level `corpus/` dir NEVER `content/*/pack`.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on white_stag (CYOA, seed 3 — this cycle's assessor rank-1 playtest target). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T10-10-18-913Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **3 distinct declared endings** (`ending_offering` the gated knowing rite, `ending_lost` the telegraphed ice death, `ending_quarry` reached in BOTH the knows_truth-informed and ignorant variants), confirmed flawless mechanics, the gated offering option absent on the ignorant bluff, and the two-text reactive kill ending — **5/5 clarity, 5/5 enjoyment, ZERO bugs.** white_stag re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5; no content defect, so the cycle's lever was the structural parser-generator slice.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** NEW `tests/unit/parser_generator.test.ts` (+27 cases): across a 24-seed spread every minted parser pack is schema-valid, `validateParser`-clean (ZERO findings of ANY severity — the strictest validator), exhaustively solvable (the shared `exhaustiveEndings` BFS reaches BOTH `ending_win` and `ending_doom`, none undeclared, no cap-out), has an EXACT 15-point reachable economy under the bug_0146 liveness policy (no overflow/farm, no phantom points), and a gate-is-load-bearing proof (restricting the search away from the key chain reaches no win). The corpus gate `tests/regression/held_out_corpus_sealed.test.ts` gains `PARSER_WINDOW=4` + a third per-entry branch asserting re-mint determinism, `generator_version` match, YAML round-trip, and `validateParser` zero findings — identical rigor to the cyoa/rpg branches. ### Cycle result — ULTRAPLAN re-aim (bug_0163): shipped **HELD-OUT CORPUS PERSISTENCE** — the throwaway in-memory generator mint windows are now a COMMITTED, content-hash-sealed held-out split under a NEW top-level `corpus/` dir, with a seal CLI and a STANDING re-mint-and-verify regression gate wired into `npm run health`. This was a **saturation-triggered ULTRAPLAN cycle**, not a content cycle: the deterministic assessor sits at its 0.5 floor (every structural proof axis closed; the generator-drift levers only fire on a rejection the fixed clean skeleton prevents), and the mandated blind pass came back blind-clean — so per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop re-aimed via a bounded multi-agent ultraplan instead of churning another polish pass.
- **The ultraplan (Workflow tool, 7 agents):** 4 parallel repo reviewers (engine/determinism · content/authoring · verification&benchmark · loop/strategy) + 2 web researchers (contamination/held-out-eval best practice; frontier IF/authoring difficulty + reward-hacking) → 1 synthesis. Grounded in `docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md` + `docs/ROADMAP.md` + the recent loop narrative (advanced, not restarted). Synthesis + the chosen move + rejected alternatives written to `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` (which was STALE — it predated the whole generator program); a FRESH implementation subagent then read ONLY that doc + the files it named.
- **Why this move (convergent across both research legs):** the contamination defense the benchmark thesis rests on is a procedural GENERATOR feeding a sealed, post-cutoff, HELD-OUT split — the credibility anchor that makes any reported number defensibly uncontaminated (sealed-exam literature; TALES held-out split; the frozen-verifier finding that a small injection of real held-out verification data raises the ceiling, arXiv 2510.14253). AdventureForge already had BOTH generators LIVE in the loop's brain (CYOA bug_0156/0157/0158 + RPG bug_0159/0160/0162) — but every minted pack was minted-and-DISCARDED in memory, so the eval distribution had NO committed, reproducible artifact. This lands the **#1 deferred slice** every generator file's own header + `docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md` (Year deliverable) named as next, now de-bundled from the finished generator/MCP/assessor work. **Rejected** (a) deepening the generators (the right NEXT cycle, but the validator surface is saturated/green-by-construction so it can't move the assessor and would land depth in a discarded window — do it after persistence, behind the new `generator_version` bump), (b) a parser generator (lowest leverage, still a discarded window, no doc names it next), wiring corpus→scorecard (meaningless until the corpus exists — the natural follow-up), routing `adapt_story`'s mock through the generator (high test coupling), and adding the generators to `PROTECTED_FILES` (a separate trust-boundary cycle). The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains the highest-value move overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]).
- **The change (KEY-FREE, deterministic, additive).** (1) `src/gen/cyoa_generator.ts` + `src/gen/rpg_generator.ts`: **additive exports ONLY** `CYOA_GENERATOR_VERSION = 1` / `RPG_GENERATOR_VERSION = 1` (recorded in the manifest, change NO emitted pack byte — every seed-pinned generator unit test + the corpus hashes are unchanged; `src/gen/*` are not in `PROTECTED_FILES`). (2) NEW `bin/seal-corpus.ts`: for a FIXED hard-coded seed window (CYOA `[0,1,2,3]`, RPG `[0,1,2,3]` — NOT read from `AI_LOOP_STATE.md`, so the corpus is a STABLE snapshot, not a moving window) it mints via `generateCyoaPack`/`generateRpgPack`, validates with production `validateCyoa`/`validateRpg` (THROWS on any finding — refuses to seal a dirty corpus), writes each pack as YAML to `corpus/{cyoa,rpg}/<pack_id>.yaml` + a key-sorted `corpus/manifest.json` of `{mode,seed,pack_id,generator_version,content_hash}` where `content_hash = hashState(pack)` (the same hash the MCP `generate_pack`/`generate_rpg_pack` tools emit — the wall-clock-free reproducibility seal, §8.5/§8.6; the git commit timestamp supplies the contamination chain-of-custody). (3) COMMITTED `corpus/` (8 packs + manifest), the NEW deliverable, under a top-level dir NEVER `content/*/pack` (so `all_packs_validated_by_bar.test.ts` discovery stays EXACTLY the 10 curated packs + no blind-playtest obligation). (4) `package.json` `corpus:seal` script.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on clockwork_heist (CYOA, seed 7 — the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0162's next-focus named it least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0153). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T09-48-05-838Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FIVE routes to **all four declared endings** — `ending_truth` via BOTH the crawlspace hidden-letter AND the vault displayed-letter (the two oppositely-framed reactive epilogues), `ending_rich` (the ledger-timed heist past the active patrol), `ending_caught` (force the vault door), `ending_patrol` (linger past the chime with no ledger) — visited every location incl. the vault, and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed state-aware action menus (`enter_panel`/`pick_lock`/`cross_to_vault_blind` appear only in the right state), the tick economy (`back_down` free, forward moves cost), real lockpick-gating on both strongbox and vault, and the patrol ending diegetically reproaching the unread ledger. **clockwork_heist re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5.** Its two soft §4 notes (the move-back-on-locked affordance; the shared-id Truth variant) the tester itself judged intentional sound design — no actionable content defect, consistent with [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]; the cycle's lever was the structural persistence slice.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** NEW `tests/regression/held_out_corpus_sealed.test.ts` (+9 cases): for EVERY `corpus/manifest.json` entry it asserts re-mint determinism (re-mint from seed ⇒ `hashState` == sealed `content_hash`), generator_version match, YAML round-trip stability (parse the committed YAML, re-hash, == content_hash), production-bar clean (`validateCyoa`/`validateRpg` ⇒ ZERO findings — the SAME bar the curated packs clear, so it STRENGTHENS the bar), and manifest count == the 4+4 seeded window. `generator_version` makes the next cycle (deepen) honest: a hash mismatch reads as "generator changed" (a deliberate bump + re-seal) vs "corpus tampered". Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0163_held_out_corpus_persistence.yaml`.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **195 test files, 1159 tests pass** (up from 194/1150; +1 file, +9 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0), NO `GUARD_WEAKENED` (the generator edits are additive exports; `verify-integrity.ts` untouched). Re-running `npm run corpus:seal` a second time produced a **byte-identical** `corpus/` tree (diff -r empty) — end-to-end determinism proven. `all_packs_validated_by_bar.test.ts` still returns EXACTLY the 10 curated packs (corpus did not pollute `content/*/pack`). No test deleted/disabled, no count regression, no hash pin moved (sunken_barrow `7791fc14…` + cold_forge `8590fefd…` unchanged), no content/engine/validator/schema/assessor change. typecheck/lint/format:check green; all 10 packs validate 0/0.
- **Next suggested focus.** The held-out corpus now EXISTS on disk (committed, sealed, re-mint-verified). Follow-ups in order: (a) **DEEPEN the generators** (multiple enemies / `combat_guaranteed` fairness / deeper maps for RPG; deeper graphs / vars / multi-axis knowledge for CYOA) so each mint stresses MORE of the validator — now safe behind a `generator_version` bump + re-seal; (b) wire `corpus/` into the **benchmark scorecard** (`buildScorecard` plays only curated disk YAML today — the thesis bridge, sensible now that the corpus is real); (c) a **parser generator** to complete the mode trilogy; (d) add the generators / `GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT` to `PROTECTED_FILES` as a separate trust-boundary cycle. The keyed real-model run remains highest-value overall but gated on owner API-key authorization. ### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0162): shipped the **RPG generator MINT-AND-CHECK lever** — every cycle the deterministic assessor now ALSO mints a fresh window of never-seen procedural **RPG** packs and asserts the production `validateRpg` bar holds on them. This is the **bug_0158 analogue one mode over** and the generator program's named open next-slice (bug_0160 and bug_0161 both named it): the CYOA generator went core (bug_0156) → MCP (bug_0157) → assessor mint-and-check (bug_0158); the RPG generator went core (bug_0159) → MCP (bug_0160), and this lands its mint-and-check half. It is the **higher-value half** because it puts the suite's RICHEST verifier surfaces on a moving target — COMBAT winnability (the bug_0097/0113/0114 best/worst-roll bound) and SCORE-ECONOMY soundness (declared `max_score` == reachable award sum, folding in combat/skill awards the parser scan can't see) — the RPG-only checks the CYOA generator never touches, which until now ran ONLY against the two FROZEN hand-authored RPG packs (sunken_barrow, cold_forge), the memorisable-target condition the frozen-verifier literature warns against ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, deterministic, no outbound call, additive.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **watchtower_road** (CYOA, seed 41) came back blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs across THREE distinct endings — the saturated state, not a content brief ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]). Per the documented deferred order (`docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` / [[fresh-pack-generator]]; bug_0160/bug_0161's named next-focus) the RPG assessor mint-and-check is the open slice — it turns the RPG validators (combat, score economy) into a LIVE, self-renewing eval signal rather than a check that only ever sees two frozen packs, the actual cure for the 0.5-floor stall on the RPG axis now that minting is reachable through the assessor.
- **The change (additive, `src/afk/assessor.ts`).** (1) `generatorRpgDriftCandidate(checks)` — the PURE RPG twin of `generatorDriftCandidate`: null on a clean sweep (the healthy state, which correctly does NOT mask the 0.5 saturation floor), and a high-priority `engine` candidate (impact 5 / effort M / score 2.0, target `src/gen/rpg_generator.ts`) IFF any minted RPG pack carries ANY finding (the same zero-findings bar the curated RPG packs + `rpg_generator.test.ts` clear, INCLUDING the RPG-only COMBAT_UNWINNABLE / SCORE_UNREACHABLE / SKILL_CHECK_IMPOSSIBLE checks), naming the offending seed + codes. (2) `assess()` now ALSO mints a WINDOW of `GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT=4` RPG packs at the SAME advancing seed base the CYOA lever uses (sourced from the tracked `AI_LOOP_STATE.md`, no clock/RNG), validates each with the production `validateRpg`, and pushes the drift candidate when non-null. The two levers SHARE the advancing base but draw from INDEPENDENT generators (`generateCyoaPack` vs `generateRpgPack`), so each confronts its own never-frozen distribution; within each generator the per-cycle windows are disjoint. `assess()` stays deterministic for a snapshot.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on watchtower_road (CYOA, seed 41 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0161's next-focus named it least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0152). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T09-36-04-895Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **three distinct declared endings** — `ending_truth` (inspect→evidence→expose with proof), `ending_captured` (no-letter bluster through the checkpoint), `ending_escape` (light the beacon then slip away) — and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed inventory-gated choices (the "present the letter" option absent without it), evidence-gated justice (no "expose" option without the ledger), and state-aware reactive epilogues (`ending_escape` folds in the beacon the player lit). **watchtower_road re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** Its two soft notes — the optional west/hermit branch is skippable; `light_beacon` doesn't require the `lantern_lit` flag — the tester itself judged intentional, sound design; no actionable content defect, so the cycle's lever was the structural RPG mint-and-check slice.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** `tests/regression/assessor_generator_rpg_mint_and_check.test.ts` (5 cases): `generatorRpgDriftCandidate` clean⇒null and dirty⇒engine-candidate proven against the **REAL** `validateRpg` (a `max_score` bumped above the reachable award sum trips the RPG-only **SCORE_UNREACHABLE** — the economy bite the CYOA lever cannot reach — the clean control is NOT blamed, evidence names only the offending seed + code); the real repo raises **NO** generator-rpg-drift candidate AND its current RPG window is non-vacuously clean (the green is real, the COMBAT/SCORE checks genuinely held on seeds past 0); `assess()` determinism preserved.

The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains the highest-value move overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization. ### Cycle result — content_fix (bug_0161): closed a RECURRING accidental-betrayal friction in *The Alchemist's Tower* — the silver-casket moral fork can be triggered without intent because its terminal/greed warning lived ONLY on the casket OBJECT's examine (bug_0123), so a player who acts on the SPIRE ROOM view (unlocks the casket without ever examining it) gets railroaded into ending_betrayal with no in-the-moment cue. The fix carries the telegraph up to the spire ROOM description, reaching the player at the actual decision point. The first genuine content lever in many cycles — driven by, and answering, this cycle's mandated blind pass (not structural churn).
- **Mandated blind pass ran on alchemists_tower (parser, seed 23 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0160's next-focus named it least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0151). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T09-26-03-752Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **all three declared endings** — `ending_cured` (canonical brew→cure, 40/40), `ending_poisoned` (drink the black phial, death), and `ending_betrayal` (unlock the casket). Rated **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. Unlike the saturated blind-clean passes of recent cycles, this one surfaced ONE substantive, ACTIONABLE design friction (§4–5): in Run 1 the tester reached the spire holding the iron key but no cure and unlocked the casket "treating it as an ordinary container to investigate — the same verb that was safe on the strongbox," firing ending_betrayal "with no in-the-moment cue that unlocking it is a choice to abandon the patient." This is the SAME accidental-betrayal friction seed 19 (bug_0123) reported — and that fix only telegraphed the casket OBJECT examine, so it survived on the room-view path the seed-23 tester actually took.
- **The change (content-only, the bug_0123 completion).** The spire ROOM description (both the base and the `has_item: antidote` variant) now carries the casket's terminal/greed telegraph, so the warning reaches the player at the moment they decide to use the key — not only in an optional object examine. Base adds "to set iron to that clasp is to turn from her pallet to her coffer — a thing chosen once and not undone, her treasure bought against her cure"; the antidote variant adds "to crack it now, with her cure in your hand, would be to choose the Work over the maker and leave her to the fever — a thing chosen once and not undone." No engine confirmation prompt (that would break the Sierra genre this pack emulates; the death/betrayal endings stay recoverable via load §8.7). The bug_0065 spire-inventory-truth anchors are preserved VERBATIM (base keeps "you are not carrying it yet" + no "antidote in your hand"; variant keeps "pale antidote in your hand is no use until you give it to her"), and the bug_0123 casket-object telegraph is untouched and complementary.
- **Held to the bar.** `tests/regression/alchemists_spire_casket_room_telegraph.test.ts` (5 cases): the no-cure room view telegraphs terminality + betrayal while keeping the bug_0065 anchor; the with-cure room view telegraphs the moral fork while keeping its anchor; unlocking the casket still ends ending_betrayal (non-death, master never cured); the canonical solve still wins ending_cured 40/40; the pack validates clean. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0161_alchemists_spire_casket_room_telegraph.yaml`.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **193 test files, 1145 tests pass** (up from 192/1140; +1 file, +5 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0). Content-only edit (text in two spire descriptions) + a new regression test + a bug artifact: no test deleted/disabled, no count regression, no guard weakening (`verify-integrity.ts` untouched), no engine/validator/schema change. The deliberate, surfaced alchemists_tower content-hash move (`99e59867…` → `7c3d8862…`, the before value is bug_0123's after) re-pins NOTHING — alchemists_tower is not a protected file and no test pins its content hash (cf. bug_0093/0103/0104/0111/0114/0123). typecheck/lint/format:check green; all 10 packs validate 0/0.
- **Next suggested focus.** This cycle confirms blind-clean is NOT permanent — a fresh seed found a real, recurring friction the structural net can't see (it is about player *intent legibility*, not reachability/soundness, which all pass). When the mandated pass surfaces an actionable content defect, that IS the lever; prefer it over structural churn. Otherwise the standing breadth lever is the generator's deferred slices (RPG assessor mint-and-check = the bug_0158 analogue still open; held-out corpus; mode-deepening) and the keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run (gated on owner API-key authorization, [[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]). Blind-pass rotation: alchemists_tower is now most-recently blind-played; reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest (alchemists bug_0161 / sunken_barrow bug_0160 / cold_forge bug_0159 / sealed_crypt bug_0158 / wreckers bug_0157 / tithe_barn bug_0156 / dead_reckoning bug_0155 / white_stag bug_0154 / clockwork bug_0153 / watchtower bug_0152), the least-recently blind-played pack is now **watchtower_road** (CYOA, last dedicated pass bug_0152) — rotate the next mandated pass there.

### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0160): shipped the **MCP slice of the RPG pack generator** — a pure, read-only `generate_rpg_pack` tool that MINTS + VALIDATES a fresh procedural RPG pack from a seed, plus a `new_game(generate_rpg_seed)` seam that PLAYS the minted pack in-memory. This is the **bug_0157 analogue one mode over**: the CYOA generator program went core (bug_0156) → MCP (bug_0157) → assessor mint-and-check (bug_0158); the RPG program built its core last cycle (bug_0159) and this lands its named next slice. The payoff is specific to RPG: it extends the **moving-target** property to the RICHEST verifier surfaces in the suite — COMBAT winnability (the bug_0097/0113/0114 best/worst-roll bound) and SCORE-economy soundness (declared `max_score` == reachable award sum) — which the CYOA generator never touches and which until now ran ONLY against the two FROZEN hand-authored RPG packs (sunken_barrow, cold_forge), the memorisable-target condition the frozen-verifier literature warns against ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, no outbound model call, NO new write capability.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **sunken_barrow** (RPG, seed 7) came back blind-clean — clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs across BOTH declared endings — the saturated state, not a content brief ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]). Per the documented deferred order (`docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` / [[fresh-pack-generator]]; bug_0159's named next-focus) the MCP tool is the RPG program's slice #1: the connective tissue that lets the loop's own play surface — and a future assessor lever — mint and confront fresh RPG packs. Thematically convergent: the playtest target was itself an RPG pack, and the validators most distinctive to RPG (combat, score economy) are exactly what this exposes to a moving target.
- **The change (additive, read-only).** `src/mcp/tools.ts`: (1) `generate_rpg_pack(seed)` mints via `generateRpgPack` and validates with the production `validateRpg` gate (zero findings of ANY severity — the full parser bar PLUS the RPG-only COMBAT_UNWINNABLE / SCORE_UNREACHABLE / SKILL_CHECK_IMPOSSIBLE checks), returning `{ ok, mode, pack_id, content_hash, seed, meta, room_count, enemy_count, ending_count, report }`; `content_hash === hashState(pack)` so the reported hash never drifts. (2) `requireGeneratedRpgPlayable(seed)` — the RPG twin of `requireGeneratedPlayable` — mints in-memory and refuses to play unless `validateRpg` is clean; the in-memory `CompiledRpgPack` is `{ pack, contentHash: hashState(pack) }`, byte-identical to what `compileRpgPack` produces from the same pack's YAML, so **NO file is written** (server stays read-only/least-privilege, §16). (3) `new_game` now accepts an optional `generate_rpg_seed` alongside `generate_seed`; with none of `pack_path`/`generate_seed`/`generate_rpg_seed` it throws clearly (message updated). `src/mcp/server.ts` registers `generate_rpg_pack` and adds the optional `generate_rpg_seed` input. Generated packs are never written under `content/rpg/pack` — no blind-playtest obligation, no pollution of the curated set.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sunken_barrow (RPG, seed 7 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0159's next-focus named it least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0150). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T09-13-43-464Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played TWO routes to **both declared endings** — `ending_victory` (the warded full-prep win, 50/50) and `ending_woken` (defy the warning, prise the sarcophagus, a death, 25/50) — and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed transparent combat math (every `d6 N + atk - def` roll shown), the +3-defense ward as a REAL effect (def 2→5, visibly reducing damage), a might check vs DC 12, one-shot dialogue, and score tallying EXACTLY to max_score (10+15+25=50). **sunken_barrow re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** Its ONE note — combat luck can let a lucky unwarded run win, so the optional ward feels non-mandatory — the tester itself judged intentional given the shade's honest framing ("the better of it is not the sure of it"); no actionable content defect, so the cycle's lever was the structural MCP-generator slice.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** `tests/unit/mcp_generate_rpg_pack.test.ts` (8 cases) reuses the production handlers (`createToolApi`), no weaker MCP-specific substitute: generate_rpg_pack reports a minted pack `validateRpg`-clean with the schema-stamped id/hash and the proven 6-room / 1-enemy / 2-ending shape, max_score within the seed-chosen [30,60] band, and `content_hash === hashState(generateRpgPack(seed))` (no mint/report drift); determinism carries through the tool (same seed ⇒ identical hash + meta); distinct themes differ; a spread of seeds all validate clean; and `new_game(generate_rpg_seed)` genuinely PLAYS a minted pack through the live engine with init stats surfaced, the **COMBAT gate load-bearing on that surface** (foe present + `attack_foe` offered in the gallery; `go_east` ABSENT while the foe stands, gated on its defeat) and the optional NPC's **+2-attack counsel applying live** (attack 4→6 through the engine). The existing bug_0157 no-pack-source test's message regex was updated to the deliberately-changed three-source message (an assertion update, not a weakening).
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **192 test files, 1140 tests pass** (up from 191/1132; +1 file, +8 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0). Additive handler/tool + a new unit test + a bug artifact + one assertion-message update: no test deleted/disabled, no count regression, no hash re-pin (sunken_barrow `7791fc14…` + cold_forge `8590fefd…` unchanged), no guard weakening (`verify-integrity.ts` untouched), no content/engine/validator/schema change. typecheck/lint/format:check green. all 10 packs validate 0/0.
- **Next suggested focus.** Continue the RPG generator's deferred slices, mirroring the CYOA program: (b) the **assessor mint-and-check lever** — have the AFK assessor mint a fresh `generate_rpg_pack` seed each cycle and assert the COMBAT/SCORE bar holds on it, turning the RPG validators into a LIVE, self-renewing eval signal (the bug_0158 analogue, the actual cure for the 0.5-floor stall on the RPG axis now that minting is reachable through the tool surface); (c) **persistence** of a post-cutoff-timestamped held-out corpus ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]); and deepen the RPG generator (multiple enemies, a `combat_guaranteed` fairness variant, deeper maps) so each mint stresses MORE of the RPG validator. The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains the highest-value move overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization. Blind-pass rotation: sunken_barrow is now most-recently blind-played. Reading the dedicated-pass log newest→oldest (sunken_barrow bug_0160 / cold_forge bug_0159 / sealed_crypt bug_0158 / wreckers bug_0157 / tithe_barn bug_0156 / dead_reckoning bug_0155 / white_stag bug_0154 / clockwork bug_0153 / watchtower bug_0152 / alchemists bug_0151), the least-recently blind-played pack is now **alchemists_tower** (parser, last dedicated pass bug_0151) — rotate the next mandated pass there.

### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0159): shipped the **procedural RPG pack generator** (`src/gen/rpg_generator.ts`) — the **MODE-WIDENING** slice of "evolve the eval distribution". The CYOA generator program (bug_0156 core → bug_0157 MCP → bug_0158 assessor mint-and-check) made the eval set a MOVING target, but only for the CYOA validator. This widens it to the RPG mode, so the loop's moving target finally exercises the **richest verifier surfaces in the suite**: COMBAT winnability (the bug_0097/0113/0114 best/worst-roll bound) and SCORE-ECONOMY soundness (declared `max_score` == reachable award sum). Until now those RPG-only validators ran ONLY against the two FROZEN hand-authored RPG packs (sunken_barrow, cold_forge) — exactly the memorisable-target condition the frozen-verifier literature warns against ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, deterministic, no outbound call, additive new files only.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on **cold_forge** came back blind-clean (5/5 clarity, 4/5 enjoyment, ZERO mechanical bugs across three runs) — the saturated state, not a content brief ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]). Per the standing plan ([[fresh-pack-generator]], `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md`) the next lever is the generator; bug_0158's named next-focus listed **mode-widening (parser/RPG generator) so the richer validators are exercised against the moving target** as a deferred slice. Thematically convergent: this cycle's playtest target was itself an RPG pack, and the validators most distinctive to RPG (combat, score economy) are precisely the ones the CYOA generator never touches.
- **The change (additive, `src/gen/rpg_generator.ts`).** `generateRpgPack(seed)` — PURE & DETERMINISTIC (mulberry32 over the integer seed, no Date/Math.random, §8.5; same seed ⇒ byte-identical pack) — emits a schema-valid `RpgPack` anchored structurally on cold_forge's CLEAN skeleton so every parser + RPG invariant holds **by construction**: a linear descent (entry → hall → gallery → hearth → vault) + one optional side cell; an NPC granting a one-shot +2 attack + signposting an optional +2-defense ward; one WINNABLE fight (enemy at cold_forge's bug_0101-proven hp18/atk7/def2 — winnable on best reachable stats, a lethal gamble at base); one PASSABLE seeded might check; a score economy whose three awards sum EXACTLY to the declared `max_score`; two endings (relic victory + the foe's death ending). The seed selects the theme (5 settings), the skill difficulty (10–14, always passable), and the three award amounts (each from {10,15,20}, so `max_score` varies 30–60 while staying tight), so the distribution genuinely varies without any path becoming unprovable, the fight unwinnable, or the score unreachable. Generated packs are NOT committed under `content/rpg/pack` (an on-demand eval distribution — NO blind-playtest obligation, no pollution of the curated set). NO engine/validator/schema/content change.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on cold_forge (RPG, seeds 47/3 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: reading the cycle log newest→oldest, cold_forge's last dedicated pass was bug_0149; every other pack has been attended more recently). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T07-43-13-701Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **both declared endings** — `ending_victory` (50/50, full-prep AND partial-prep knife's-edge at HP 4) and `ending_fallen` (under-armed death) — and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed transparent combat math, persistent stat buffs (spirit +2 atk, plate +2 def), a retryable non-punishing might check (cannot softlock even at HP 4 / might 3), score tying exactly to max_score (50), one-shot dialogue (no buff-farming), and a real lethal failure state. **cold_forge re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4** — only note was deliberate brevity (the standing breadth brief the generator answers at scale), so the cycle's lever was structural.
- **Held to the shipped bar (no weaker generator-specific check).** `tests/unit/rpg_generator.test.ts` (27 cases): determinism (same seed ⇒ byte-identical) + distinct-themes anti-vacuity; across 24 seeds each pack is schema-valid, **`validateRpg`-CLEAN (ZERO findings of ANY severity** — the full parser bar PLUS the RPG bar, so COMBAT_UNWINNABLE / SCORE_UNREACHABLE / SKILL_CHECK_IMPOSSIBLE are all genuinely exercised), and exhaustively solvable by the shared `exhaustiveEndingsMulti` best/worst-roll bracket (both endings reached, none undeclared, no cap-out), guarded by the same no-HP-gated-routing assertion the shipped RPG suites use; and the path to victory is gated on BOTH the fight (east exit on the defeat flag) and the skill check (down exit on the levered quest stage) — the RPG analogue of the CYOA generator's truth-gate proof.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **191 test files, 1132 tests pass** (up from 190/1105; +1 file, +27 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0). Additive new files only (generator + unit test + bug artifact): no test deleted/disabled, no count regression, no hash re-pin, no guard weakening (`verify-integrity.ts` untouched), no content/engine/validator/schema change. typecheck/lint/format:check green. all 10 packs validate 0/0.
- **Next suggested focus.** Continue the RPG generator's deferred slices, mirroring the CYOA program's order: (a) an **MCP tool surface** (`generate_rpg_pack` / `new_game(generate_rpg_seed)`) — the bug_0157 analogue; (b) an **assessor mint-and-check lever** that mints-and-validates a fresh RPG pack each cycle so the COMBAT/SCORE validators become a LIVE, self-renewing eval signal — the bug_0158 analogue, the actual cure for the 0.5-floor stall on the RPG axis; (c) **persistence** of a post-cutoff-timestamped held-out corpus (the contamination control, [[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]); and deepen the RPG generator (multiple enemies, a `combat_guaranteed` fairness variant, deeper maps) so it stresses MORE of the RPG validator each cycle. The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains the highest-value move overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization. ### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0158): shipped the **assessor generator MINT-AND-CHECK lever** — every cycle the deterministic assessor now MINTS a fresh window of never-seen procedural CYOA packs and asserts the production `validateCyoa` bar holds on them, turning the generator into a **live, self-renewing eval signal** rather than a one-off tool. This lands bug_0157's named next slice (`docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` / [[fresh-pack-generator]]): bug_0156 built the minting core, bug_0157 exposed it through MCP, and this makes it LIVE in the loop's brain so the verifier is provably exercised against a MOVING target each pass — not just the curated ten ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, deterministic, no outbound call, additive.
- **Why this slice (not content).** The mandated blind pass on sealed_crypt came back blind-clean (5/5 clarity, 4/5 enjoyment, ZERO bugs across all three endings) — the saturated state, not a content brief ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]). Every structural proof in the suite runs only against a FIXED ten-pack set, and a frozen eval set is the exact condition under which a self-improving agent's verifier becomes memorisable rather than a moving target (the assessor's own 0.5-floor collapse). bug_0157 made minting reachable through MCP; this is the documented next lever — wire mint-and-check into the per-cycle assessment so the loop confronts fresh packs automatically.
- **The change (additive, `src/afk/assessor.ts`).** Three pieces: (1) `generatedEvalSeedBase(loopStateText)` — a PURE count of the `### Cycle result` headers (one prepended per cycle), so the seed base is deterministic for a given log yet ADVANCES every cycle; (2) `generatorDriftCandidate(checks)` — a PURE verdict over this cycle's minted-and-validated packs, null on a clean sweep (the healthy state, which correctly does NOT mask the 0.5 saturation floor) and a high-priority `engine` candidate (impact 5 / effort M / score 2.0, target `src/gen/cyoa_generator.ts`) IFF any minted pack carries ANY finding (the same zero-findings bar the curated packs + `cyoa_generator.test.ts` clear), naming the offending seed + codes; (3) `assess()` mints a WINDOW of `GEN_EVAL_CHECK_COUNT=4` packs at seeds `[base*4 .. base*4+3]`, validates each with the production `validateCyoa`, and pushes the drift candidate when non-null. Seeds come from the tracked `AI_LOOP_STATE.md` (no clock/RNG), so `assess()` stays deterministic for a snapshot while successive cycles sweep DISJOINT windows. Self-extinguishing exactly like the eslint-coverage / doc-staleness / frontier-benchmark levers.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sealed_crypt (parser, seed 31 — the assessor's rank-1 target this cycle). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T07-31-29-468Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **all three declared endings** — `ending_victory` (the honourable 35/35 win: headstone clue → brass key → rope-to-well → oak chest → iron key → catacombs gate), `ending_plunder` (rob the founder's coffin, greed, 10/35 non-death), `ending_doom` (break the lead-sealed plague tomb despite the explicit warning, a DEATH) — and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed contextual action surfacing (tie/unlock options appear only with the right item held), one-shot retirement, reactive room text (bare bell-tower peg, open well, state-aware crypt), and path-dependent scoring (death/greed correctly leave score pre-climax, not the +20 win award). It specifically praised the three-way iron-key climax as earned/well-telegraphed, not arbitrary. **sealed_crypt re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4** — only note was deliberate brevity (a breadth brief the generator already answers at scale), so the cycle's lever was structural.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** `tests/regression/assessor_generator_mint_and_check.test.ts` (8 cases): `generatedEvalSeedBase` counts line-start headers (advancing + deterministic, ignores prose mentions); `generatorDriftCandidate` clean⇒null and dirty⇒engine-candidate proven against the **REAL** `validateCyoa` (an injected orphan ending trips `ENDING_UNREACHABLE`, the clean control is NOT blamed, evidence names only the offending seed + code); the real repo raises **NO** generator-drift candidate AND its current window is non-vacuously clean (the green is real, the check genuinely ran on seeds past 0); `assess()` determinism preserved.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **190 test files, 1105 tests pass** (up from 1097; +8 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0). Additive: new pure functions + one `assess()` block + a regression test + a bug artifact — no test deleted/disabled, no count regression, no hash re-pin, no guard weakening (`verify-integrity.ts` untouched), no content/engine/validator/schema change beyond the assessor. typecheck/lint/format:check green.
- **Next suggested focus.** Continue the generator's deferred slices: (b) **persistence** of a post-cutoff-timestamped held-out corpus (the contamination control no other IF eval has, [[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]); (c) **mode-widening** — a parser/RPG generator so the richer validators (score economy, combat winnability) are also exercised against the moving target; and deepen the CYOA generator (deeper graphs, vars/deadline, multi-axis knowledge) so the minted distribution stresses MORE of the verifier each cycle. The keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run remains the highest-value move overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization. Blind-pass rotation: sealed_crypt is now most-recently blind-played; next least-recent CYOA target is watchtower_road or clockwork_heist.

### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0157): shipped the **MCP slice of the fresh-pack generator** — a pure, read-only `generate_pack` tool that MINTS + VALIDATES a fresh procedural CYOA pack from a seed, plus a `new_game(generate_seed)` seam that PLAYS the minted pack in-memory. This advances the plan's standing next lever (`docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` / [[fresh-pack-generator]]): bug_0156 built the generator's minting core but left it test-only; this exposes it through the agent-facing MCP surface so a never-authored, never-seen pack can be confronted — held to the SAME validator the curated packs clear — the credible demonstration that the verifier faces a MOVING target, not a frozen set ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, no outbound model call, NO new write capability.
- **Why this slice.** Per the documented deferred order (MCP tool → assessor mint-and-check lever → held-out corpus → mode-widening), the MCP tool is slice #1: it is the connective tissue that lets the loop's own play/playtest surface — and a future assessor lever — mint and confront fresh packs. The whole generator program is L-effort; this lands one focused, green-bar piece and defers the rest.
- **The change (additive, read-only).** `src/mcp/tools.ts`: (1) `generate_pack(seed)` mints via `generateCyoaPack` and validates with the production `validateCyoa` gate (zero findings of ANY severity), returning `{ ok, mode, pack_id, content_hash, seed, meta, scene_count, ending_count, report }`; `content_hash === hashState(pack)` so the reported hash never drifts from the compiled pack. (2) `new_game` now accepts an optional `generate_seed`: when present it mints the pack IN-MEMORY via `requireGeneratedPlayable` (refusing to play unless `validateCyoa` is clean) — the in-memory `CompiledPack` is `{ pack, contentHash: hashState(pack) }`, byte-identical to what `compilePack` produces from the same pack's YAML, so **NO file is written** and the server keeps its least-privilege read-only stance (§16). `generate_seed` (theme/structure) and the runtime `seed` are independent; `new_game` with neither `pack_path` nor `generate_seed` throws clearly. `src/mcp/server.ts` registers `generate_pack` and adds the optional `generate_seed` input. Generated packs are never written under `content/cyoa/pack` — they incur NO blind-playtest obligation and never pollute the curated showcase.
- **Held to the shipped bar.** `tests/unit/mcp_generate_pack.test.ts` (8 cases) reuses the production handlers (`createToolApi`), no weaker MCP-specific substitute: generate_pack reports a minted pack validator-clean with the schema-stamped id/hash; determinism carries through the tool (same seed ⇒ identical hash + meta); distinct themes differ; a spread of seeds all validate clean; and `new_game(generate_seed)` genuinely PLAYS the minted pack through the live engine with the truth gate **load-bearing on that surface** (`act_on_truth` ABSENT at the pristine hub, PRESENT only after the investigation sets the flag, ending at `ending_truth`) and an un-gated stance reaching its own ending.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on wreckers_light (CYOA, seed 29 — the assessor's rank-1 target). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T07-17-46-701Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all four declared endings** — `ending_saved` (the knowing rescue), `ending_wrecker` (the false light), `ending_mercy` (tend the keeper), `ending_drowned` (climb the rail) — and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed the inventory-reactive gating live (`hang_false_light` present only when arriving with oil+striker, absent on a bare visit) and the reactive prose (bare mantel after the striker, gallery rewrite, `knows_truth` lantern reframe) all in sync. **wreckers_light re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5.** Its two soft notes (the iron key's "guards the lamp" framing vs. its sea-chest use; `read_journal` gating no ending) the tester itself judged thematically intentional — no actionable content defect, consistent with [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]; the cycle's lever was the structural MCP-generator slice.

Additive new files + additive handler/tool only: no test deleted/disabled, no count regression, no hash re-pin, no guard weakening (`verify-integrity.ts` untouched). typecheck/lint/format:check green.

### Cycle result — engine/eval-distribution (bug_0156): shipped the **first slice of the fresh-pack generator** — a PURE, DETERMINISTIC procedural CYOA pack generator (`src/gen/cyoa_generator.ts`) that MINTS new packs, each held to the identical shipped bar. This executes the plan's named **"strongest NEXT move"** (`docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md`): the SECOND convergent research lever after last cycle's trust-boundary hardening (bug_0155) — **evolve the evaluation distribution** so the verifier stops being a frozen, memorisable target ([[verifier-assertion-guard]], the documented cure for the assessor's 0.5-floor collapse, arXiv 2510.14253). KEY-FREE, no outbound model call.
- **Why a generator, not an 11th hand pack.** The mandated blind pass came back blind-clean with only the standing "too short / vignette" note; per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] that is a breadth brief, not a polish edit. But every structural proof in the suite runs only against a FIXED ten-pack set, and a frozen eval set is exactly the condition the ultraplan named as making the verifier memorisable rather than a moving target. Hand-authoring CYOA #11 (already 6 of 10 packs) is the churn the plan explicitly REJECTED; the plan's standing next lever is the generator. The whole generator (MCP tool + assessor mint-and-check lever + held-out corpus persistence) is L-effort, so this cycle lands the disciplined FIRST SLICE — the provably-valid minting core — and defers the rest (kept out so this stays one focused, green-bar change).
- **The change.** `generateCyoaPack(seed)` — pure & deterministic (mulberry32 over the integer seed, no Date/Math.random, §8.5; same seed ⇒ byte-identical pack) — emits a schema-valid `CyoaPack` of the signature AdventureForge shape: a hub of plainly-labelled moral stances, an optional investigation that sets a `truth` flag, a truth-GATED "best" ending (the tithe_barn `knows_truth` / white_stag carved-stone / watchtower proof-gated device), and one plainly-telegraphed lethal gamble. The seed selects one of 6 themes × the 2-vs-3-stance branch, so the distribution varies while every path stays provable. The emitted object is run through `CyoaPackSchema.parse` as a generator self-check (a malformed emission throws at the source). Generated packs are NOT committed under `content/cyoa/pack` — they are an on-demand eval distribution, so they incur NO blind-playtest obligation and never pollute the curated showcase set. NO existing src/engine/validator/schema/content change; additive new files only.
- **Held to the shipped bar (no weaker generator-specific check).** `tests/unit/cyoa_generator.test.ts` (27 cases) proves, across 24 seeds, that every minted pack: (1) is deterministic + distinct-per-seed, (2) schema-parses, (3) `validateCyoa` reports **ZERO findings of ANY severity** (no soft-lock / unreachable-or-dead ending / impossible gate / inert flag / shadowed-or-unsatisfiable variant / duplicate ending), and (4) the **shared `exhaustiveEndings` BFS — the bug_0121 ground-truth solver, reused** — reaches EVERY declared ending and no undeclared one with no state-cap-out. A dedicated case proves the truth gate is load-bearing (from the pristine hub `act_on_truth` is NOT offered, `investigate` IS).
- **Mandated blind pass ran on tithe_barn (CYOA, seed 13 — the assessor's rank-1 target). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T07-04-25-204Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to `ending_fall`, `ending_doors` (the `knows_truth`-gated public-justice finale), and `ending_bounty`, and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed the `knows_truth` gate is load-bearing (`open_doors` present when the book is read, ABSENT when ignorant) and the state-aware scene/ending variants are knowledge-consistent. **tithe_barn blind-clean at 5/5.** Its only note — deliberate vignette brevity — became this cycle's design brief (the generator emits the same gated-fork shape at scale) rather than a manufactured edit to a clean pack.
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **188 test files, 1089 tests pass** (up from 1062; +27 cases). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0) AND drift vs HEAD OK (0/0) — additive new files only: no test deleted/disabled, no count regression, no hash re-pin, no guard weakening. typecheck/lint/format:check green.
- **Next suggested focus.** Continue the generator's deferred slices, in order of leverage: (a) an **MCP tool** exposing `generateCyoaPack` so the loop (and a real authoring model) can mint packs on demand; (b) an **assessor lever** that mints-and-checks a fresh pack each cycle (turns the generator into a live, self-renewing eval signal — the actual cure for the 0.5-floor stall); (c) **persistence** of a post-cutoff-timestamped held-out corpus (the contamination control no other IF eval has, [[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]); and (d) widen the generator (deeper graphs, vars/deadline, parser/RPG modes) so it exercises richer validators. The keyed real-model run remains the highest-value move overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization. Blind-pass rotation: tithe_barn is now most-recently-played; next least-recent CYOA target is wreckers_light or watchtower_road.

### Cycle result — ULTRAPLAN re-aim (bug_0155): hardened the TRUST BOUNDARY — weakening the verifier guard's OWN defensive constants is now a hard drift ERROR (`GUARD_WEAKENED`), closing the DGM "edit-the-checker" launder. This was a **saturation-triggered ULTRAPLAN cycle**, not a content cycle: the deterministic assessor has fully collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural proof axis closed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]/[[verifier-assertion-guard]]; menu-integrity trilogy bug_0151/0152/0153 closed the last one), so per `docs/afk_loop.md` the loop re-aimed via a bounded multi-agent ultraplan instead of churning another polish pass.
- **The ultraplan (Workflow tool, 7 agents):** 4 parallel repo reviewers (engine/determinism · content/authoring · verification&benchmark · loop/strategy) + 2 web researchers (frontier IF/agentic + authoring benchmarks; reward-hacking/verifier-gaming + demo credibility) → 1 synthesis. Grounded in `docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md` + `docs/ROADMAP.md` (advanced, not restarted). Synthesis + the chosen move + rejected alternatives written to `docs/CURRENT_PLAN.md` (the hand-off doc); a FRESH implementation subagent then read ONLY that doc + the files it named.
- **Why this move (convergent across both research legs):** the single credibility-defining property of a self-improving-agent demo is a verifier the agent **cannot observe-and-rewrite**. The Darwin Gödel Machine produced the textbook incident — an agent DELETED the marker-emitting code its checker scanned for, faking a perfect pass (arXiv 2505.22954). EvilGenie (2511.21654) found held-out tests weak + recommends combining edit-detectors; 2510.14253 shows a frozen verifier induces reward hacking (exactly the assessor's 0.5 collapse). Our own `scripts/verify-integrity.ts` CONCEDES this hole in its docstring (lines 33–35) yet only WARNED on guard edits, and its floors (`MIN_TEST_CASES/ASSERTIONS/STRONG_ASSERTIONS`) + lists (`PROTECTED_FILES/HASH_PIN_FILES`) could be silently weakened. That DGM-class launder was live in our tree; this cycle closes it. Rejected the fresh-pack generator (the strongest NEXT move + the other convergent lever, but L-effort/too large for one clean-bar cycle — it is the next cycle), the `adapt_story`→`resolveProvider` swap (no-op without the unauthorized key), CYOA `hide_graph` (marginal extension of an existing axis), and UI-in-CI (already done).
- **The change (test/structural, KEY-FREE):** added two PURE functions to `scripts/verify-integrity.ts` — `parseGuardConstants(text)` (regex/string, no eval; null on malformed) and `detectGuardWeakening(before, now)` (emits `severity:error code:GUARD_WEAKENED` iff any `MIN_*` floor is LOWERED or any `PROTECTED_FILES`/`HASH_PIN_FILES` entry is REMOVED; raising/adding/identical → ok) — mirroring the existing pure `detectCountRegressions`/`classifyDrift` exactly. Wired into `runDrift` ONLY (static check untouched): parse the ref's guard text via `git show ${ref}:scripts/verify-integrity.ts` (try/catch → skip) + the working tree with the same parser; if both parse, push the findings. `AI_LOOP_ALLOW_VERIFIER_EDITS=1` downgrades `GUARD_WEAKENED` to a warning (the same acknowledgment path `HASH_PIN_UNACCOMPANIED` uses). NO floor lowered, NO protected/hash-pin entry removed, NO content/engine/schema change. Locked by 20 new cases in `tests/unit/verifier_integrity.test.ts` (parseGuardConstants round-trip + null-on-malformed; detectGuardWeakening lower/remove → error, raise/add/identical → ok; a runDrift synthetic GUARD_WEAKENED + env-override-downgrade test, + an honest-tree HEAD~1 no-false-fire test) + artifact `traces/bugs/bug_0155_guard_self_integrity.yaml`.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on dead_reckoning (CYOA, seed 7 — the project's 10th pack, getting its FIRST independent blind pass: its authoring cycle bug_0154 ran the mandated pass on white_stag, so dead_reckoning is genuinely the least-recently blind-played per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T06-37-51-894Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played SIX routes across both knowledge axes to **all four declared endings** — `ending_landfall` (the `knows_pilot`-gated `trust_pilot` route, both-knowledge AND pilot-only variants), `ending_jonah`, `ending_holdfast` (informed AND uninformed reactive variants), `ending_adrift` — and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It specifically probed for the reactive-text contradiction and confirmed every ending's narration is knowledge-consistent (uninformed `holdfast` correctly says "a coast you cannot name"; pilot-only `landfall` drops the reckoning reference) and `trust_pilot` is correctly gated, never an unselectable option. **dead_reckoning blind-clean at 5/5.** No actionable content defect → consistent with [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]; the cycle's lever was the structural trust-boundary hardening, not content. (Structural pre-check: validate 0/0; run_playtest scenes 4/4, endings 3/4 — `ending_landfall` unreachable by the coverage bot because it is gated, by design.)
- **Verification.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **187 test files, 1062 tests pass** (up from 1042; the +20 cases also keep the guard's own count-regression checks satisfied). `verify:integrity` static OK (0/0); the honest diff of this cycle does NOT trip `GUARD_WEAKENED` (confirmed by the HEAD~1 runDrift test) — only the expected non-blocking `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` warning on the guard edit. typecheck/lint/format:check green.
- **Next suggested focus.** The OTHER convergent research lever and the documented next move: build the **parameterized fresh-pack generator** (TextWorld-style: map size / puzzle depth / mechanic mix → a new validated, post-cutoff-timestamped pack per run) — it EVOLVES the evaluation distribution (the published cure for the frozen-verifier stall, 2510.14253), is KEY-FREE, and is the contamination control no other IF eval has (ULTRAPLAN §Week.2/§Month.2). It is L-effort, so scope it as its own cycle. The keyed real-model run ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]) remains the highest-value move overall but stays gated on owner API-key authorization. Blind-pass rotation: dead_reckoning is now most-recently-played; next least-recent CYOA target is wreckers_light or watchtower_road.

### Cycle result — content_new (bug_0154): shipped *Dead Reckoning*, the project's **10th pack** and the **first deliberately TWO-AXIS branching CYOA**. Where white_stag/wreckers_light turn on a SINGLE hidden truth, this pack presses the standing breadth lever one step further: TWO independent investigations — the captain's log (`knows_course`) and the stowaway's identity (`knows_pilot`), learnable in EITHER order — that COMBINE into a 2×2 of knowledge over one climactic decision at the water cask. Additive content only: NO engine/validator/schema/helper change, NO hash pin moved; validator 0/0; the bug_0121 exhaustive solver + bug_0145 variant-liveness + bug_0153 action-id-uniqueness all auto-discover and pass it.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on white_stag (CYOA, seed 5 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: white_stag's last dedicated pass was its authoring cycle bug_0135; every other pack has been attended more recently). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T06-08-19-268Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FIVE routes to **all of white_stag's distinct outcomes** (ending_offering, ending_lost, ending_quarry both ignorant+knowing variants, ending_thaw), and rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed the gated `lay_offering` (absent without `knows_truth`, present with) and the flag-reactive `ending_quarry` as working design, not bugs. **white_stag re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** Its ONE substantive note was the standing single-fork critique — "short, essentially one rich decision" — which became the **design brief** for this cycle's new pack rather than a manufactured edit to a clean pack.
- **The improvement — answering the brevity note with a genuinely two-axis pack.** Both the content-polish backlog ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]) and the structural-proof axes (the menu-integrity trilogy bug_0151/0152/0153 closed the last one) are saturated; the assessor's whole top-6 is identical 0.5 blind-playtest stubs. The one live PRODUCT lever is BREADTH via branching. *Dead Reckoning*: a becalmed merchantman, the captain dead, the water cask near dry, you the mate holding the only key. Four telegraphed climactic acts — ration (ending_holdfast / discipline), trust the stowaway with the helm (ending_landfall / GATED on knows_pilot, the surest), give her to the crew's superstition (ending_jonah / the dark choice), seize the cask (ending_adrift / greed). Knowledge never adds routes, it REFRAMES them (the paired-epilogue device): knows_pilot UNLOCKS trust_pilot; knows_course fires reactive endingText on holdfast/adrift/landfall; knows_pilot on jonah. Dramatic irony: the crew's "obvious" remedy (drown the Jonah to free the wind) kills the only navigator aboard, while the harder mercies buy the landfall.
- **Verification / wiring.** `npm run health` EXIT 0 — **186 test files, 1042 tests pass**. Wired into the bar per [[verification-bar-covers-all-packs]]: added a `validate -- content/cyoa/pack/dead_reckoning.yaml` step to `health`, and extended the `all_packs_validated_by_bar` discovery list (nine → ten). list_stories + UI catalog auto-discover (no code change). Locked by `tests/regression/dead_reckoning_branching.test.ts` (11 cases: four endings reachable+distinct; the moral fork real; trust_pilot gated on knows_pilot; the two truths independent + order-free across all four 2×2 cells; reactive variants on holdfast/adrift/jonah; variants cosmetic not reroutes) + artifact `traces/bugs/bug_0154_dead_reckoning_branching_pack.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus.** Breadth is now demonstrably the only live lever AND it has a proven, repeatable shape (validate-clean CYOA + auto-discovered structural proofs + a branching regression test). Two genuine directions: (a) continue breadth — an 11th pack, ideally a PARSER or RPG pack to balance mode-diversity (currently 6 CYOA / 2 parser / 2 RPG), each of which exercises richer validators (score economy, combat winnability); or (b) advance the documented TRUE goal ([[ultraplan-true-goal-pivot]]) — benchmarking REAL-model authoring, whose keystone is running the loop once against a real frontier model. Blind-pass rotation: dead_reckoning is now newest/most-recently-touched; next cycle's least-recent CYOA target is wreckers_light or watchtower_road.

### Cycle result — test/structural (bug_0153): every reachable action MENU of every shipped CYOA pack is proven WELL-FORMED at RUNTIME — no two actions in any one observation ever share an `id`. The CYOA leg of the menu-integrity trilogy, completing bug_0151 (parser, runtime) + bug_0152 (RPG, runtime): all three modes now carry a uniform RUNTIME proof of the find-by-id contract, no leg resting on a by-construction argument alone. Test-only; no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO helper change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on clockwork_heist (CYOA, seeds 17/23/42/99 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0152's next-focus named it as least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0144; reading the rotation newest→oldest every other pack has been attended more recently). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T05-32-29-868Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all three declared endings** — `ending_truth` via BOTH the vault displayed-letter route AND the crawlspace hidden-letter route (the two oppositely-framed reactive epilogues, bug_0051), `ending_caught` (force the vault door), `ending_rich` (grab the gold) — visited every location including the vault, and rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed flawless action-set updates and real lockpick-gating on BOTH the strongbox and the vault. **clockwork_heist re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** Its TWO §5 soft notes are the documented clockwork "cosmetic clock" false-positive magnet ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]) and BOTH are **FALSE POSITIVES** verified in the YAML: (1) "toothless deadline" — `meta.deadline` fires `ending_overstayed` at `ticks>=10` (a real loss); the tester's efficient routes finished at ticks 2–6 and never lingered there; the deadline is tuned generous on purpose (bug_0079); already locked by `clockwork_deadline_enforced`/`clockwork_deadline_escalation` + bug_0121 reachability. (2) "ledger is decorative" — `cross_to_vault_blind` (`ticks>=4` AND `not read_ledger`) routes to `ending_patrol` (a real loss); the ledger is load-bearing for the LATE no-ledger player, and the tester's fast dumbwaiter route (ticks<4) got the safe crossing by design; already locked by `clockwork_guard_patrol_deadline`/`clockwork_patrol_reachable_in_careful_play`/`clockwork_timed_crossing_payoff`. No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **Why test/structural (justified, the on-plan KEY-FREE lever bug_0151's next-focus named):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect, the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), and the keystone deferred for want of a provider key (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md), the highest-value move is bug_0151's OWN named CYOA follow-on. bug_0152 closed the CYOA leg with PROSE ("unique by construction") and explicitly argued it "needs no dynamic analogue"; this cycle converts that argument into a direct RUNTIME assertion, closing the one residual COUPLING the prose silently assumed (below). **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack), re-polishing the blind-clean prose (both §5 notes are false positives already locked by 26 clockwork regression tests), and the API-keyed keystone (no outbound calls without owner authorization).
- **The gap (a LOAD-BEARING contract whose CYOA leg had only a by-construction proof):** the MCP/UI resolves a chosen action by FIRST-MATCH id (`src/mcp/tools.ts` actionForId: `available_actions.find((a) => a.id === id)`); a duplicate id makes the SECOND option permanently UNSELECTABLE — a phantom menu entry. bug_0151/0152 proved no parser/RPG pack's runtime menu collides. The CYOA leg rested on prose: a CYOA observation's action ids ARE the scene's declared choice ids (`src/cyoa/observation.ts` buildObservation: `available = scene.choices.filter(cond).map((c) => ({ id: c.id, text: c.text }))`), `cyoa_validator` already dup-checks choice ids WITHIN each scene (DUPLICATE_ID, `dupCheck` over `scene.choices.map((c) => c.id)`), and a condition-filtered SUBSET of statically-unique ids is unique. That is sound TODAY but rests on an UNTESTED COUPLING: that `buildObservation` never injects an action id beyond one scene's declared choices (no synthetic/global "restart"/meta option, no cross-scene merge). Nothing enforces it. A future change to observation.ts that injected such an id could collide with a scene's own choice id — and NO static check would catch it, because `cyoa_validator` only ever inspects the STATIC per-scene choices, never the RUNTIME observation menu the player picks from (same class bug_0151/0152 close for parser/RPG; the difference is only that the CYOA menu MERELY reflects the choices today rather than minting new ids — and "merely reflects" is itself the invariant worth locking).
- **The lock (test-only, REUSES the existing seam):** new `tests/regression/cyoa_action_id_unique.test.ts` runs the shared exhaustive BFS (`support/exhaustive_endings.ts`, the bug_0121 solver also backing bug_0145) over every auto-discovered CYOA pack; CYOA's only action is CHOOSE so the default progress-action policy explores the COMPLETE reachable region (no liveness/roll widening needed, unlike parser/RPG), and at EVERY distinct reachable non-terminal state it calls the engine's own `buildObservation` and asserts the `available_actions` ids are pairwise distinct — exactly the menu the MCP/UI resolves a pick against. **All 5 shipped CYOA packs pass — no reachable state ever offers a duplicate action id.** Controls: a planted two-same-id-choices-in-one-scene pack (compiles via `compilePack`, which runs only the Zod schema — the structural DUPLICATE_ID check is the SEPARATE `cyoa_validator` bar step — so the runtime menu carries a duplicate the compile step does not reject, the same class a future synthetic-action injection would create) BITES; a clean twin (distinct ids) passes (no false alarm). Anti-vacuity guards (statesChecked > 0, actionsSeen > statesChecked). NO support-helper change.
- **One robustness fix (within the bar, not a weakening):** the added parallel CYOA-BFS test load tipped the PRE-EXISTING `cyoa_all_endings_reachable.test.ts` (bug_0121) watchtower walk (the largest CYOA region, ~83k states, ~2s standalone) just past vitest's 5s DEFAULT timeout under contention — the same wall-clock flake bug_0150/bug_0152 documented and fixed for their own large-watchtower tests. Fix: give that per-pack `it` an explicit `TEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 60_000` (matching `cyoa_variant_liveness`/`no_dead_pocket`). This guards ONLY against a wall-clock flake on a test that genuinely passes — a real non-termination still trips the `MAX_STATES` cap (a loud `cappedOut` failure), never the wall clock. No assertion changed.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test + one timeout-only edit. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine/helper change; **NO hash pin moved** (all 9 packs validate 0/0, content_hashes unchanged). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key. Sound: every state visited is a real legal CYOA playthrough, the menu is the engine's own `buildObservation` output, the search FAILS on `cappedOut` (never truncates silently), terminal states (no menu) skipped.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0153_cyoa_action_id_unique.yaml` + the new regression test (discovery guard + 5 per-pack uniqueness assertions with anti-vacuity + 1 planted-duplicate negative control + 1 clean-twin no-false-alarm control).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **185 files / 1028 tests pass** (+1 file / +8 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** menu-integrity is now a UNIFORM RUNTIME proof across all three modes (PARSER bug_0151 + RPG bug_0152 + CYOA bug_0153) — the FIFTH exhaustive structural axis, joining every-ending reachability (bug_0121/0122/0124), every-variant liveness (bug_0145/0146/0147), score-economy soundness (bug_0148/0149), and no-dead-pocket liveness (bug_0150). The deterministic structural net is now deeply exhaustive AND uniform across all three modes; content is blind-exhausted, authoring mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark/debugging tools feature-complete (bug_0142/0143). The one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** — a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs so the turns-to-end / scene-coverage (hide_graph) pairings populate beyond the baseline `—` — deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). If rotating off the keystone, the structural levers are now VERY deeply disarmed (five exhaustive axes, all three modes, all uniform-runtime) — prefer §15 invariant-locks of recurring blind-tester false positives over content/structural churn, and resist re-polishing blind-clean prose. Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] clockwork_heist is now freshly attended (seeds 17/23/42/99); reading the rotation newest→oldest (clockwork bug_0153 / watchtower bug_0152 / alchemists bug_0151 / sunken_barrow bug_0150 / cold_forge bug_0149 / sealed_crypt bug_0148 / wreckers bug_0147 / tithe_barn bug_0146 / white_stag bug_0145), the least-recently blind-played pack is now **white_stag** (CYOA, last dedicated pass bug_0145) — rotate the next mandated pass there.
