Test-only + ONE robustness timeout on a pre-existing large-watchtower test; no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO helper change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on watchtower_road (CYOA, seed 13 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0151's next-focus named it as least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0143; reading the rotation newest→oldest every other pack has been attended more recently). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T05-17-00-132Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **three declared endings** — `ending_truth` (east evidence route: bootprint clue → cart lantern+letter → lit cellar → barrels "fire set on purpose" → hidden-cache ledger → tower-top beacon → expose), `ending_captured` (west, no-proof bluster through the checkpoint), `ending_escape` (west, letter-confrontation: hermit breaks the seal → present papers → reveal evidence → slip away) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs**. It confirmed reactive item-gating in BOTH directions (cellar needs a lit lantern with a no-softlock bounce; checkpoint/hermit letter options appear ONLY with the letter held; hermit greeting rewrites after first meeting). **watchtower_road re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** Its ONE §5 note — that `ending_truth` hardcodes "the beacon you lit still burns" — is a **FALSE POSITIVE**: that line lives in `ending_truth`'s `raised_alarm`-gated VARIANT (the bug_0098 fired-Chekhov's-gun design), and the BASE text is beacon-neutral, so a west-route player (`raised_alarm` unset) gets the neutral base by first-match-wins — never a beacon they never lit (bug_0145 already proves that variant is live/reachable). No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **Why test/structural (justified, the on-plan KEY-FREE lever bug_0151's next-focus named):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect, the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), and the keystone deferred for want of a provider key (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md), the highest-value move is bug_0151's OWN named next-focus: "the natural follow-on is the same action-id-uniqueness assertion for the other two modes — RPG (reuses the parser enumerator but adds seeded combat actions → drive the best/worst-roll bracket) and CYOA (CHOOSE ids minted from scene choice ids)." This cycle lands the RPG half (and resolves the CYOA half by showing it needs no dynamic proof — below). **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack), re-polishing the blind-clean prose (the §5 note is a false positive), and the API-keyed keystone (no outbound calls without owner authorization).
- **The gap (a LOAD-BEARING contract NO existing check covers for RPG):** the MCP/UI (and the RPG runner) resolve a chosen action by FIRST-MATCH id (`src/mcp/tools.ts` actionForId: `available_actions.find((a) => a.id === id)`); a duplicate id makes the SECOND option permanently UNSELECTABLE — a phantom menu entry. bug_0151 proved no PARSER pack's menu collides, but left RPG uncovered on TWO counts: (1) bug_0151 globs `content/parser/pack` ONLY, so the RPG packs' parser-shaped menus were never inspected — and combat opens post-defeat states the parser BFS can't reach, so coverage does not transfer; (2) `enumerateRpgActions` returns the full parser set PLUS one `attack_<enemy.id>` per living enemy — an id family the parser enumerator never mints. The parser validator dup-checks room/object/npc ids and the RPG validator adds enemy room/death_ending checks but **NO enemy-id uniqueness check**, so two enemies sharing an id in one room mint two identical `attack_<id>` options no static check catches (and two same-direction exits still mint a duplicate `go_<dir>` as in bug_0151). `option()` already filters to resolvable actions, so "every offered action resolves" is vacuous; UNIQUENESS is the orthogonal uncovered property.
- **CYOA needs no dynamic analogue (the trilogy's third leg, closed by construction):** a CYOA observation's action ids ARE the scene's declared choice ids (`available_actions = scene.choices.filter(cond).map(c => c.id)`, src/cyoa/observation.ts), and `cyoa_validator` already dup-checks choice ids WITHIN each scene (DUPLICATE_ID). The runtime menu is a condition-filtered SUBSET of those statically-unique ids → unique by construction. So PARSER (bug_0151, runtime proof) + RPG (bug_0152, runtime proof) + CYOA (static, by construction) close menu-integrity across all three modes — the modes that MINT ids the static checks can't see are exactly the two needing a runtime proof.
- **The lock (test-only, REUSES the existing seam):** new `tests/regression/rpg_action_id_unique.test.ts` drives `exhaustiveEndingsMulti([bestRng-rules, worstRng-rules])` (the bug_0124 bracket) under the bug_0146 LIVENESS action policy with the `onState` hook, and at EVERY distinct reachable non-terminal state calls the engine's own `enumerateRpgActions` and asserts its option ids are pairwise distinct. The menu inspected at each state is independent of which actions the BFS steps AND of the roll regime (enumeration is rng-independent), so the bracket only widens the set of menus examined — strictly more thorough. Reuses the bug_0147 `readsHpInCondition` guard (the bracket reaches every routing-relevant state soundly only if no route gates on a raw HP value the extremes skip — both packs pass). **Both shipped RPG packs pass — no reachable state ever offers a duplicate action id.** Controls: a planted two-same-direction-exits (`go_north`) bites; a planted **RPG-specific** two-same-id-enemies (`attack_guard`) bites — the collision NO static check catches; a clean twin (distinct directions + distinct enemy ids) passes (no false alarm). Anti-vacuity guards (statesChecked > 0, actionsSeen > statesChecked). NO support-helper change.
- **One robustness fix (within the bar, not a weakening):** the added parallel test load tipped the PRE-EXISTING `cyoa_variant_liveness.test.ts` watchtower walk (the largest CYOA region, ~2s standalone) just past vitest's 5s DEFAULT timeout under contention — the same wall-clock flake bug_0150 documented and fixed for its own large-watchtower test. Fix: give that bug_0145 per-pack `it` an explicit `TEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 60_000` (matching no_dead_pocket). This guards ONLY against a wall-clock flake on a test that genuinely passes — a real non-termination still trips the `MAX_STATES` cap (a loud `cappedOut` failure), never the wall clock. No assertion changed.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test + one timeout-only edit. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine/helper change; **NO hash pin moved** (sunken_barrow `7791fc14…`, cold_forge `8590fefd…`, all 9 packs validate 0/0). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key. Sound: every state visited is a real legal playthrough on a legal die face, the menu is the engine's own `enumerateRpgActions` output, the search FAILS on `cappedOut` (never truncates silently), terminal states (no menu) skipped.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0152_rpg_action_id_unique.yaml` + the new regression test (discovery guard + 2 per-pack uniqueness assertions with HP-guard + anti-vacuity + a parser-template duplicate control + an RPG-only `attack_<id>` duplicate control + a clean-twin no-false-alarm control).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **184 files / 1020 tests pass** (+1 file / +6 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** menu-integrity is now proven across all three modes (PARSER bug_0151 runtime + RPG bug_0152 runtime + CYOA static-by-construction) — the FIFTH exhaustive structural axis, joining every-ending reachability (bug_0121/0122/0124), every-variant liveness (bug_0145/0146/0147), score-economy soundness (bug_0148/0149), and no-dead-pocket liveness (bug_0150). The deterministic structural net is now deeply exhaustive across all three modes; content is blind-exhausted, authoring mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark/debugging tools feature-complete (bug_0142/0143). The one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** — a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs so the turns-to-end / scene-coverage (hide_graph) pairings populate beyond the baseline `—` — deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). If rotating off the keystone, prefer §15 invariant-locks of recurring blind-tester false positives over content/structural churn (the structural levers are now very deeply disarmed). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] watchtower_road is now freshly attended (seed 13); reading the rotation newest→oldest (watchtower bug_0152 / alchemists bug_0151 / sunken_barrow bug_0150 / cold_forge bug_0149 / sealed_crypt bug_0148 / wreckers bug_0147 / tithe_barn bug_0146 / white_stag bug_0145 / clockwork bug_0144), the least-recently blind-played pack is now **clockwork_heist** (CYOA, last dedicated pass bug_0144) — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn.

### Cycle result — test/structural (bug_0151): every reachable action MENU of every shipped PARSER pack is proven WELL-FORMED — no two actions in any one observation ever share an `id`. A menu-integrity invariant over the shared exhaustive BFS, the action-set analogue of the variant-liveness (bug_0145/0146/0147) and score-economy (bug_0148/0149) proofs. Test-only, REUSES the existing seam (no helper change); no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on alchemists_tower (parser, seeds 11/22/33 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0150's next-focus named it as least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0142; every other pack has been attended more recently in the cycle log). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T05-02-45-372Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **all three declared endings** — the noble WIN (40/40, ending_cure: read recipe +5 → brass key → unlock strongbox → iron key → unlock cellar → steep herb +10 → pour spring water +20 → give antidote to the fevered master +5), the signposted DEATH (0/40, ending_poisoned, drank the black phial despite the read warning), and the greedy BETRAYAL (0/40, ending_betrayal, robbed the silver casket instead of curing her) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO real bugs**. It confirmed flawless mechanics (locks/containers/two-stage crafting/give-to-NPC, the iron key correctly NOT consumed by the cellar so it remains for the casket, reactive prose in sync, score lands exactly at 40). **alchemists_tower re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** Its ONE §5 note was cosmetic: "after taking the herb, the room reads 'the herb bed is bare' yet the action list still offered `examine_herb` for one observation." Verified a **FALSE POSITIVE** — examining a HELD item is intended, `visibleObjectIds` excludes held objects so a taken item is offered examine EXACTLY once (inventory loop, never duplicated with the room loop, src/parser/model.ts), and the herb's description is location-neutral. No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **Why test/structural (justified, the on-plan KEY-FREE lever — a §15 invariant-lock of the cycle's blind-tester false positive, exactly what bug_0150's next-focus prescribed):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect, the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), and the keystone deferred for want of a provider key (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md), the highest-value move is to CERTIFY the tester's exact worry — that an examine action has been duplicated — can never actually happen, at any reachable state. **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack), re-polishing blind-clean prose (the cosmetic finding is a false positive), and the API-keyed keystone (no outbound calls without owner authorization).
- **The gap (a LOAD-BEARING contract NO existing check covers):** the MCP/UI layer resolves a player's chosen action by a FIRST-MATCH id lookup (`src/mcp/tools.ts` actionForId: `available_actions.find((a) => a.id === id)`). If an observation ever offered two options with the same `id`, the SECOND would be permanently UNSELECTABLE — picking it silently yields the first — a phantom menu entry. Nothing proved this can't happen: enumerateActions mints runtime ids from templates (`go_<dir>`, `examine_<oid>`, `use_<item>_on_<target>`, …); the static validators check only ROOM/OBJECT/ENDING ids (parser_validator DUPLICATE_ID), never the enumerator-minted runtime ids (two same-direction exits — allowed by the schema — mint two `go_<dir>` the static check can't see); and `option()` already filters by resolvability so "every offered action resolves" is true by construction (vacuous). Uniqueness is the orthogonal, uncovered property. The shipped packs avoid collisions only incidentally (object ids unique + visibleObjectIds excludes held → room-visible and held examine/read ids are disjoint).
- **The lock (test-only, REUSES the existing seam):** new `tests/regression/parser_action_id_unique.test.ts` runs the shared exhaustive BFS (`support/exhaustive_endings.ts`) under the bug_0146 LIVENESS action policy (step every action except DROP/CLOSE + the pure-observation verbs — the widest tractable region, visiting read-flag states too) and, at EVERY distinct reachable non-terminal state, calls the engine's own `enumerateActions` and asserts its option ids are pairwise distinct. The menu inspected at each state is independent of which actions the BFS STEPS, so the policy only widens the set of menus examined — strictly more thorough. Terminal states (no menu) are skipped. **Both shipped parser packs pass — no reachable state ever offers a duplicate action id** (same reachable region as bug_0146: alchemists ~5.7k / sealed_crypt ~9.6k states, well under the 200k cap). Controls: a planted bounded-duplicate (two same-direction exits → two `go_north`) the static DUPLICATE_ID validator does NOT catch is FLAGGED; a clean twin (same shape, distinct directions) passes — proving the control bites on the duplication, not the shape. Anti-vacuity guards assert real menus were inspected (statesChecked > 0, actionsSeen > statesChecked). NO new support-helper code.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test ONLY. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine/helper change; **NO hash pin moved** (all 9 packs validate 0/0, content_hashes unchanged). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key. Sound: every state visited is a real legal playthrough, the menu is the engine's own `enumerateActions` output, the search FAILS on `cappedOut` (never truncates silently).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0151_parser_action_id_unique.yaml` + the new regression test (discovery guard + 2 per-pack uniqueness assertions with anti-vacuity + 1 planted-duplicate negative control + 1 clean-twin no-false-alarm control).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **183 files / 1014 tests pass** (+1 file / +5 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** menu-integrity is now proven for PARSER; the natural follow-on (if rotating off the keystone) is the same action-id-uniqueness assertion for the other two modes — RPG (reuses the parser enumerator but adds seeded combat actions → drive the best/worst-roll bracket, `exhaustiveEndingsMulti`, with the same liveness policy + no-HP-gating guard, exactly as bug_0147/0149 did) and CYOA (CHOOSE ids minted from scene choice ids over the deterministic BFS). The deterministic structural net is otherwise exhaustive across all 3 modes on FOUR content axes (every-ending reachability bug_0121/0122/0124 + every-variant liveness bug_0145/0146/0147 + score-economy soundness bug_0148/0149 + no-dead-pocket liveness bug_0150), now joined by parser menu-integrity. Content is blind-exhausted, authoring mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark/debugging tools feature-complete (bug_0142/0143). The one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** — a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs so the turns-to-end / scene-coverage (hide_graph) pairings populate beyond the baseline `—` — deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). ### Cycle result — test/structural (bug_0150): every PROGRESS-REACHABLE state of every shipped pack (all 3 modes) is proven LIVE — it can still reach SOME declared ending. NO reachable state is a dynamic SOFT-LOCK POCKET (a place normal play wanders into and can never finish from). The DYNAMIC ground-truth complement to the STATIC `SOFTLOCK` validator (which has KNOWN false negatives — bug_0092 found a real soft-lock masked behind a provably-dead terminal), and a fourth exhaustive structural axis over the shared BFS alongside endings-reachable / variant-liveness / score-economy. Test-only + ONE backward-compatible helper hook (`onEdge`); no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sunken_barrow (RPG, seed 59 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0149's next-focus named it as least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0141 seed 29). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T04-44-34-197Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **all three declared endings** — the full-prep WIN (50/50, ending_victory: shade's +3-defense counsel → kill the barrow-wight +10 → lever the slab +15 → take the circlet +25), the under-armed DEATH (0/50, ending_fallen, a fair earned loss at base defense), and the FORBIDDEN path (25/50, ending_woken — prise the sealed sarcophagus instead of taking the crown, a foreshadowed "you should have listened" bad ending) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs**. It confirmed transparent combat math, the slab might-check retry loop has **no HP cost and is safe to repeat**, and all three endings "fire cleanly with empty action lists" — i.e. it probed for, and did not find, a place play gets stuck. **sunken_barrow re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** That very observation — *can you ever get stuck?* — is exactly the property no machine check yet PROVED. No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **Why test/structural (justified, KEY-FREE):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect, the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every prior structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), and the keystone deferred for want of a provider key (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md), the highest-value move is a §15 structural GUARANTEE closing a genuine residual gap the playtest's "can you get stuck?" focus surfaced. **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack) and the API-keyed keystone (no outbound calls without owner authorization).
- **The gap (a defect class NO existing check covers):** the every-ending-reachable proofs (bug_0121/0122/0124) certify `declared endings ⊆ reachable-FROM-START` — a statement about the START state ONLY. A pack can have every declared ending reachable by some route AND still contain a reachable SOFT-LOCK POCKET: a region a legal progress move drops you into and from which no ending is reachable at all. The ending census passes (the endings fire via OTHER routes) while a player who took the wrong door is permanently stuck — the single worst structural defect in an adventure, and the ending proof is blind to it. The static `SOFTLOCK` validator approximates it on the condition graph but has KNOWN false negatives (bug_0092: a real soft-lock MASKED because a dead terminal counted as a live escape edge). This is the DYNAMIC complement — same static→dynamic relationship as bug_0146 vs static parser_variant_shadowing and bug_0148 vs static `SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN`.
- **The lock (test + ONE backward-compatible helper hook):** the shared seam `support/exhaustive_endings.ts` gains an optional `onEdge(fromKey,toKey)` in `SearchOpts` that surfaces every transition the BFS produces (incl. edges into already-seen states — the graph JOINS a backward fixpoint needs); when omitted the BFS is byte-for-byte the original, so all 8 prior consumer suites (44 tests) stay green. New `tests/regression/no_dead_pocket.test.ts` drives the BFS over the PROGRESS-action policy (the EXACT region/policy the ending proofs certify), reconstructs the reachable transition graph via `onEdge`, runs a backward-liveness fixpoint (seed LIVE with every terminal, propagate over reverse edges), and asserts every reachable state is LIVE. Mode-agnostic: CYOA/parser drive one deterministic rule set, RPG drives the best/worst-roll bracket (`exhaustiveEndingsMulti`, bug_0124) guarded by the reused no-HP-gating assertion. **Every shipped pack passes — 0 dead pockets across all 9** (largest: watchtower_road ~82.6k progress-reachable states, cold_forge ~19k, all fully live). Controls bite both deterministic modes: a CYOA self-looping trap scene with no exit is FLAGGED; a parser exit-less trap room is FLAGGED; each clean twin (an exit to an ending / a way back) is fully live (no false positive).
- **Soundness & honest scope:** the proof covers the PROGRESS-action region (skip the reversible DROP/CLOSE + pure-observation verbs) — so it proves a player using progress moves can never strand themselves out of reach of every ending; it deliberately does NOT claim DROP/CLOSE cannot self-strand (no shipped route gates on a drop, the helper's MONOTONE-RESTRICTION note; dropping a needed key is self-inflicted). PASS is sound: CYOA/parser exact; for RPG the best/worst bracket reaches every routing-relevant outcome (monotone in the roll) so a state LIVE under the bracketed edges is LIVE under some real play, and dead under BOTH extremes is genuinely dead. A cap-out makes the graph partial → the test FAILS, never a silent pass.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test + a backward-compatible optional helper hook (`onEdge`), added the SAME way bug_0145/0146 added `onState`/`SearchOpts` to this seam; default path unchanged, all 44 prior consumer tests green. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine change; **NO hash pin moved** (all 9 packs validate 0/0, content_hashes unchanged). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key. The largest-pack BFS gets a generous per-test `TEST_TIMEOUT_MS` (the edge bookkeeping nudges watchtower's ~83k-state walk just past vitest's 5s default under parallel load; a true blowup still trips the `MAX_STATES` cap, not the wall-clock).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0150_no_dead_pocket.yaml` + the new regression test (3-mode discovery guard + per-pack liveness assertions for all 9 packs + 2 CYOA controls [bite + no-false-alarm] + 2 parser controls [bite + no-false-alarm]).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **1009 tests / 182 files pass** (+1 file / +14 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the deterministic structural net is now exhaustive across all 3 modes on FOUR axes: every-ending reachability (bug_0121/0122/0124) + every-variant liveness (bug_0145/0146/0147) + score-economy soundness (bug_0148/0149) + no-dead-pocket liveness (bug_0150). The reachable region is now proven both fully terminating-reachable AND fully forward-live. Content is blind-exhausted, authoring is mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark/debugging tools feature-complete (bug_0142/0143). The one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** — a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs so the turns-to-end / scene-coverage (hide_graph) pairings populate beyond the baseline `—` — deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). If rotating off the keystone, prefer §15 invariant-locks of recurring blind-tester false positives over content/structural churn (the structural levers are now deeply disarmed). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] sunken_barrow is now freshly attended (seed 59); reading the rotation newest→oldest (sunken_barrow bug_0150 / cold_forge bug_0149 / sealed_crypt bug_0148 / wreckers bug_0147 / tithe_barn bug_0146 / white_stag bug_0145 / clockwork bug_0144 / watchtower bug_0143 / alchemists bug_0142), the least-recently blind-played pack is now **alchemists_tower** (parser, last dedicated pass bug_0142) — rotate the next mandated pass there. It confirmed the transparent combat/skill dice math (`d6 X+atk−def`, the retry-until-success might check), every reactive room/object text in sync, and the spirit's four counsels each mapping to a concrete mechanical lever. **cold_forge re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** Its ONE §5 structural note — "Max score is exactly reachable and the +15/+15/+20 awards sum cleanly to the declared 50" — is **precisely the score-economy property bug_0148 machine-proved for PARSER but which the RPG mode never had**, pointing straight at this cycle's lever. - **Why test/structural (justified, the on-plan KEY-FREE lever bug_0148's next-focus named):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect, the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), and the keystone deferred for want of a provider key (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md), the highest-value move is bug_0148's OWN named next-focus: "the natural extension is the RPG mode (also score-bearing: cold_forge 50, sunken_barrow 50), driving the best/worst-roll bracket under the same liveness policy — but it must first confirm no score award is gated on a roll-dependent transient the best/worst extremes skip (the same caveat bug_0147 resolved for RPG variant liveness)." This cycle lands exactly that, having RESOLVED the caveat (below). **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack) and the API-keyed keystone (no outbound calls without owner authorization).
- **The gap (a defect class NO existing RPG check covers):** two real score defects slip through every current check. (1) **BOUNDED OVERFLOW** — a double-counted/re-farmable award pushes the reachable score PAST the ceiling (a "55/50"). The exhaustive ending solver catches only an UNBOUNDED farm (score never settles → state cap → loud `cappedOut`); a BOUNDED over-award keeps the space finite, so the solver passes and nothing flags it. (2) **PHANTOM POINTS** — `max_score` declared HIGHER than any route can reach (the completionist's "25/50 and no points left anywhere"). The reachability proof checks only WHICH endings fire, never the score AT them.
- **The caveat, confirmed and resolved (the SCORE specialization of the bracket monotonicity):** `observed max ≤ true reachable max` ALWAYS (every visited state is a real legal play on a legal die face), so OVERFLOW detection is unconditionally sound. The PHANTOM direction needs `observed max ≥ true max`, i.e. the bracket must reach a true-maximum path. It does, because the **BEST-roll regime collects every roll-INDEPENDENT award AND every roll-WINNING award** — an enemy's `on_defeat` fires when it dies (best regime wins every fight), a skill check's `on_success` fires on the max d20 (best regime passes every check). The ONLY award site the best regime skips is a skill-check `on_failure` (roll-LOSING); there is **no "survive a lost fight" award** (a lost fight ends at `death_ending`, terminal). So the suite ASSERTS no score award rides an `on_failure` (the new `scoreAwardOnFailure` guard) — a future one fails LOUD rather than under-counting into a FALSE phantom alarm. It also reuses the bug_0124/0147 **no-HP-gating guard** (the bracket reaches the right STATES only if no routing condition reads a roll-dependent HP transient). Both shipped packs satisfy both guards.
- **The lock (test-only, REUSES the existing seam):** new `tests/regression/rpg_score_economy_sound.test.ts` drives `exhaustiveEndingsMulti([bestRng-rules, worstRng-rules])` (the bug_0124 bracket) with the bug_0146 liveness `explore` policy + the `onState` hook to record the maximum `score` var over the COMPLETE reachable region — crucially **including TERMINAL states**, because both packs' TOP award lands AT the win (cold_forge's `+20` ember `on_enter`, sunken_barrow's `+25` circlet `take_effects` both fire as the win condition trips; the shared BFS calls `onState` before its `s.ended` check, so terminal awards are captured). It asserts the reachable maximum equals `pack.meta.max_score` EXACTLY for every auto-discovered RPG pack. **Both shipped RPG packs pass — reachable max == declared (cold_forge 50, sunken_barrow 50).** Controls: a planted bounded-OVERFLOW pack the ending solver would NOT catch (35 reachable vs declared 30); a planted PHANTOM pack (20 vs 50); a **win-a-fight load-bearing control** (a +10 `on_defeat` award the player reaches ONLY under best rolls — worst regime dies at 0, proving the best-roll regime is load-bearing for the score economy, the score analogue of bug_0147's win-a-fight liveness control); and an **on_failure-guard bite control** (a +5 award on a skill-check `on_failure` trips `scoreAwardOnFailure`, while both shipped packs' narrate-only `on_failure` do not). NO new support-helper code.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test ONLY. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine change; **NO hash pin moved** (sunken_barrow `7791fc14…`, cold_forge `8590fefd…`, all 9 packs validate 0/0). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0149_rpg_score_economy_sound.yaml` + the new regression test (discovery guard + scoring-anti-vacuity guard + 2 per-pack economy assertions + OVERFLOW control + PHANTOM control + win-a-fight best-roll-load-bearing control + on_failure-guard bite control).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite (+1 file / +8 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the score-economy class is now closed for BOTH scoring modes (parser bug_0148 + RPG bug_0149); CYOA uses no milestone scoring, so the scope is complete. The deterministic structural-verification net is now exhaustive across all three modes on THREE axes: every-ending reachability (bug_0121/0122/0124) + every-variant liveness (bug_0145/0146/0147) + score-economy soundness for the two scoring modes (bug_0148/0149). Content is blind-exhausted, authoring is mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark + debugging tools are feature-complete (bug_0142/0143). The one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** — a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs so the turns-to-end / scene-coverage (hide_graph) pairings populate beyond the baseline `—` — deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). If rotating off the keystone, prefer §15 invariant-locks of recurring blind-tester false positives over content/structural churn (the structural levers are now disarmed). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] cold_forge is now freshly attended (seed 53); reading the rotation newest→oldest (cold_forge bug_0149 / sealed_crypt bug_0148 / wreckers bug_0147 / tithe_barn bug_0146 / white_stag bug_0145 / clockwork bug_0144 / watchtower bug_0143 / alchemists bug_0142 / sunken_barrow bug_0141), the least-recently blind-played pack is now **sunken_barrow** (RPG, last dedicated pass bug_0141 seed 29) — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn.

### Cycle result — test/structural (bug_0148): every shipped PARSER pack's SCORE ECONOMY is proven sound — the maximum score reachable by concrete play equals the declared `max_score`, EXACTLY. The dynamic complement to bug_0116's STATIC `SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN`, and the score analogue of the bug_0145/0146/0147 variant-liveness proofs (same exhaustive BFS, same `onState` hook, a new property asserted at every reachable state). Test-only, REUSES the existing seam (no helper change); no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sealed_crypt (parser, seed 37 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0147's next-focus named it as least-recent, last dedicated pass bug_0139; every other pack has been attended more recently in the cycle log). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T04-17-43-894Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **all three declared endings** — ending_victory 35/35 (the canonical rope→well→chest→iron-key→gate solve), ending_doom (death, broke the lead plague-seal), ending_plunder (greed, robbed the founder's coffin) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs**. It confirmed the reactive prose firing live (bell-tower peg reads bare after taking the rope; well/crypt rewrite on tie/unlock) and the three-way iron-key fork well-telegraphed. Its ONE structural note was about the SCORE shown at the bad endings (10/35), which it **correctly judged conventional IF behaviour, not a bug** — but that focus on the score readout pointed at the one economy property the verification net never machine-checked. **sealed_crypt re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.** No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **Why test/structural (justified, KEY-FREE):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect, the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), and the keystone deferred for want of a provider key (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md), the highest-value move is a §15 structural GUARANTEE the playtest's own focus surfaced. The corpus already proves every ENDING reachable (parser_all_endings_reachable) and every VARIANT live (parser_variant_liveness) — but NOTHING proved the score ECONOMY sound. **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack) and the API-keyed keystone (no outbound calls without owner authorization).
- **The gap (a defect class NO existing check covers):** two real score defects slip through every current check. (1) **BOUNDED OVERFLOW** — a misconfigured / double-counted / re-farmable award pushes the reachable score PAST the ceiling (a "37/35"). The exhaustive ending solver catches only an UNBOUNDED farm (score never settles → state cap → loud `cappedOut`); a BOUNDED over-award (two one-time awards summing above max, or a miscalibrated `inc_var`) keeps the space finite, so the solver passes and nothing flags it. (2) **PHANTOM POINTS** — `max_score` declared HIGHER than any route can reach (the completionist's "35/40 and no points left anywhere"). The reachability proof checks only WHICH endings fire, never the score AT them. This is the exact DUAL of bug_0104 ("score peaks early": there the max is reached too SOON, here never at all); bug_0116's `SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN` reasons about WHEN the peak lands relative to the win act, never that it EQUALS the declared max.
- **The lock (test-only, REUSES the existing seam):** new `tests/regression/parser_score_economy_sound.test.ts` drives the shared exhaustive BFS (`support/exhaustive_endings.ts`) with the bug_0146 LIVENESS action policy (step every action except the pure-observation verbs + DROP — crucially DOES step READ, which carries sealed_crypt's +5 headstone award the reachability policy skips) and, via the `onState` hook, records the maximum `score` var over the COMPLETE reachable region, asserting it equals `pack.meta.max_score` EXACTLY for every auto-discovered parser pack. ONE tight invariant catches both directions (reachable max > declared = overflow/farm; < declared = phantom). **Both shipped parser packs pass — reachable max == declared (sealed_crypt 35, alchemists_tower 40).** Two negative controls bite (a planted bounded-OVERFLOW pack the ending solver would NOT catch — 20 reachable vs declared 15; a planted PHANTOM pack — 10 reachable vs declared 20) plus a no-false-alarm positive control (two gated awards summing EXACTLY to 15). NO new support-helper code — reuses the behaviour-preserving `explore`/`onState` params bug_0145/0146 added.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test ONLY. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine change; **NO hash pin moved** (all 9 packs validate 0/0, content_hashes unchanged). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key. Sound: the liveness policy steps READ and every progress action so every score-award state is visited; every state it visits is a real legal playthrough so the observed maximum is the TRUE reachable maximum (no award gates on a dropped-item location, the one thing skipped DROP could affect); the search FAILS on `cappedOut`, never a silent truncation.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0148_parser_score_economy_sound.yaml` + the new regression test (discovery guard + scoring-anti-vacuity guard + 2 per-pack economy assertions + OVERFLOW control + PHANTOM control + positive control).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **180 files / 987 tests pass** (+1 file / +7 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).

The natural extension is the **RPG** mode (also score-bearing: cold_forge 40, sunken_barrow 50), driving the best/worst-roll bracket (`exhaustiveEndingsMulti`, bug_0124) under the same liveness policy — but it must first confirm no score award is gated on a roll-dependent transient the best/worst extremes skip (the same caveat bug_0147 resolved for RPG variant liveness). CYOA packs use no milestone scoring, so parser+RPG are the whole scope. Otherwise the standing position holds: content blind-exhausted, authoring mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark + debugging tools feature-complete (bug_0142/0143), structural net exhaustive (every-ending reachability + every-variant liveness across all three modes + now parser score-economy soundness). The one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** — a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs — deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). ### Cycle result — test/structural (bug_0147): every reactive `variant` of every shipped RPG pack is proven LIVE — the RPG completion of the dead-reactive-prose liveness trilogy (CYOA bug_0145 + parser bug_0146 + RPG here). For each auto-discovered RPG pack it drives the existing best/worst-roll bracket (`exhaustiveEndingsMulti`, bug_0124) under the bug_0146 liveness action policy, walks the complete reachable state region, and asserts every declared room/object variant is the FIRST match in some state where its subject is in view — exactly what `roomDescription`/`objectDescription` would display. Together with the static `parser_variant_shadowing` (bug_0091, which already covers RPG rooms/objects since RPG reuses the parser schema) this closes the "dead reactive prose" class for the LAST of the three modes. Test-only, REUSES the existing support seam (no helper change); no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on wreckers_light (CYOA, seeds 11–14 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0144's next-focus named it as least-recent, last attended bug_0138 seed 53; every other pack has been attended more recently in the cycle log). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-03T04-05-46-678Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all four declared endings** — ending_saved (read journal → light the lamp, the knowing rescue), ending_drowned (climbed the rail unprepared), ending_wrecker (oil+striker → false low light, the greed route), ending_mercy (tended the dying keeper) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs**. It specifically confirmed the reactive prose firing live: the mantel reads bare after taking the striker, the gallery rewrites and surfaces `hang_false_light` once you carry the means, and the lantern reframes the rescue once `knows_truth` is set. **A point higher than the bug_0138 seed-53 pass — wreckers_light re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5.** No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **Why test/structural (justified, the on-plan KEY-FREE lever bug_0146's next-focus named):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the genuine highest-value move is bug_0146's OWN named next-focus: "an RPG liveness proof would drive the multi-rule `exhaustiveEndingsMulti` (best/worst-roll bracket, bug_0124) with the same liveness action policy — but must first confirm no RPG variant guard turns on a roll-dependent transient (e.g. a mid-combat HP threshold) that the best/worst bracket skips over." This cycle lands exactly that, having RESOLVED the caveat (below). **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack) and the API-keyed keystone (deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md; needs a provider key — no outbound API calls without owner authorization).
- **The caveat, confirmed and resolved (the crux of this cycle):** the best/worst-roll bracket is COMPLETE for variant liveness only if no variant's `when` reads a roll-dependent TRANSIENT the two extremes skip over — i.e. a raw HP value (a middle roll can land an intermediate HP the extremes never visit). It does not: RPG variants gate on flags / items / non-HP vars / object state / visited, all of which evolve by roll-INDEPENDENT actions or by MONOTONE combat consequences the bracket reaches (an enemy's `defeat_flag` + `on_defeat` fire on a kill — driven by the BEST regime; a skill check's `on_success`/`on_failure` fire on the max/min d20 — both regimes). The suite ASSERTS no condition reads an HP var (player `hp` / hidden `__enemy_hp_*`), the SAME load-bearing guard `rpg_all_endings_reachable` makes (bug_0124), here covering variant `when`s as a subset of all pack conditions; both shipped packs pass it. A future HP-gated variant would FAIL LOUD (branch the HP in the solver), never silently under-credit.
- **The lock (test-only, REUSES the existing seam):** new `tests/regression/rpg_variant_liveness.test.ts` drives `exhaustiveEndingsMulti([bestRng-rules, worstRng-rules])` (the bug_0124 bracket) with the bug_0146 `explore` liveness policy + the `onState` hook to record, at each reachable state, the current room's first-match variant and each PRESENT object's first-match variant (computed with the engine's own `evalConditions`), asserting every declared variant of every auto-discovered RPG pack is displayed somewhere. **Both shipped RPG packs pass — every reactive room/object variant is live** (cold_forge 7 variants / 19005 states; sunken_barrow 4 / 1885 states, both well under the 200k cap). A planted-dead-variant **negative control** proves the check bites; a **win-a-fight load-bearing control** (an enemy tuned so the player WINS under best rolls but DIES under worst — its `defeat_flag` variant credited by the full bracket but NOT by a worst-regime-alone twin) proves the best-roll regime is essential, the combat analogue of bug_0146's READ-load-bearing control. RPG endings reuse `ParserEndingSchema` (no `variants`), so room + object variants are the whole scope, exactly as in the parser proof. NO new support-helper code — it reuses the behaviour-preserving params bug_0145/bug_0146 added.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test ONLY. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine change; **NO hash pin moved** (sunken_barrow `7791fc14…`, cold_forge `8590fefd…`, all 9 packs validate 0/0). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key. Sound: every successor is a real `makeStep` on a LEGAL die face (1/6, 1/20) so nothing spurious is visited; the only routing-relevant combat/skill consequence is MONOTONE in the roll so the best/worst extremes bracket every middle-roll outcome; the search FAILS on `cappedOut` (never truncates silently); the no-HP-gating guard closes the one middle-roll-transient gap.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0147_rpg_variant_liveness.yaml` + the new regression test (discovery guard + 2 per-pack liveness assertions + 1 negative control + 1 win-a-fight best-roll-load-bearing control).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **179 files / 980 tests pass** (+1 file / +5 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the dead-reactive-prose class is now **fully closed across ALL THREE modes** — CYOA (bug_0085 static + bug_0145 dynamic), parser (bug_0091 static + bug_0146 dynamic), RPG (bug_0091 static, shared parser schema, + bug_0147 dynamic here). The deterministic structural-verification net is genuinely exhaustive: every-ending reachability (bug_0121/0122/0124) AND every-variant liveness across all three modes. Content is blind-exhausted, authoring is mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark + debugging tools are feature-complete (bug_0142/0143). The one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** — a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs so the turns-to-end / scene-coverage (hide_graph) pairings populate beyond the baseline `—` — deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). If rotating off the keystone, prefer §15 invariant-locks of recurring blind-tester false positives over content/structural churn; the structural levers are now disarmed. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn.

### Cycle result — test/structural (bug_0146): every reactive `variant` of every shipped PARSER pack is proven LIVE — the parser half of bug_0145's CYOA liveness proof, and the DYNAMIC complement to bug_0091's static `parser_variant_shadowing` check. For each auto-discovered parser pack it walks the complete reachable state region and asserts every declared room/object variant is the FIRST match in some state where its subject is in view (the room you stand in / an object present to examine) — exactly what `roomDescription`/`objectDescription` would display. Together bug_0091 + bug_0146 close the "dead reactive prose" class for parser, as bug_0085 + bug_0145 did for CYOA. Test-only + two backward-compatible test-support params; no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on tithe_barn (CYOA, seed 67 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0145's next-focus named tithe_barn as least-recent (last attended bug_0136); every other pack has been attended more recently in the cycle log). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T18-00-15-795Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent reached **three declared endings** — ending_doors (the gated systemic justice: read the ledger → throw the doors open), ending_fall (the honestly-telegraphed rotten-loft death), ending_bounty (uninformed betrayal, took the lord's silver) — and confirmed the fourth (`let_her_go` mercy) present as a legal action, rating the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs**. It specifically verified the highest-risk gate live via MCP: `open_doors` is correctly ABSENT at the reckoning uninformed and PRESENT after reading the ledger (`knows_truth`), with matching scene-text variants; the granary-floor rewrite and the reckoning's two `knows_truth` variants all in sync. **A point higher than the bug_0136 pass — tithe_barn re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5.** No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **Why test/structural (justified, the on-plan KEY-FREE lever bug_0145's next-focus named):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the genuine highest-value move is bug_0145's OWN named next-focus: "the natural EXTENSION is the parser mode (also deterministic, also variant-bearing — `parser_variant_shadowing` is its static half) — but a parser liveness proof must first confirm no variant guard turns on a BFS-skipped (reversible/observation) action's effect before reusing the `onState` hook." This cycle lands exactly that, having first RESOLVED the caveat (below). **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack) and the API-keyed keystone (deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md; needs a provider key — no outbound API calls without owner authorization).
- **The caveat, confirmed and resolved (the crux of this cycle):** the reachability BFS skips `{DROP, CLOSE, LOOK, INVENTORY, READ, INSPECT}` — a MONOTONE restriction that is sound for the every-ending PROOF (restricting actions only HIDES an ending → loud failure) but NOT for liveness (skipping a display state → FALSE dead). The caveat bit for real: **alchemists_tower's spellbook READ sets `read_recipe`** (an interaction `effects`, legal_actions.ts READ case appends `it.effects`), and **two variants (cauldron, black_phial) gate on `read_recipe`** — yet READ is in the skipped set, so a naive bug_0145 port would FALSELY flag both dead. So the liveness search WIDENS the action policy to step every action except the ones that provably cannot gate a variant: LOOK/INVENTORY/INSPECT (narrate-only / no resolver case → mutate no state field any `Condition` reads), CLOSE (no resolver case → never legal), and DROP (boundary below). READ and every progress action ARE stepped.
- **The DROP boundary (documented, fails LOUD not silent):** DROP is the one skipped action that still mutates state. It is skipped because (1) **stepping it blows the 200k cap on BOTH shipped packs** (measured: full-action search caps out at 200k; the progress+READ policy settles at **alchemists 5742 / sealed_crypt 9602** states) — the inventory-subset × drop-location explosion the progress restriction exists to avoid; and (2) DROP's only variant-OBSERVABLE effect is `not_item: X` — its `place_object` sets the object's room, and **NO condition in the closed DSL reads object location** (conditions.ts has only is_open/is_unlocked for object state), so a dropped item's whereabouts is invisible to every `when`. The residual gap — a variant satisfiable ONLY by taking X then dropping it (X force-held everywhere its context is reachable) — is an anti-pattern (gating prose on a put-it-back state); NO shipped variant uses it (every shipped `not_item: X` variant is satisfiable on the natural pre-acquisition / post-consumption route, all found live), and a future one would surface as a LOUD false-alarm failure, never a silent pass of dead prose.
- **The lock (test-only + behaviour-preserving params):** (a) the shared mode-agnostic BFS (`tests/regression/support/exhaustive_endings.ts`) gained two OPTIONAL knobs — `explore` (action policy) and `key` (dedupe fingerprint) — both defaulting to the existing reachability behaviour, so every current consumer (cyoa/parser/rpg all-endings-reachable, cyoa_variant_liveness) is byte-for-byte unchanged and stays green (19 cases re-run, all pass). (b) new `tests/regression/parser_variant_liveness.test.ts` drives the BFS with the liveness policy + the `onState` hook to record, at each reachable state, the current room's first-match variant and each PRESENT (visible-or-held → examinable) object's first-match variant (computed with the engine's own `evalConditions`), asserting every declared variant of every auto-discovered parser pack is displayed somewhere. **Both shipped parser packs pass — every reactive room/object variant is live.** A planted-dead-variant **negative control** proves the check bites; a **READ-load-bearing control** (the same read-flag variant credited WITH READ stepped, then a READ-excluded run that FAILS to credit it) proves stepping READ is essential — the caveat made concrete. Parser endings carry no variants (`ParserEndingSchema` has none), so only room + object variants are in scope.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test only, plus two optional, behaviour-preserving params on a TEST-support helper. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine change; **NO hash pin moved** (all 9 packs validate 0/0, content_hashes unchanged). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key. Sound: the search FAILS on `cappedOut` (never truncates silently); a variant is credited only when it is the first match AND its subject is in view in a genuinely-reachable state.

### Cycle result — test/structural (bug_0145): every reactive `variant` of every shipped CYOA pack is proven LIVE — a §15 structural lock that, for each auto-discovered pack, walks the COMPLETE concrete reachable state region (the bug_0121 exhaustive BFS) and asserts every declared scene/ending variant is the FIRST match in some reachable state (exactly what `sceneText`/`endingText` would display there). The dynamic complement to bug_0085's static shadowing check: together they close the "dead reactive prose" defect class. Test-only + a backward-compatible test-support hook; no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on white_stag (CYOA, seed 91 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: reading the cycle log newest→oldest, white_stag's most recent attendance (bug_0135 seed 23) is the OLDEST of all nine packs' most-recent attendances). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T17-47-04-289Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **four endings** — ending_offering (the gated knowing rite: read stone → lay bow+arrow), ending_lost (chanced the rotten ice → drowned), ending_quarry (uninformed kill, gold + a doomed spring), ending_thaw (uninformed mercy, spring comes kind) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs**. Flawless gating (`lay_offering` appears at the bluff ONLY after `knows_truth`), reactive tarn/bluff prose in sync, all four endings distinct and emotionally resonant. **A point higher on enjoyment than the bug_0135 seed-23 pass (4/5)** — white_stag re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5. No actionable content defect; content is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]].
- **The seam the playtest illuminated (justified structural move, NOT content churn):** the tester's one structural observation was "five reachable outcomes exist; I confirmed four — the fifth (loosing the arrow AFTER reading the stone, the *knowing-kill* reactive epilogue) I did not trigger this run." Reactive ending/scene VARIANTS are prose a single blind run can silently miss, and nothing PROVED each declared variant is actually reachable as displayed text. "Dead reactive prose" has two causes and only one was machine-checked: (1) SHADOWING — a later sibling entailed by an earlier one can never be first match (bug_0085, caught statically but soundly partial: reasons only over pure literal/var-bound conjunctions, never the pack's real gating); (2) UNREACHABLE GUARD — a variant whose `when` no reachable state at that scene/ending can satisfy (a guard flag set only on a branch that never returns, a var threshold the gating never reaches, a forbidden flag pair). Cause (2) was unchecked, and no blind run is GUARANTEED to surface it (the prose just never appears on the routes a tester walks). The bug_0121 BFS already enumerates the full reachable region; it just discarded everything but terminal ending ids (the bug_0138 "computed-but-invisible" class).
- **The lock (test-only + behaviour-preserving hook):** (a) added an OPTIONAL `onState` visitor to the shared mode-agnostic BFS (`tests/regression/support/exhaustive_endings.ts`) that observes every DISTINCT reachable state exactly once — no behaviour change for existing callers (cyoa/parser/rpg all-endings-reachable stay green). (b) new `tests/regression/cyoa_variant_liveness.test.ts` uses it to record, at each reachable state, which scene/ending variant is the first match (computed with the engine's own `evalConditions`, identical to `runner.ts`) and asserts every declared variant of every shipped CYOA pack is displayed somewhere. **All 5 shipped CYOA packs pass — every reactive variant is live.** A planted-dead-variant **negative control** (a scene variant guarded on flags no path sets) proves the check actually bites; a discovery guard prevents a vacuous pass.
- **Why WITHIN the bar / sound (no false positives):** for CYOA the only action is CHOOSE so the BFS is COMPLETE; the search FAILS on `cappedOut` (never truncates silently); a variant is called dead ONLY when the full reachable region genuinely never displays it. Regression test only, plus an optional, behaviour-preserving param on a TEST-support helper. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine change; **NO hash pin moved** (all 9 packs validate 0/0, content_hashes unchanged). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0145_cyoa_variant_liveness.yaml` + the new regression test above (discovery guard + 5 per-pack liveness assertions + 1 negative control).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **177 files / 970 tests pass** (+1 file / +7 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the dead-reactive-prose class is now fully closed for CYOA (bug_0085 static shadowing + bug_0145 dynamic liveness). The natural EXTENSION is the **parser** mode (also deterministic, also variant-bearing — `parser_variant_shadowing` is its static half) — but a parser liveness proof must first confirm no variant guard turns on a BFS-skipped (reversible/observation) action's effect before reusing the `onState` hook; that scoping is the next key-free structural lever if rotating off the keystone. Otherwise the standing position holds: content blind-exhausted, authoring mode-complete (bug_0139/0140), benchmark + debugging tools feature-complete (bug_0142/0143); the one remaining HIGH-value lever is the **keystone** (a REAL frontier model driving the author→validate→revise loop and playing the spatial packs), deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key — do NOT make outbound API calls without owner authorization). ### Cycle result — test/lock (bug_0144): the gallery's reactive text is locked as ENTRY-PATH-INVARIANT — a §15 regression that pins `landing`'s scene prose as a pure function of `ticks`/flags, retiring the recurring "same scene, two descriptions by entry path" blind-tester false positive AND catching a genuine future regression (folding the dumbwaiter's lift narration into the persistent room description). Test-only; no content/engine/validator/schema change, NO hash pin moved; deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on clockwork_heist (CYOA, seed 23 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: last attended bug_0135 seed 5, and the last two cycles' next-focus both named it as least-recent). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T17-36-51-091Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent reached **THREE declared endings** — ending_rich (greedy heist, grab gold), ending_caught (forced the vault door → alarm), ending_truth in BOTH letter-variant prose forms (the hidden crawlspace confession AND the displayed vault copy) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO true bugs**. Flawless gating (strongbox/vault present-vs-absent variants, study-the-lock vs pick-the-lock), reactive prose in sync, the two-letter dual-route design landed as a "genuinely clever touch."
- **Both §4/§5 notes are the standing clockwork "cosmetic clock" false-positive class** ([[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]] / [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]): (1) "the ticks/chime urgency writes a check the mechanics don't cash on a fast route" is INTENDED — `meta.deadline` fires `ending_overstayed` at ticks>=10, tuned GENEROUS on purpose (bug_0079, test-locked by `clockwork_deadline_enforced`) so it never punishes honest play (~4–6 ticks) and only bites aimless circling; the patrol's real bite is the on-the-hour blind crossing (`cross_to_vault_blind` → `ending_patrol`), which a fast route avoids. (2) "the gallery shows different text by entry path (dumbwaiter vs grand stairs)" is **EMPIRICALLY FALSE** — verified live via MCP (sessions 5/6/7, seed 23): the `landing` scene text is a pure function of `ticks`/flags. Reaching the gallery at ticks 2 via the grand STAIRS (kitchens→back_foyer→climb_stairs) yields **byte-identical** scene text to reaching it at ticks 2 via the DUMBWAITER (kitchens→dumbwaiter) — both show the ticks>=2 "no place to be caught standing when it chimes" warning; reaching it at ticks 1 via the stairs shows the bare base text with NO warning. The tester's two runs merely correlated the dumbwaiter with a higher tick count, and the dumbwaiter's "cramped servants' lift / complaining rope" flavor is a transient `narration` EVENT for that one step, never part of the room description.
- **Why test/lock (justified OFF-LIST, the playtest-informed move):** the mandated playtest yielded NO actionable content defect (clockwork re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4) and the deterministic assessor is collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]; benchmark + debugging tools feature-complete for the baseline bot per bug_0143). The remaining genuine levers all need the **keystone** (a REAL frontier model driving the now mode-complete author→validate→revise loop bug_0139/0140 and playing the spatial packs), which stays deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; no outbound API calls without owner authorization). With content blind-clean and the keystone off-limits, the highest-value KEY-FREE move is to LOCK the one concrete finding this pass produced: convert the recurring entry-path misread into a codified invariant (§15 step 5), which both retires the false positive (saving future cycles re-investigating the same non-bug) and guards a real regression. **Resisted re-fixing the clock** (the false-positive magnet — its timing teeth are real + test-locked) and **resisted content churn** (a 10th pack).
- **The lock (test-only):** new `tests/regression/clockwork_gallery_entry_path_invariant.test.ts` pins (1) `landing` scene text is byte-identical via the grand stairs vs the dumbwaiter at the same ticks (2) AND both carry the ticks>=2 chime warning; (2) the variant is tick-gated not path-gated — bare base text at ticks 1 (no chime warning, no "grinds toward the hour"), the warning at ticks 2; (3) the dumbwaiter's lift flavor is a transient `narration` event of that step (asserted present in the step's events) and is NEVER in `landing`'s persistent scene text. This is the entry-path-invariance complement to the existing `clockwork_gallery_crossing_legibility` (reader/non-reader split) and `clockwork_dumbwaiter_honest_framing` tests.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** regression test ONLY. No pack edited; no validator/schema/engine change; **NO hash pin moved** (clockwork_heist content_hash unchanged, all 9 packs validate 0/0). Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0144_clockwork_gallery_entry_path_invariant.yaml` + the new regression test above (3 cases).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **176 files / 963 tests pass** (+1 file / +3 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).

### Cycle result — engine/MCP (bug_0143): `inspect_trace` now SURFACES the first divergent step (`diverged_at_step`) — the per-step divergence localization was already COMPUTED by `replayTrace` and then thrown away by the project's own bug-summarizing tool. Trace-v2 localization (bug_0142 verified it complete in `replay_trace`) reached every consumer EXCEPT the §9.4/§12.5 debugger entry point, which reported `hash_ok:false` without saying WHERE. This closes that "computed-but-invisible" gap (the bug_0138 class) and corrects the two stale comments. Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key; additive tool field, no state/hash change.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on watchtower_road (CYOA, seed 17 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: reading the cycle log newest→oldest, watchtower (bug_0134) is OLDER than clockwork (bug_0135), so bug_0142's "clockwork is least-recent" note overlooked it; the deterministic assessor and the rotation agree on watchtower). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T17-25-16-992Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent reached **all THREE declared endings** — ending_truth (seed 17: the heroic expose, beacon + ledger proof), ending_captured (seed 42: forced the gate empty-handed), ending_escape (seed 7: letter→hermit→checkpoint papers→slip away) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs**. Flawless gating (lantern-gated cellar, letter-gated hermit + checkpoint), state-aware rewrites all in sync, the two branches reconverge cleanly. **Its ONE friction note — `inspect_ground` "reappears on revisit" — was checked EMPIRICALLY via MCP and is FALSE:** the option is gated `not_flag: found_bootprints` and is correctly GONE from the very next observation after one use (verified live, sess_4: flag set → option absent). The static crossroads description contradicts nothing the player did (NOT the bug_0134 hermit-stranger honesty class), so "fixing" it would be re-polishing blind-clean prose — resisted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]. **No actionable content defect — watchtower_road re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.**
- **Why engine/MCP (justified OFF-LIST, the playtest-informed higher-value pick):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every structural lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the highest-value KEY-FREE move is the cleanup bug_0142's next-focus named ("the stale src/mcp/tools.ts divergence comment"). Inspecting it revealed the comment was the symptom of a REAL surfacing gap, not just stale prose: `inspect_trace` called `replayTrace` (which returns `divergedAtStep`) but dropped that field, returning only `hash_ok/final_hash/expected_final_hash`. So the one tool whose job is to LOCALIZE a bug discarded the localization it had already computed. Fixing the surfacing (not just the comment) is the strictly higher-value version of the named lever. **Rejected** content churn (a 10th pack) and the API-keyed keystone (deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md; needs a provider key — no outbound API calls without owner authorization).
- **The gap (src/mcp/tools.ts):** per-step divergence is a finished Trace-v2 feature — `recordTrace` stamps `per_step_hashes`, `replayTrace` returns `divergedAtStep` (the first action whose post-state diverged from that baseline; src/trace/replay.ts, covered by tests/unit/save_trace.test.ts), and `replay_trace` returns it straight through. But `inspect_trace` — the §9.4/§12.5 bug-summarizer — built its own return object and surfaced only the final-hash verdict, omitting `divergedAtStep`. A trace that reaches the CORRECT final state but diverged MID-trace (a self-correcting non-determinism) would read `hash_ok` true with no warning at all; a trace that ended wrong said `hash_ok:false` without the step.
- **The fix (engine/MCP, trust-but-verify — deterministic, NO API key):** (1) `inspect_trace`'s return gains `diverged_at_step: replay.divergedAtStep ?? null` — the first divergent action's index (1:1 with `step_summary`/`actions`), or null when faithful / when the trace carries no per-step baseline (v1). The headline value: it flips `hash_ok` false on a mid-trace divergence the final-hash check alone MISSES. (2) Corrected the two stale comments (`replay_trace`'s "a trace stores only the final hash … needs a Trace v2 field" and `inspect_trace`'s "asserts the recorded final hash") to describe the shipped localization. `replayTrace`/`recordTrace` are untouched.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** additive MCP-tool field. No engine/runtime/validator/schema change; replay/record reused as-is. No content/pack edited; **NO hash pin moved.** The new field is surfaced (deliberately STRENGTHENED) in the inspect_trace unit test (now asserts `diverged_at_step` null on the faithful ending_escape route). Test fixtures write to top-level `traces/*.json`, which is gitignored — not committed.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0143_inspect_trace_divergence_surfaced.yaml` + new `tests/regression/inspect_trace_divergence.test.ts` (3 cases: a faithful Trace-v2 trace localizes nothing — `diverged_at_step` null, `hash_ok` true; a MID-trace baseline divergence is localized to the exact step AND flips `hash_ok` false EVEN WHEN the final hash still matches — the value the final-hash check alone misses; a v1 trace reports null — no per-step baseline). `tests/unit/mcp_tools.test.ts` strengthened in place.
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **175 files / 960 tests pass** (+1 file / +3 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** with `inspect_trace` now surfacing divergence, the full Trace-v2 localization reaches every consumer (record → replay → replay_trace → inspect_trace). The deterministic benchmark + debugging tools are feature-complete for the baseline bot. The remaining genuine levers all need the keystone: a **REAL frontier model** driving the mode-complete author→validate→revise loop (bug_0139/0140) and playing the spatial packs so the turns-to-end / scene-coverage pairings populate beyond the baseline `—`. That keystone stays deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md (needs a provider key; no outbound API calls without owner authorization). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] watchtower_road is now freshly attended (seed 17); reading the rotation newest→oldest, the least-recently blind-played pack is now **clockwork_heist** (CYOA, last attended bug_0135 seed 5) — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn; prefer on-plan structural work.

- **Why engine/benchmark (justified OFF-LIST, the playtest-informed higher-value pick):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every self-extinguishing lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the genuine highest-value move is the on-plan benchmark axis bug_0138/0139/0140/0141 all named as the remaining #2 lever: "more benchmark axes ONLY if a genuinely bot-measurable one is found (turns-to-end is the strongest candidate — it pairs with hide_graph as a second spatial signal)." - **The axis (src/mcp/tools.ts + src/afk/benchmark.ts):** both `run_playtest` summaries (`summarizeCyoa`, `summarizeParserLike`) accumulate, over runs that REACH an ending, the action count (`path.length - 1`) and return `mean_turns_to_end` (mean rounded to 1 dp via a shared `meanTurns()` helper, 0 when none completed). It counts COMPLETED runs only, so timed-out runs never inflate it. `BenchmarkRow` gains the field; the markdown render adds a `Turns→end` column that prints `—` (not a misleading `0.0`) for 0%-completion rows.
- **Honesty note (the pairing is forward-looking):** the baseline coverage bot completes **0%** of the parser/RPG PUZZLE packs (it can't plan multi-step solves), so the shown→hidden turns-to-end RISE does NOT yet appear there — those baseline spatial rows read `—`. The scorecard text says so plainly: the pairing populates as capable agent rows are added. Today the column carries real numbers on every completing CYOA/random row (e.g. watchtower coverage 18.5 vs random 6.7; wreckers coverage 4.1 vs random 8.4 at 50 runs/cell — the coverage bot explores thoroughly, random beelines short paths by luck).
