### Cycle result — engine/legibility (bug_0141): the skill-check narration now NAMES its die (`d20`), the symmetric follow-on to bug_0131's combat legibility fix — combat shows `d6 …`, but the might check showed only `rolled 7 + 3 = 10 vs 12`, so a player reading a FAILED heave against the visible d6 combat die could fear the puzzle impossible (might 3 + d6-max 6 = 9 < DC 12). It is a d20 (max 20 + 3 = 23 ≥ 12, always passable on a better roll); naming the die surfaces that ceiling. Deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key, narration-only — no state/hash change.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sunken_barrow (rpg, seed 29 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]: bug_0140's next-focus named it as least-recent, last attended at bug_0132 seed 19). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T16-59-57-928Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent reached **all THREE declared endings** — ending_victory 50/50 (wise/optimal: ward → wight → slab → claim), ending_fallen (death, skipped the shade's ward → wight hit for 6–9/turn → died at the wight's 4 HP), ending_woken (death, prised the sarcophagus, score frozen 25/50) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs**. Flawless mechanics, reactive room text in sync, the shade's two warnings each honestly cashed, the under-armed death a FAIR earned loss. Its ONE concrete §4/§5 friction note: the might-check die is **opaque** — "Combat is clearly d6, but the slab/sarcophagus might checks roll a much larger, undisclosed die … a solo failed slab heave can't tell you whether you can ever pass (3 + d6 max 9 would be impossible), which briefly made me wonder if I was stuck." That is the actionable finding this cycle fixes.
- **Why engine/legibility (the playtest-informed pick, on-list as the playtest's OWN finding):** unlike ~12 prior sunken_barrow passes that surfaced no actionable content defect (the prose is blind-exhausted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]), this pass found a GENUINE transparency seam — and it is the exact mirror of bug_0131, which made COMBAT damage legible after a blind tester said the plate's +2 defense "felt invisible." bug_0131's own code comment claims combat "mirrors the skill-check narration's transparent format," but the skill check named no die, so combat was actually the MORE legible of the two. This closes that asymmetry. **Rejected** re-polishing the (blind-clean) retry prose — the "keep at it" text papers over the gap but doesn't surface the die, and the tester explicitly named the missing die type, not the wording, as the friction.
- **The asymmetry (src/rpg/combat.ts):** after bug_0131 `resolveAttack` narrates `d6 <roll> + <atk> atk - <def> def`, but `resolveSkillCheck` narrated `rolled <roll> + <bonus> = <total> vs <DC>` — naming NO die. A player reading both systems together infers the unnamed check shares combat's d6, under which might 3 can never clear DC 12, so a winnable puzzle reads as a dead end (the same bug_0027 quit-a-winnable-game failure mode, here from die opacity rather than over-sold grind prose).
- **The fix (engine, trust-but-verify — deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key):** added the `d20 ` prefix to the skill-check lead narration, so a check now reads `might check: d20 <roll> + <bonus> = <total> vs <DC> — success/failure.`, mirroring combat's d6 breakdown. The roll, bonus, total, DC, and the success/failure DECISION are computed exactly as before — only the narration string changed. Updated bug_0131's cross-referencing doc comment + test header (they pointed at the old `rolled X` format).
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** narration is NOT part of the state hash (cf. bug_0119/0131), and the pass/fail ROUTING is identical across the full d20 range, so determinism, replay, and every trace's `expected_final_hash` are unchanged. No content/pack edited; no validator/schema change; **NO hash pin moved** (sunken_barrow content_hash still `7791fc14…`, cold_forge `8590fefd…`). No trace pins the skill-check narration string (traces store state-based hashes, not narration logs).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0141_skillcheck_die_legibility.yaml` + new `tests/regression/rpg_skillcheck_die_legible.test.ts` (4 cases: a FAILED check shows `d20 7 + 3 = 10 vs 12 — failure` AND the die name surfaces the ceiling so the player sees success is reachable; a PASSED check reads `d20 … success` and fires on_success not on_failure; a missing skill var reads `+0`, no NaN; the pass/fail decision is unchanged across the full d20 range 1–20 — proving it is a narration-only refactor).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **173 files / 952 tests pass** (+1 file / +4 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the combat/skill-check legibility pair is now symmetric and complete (bug_0131 + bug_0141) — both dice are named with full breakdowns. Remaining KEY-FREE, on-plan levers: (1) the per-step-divergence localization (bug_0137 next-focus #3) if not already complete; (2) more benchmark axes ONLY if a genuinely bot-measurable one is found. The API-keyed keystone (a REAL frontier model driving the now mode-complete author→validate→revise loop, bug_0140) stays deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md. Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] sunken_barrow is now freshly attended (seed 29); reading the rotation newest→oldest, the least-recently blind-played pack is now **alchemists_tower** (parser) — its last dedicated blind pass predates the recent rpg/parser sweep; rotate the next mandated pass there (watchtower/clockwork/wreckers/white_stag/tithe_barn/sealed_crypt/cold_forge/sunken_barrow all attended more recently). Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn; prefer on-plan structural work.

### Cycle result — engine/authoring (bug_0140): the author → validate → revise loop now covers RPG packs — `runRpgAdapter` routes a Stage-4 pack through `validateRpg`, the project's RICHEST validator (every parser invariant PLUS player stats / enemies-name-a-death-ending / combat-winnability / skill-check-passability). Authoring is now MODE-COMPLETE across all three engine modes (CYOA + parser bug_0139 + RPG here) — the exact bug_0139 next-focus #1. Key-free (deterministic mock author), no engine/validator/schema change, no content, no hash pin moved.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on cold_forge (rpg, seed 41 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND genuinely the least-recently blind-played pack: reading AI_LOOP_STATE newest→oldest, last-attended order is sealed_crypt/wreckers/tithe_barn/white_stag/clockwork/watchtower/alchemists/sunken_barrow/**cold_forge** (bug_0131, the oldest), so the mandated target is correct and consistent with [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] — the memory's stale "clockwork is least-recent" line is corrected here). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T16-44-22-598Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played BOTH strategies to **both declared endings** — ending_victory 50/50 on the careful "gear up" route (spirit +2 attack, founder's plate +2 defense, sentinel down in 2 hits, grate levered 19 vs 12), ending_fallen (death) on the hasty "rush under-armed" route (died by a single exchange with the boss at 2 HP) — rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs**. Flawless mechanics, reactive room text all in sync (trough goes bare, cell text updates after the plate is taken, sentinel-blocked east exit only appears post-kill), the under-armed death a FAIR earned loss (the room's "don't meet it under-armed" warning is honestly cashed by the bug_0101 retune). Its only notes are the standing-known short/linear-for-its-length 4/5 ceiling and the intentional silent +2-attack dialogue gate (already mitigated for the plate by bug_0118's pre-combat variant). **No actionable content defect — cold_forge re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/4.**
- **Why engine/authoring (justified OFF-LIST, the playtest-informed higher-value pick):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every self-extinguishing lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the genuine highest-value move is the on-plan lever bug_0139 named as next-focus **#1**: "the symmetric follow-on is RPG authoring — the same runAdaptLoop with an RpgAdapterOutputSchema + a canned RPG pack routed through validateRpg (the Stage-4 skill-check validator), which would close 'authoring covers all three modes.'" This cycle lands exactly that. **Rejected content churn** (a 10th pack after shipping the 8th+9th two cycles ago) and a fourth benchmark-scorecard column (weak for the deterministic bot) as lower-value, churn-adjacent options.
- **The gap:** the authoring pipeline reached CYOA (validateCyoa) and, since bug_0139, parser (validateParser) — but never validateRpg, the project's DEEPEST validator (all parser checks PLUS the Stage-4 layer). So the Stage-4 RPG invariants — player stat vars, enemies standing in real rooms and naming declared DEATH endings, combat winnability, skill-check passability — were never exercised by the author→validate→revise loop, underselling the true goal (a benchmark of a real model AUTHORING validated games) by leaving the richest mode unreachable.
- **The fix (engine/agent, trust-but-verify — deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key; runs under the default MockAuthorProvider):** (1) added `runRpgAdapter` — a third call into the already-generic `runAdaptLoop` (bug_0139), parametrized to route an RPG pack through `validateRpg`; `runAdapter`/`runParserAdapter` are untouched. (2) Added `RpgAdapterOutputSchema` (RpgPackSchema + the same per-beat classification) to `agents/authoring/schemas.ts`. (3) Extended `MockAuthorProvider` with a canned RPG pack — the same Lighthouse story adapted to a Stage-4 shape: player stats (hp/attack/defense/might), a drowned-wrecker foe whose defeat_flag opens the stair, and a might skill check to free the salt-locked lamp; the win turns on **both** (beat the wight AND pass the check) so combat and the skill check are each LOAD-BEARING. Its FIRST attempt ships an RPG-SPECIFIC defect — a wight whose `death_ending` names an undeclared ending (`ENEMY_DEATH_ENDING_UNDECLARED`, a Stage-4-only code) — corrected once the validator's errors are fed back. (4) `bin/author` gained `--mode rpg` (default cyoa unchanged). Confirmed live via the CLI: rpg mode reaches a GREEN pack in 2 rounds, 0 errors / 0 warnings.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** NO engine/runtime/validator/schema CHANGE — `validateRpg` + `RpgPackSchema` are reused as-is. No content pack edited. **NO hash pin moved.** `runAdaptLoop` was already generic, so the CYOA + parser paths are behaviour-preserving (their existing authoring tests still pass). The mock RPG pack is in-memory only (not under `content/rpg/pack`), so it does NOT enter the health-covers-all-packs bar or the exhaustive RPG ending-reachability solver.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0140_rpg_authoring_loop.yaml` + new `tests/regression/rpg_authoring_loop.test.ts` (5 cases: the RPG validator REJECTS the first attempt with `ENEMY_DEATH_ENDING_UNDECLARED` at maxRounds:1 AND that same pack passed to `validateParser` does NOT raise it — proving the RICHEST validator decides correctness, not the model; the loop converges GREEN in a corrective round; the pack independently re-validates green AND is a genuine Stage-4 shape (enemies + positive stat vars + a skill check); it is actually playable to its win through the RPG engine under forced best rolls — combat 2 rounds AND the might skill check both required; the shared-loop refactor leaves the parser + CYOA paths green). `tests/unit/authoring.test.ts` extended with an rpg-adapter parity block (converges green + re-validates + has enemies; classifies the same mode-agnostic beats).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **172 files / 948 tests pass** (+1 file / +7 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** authoring is now **mode-complete across all three engine modes**, each behind its real validator — the last named authoring lever is closed. Remaining KEY-FREE, on-plan levers: (1) the per-step-divergence localization (bug_0137 next-focus #3) if not already complete; (2) more benchmark axes ONLY if a genuinely bot-measurable one is found (turns-to-end pairs with hide_graph as a second spatial signal; deaths/illegal-rate stay weak for the deterministic bot). The API-keyed keystone (a REAL frontier model driving runWriter/runRpgAdapter) stays deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md — all three mode adapters are now ready for a real provider to slot in behind the env var with no further wiring. Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] cold_forge is now freshly attended (seed 41); reading the rotation, **sunken_barrow is now the least-recently blind-played pack** (bug_0132, seed 19) — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn; prefer on-plan structural work.

### Cycle result — engine/authoring (bug_0139): the author → validate → revise loop now covers PARSER packs, not just CYOA — `runParserAdapter` routes a Zork-style pack through the project's RICHEST validator (`validateParser`: reference integrity / reachability / soft-lock / win-reachability), the exact ULTRAPLAN §Week.4 lever bug_0137 AND bug_0138 both named as next-focus #2. Key-free (deterministic mock author), no engine/validator/schema change, no content, no hash pin moved.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sealed_crypt (parser, seeds 47 victory / 99 plunder / 123 doom — the assessor's rank-1 target this cycle). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T16-27-01-825Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent reached **ALL THREE declared endings** (ending_victory 35/35, ending_plunder non-death, ending_doom death), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — flawless gating (no down-exit until the rope is tied; the oak chest exposes no unlock action without the brass key), dynamic room text in sync after every state change, independent score sources (5+10+20=35), the three-way iron-key moral fork all reachable and each telegraphed by its examine text before commit. Its only knocks were the standing-known "short, linear, redundantly-hinted middle act" (the linear-puzzle 4/5 ceiling, not a defect) and the headstone+sexton delivering the same two hints (intentional safety-net redundancy). **No actionable content defect — sealed_crypt re-confirmed blind-clean.**
- **Why engine/authoring (justified OFF-LIST, the playtest-informed higher-value pick):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every self-extinguishing lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the genuine highest-value move is the on-plan lever the last TWO cycles' next-focus both named #2: "extend authoring beyond CYOA to parser+RPG — `agents/authoring` has zero parser/rpg refs, putting the richest validators behind a real authoring loop." **Rejected a third consecutive benchmark-scorecard column** (deaths/illegal-rate/turns axes) as the lower-value, churn-adjacent option — illegal-action rate is ~0 for a bot that only picks legal actions, and CYOA endings carry no `death` flag (death is parser/RPG-only), so a deaths axis would be mode-asymmetric noise. The authoring-mode gap is the bigger structural lever and directly advances the true goal (a real-model AUTHORING benchmark).
- **The gap:** the authoring pipeline only ever produced CYOA packs — the adapter hard-coded `validateCyoa` + `AdapterOutputSchema` (CyoaPackSchema) and the mock author emitted only a CYOA pack. So the Zork-style parser validator — the project's richest — was never exercised by the author→validate→revise loop, underselling the true goal whose whole point is benchmarking a model AUTHORING validated games.
- **The fix (engine/agent, trust-but-verify — deterministic, no LLM-judge, NO API key; runs under the default MockAuthorProvider):** (1) generalized the adapter's revise loop into a mode-agnostic `runAdaptLoop(provider, opts, cfg)` parametrized by `{system, schemaName, schema, validate}`; `runAdapter` (CYOA) keeps its exact public signature + behaviour, and a new `runParserAdapter` routes a parser pack through `validateParser`. (2) Added `ParserAdapterOutputSchema` (ParserPackSchema + the same per-beat classification) to `agents/authoring/schemas.ts`. (3) Extended `MockAuthorProvider` with a parser pack — the same Lighthouse story adapted to rooms+exits+a `visited`-gated win (the canonical parser win shape) — whose FIRST attempt ships a dangling exit target (the exact parser analogue of the CYOA mock's dangling choice ref), corrected once the validator's errors are fed back. (4) `bin/author` gained `--mode cyoa|parser` (default cyoa, byte-identical to before). Confirmed live: both modes reach a GREEN pack in 2 rounds via the CLI.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** NO engine/runtime/validator/schema CHANGE — `validateParser` + `ParserPackSchema` are reused as-is. No content pack edited. **NO hash pin moved.** The `runAdapter` refactor is behaviour-preserving (the existing CYOA authoring tests still pass: converges green in ≥2 rounds, classifies beats, plays to ending_saved). A Zod input/output variance error in the generic config was fixed by mirroring `CompletionRequest`'s `ZodType<T, ZodTypeDef, unknown>` (open input for `.default()` schemas) — typecheck clean.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0139_parser_authoring_loop.yaml` + new `tests/regression/parser_authoring_loop.test.ts` (5 cases: the parser validator REJECTS the first attempt with `EXIT_TARGET_MISSING` at maxRounds:1 — the validator decides correctness, not the model; the loop converges GREEN in a corrective round; the pack independently re-validates green AND is a genuine parser shape; beats are classified; it is actually playable to ending_saved through the parser engine via go_north→go_up; the shared-loop refactor leaves the CYOA path green). `tests/unit/authoring.test.ts` extended with a parser-adapter parity block (converges green + re-validates; classifies the same mode-agnostic beats).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **171 files / 941 tests pass** (+1 file / +7 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** authoring is now mode-complete for parser; the symmetric follow-on is **RPG authoring** — the same `runAdaptLoop` with an `RpgAdapterOutputSchema` + a canned RPG pack routed through `validateRpg` (the Stage-4 skill-check validator), which would close "authoring covers all three modes." Other KEY-FREE on-plan levers: the per-step-divergence localization (bug_0137 next-focus #3) if not already complete; more benchmark axes ONLY if a genuinely bot-measurable one is found (turns-to-end is the strongest candidate — it pairs with hide_graph as a second spatial signal — but deaths/illegal-rate are weak for the deterministic bot). The API-keyed keystone (a REAL frontier model driving runWriter/runParserAdapter) stays deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md — this lands the key-free half, ready for a real provider to slot in behind the env var. Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] sealed_crypt is now freshly attended (seed 47); among the CYOA packs **clockwork_heist remains the least-recently blind-played** (bug_0135, seed 5) — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn; prefer on-plan structural work.

### Cycle result — engine/benchmark (bug_0138): the benchmark scorecard gains a `hide_graph` column — the spatial-reasoning difficulty bug_0137 added is now MEASURABLE, not just available. The coverage bot is scored both with the room graph shown and HIDDEN; the shown→hidden scene-coverage drop on parser/RPG packs is the deterministic baseline a spatial model is scored against. The second on-plan ULTRAPLAN §Week.4 lever, the exact deliverable bug_0137's own next-focus named (#1), NO API key, NOT content churn.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on wreckers_light (CYOA, seed 53 — the assessor's correctly-rotated rank-1 target AND the least-recently blind-played CYOA pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]; bug_0137's next-focus named wreckers as least-recent at bug_0129 seed 41). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T16-14-57-595Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all four endings (ending_saved, ending_wrecker, ending_mercy, ending_drowned)**, rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs** — the 5/5 high-water mark re-confirmed. Its two §4 friction notes are both judged intentional design (the first-visit gallery's only forward action is the telegraphed fatal climb until you return equipped; the great-lamp ending never *requires* reading the journal — the wreckers analog of tithe_barn's "beelining player skips the book," good design). **No actionable content defect — wreckers_light re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5.**
- **Why engine/benchmark (justified OFF-LIST, the playtest-informed higher-value pick):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every self-extinguishing lever disarmed per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the genuine highest-value move is bug_0137's OWN named next-focus #1: "populate the benchmark scorecard with a hidden-graph row so the difficulty is measurable, not just available." A difficulty nothing measures can't be compared — a spatial model would have no baseline to be scored against. **Rejected content churn** (a 10th pack after shipping the 8th+9th last two cycles) per the memory's diminishing-returns warning.
- **The gap (ULTRAPLAN §Week.4):** bug_0137 added the opt-in `hide_graph` observation difficulty (parser/RPG exits render `{ direction }` only, dropping `to`, so the map must be reasoned out) — but INVISIBLE: no tool/report exercised it, so the scorecard (the project's one comparable per-(pack,agent) number) had no hidden-graph column.
- **The fix (engine/benchmark, trust-but-verify — deterministic, no LLM-judge):** (1) threaded the existing `hide_graph` flag through `run_playtest → summarizePlaytest → summarizeParserLike`'s `buildObsFor` call, so the deterministic coverage bot can run with the graph hidden — `coverageActionIndex`'s prefer-unvisited branch then falls through to blind navigation (investigative, else first), the deterministic FLOOR for spatial reasoning. (2) Added a third default scorecard cell `{coverage, hide_graph:true}`; replaced `Scorecard.strategies: string[]` with `cells: BenchmarkCell[]`, added `hide_graph` to each `BenchmarkRow` + a `Graph` (shown/hidden) markdown column. Random is graph-agnostic (never reads exit destinations), so it is scored only graph-shown — a hidden-graph random row is redundant noise. **Result (50 runs/cell):** every parser/RPG pack drops sharply when the graph is hidden (alchemists 75%→12.5%, sealed_crypt 30%→10%, cold_forge 50%→16.7%, sunken_barrow 50%→16.7%) while every CYOA pack's hidden row EQUALS its shown row — the honest "no spatial dimension" for a mode with no room graph.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** the observation is not part of the state hash, so determinism/replay/every trace's expected_final_hash are unchanged and the scorecard stays byte-identical run-to-run (the determinism test still passes). No content/pack edited, no validator/schema change, **NO hash pin moved**. The `strategies → cells` reshape + new row field are surfaced in the scorecard unit test (deliberately STRENGTHENED, not weakened). The committed `traces/benchmark/scorecard.{md,json}` was regenerated to show the new column.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0138_benchmark_hide_graph_axis.yaml` + new `tests/regression/benchmark_hide_graph_axis.test.ts` (4 cases: hide_graph strictly degrades the coverage bot's scene coverage on a parser AND an rpg pack with the SAME declared endings — observation-only; it is a NO-OP for CYOA — identical coverage + ending distribution; random play is graph-agnostic — hide_graph leaves its visited scenes + endings unchanged). `tests/unit/benchmark.test.ts` strengthened (default cells include a hidden-graph coverage cell; CYOA hidden==shown across all metrics while spatial coverage never IMPROVES and drops somewhere; markdown carries a Graph column).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **170 files / 934 tests pass** (+1 file / +5 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** TWO ULTRAPLAN §Week.4 levers now landed (hide_graph observation in bug_0137, its scorecard column here). Remaining KEY-FREE, on-plan benchmark levers, all deterministic engine, no churn: (1) **extend authoring beyond CYOA to parser+RPG** — `agents/authoring` has zero parser/rpg refs, putting the richest validators behind a real authoring loop (bug_0137 next-focus #2); (2) the per-step-divergence localization if not already complete (#3); (3) emit MORE benchmark axes (deaths, illegal-action rate, turns-to-win) the assessor candidate text already names. The levers that genuinely NEED an API key (the keystone real-model author→play→fix→lock run) stay deferred per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md. Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] wreckers_light is now freshly attended (seed 53); among the CYOA packs **clockwork_heist is now the least-recently blind-played** (bug_0135, seed 5 — watchtower bug_0134 seed 88, white_stag bug_0136 seed 23, tithe_barn bug_0137 seed 5) — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn; prefer on-plan structural/benchmark work.

### Cycle result — engine/benchmark (bug_0137): the agent-facing observation gains an opt-in `hide_graph` difficulty — parser/RPG exits stop leaking their destination (`exit.to`), so the spatial map must be REASONED OUT, not read off. The first on-plan ULTRAPLAN §Week.4 lever; advances the true goal (a real spatial-reasoning benchmark) with NO API key, NOT content churn.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on tithe_barn (CYOA, seed 5 — the assessor's correctly-rotated rank-1 target AND the newest pack joining the rotation per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]; bug_0136's next-focus named the newest pack to attend). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T16-00-34-202Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all five endings (ending_doors, ending_fall, ending_bounty, ending_mercy + the ignorant/knowing variants)**, rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs** — the wreckers_light high-water mark, confirming the four-NON-converging-stances structure (not just the knows_truth reframe) is what lifts a branching pack from 4/5 to 5/5. Its one §4 note (a beelining player who skips the book never sees the gated `open_doors` ending) the tester judged **good design**, not a flaw — and the granary_floor hub offers "Read the steward's book" as a top-level choice, so it is highly discoverable. **No actionable content defect — tithe_barn re-confirmed blind-clean at 5/5.**
- **Why engine/benchmark (justified OFF-LIST, the playtest-informed higher-value pick per the prompt + [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]):** with the mandated playtest yielding no content defect and the deterministic assessor correctly collapsed to the 0.5 floor (every self-extinguishing lever disarmed — frontier benchmark scorecard SHIPPED, woken-Barrow-Lord fork SHIPPED in bug_0083, eslint/doc-staleness all green), the genuine highest-value move is the next on-plan benchmark lever that needs no API key. **Weighed and REJECTED two tempting-but-worse options:** (1) a 10th pack right after shipping the 8th+9th (bug_0135/0136) is content churn with diminishing returns the memory warns against; (2) re-architecting white_stag's converging mercy into a distinct fourth stance — I READ white_stag and judged its stag-keeper premise is fundamentally binary (kill→doom / spare→thaw) with NO natural analog to tithe_barn's private-mercy-vs-public-justice split, so a forced fourth stance would risk damaging a blind-clean 4/5 pack (the prior author's own "weigh against re-touching" caution). **STALE-MEMORY CORRECTION:** the "dormant-enemy / woken Barrow-Lord" item named as a standing structural lever in [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] already SHIPPED as bug_0083 — that lever is done.
- **The leak (ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02 §Week.4):** the structured observation handed the agent the full room adjacency — every parser/RPG exit rendered as `{ direction, to }`, where `to` is the destination room id ("north → guard_crypt"). A model reading one observation gets the local map for free and never reasons about where a move leads, trivializing the spatial task TALES/Jericho measure. (CYOA already hides its branch graph — the observation surfaces only choice text/id, never `choice.next`.)
- **The fix (engine, trust-but-verify — deterministic, no LLM-judge):** an opt-in `hide_graph` difficulty, strictly ADDITIVE and DEFAULT-OFF. New `ObservationOptions { hideGraph? }` (src/parser/observation.ts) threads from MCP `new_game`/`start_game(hide_graph)` → `Session.hideGraph` → `buildObsFor` → `buildParserObservation`/`buildRpgObservation`. When set, each exit renders as `{ direction }` only — the agent still sees it CAN go a direction but not where until it moves. The legal MOVE action + the engine's destination resolution are untouched, so the game stays fully playable. `exits[].to` is now optional to express the hidden shape; CYOA accepts the option as a documented no-op. **The internal coverage bot never sets hide_graph, so it keeps the destinations it uses to plan** (`coverageActionIndex` already guarded `to !== undefined`) — exactly the plan's "kept for the internal coverage bot".
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** observation is NOT part of the state hash, so determinism/replay/every trace's expected_final_hash are unchanged (the new test PINS that the state_hash is identical with and without the flag). No content/pack edited, no validator/schema change, **NO hash pin moved**. The widened `exits[].to?` typechecks clean across all consumers (the validator reads the PACK's exits, not the observation's; the bot's guard already handles undefined).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0137_observation_hide_graph.yaml` + new `tests/regression/observation_hide_graph.test.ts` (10 cases across parser sealed_crypt + rpg sunken_barrow + cyoa watchtower: DEFAULT exits carry a string `to`; HIDDEN drops every `to` while keeping the SAME directions; hide_graph is observation-only — state_hash identical; still playable — a MOVE under hide_graph relocates the player via the engine's hidden resolution and the flag persists across later observations; CYOA no-op — observation + state_hash unchanged and still playable; the start_game alias honors the flag and keeps the full graph without it).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **169 files / 929 tests pass** (+1 file / +10 cases, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** `hide_graph` is the first ULTRAPLAN §Week lever landed; the remaining KEY-FREE, on-plan benchmark levers (all deterministic engine, no churn): (1) **populate the benchmark scorecard with a hidden-graph row** — `bin/benchmark.ts` could now emit a `hide_graph` column so the difficulty is measurable, not just available; (2) extend authoring beyond CYOA to parser+RPG (`agents/authoring` has zero parser/rpg refs — puts the richest validators behind a real authoring loop); (3) the per-step-divergence localization if not already complete. The levers that genuinely NEED an API key (the keystone keyed real-model author→play→fix→lock run; wiring a real provider into the loop's content_new path) stay deferred — the loop is intentionally stopped for them per docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md. Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] tithe_barn is now freshly attended (seed 5); among the CYOA packs **wreckers_light remains the least-recently blind-played** (bug_0129, seed 41) — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose and resist content churn; prefer on-plan structural/benchmark work.

### Cycle result — content_new/breadth (bug_0136): The Tithe-Barn — the project's 9th pack and THIRD purpose-built BRANCHING CYOA, authored to close the white_stag 4/5 → 5/5 gap by building for FOUR GENUINELY-DISTINCT moral stances (the wreckers_light edge). A famine night, a lord's full grain-barn, a thief caught at it: the OBVIOUS sanctioned goal is the dark one (you are the night watch the lord pays, the law hangs thieves, the bounty is silver) and the depth is the player who reads the steward's own account-book and learns the barn is held full to rot to keep the price up — then throws the doors wide.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on white_stag (CYOA, seed 23 — the assessor's correctly-rotated rank-1 target AND the newest pack joining the rotation per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]; bug_0135's next-focus named white_stag/clockwork as the 4/5 packs to attend). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T15-44-05-546Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all four endings (ending_thaw, ending_lost, ending_quarry, ending_offering)**, rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — flawless mechanics, deterministic, the gated offering + reactive bluff confirmed. **Its one "seam" finding (ending_quarry's "a stone they should have read" on an uninformed kill) the tester itself judged "defensible":** the line is the village elders' hindsight, NOT a contradiction of the player's own act — it is the intended dramatic irony of the *ignorant* kill (you don't know what you killed; the old folk piece it together after). Unlike bug_0120/0127/0134 (genuine stale-on-re-entry text that contradicted a completed action), nothing here contradicts the player, so "fixing" it would be re-polishing blind-clean prose — resisted per [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]. **No actionable content defect — white_stag re-confirmed blind-clean at 4/5.**
- **Why content_new/breadth (justified on-list per the prompt + [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]], applying the playtest's OWN structural finding):** the deterministic assessor is collapsed to six identical 0.5 blind-playtest stubs with every structural lever disarmed; content is blind-exhausted. The single highest PRODUCT-value lever named by ~12 cycles is **breadth via branching** (wreckers_light, the only 5/5; linear/converging packs top out at 4/5). bug_0135's next-focus named the EXACT 4/5→5/5 gap: "wreckers's edge is its FOUR genuinely-distinct moral stances + the cross-branch knows_truth reframe; white_stag has the reframe but its mercy outcomes converge — a distinct fourth moral stance, not death, is the lever." This cycle ships a pack built precisely for that.
- **The pack (`content/cyoa/pack/tithe_barn.yaml`, id tithe_barn_v1) — a MORAL FORK with FOUR NON-CONVERGING stances:** four scenes (watch_post → granary_floor hub → ledger / reckoning) and FOUR distinct endings. The hidden "truth" (the steward's open account-book: the barn held full to rot on the lord's sealed order to keep the price climbing — "the town is not starving because the harvest failed; it is starving because starving men pay more") is the wreckers_light/white_stag device: reading it sets `knows_truth`, which (a) fires reactive endingText variants on the bounty and the mercy, and (b) UNLOCKS throwing the doors. The four stances, built NOT to converge: **ending_bounty** (seize the thief for the lord's silver; reactive ignorant/knowing variants), **ending_mercy** (let her slip out with one sack — private mercy, the barn stays locked on the rest; reactive variant), **ending_doors** (GATED on knows_truth — throw the barn open to the whole town: public justice, the hoarding broken, you outlawed — DISTINCT from private mercy, the lever's whole point), **ending_lost→ending_fall** (greed-death: climb the rotten loft after the older stores — telegraphed in the base hub text "rungs grey and soft with rot" and named in the choice label, never an ambush, per the white_stag/bug_0123/0130 discipline).
- **New-pack blind pass (quality check before locking, seed 31). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T15-44-05-546Z/playtest_tithe_barn.md`.** A second fresh source-blind subagent reached **all FOUR declared endings**, rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs** — "genuinely excellent short literary CYOA... every ending morally distinct and well-earned." Confirmed the state-aware granary_floor + reckoning reframes (knows_truth adds the reframe prose AND unlocks open_doors), the gated doors ending, the telegraphed loft-death, clean flag/journal persistence, and that **private mercy and public liberation do NOT converge** — the white_stag 4/5 gap this pack was built to close. **5/5 enjoyment = the wreckers_light high-water mark, hit by a second branching device.**
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** additive content only — no engine/validator/schema change, and a NEW pack pins no test hash (only watchtower_road pins its content_hash in test files), so **NO hash pin moved**. Validator 0/0 (content_hash `bcd6e2a6…`). The bug_0121 exhaustive CYOA solver **auto-discovers** the pack and reaches all four declared endings with none undeclared and no state-cap-out. **Wired into the bar** per [[verification-bar-covers-all-packs]]: added a `validate -- content/cyoa/pack/tithe_barn.yaml` step to the `health` npm script, and extended the `all_packs_validated_by_bar` discovery list (eight → **nine**). `list_stories` + the UI pack catalog auto-discover it (no code change). README "Content library" updated (8 → 9 packs).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0136_tithe_barn_branching_pack.yaml` + new `tests/regression/tithe_barn_branching.test.ts` (9 cases: all four endings reachable + distinct; the moral fork is real (same approach → bounty or mercy by choice alone); `open_doors` is ABSENT at the reckoning until the book is read and PRESENT after — knows_truth is mechanically load-bearing; the doors ending is reachable only via the truth; **the public liberation does NOT converge with private mercy — distinct endings + distinct outcomes, the lever's point**; knows_truth fires reactive variants on the bounty AND mercy endings while uninformed renders base; the variants are cosmetic — informed/uninformed bounty & mercy converge on the same endingId; the truth reframes the reckoning scene in the moment, not only the epilogue).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **168 files / 919 tests pass** (+1 file / +11 cases — 9 new branching cases + the solver/discovery tests auto-covering the 9th pack, 0 regressions), **all 9 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the project now has THREE purpose-built branching CYOA packs (wreckers_light 5/5, tithe_barn 5/5, white_stag 4/5) — and tithe_barn proves the four-distinct-stances structure (not just the reframe) is what lifts a branching pack from 4/5 to 5/5, replicable beyond wreckers. The remaining breadth levers: (1) another branching pack OR re-architecting white_stag's converging mercy outcomes into a genuinely distinct fourth stance (the same lever applied to the one 4/5 branching pack — though it's blind-clean, so weigh against re-touching); (2) the standing structural items if rotating off content: the dormant-enemy engine feature (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in mandatory-prep boss); (3) the true-goal pivot — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] white_stag is now freshly attended (seed 23); among the CYOA packs **wreckers_light is now the least-recently blind-played** (bug_0129, seed 41 — clockwork was bug_0135 seed 5, watchtower bug_0134 seed 88) — rotate the next mandated pass there, and tithe_barn joins the rotation as the newest pack. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose.

### Cycle result — content_new/breadth (bug_0135): The White Stag — the project's 8th pack and second purpose-built BRANCHING CYOA, authored to the wreckers_light quality bar — a starving hunter, the white stag run to ground on a frozen tarn, where the OBVIOUS sanctioned goal is the dark one (kill it for meat + the lord's gold) and the depth is the player who reads the carved stone, learns the stag is the winter's keeper, and stays the hand
- **Mandated blind pass ran on clockwork_heist (CYOA, seed 5 — the assessor's rank-1 target AND the correctly-rotated least-recently blind-played pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], last attended at bug_0128 seed 3). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T15-26-15-398Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **ending_truth (both letter variants), ending_caught, ending_rich**, rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — flawless mechanics, the two-letter moral twist well-foreshadowed, clean gating, deterministic. **Its only §4/§5 finding is the DOCUMENTED "cosmetic clock" false positive** ([[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]]): the deadline/patrol teeth ARE real and test-locked (`meta.deadline ticks>=10 → ending_overstayed`; `cross_to_vault_blind → ending_patrol` at ticks>=4 without the ledger; bug_0121 solver reaches all 5 endings), but efficient blind play reaches the vault at ticks≤3 and never lingers to witness them — which is the CORRECT behaviour of a failure pole (you don't want good players hitting it). Ten prior prose cycles already addressed the clock; per memory it must NOT be re-fixed. **No actionable content defect — pack re-confirmed blind-clean.**
- **Why content_new/breadth (justified on-list per the prompt + [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]):** the deterministic assessor is collapsed to six identical 0.5 blind-playtest stubs with every structural lever disarmed; content polish is blind-exhausted across all packs. The single highest PRODUCT-value lever named by ~10 consecutive cycles is **breadth via branching** — the one device proven to lift enjoyment to 5/5 (wreckers_light, bug_0099), where the linear single-chain packs top out at 4/5. This cycle ships exactly that: a NEW branching CYOA pack.
- **The pack (`content/cyoa/pack/white_stag.yaml`, id white_stag_v1) — a MORAL FORK, not a lock-and-key chain (the wreckers_light thesis):** four scenes (trail_end → tarn hub → shrine / bluff) and FOUR distinct endings. The goal is legible and DARK from the first beat (a starving village + the lord's bounty make killing the stag the easy, sanctioned choice). A hidden "truth" reframes rather than adds a route (the manor-letter / keeper-journal device): a carved boundary-stone names the white stag as the winter's keeper, whose killing brings an unbreaking winter that empties the valley — reading it sets `knows_truth`, which (a) fires reactive endingText variants on the kill (ignorant tragedy vs a knowing, desperate damnation) and the sparing (pity vs understanding), and (b) **UNLOCKS the offering — the gated "best" ending** (lay the bow + the one arrow down as a rite, the mirror of the kill), a payoff reserved for the player who stopped to read the stone (cf. watchtower's proof-gated finale). The dramatic irony the truth reveals: the desperate obvious kill is what DOOMS the valley while the harder mercy buys the thaw — so the ignorant kill is a tragedy of not knowing, and the worst kill is the knowing one. The four endings: **ending_quarry** (kill; reactive ignorant/knowing variants), **ending_thaw** (mercy on instinct; reactive variant), **ending_offering** (GATED on knows_truth; the fullest ending), **ending_lost** (the rotten-ice death — telegraphed in the base tarn text, "rotten, ticking… fit to drop a man through," and named in the choice label, never an ambush, per the bug_0123/0130/wreckers discipline).
- **New-pack blind pass (quality check before locking, seed 9). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T15-26-15-398Z/playtest_white_stag.md`.** A second fresh source-blind subagent reached **all FOUR declared endings**, rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — "clean, finished, and emotionally resonant." Confirmed the state-aware bluff (knows_truth adds the reframe prose AND unlocks lay_offering), the gated offering, the telegraphed ice-death, and clean flag/journal persistence. Its one thematic note (spare survives regardless of knowledge) is INTENTIONAL: the stag lives either way; reading the stone buys the offering ending + richer prose, not a different survival outcome. 4/5 matches clockwork; wreckers (the only 5/5) remains the breadth high-water mark.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** additive content only — no engine/validator/schema change, and a NEW pack pins no test hash (only watchtower_road pins its content_hash in test files), so **NO hash pin moved**. Validator 0/0. The bug_0121 exhaustive CYOA solver **auto-discovers** the pack and reaches all four declared endings with none undeclared and no state-cap-out. **Wired into the bar** per [[verification-bar-covers-all-packs]]: added a `validate -- content/cyoa/pack/white_stag.yaml` step to the `health` npm script, and extended the `all_packs_validated_by_bar` discovery list (seven → **eight**). `list_stories` + the UI pack catalog auto-discover it (no code change). README "Content library" updated (7 → 8 packs).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0135_white_stag_branching_pack.yaml` + new `tests/regression/white_stag_branching.test.ts` (8 cases: all four endings reachable + distinct; the moral fork is real (same approach → kill or mercy by choice alone); `lay_offering` is ABSENT at the bluff until the stone is read and PRESENT after — knows_truth is mechanically load-bearing, not just flavor; the offering is reachable only via the truth; knows_truth fires reactive variants on the quarry AND thaw endings while the uninformed route renders base; the variants are cosmetic — informed/uninformed kills & sparings converge on the same endingId; the truth reframes the bluff scene in the moment, not only the epilogue).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **167 files / 908 tests pass** (+1 file / +10 cases — 8 new branching cases + the solver/discovery tests auto-covering the 8th pack, 0 regressions), **all 8 packs validate 0/0**, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the project now has TWO purpose-built branching CYOA packs (wreckers_light 5/5, white_stag 4/5) — breadth via branching remains the only proven enjoyment lever above the linear-puzzle 4/5 ceiling, so the next breadth move is either another branching pack OR pushing white_stag/clockwork from 4/5 toward 5/5 (wreckers's edge is its FOUR genuinely-distinct moral stances + the cross-branch knows_truth reframe on three endings; white_stag has the reframe but its mercy outcomes converge — a distinct fourth moral stance, not death, is the lever). Standing structural items if rotating off content: (1) the dormant-enemy engine feature (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in mandatory-prep boss); (2) the true-goal pivot — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] clockwork_heist is now freshly attended (seed 5); **wreckers_light is now the least-recently blind-played CYOA pack** — rotate the next mandated pass there (and white_stag joins the rotation as the newest pack). Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose.

### Cycle result — content/honesty (bug_0134): The Watchtower Road's hermit no longer re-greets a returning player as a STRANGER — hermit_talk gains a reactive re-entry variant so a player who already heard the smuggler's tale (or broke the letter's seal) sees a hermit who knows them, instead of the cold first-meeting line "You look lost, traveler" on every return
- **Mandated blind pass ran on watchtower_road (CYOA, seed 88 — the assessor's correctly-rotated rank-1 target; watchtower_road was the least-recently blind-attended pack per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], last at bug_0127). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T15-08-35-167Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all three endings** (ending_truth/win, ending_escape, ending_captured) plus the proof-gated decision_point variant, rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — flawless mechanics, cross-branch payoff (the cart letter → the hermit on the OTHER branch unlocks the checkpoint pass + the confrontation), proof-gated finale, deterministic replay. **Its one §4 friction note:** "the exhausted Hermit conversation is re-enterable and just re-shows the greeting with only 'say goodbye' — a harmless micro-loop." Reproduced live via MCP (go_west → follow_to_camp → talk_hermit → ask_about_tower → back_from_tower_talk re-shows the stranger greeting).
- **Why this finding (a genuine stale-on-re-entry defect, the pack's own discipline — not new prose polish):** project memory says content is blind-CLEAN and to resist re-polishing prose ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]). This is neither balance nor polish — it is the pack's recurring **reactive-text honesty class**: scene text must not contradict the player's own completed actions. bug_0120 cured tower_top re-showing "a cold brazier waits, begging for a flame" after the beacon was lit; confront_smuggler carries not_flag learned_truth variants for the same reason. Here the hermit, seconds after telling the whole smuggler's tale, greeted the player as a stranger ("You look lost, traveler. Or maybe you found something you weren't meant to") on every re-entry. Same gap, same fix shape.
- **The fix (content only — `scene hermit_talk`):** added a reactive scene `variant` gated on `any_of [has_flag heard_hermit_lore, has_flag seal_broken]` (first-match-wins, the bug_0120 machinery): a player who has already spoken now reads "The hermit looks up from his net again. 'Still here,' he says, not unkindly. 'I've told you what I know, and the road north is where the rest of it waits. Ask what's left to ask, or go and see for yourself.'" — a hermit who knows them, re-pointed at the goal (north). The un-spoken player (neither flag set on first entry) still sees the original greeting **verbatim**. Reactive TEXT ONLY — no choice/flag/route/ending change; consumed topics retire via their OWN conditions, not the variant. The re-entry **state_hash is unchanged** by the fix (variants alter displayed text, not state — confirmed live: f43e9d79… before and after).
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** content-only; no engine/validator/schema change. Validator stays 0/0; the bug_0122 exhaustive CYOA solver + every existing watchtower route/reactive test (beacon_relight, confront_reactive, letter_signposting, blind_fixes, …) pass unchanged. **Deliberate, surfaced re-pin:** watchtower_road's compiled content_hash is pinned in TWO HASH_PIN_FILES (`tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts` + `traces/bugs/bug_0002_watchtower_blind_polish.yaml`); both moved `f989028090…` → `8990abbad9…` alongside this real content change (verify:integrity static OK, 0/0).
- **Also fixed a STALE TEST surfaced by the rotation (not a weakening — a strengthening):** `tests/regression/assessor_blind_pass_rotation.test.ts`'s real-repo case hardcoded `expect(topStem).not.toBe("watchtower_road")` from the bug_0128 era (when watchtower was the MOST-recently attended). The rotation has since advanced — watchtower is now correctly the LEAST-recent and so legitimately nominated this cycle (offsets: watchtower 34647 = oldest topmost mention … alchemists_tower 388 = newest), which tripped the stale hardcode. Replaced the literal with the dynamically-computed most-recently-attended stem, so the test now checks the true invariant ("nominee ≠ most-recent pack") and is self-updating as the rotation advances. This failure was independent of the content edit (the test reads AI_LOOP_STATE.md + assess(), not the pack).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0134_watchtower_hermit_reentry_text.yaml` + new `tests/regression/watchtower_hermit_reentry_text.test.ts` (4 cases: first entry shows the base stranger-greeting and not the variant; after hearing the lore + returning the variant shows and the stranger line is GONE while choices are untouched; the seal_broken arm fires the same variant with no lore; the variant is cosmetic — the route still reaches ending_truth).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **166 files / 898 tests pass** (+1 file / +4 cases, 0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** watchtower_road is now blind-clean AND its hermit conversation is reactive on re-entry (the last stale-greeting in the pack). Content stays blind-exhausted; the deterministic assessor is at the 0.5 floor with all structural levers disarmed ([[verifier-assertion-guard]]). The remaining genuine levers are all BREADTH/product: (1) author a NEW branching pack (the bug_0099/wreckers_light 5/5 lever — the largest unclaimed product win); (2) the dormant-enemy engine feature (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in mandatory-prep boss); (3) the true-goal pivot — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] watchtower_road is now freshly attended (seed 88); **clockwork_heist / wreckers_light are now the least-recently blind-played** — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose.

### Cycle result — verifier/integrity (bug_0133): the trust-but-verify guard now catches the count-preserving STRICT→LOOSE matcher swap — the last laundering hole bug_0129 EXPLICITLY left open and declared unreachable "without a semantic judge." It needs no judge: counting value-PINNING matchers as a third artifact count makes the mechanical swap a hard regression
- **Mandated blind pass ran on alchemists_tower (parser, seed 73 — the assessor's recommended rank-1 target; per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] sunken_barrow + cold_forge were freshest, alchemists_tower among the least-recently attended). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T14-47-19-549Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent reached ALL THREE endings (ending_cured/win 40/40, ending_poisoned/death, ending_betrayal/non-death greed fork), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO real bugs** — reactive prose all in sync, clean clue chain, three thematically coherent endings. **Its one §5 "bug" was reproduced via MCP and confirmed a NON-bug:** after taking the herb, the action list still offered "look at pale herb" — but that is the *carried* herb's examine from the held-objects loop (legal_actions.ts:295), paired with `drop_herb`; `visible_objects` correctly drops it. The tester misread a legitimate held-item action as a stale room action. Honestly triaged, NOT "fixed" — and the cleanest cosmetic "fix" (suffixing the command label) would have broken the bin/parser_play `--commands` typed-input contract, a worse regression. §4's shared-iron-key friction is the already-telegraphed bug_0123 fork, judged "fair." So content stays blind-CLEAN ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]).
- **Why the verifier (justified off-list, the playtest-informed higher-value pick):** with the mandated playtest yielding no actionable content defect and the deterministic assessor collapsed to six identical 0.5 blind-playtest stubs ([[verifier-assertion-guard]]), the highest-leverage move is again to harden the project's CORE trust mechanism — every future autonomous cycle's credibility rests on the verifier being un-gameable. This is the **direct continuation of bug_0129**, which closed the gut-the-body launder (keep the `it()` shell, delete the `expect()`s) but its own header + artifact then CONFESSED a deeper one it could not catch: a COUNT-PRESERVING swap of `expect(x).toBe(value)` → `expect(x).toBeDefined()` — every shell AND every `expect()` intact, so both bug_0129 guards stay silent, the green suite stays green (a `toBeDefined()` on a defined value passes), and the test now pins nothing. bug_0129 declared this needed "a semantic judge, which would forfeit pure-determinism."
- **The insight (it is MECHANICAL, not semantic):** the strict→loose swap is a change in the MATCHER, catchable by a third deterministic count parallel to the existing two. `STRONG_ASSERTION_RE` + `countStrongAssertions()` count the value-PINNING matchers (toBe/toEqual/toStrictEqual/toContain[Equal]/toMatch[Object]/toThrow/toHaveLength/toHaveProperty/toHaveBeenCalledWith/the ordering comparators/toBeCloseTo/toBeInstanceOf); the weak existence matchers (toBeDefined/toBeTruthy/toBeUndefined/toBeNull/toBeFalsy) — the swap TARGETS — are deliberately EXCLUDED. The `\s*\(` anchor stops the `toBe` alternative from also matching the `toBe`-prefixed weak matchers; negated specific matchers (`.not.toContain(`, `.not.toBe(`) still count (they pin a value). Swapping strict→loose holds the `expect()` count but DROPS the strong count.
- **The fix (engine/verifier, trust-but-verify — fully deterministic, no LLM-judge):** three counts now move together for any honest +tests cycle and pull apart only under a launder, each pinning it one level deeper: shells (`cases`), assertions (`expect()`), and value-pins (`strong`). Added `MIN_STRONG_ASSERTIONS` static floor (`STRONG_ASSERTION_FLOOR`, mass tripwire ~2890→400) + a `STRONG_ASSERTION_REGRESSION` branch in `detectCountRegressions` (hard error when strong drops while expect() holds). `strong` is OPTIONAL on `TestArtifactCounts` so legacy `{cases, assertions}` call sites (and bug_0129's tests) stay valid; the strong guard fires only when both sides supply it (runDrift always does). `countTestArtifactsAtRef` returns `{cases, assertions, strong}` in the same single git pass (no extra git cost). Header documents the new honest limit: a swap that keeps a STRONG matcher but makes it vacuous (`expect(true).toBe(true)`) still needs a semantic judge — the *mechanical* matcher swap no longer does.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** the guard itself is engine; no content/pack/validator/schema touched, **no hash pin moved**. Drift vs HEAD correctly surfaces the expected `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` warning (this cycle edited the protected guard) with 0 regression errors — the strong count went UP this cycle. The static + the three count-regression branches all stay green.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0133_verifier_strict_to_loose_swap.yaml` + new `tests/regression/verifier_strict_to_loose_swap.test.ts` (7 cases: strong count is independent of the expect() count under a strict→loose swap; negated specifics count, weak existence checks don't; `toBe`-prefixed weak matchers aren't mistaken for strong while the comparators are; the swap is a hard STRONG_ASSERTION_REGRESSION while neither prior guard fires; an honest +tests cycle trips nothing; the real repo is above the floor). `tests/unit/verifier_integrity.test.ts` extended (countStrongAssertions, the STRONG_ASSERTION_REGRESSION + legacy-absent cases, the real-repo STRONG_ASSERTION_FLOOR check).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **165 files / 894 tests pass** (0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** the verifier-integrity guard is now closed on all three MECHANICAL launders (shell-delete, body-gut, strict→loose swap); the only weakening left is genuinely SEMANTIC (a vacuous-but-strong assertion), which needs a judge and would forfeit the script's pure-determinism — do NOT chase it deterministically. With the verifier hardened and content blind-exhausted, the remaining genuine levers are all BREADTH/product: (1) author a NEW branching pack (the bug_0099/wreckers_light 5/5 lever — the largest unclaimed product win); (2) the dormant-enemy engine feature (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in mandatory-prep boss); (3) the true-goal pivot — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]] alchemists_tower is now freshly attended; **clockwork_heist / wreckers_light / watchtower_road are now the least-recently blind-played** — rotate the next mandated pass there. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose.

### Cycle result — content/honesty (bug_0132): the reaver's shade's ward counsel no longer OVER-PROMISES the warded wight fight — it kept bug_0119's anti-retreat message but had hardened into a flat GUARANTEE ("not the truth of the odds"), which bug_0113's intentional ~12% warded loss makes a broken promise; the counsel now admits warded play can still lose while still steering the player to press on
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sunken_barrow (rpg, seed 19 — the assessor's recommended rank-1 target). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T14-34-47-326Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent reached ALL THREE endings (ending_victory 50/50; ending_woken doom 25/50; ending_fallen death 0/50), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical bugs** — flawless reactive room text, legible damage breakdown (bug_0131 landing), well-signposted goal and forks. **Its one substantive finding (§5):** on seed 19 it took the shade's +3 ward and obeyed the counsel to the letter ("set your feet and keep striking, do not break off") and STILL DIED, wight on 4 HP, two max cold-rolls in the last rounds; it named the shade's promise as not strictly true ("obey the hint and you'll *probably* be fine") and called a full-restart on a broken promise the one thing that would leave a real player unsatisfied.
- **Why this finding (a genuine honesty defect, the pack's own discipline — not balance, not prose-polish):** project memory says content is blind-CLEAN and to resist re-polishing prose ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]). This is neither. It is the pack's recurring `text-over-promises-a-contract-the-engine-does-not-cash` class — bug_0069 (slab text over-sold a grind), bug_0095 (sarcophagus examine promised the slab's skill_check), bug_0027/0029 (failure feedback implying a missing item) — here on the shade's spoken counsel. bug_0119 added "that is the wight's way of wearing a man down, **not the truth of the odds**. Set your feet and keep striking." to cure a *prior* blind tester retreating from a winnable fight (the bug_0027 quit-a-winnable-game mode). That cure was right but over-corrected into a categorical guarantee, while bug_0113 **deliberately keeps ~12% of warded seeds a genuine loss** (the "careful play isn't free" identity, verified live over seeds 1-40). seed 19 is one of those ~12% — so the line lies ~1 run in 8. The ~12% loss is INTENDED and stays; the false certainty was the only defect.
- **The fix (content/hint_text only — `npc reaver_shade`, node `shade_wight`):** the counsel KEEPS the load-bearing bug_0119 anti-retreat message (the dread is MOSTLY the wight's wearing-down trick; there is no balm/second blade/charm to find; breaking off only returns you to the same fight no fresher) but now ADMITS the warded fight is not a sure thing — "the better of it is not the sure of it … it can yet go hard against you, even done right … not every time, and I will not swear you safe." Pressing on is honestly framed as the BEST chance, not a guarantee: the retreat cure survives, the broken promise does not. PURE spoken npc_text — no flag/var/journal/score/topic/DC/stat/enemy/ending change; the +3 ward, its one-shot retire, the wight's bug_0102 stats, and the canonical trace's recorded JOURNAL are all untouched.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0132_barrow_ward_honest_odds.yaml` + new `tests/regression/rpg_barrow_ward_honest_odds.test.ts` (4 assertions: (a) concedes the fight can be lost, (b) the bare "not the truth of the odds" guarantee is GONE, (c) the anti-retreat cure survives, (d) ward mechanic + wight stats untouched). bug_0119's own test was tightened to drop its now-dead `|not the truth of the odds` alternative (stricter, not weaker). Deliberate re-pin: `traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json` content_hash `7546c419… → 7791fc1…`; expected_final_hash UNCHANGED (only spoken text changed, not state — trace replays identically). bug_0119's historical `content_hash_after` left at `7546c419…` (the state THAT bug produced). No test SOURCE pins this pack's hash. `npm run health` green (exit 0).
- **Next suggested focus:** the deterministic assessor is collapsed to the 0.5 floor with all structural levers disarmed ([[verifier-assertion-guard]]); the remaining genuine lever is BREADTH ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] — an 8th pack lifting branching/reactive prose) OR continued blind-pass rotation. Per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]], sunken_barrow + cold_forge are now the most-recently blind-attended; rotate the next mandated pass onto the least-recently-played (clockwork_heist / wreckers_light / watchtower_road / sealed_crypt / alchemists_tower).

### Cycle result — engine/legibility (bug_0131): combat damage narration now DECOMPOSES every blow into `d6 <roll> + <atk> atk - <def> def` — mirroring the skill-check resolver's transparent format — so a player can finally SEE their defense (gear like the cold-iron plate / barrow ward) at work, instead of an opaque final number swamped by d6 variance
- **Mandated blind pass ran on cold_forge (rpg, seed 13 — the assessor's correctly-rotated rank-1 target; cold_forge + clockwork_heist were the least-recently blind-attended packs per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T14-22-55-560Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played BOTH endings (ending_victory 50/50; ending_fallen, death 0/50), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — clean mechanics, reactive room text all in sync, well-telegraphed prep-then-fight loop. **Its one concrete, REPEATED friction note (§4 AND §5):** the cold-iron plate's +2 defense "felt invisible" — "with def 4 the sentinel hit me for 9/7; with def 2 it hit me for 8/8/9 — barely different, so the armor's value isn't obvious from the numbers"; the verdict asked outright to "make the plate visibly reduce incoming damage." A blind player couldn't perceive their optional prep mattered.
- **Why this finding (a genuine cross-pack legibility gap, not prose-polish — justified per the prompt's playtest-informed mandate):** project memory says content is blind-CLEAN everywhere and to resist re-polishing prose ([[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]]); this is NOT prose — it is an ENGINE legibility defect surfaced by THIS playtest and independently by the barrow pass (bug_0119 drew the same "is my prep working?" note). The root cause is an asymmetry in `src/rpg/combat.ts`: `resolveSkillCheck` already narrates its full breakdown ("rolled <d20> + <bonus> = <total> vs <DC> — success/failure"), but `resolveAttack` reported only "<foe> hits you for <N>", folding the d6 roll + attack + defense into one opaque number — so the defense term, the ONLY signal that gear matters, was never shown, and a +2 reduction is imperceptible against the d6's range.
- **The improvement (engine, trust-but-verify — deterministic, no LLM-judge):** extracted a `rollDamage(roll, atk, def)` helper returning `{dealt, how}`. `dealt = max(1, roll + atk - def)` is **byte-identical** to the removed `dmg()`; `how` is the legible breakdown "d6 R + A atk - D def". Both combat narrations now read "You strike <foe> for N (d6 R + A atk - D def; it has <HP> left)." and "<foe> hits you for N (d6 R + A atk - D def; you have <HP> left)." A floored blow states it honestly ("… = -6, blunted to the floor of 1"), so the shown arithmetic can never silently fail to equal the damage dealt. The two d6 draws (player strike, then enemy reply) happen in the SAME order from the SAME step-keyed stream → **determinism, replay, and every trace's `expected_final_hash` are unchanged** (narration is not part of the state hash, cf. bug_0119). Now a geared player reads "- 4 def" where the under-armed one reads "- 2 def", and the same roll visibly lands for +2 less — the plate is finally perceptible.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** engine-only narration; ALL numeric combat behaviour identical. No content/pack edited, no validator/schema change, **no hash pin moved** (cold_forge content_hash unchanged at `8590fefd…`). The existing seeded-combat determinism tests, the exhaustive RPG ending-reachability proof (`rpg_all_endings_reachable.test.ts`, which forces best/worst rolls via the rng seam and asserts on ROUTING not narration text), and all 7 packs' validators (0/0) pass unchanged. No trace pins the combat narration string.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0131_combat_damage_legibility.yaml` + `tests/regression/rpg_combat_damage_legible.test.ts` (4 cases: the strike narration decomposes and matches the HP write; **defense is legible — the SAME forced roll lands for exactly +2 less when plated, and the breakdown spells out the "- N def" term**; a floored blow states "blunted to the floor of 1" and removes exactly 1 HP; the numeric damage is unchanged vs `max(1, roll + atk - def)` across rolls 1-6 and defs 0/1/2/4/9, guarding the byte-identical claim).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **163 files / 880 tests pass** (+1 file / +4 cases, 0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** combat is now legible across BOTH rpg packs; the same playtest's secondary note (the fully-prepped win at seed 13 still finished at 4 HP — "thin margin") is a BALANCE observation against the bug_0101 retune that was verified comfortable over seeds 1-40, so do NOT chase it as a bug (4 HP is a win; the death pole is the intended teeth). Content stays blind-exhausted; the deterministic assessor is at the 0.5 floor. Genuine breadth levers left: (1) **author a NEW branching pack** (the bug_0099/wreckers_light lever, the project's only 5/5 — the largest unclaimed product win); (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in mandatory-prep boss); (3) the **true-goal pivot** — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind pass — cold_forge freshly attended (seed 13); **clockwork_heist is now the least-recently blind-played pack.** Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose.

### Cycle result — content_new/breadth (bug_0130): The Sealed Crypt gains its FIRST FAILURE POLE — a third iron-key fork (the lead-sealed plague tomb → ending_doom, a death), the breadth lever project memory has named for ~10 cycles, executed where every blind pass pointed
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sealed_crypt (parser, seeds 47/99 — the assessor's correctly-rotated rank-1 target; least-recently blind-attended per [[assessor-blind-pass-rotation]]). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T14-04-41-399Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played BOTH endings (ending_victory 35/35; ending_plunder 10/35), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — flawless mechanics, exemplary triple-redundant clue chain, reactive room text all in sync. **Its single knock was the standing one:** "tight and satisfying; loses a point only for being short and fully linear once the chain is understood" — i.e. the pack still had **NO failure state**: victory and plunder (bug_0105) are both "complete" outcomes, so the iron-key fork weighs two SAFE choices and carries no risk.
- **Why breadth (justified on-list per the prompt + [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] / [[verifier-assertion-guard]]):** the deterministic assessor is collapsed to the 0.5 floor (six identical blind-playtest stubs); content is blind-CLEAN everywhere; the genuinely highest PRODUCT-value lever named repeatedly is **breadth — "deepen the thinnest pack (sealed_crypt, 2 endings) with one meaningful branch + alternate ending."** This cycle is exactly that, and adds the missing STAKES the blind pass flagged. Branching is the evidence-backed enjoyment lever (wreckers_light, the only 5/5).
- **The improvement (content only — the proven first-class UNLOCK + end_game device, bug_0077/0105/0123):** a new object **bound_tomb** sits among the lesser dead in the crypt — a squat tomb banded in iron and run round with lead, keyed (like the gate and the founder's coffin) to the iron key the player already carries. "THE CRYPT YIELDS ONLY TO IRON" now reads **THREE** ways:
  - UNLOCK the catacombs gate → press on → **ending_victory** (the UNCHANGED win)
  - UNLOCK the founder's coffin → take its silver → **ending_plunder** (greed; terminal, bug_0105)
  - UNLOCK the lead-sealed tomb → break the plague-seal → **ending_doom** (hubris; the pack's first DEATH ending, `death: true`, recoverable via load §8.7)
  The third fork also **answers a question the fiction never did** — WHY the crypt is "sealed" and the chapel above a "burnt shell": the parish plague was leaded shut down here and the chapel burned to be rid of it. Per the founders_coffin / bug_0123 discipline the doom fork is **NEVER an ambush** — its examine telegraphs in plain terms that the seal keeps the pestilence IN, that opening it is one-way, and that it is unlike the gate (the way on) and the coffin (greed).
- **Why WITHIN the bar (victory + plunder byte-identical):** bound_tomb adds no inc_var (`max_score` stays **35**), no flag, and no exit/gating to either prior route, so every existing route/score test (sealed_crypt_scoring, ending_score_summary, parser_acceptance, sealed_crypt_final_step_legibility, sealed_crypt_plunder_fork) passes unchanged. The two test-pinned crypt phrases are kept verbatim (base still "bars the way"; catacombs_open variant still "north" + "catacombs you came so far to find"). Validator stays **0/0**: END_GAME_UNDECLARED satisfied (ending_doom declared); WIN_IS_DEATH cannot fire (no win_condition resolves to the death ending); NO_WINNABLE_ENDING cannot fire (ending_victory remains). No new exit → no SOFTLOCK; a persona that opens the tomb ends DEAD, never wedges.
- **Auto-proven reachable:** the bug_0122 exhaustive parser solver (`parser_all_endings_reachable.test.ts`, auto-discovers packs) now exhausts **5160** crypt states (cap 200k, no cap-out) and reaches all THREE declared endings — declared == reached == {victory, plunder, doom}. A dead/dangling ending would fail it.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0130_sealed_crypt_doom_fork.yaml` + `tests/regression/sealed_crypt_doom_fork.test.ts` (6 cases: three-way fork live in one state; doom is a death, names plague/seal, never reaches catacombs, awards no score; doom is the ONLY death ending, reached only by end_game, victory the sole win; tomb needs the iron key; victory 35/35 + plunder both unchanged, max_score 35; pack validates clean). **Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change** (sealed_crypt pins no test hash — only watchtower does): `0ff57a59…` → `c3c42984…`, recorded in the bug artifact.
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **162 files / 876 tests pass** (+1 file / +6 cases, 0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).

The remaining linear-ish parser pack is **alchemists_tower** (3 endings already, bug_0114) — but it is blind-clean and branch-complete, so resist re-touching. The genuine breadth levers left: (1) **author a NEW branching pack** (the bug_0099/wreckers_light lever, the project's only 5/5) — the largest unclaimed product win; (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in mandatory-prep boss); (3) the **true-goal pivot** — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind pass — sealed_crypt freshly attended (seeds 47/99); cold_forge / clockwork_heist are now least-recently blind-played. **Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose.**

### Cycle result — engine/verifier (bug_0129): the verify-integrity guard now catches ASSERTION GUTTING, not just shell deletion — a cycle can no longer keep every `it()` shell (so the case count holds) while deleting the `expect()`s inside a test body, leaving a green test that verifies nothing. This closes the long-standing "verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot" the script's own header confessed.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on wreckers_light (CYOA, seed 41 — the assessor's correctly-rotated rank-1 target, least-recently blind-attended per the bug_0128 fix). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T13-49-47-140Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all four meaningful endings** (ending_saved / ending_wrecker / ending_mercy / ending_drowned), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO bugs** — "the strongest pack played here": precise gating (unlock_chest only with the iron key; hang_false_light only with oil+striker; tend/search only after hearing the keeper), state-reactive scene text, deterministic state_hash across sessions, and the mercy ending delivering the Mourning-Star backstory even to a player who never read the journal. **No content fix warranted** — this re-confirms [[content-polish-backlog-exhausted]] AND the branching=5/5 vs linear-puzzle=4/5 enjoyment evidence: wreckers_light (the bug_0099 branching pack) is the only pack to score 5/5 enjoyment.
- **Why this cycle pivoted structural (justified off-list per the prompt's allowance):** the deterministic assessor has FULLY COLLAPSED — `npx tsx bin/assess.ts` returns SEVEN identical 0.5 `playtest-*` stubs and nothing above the floor, because every self-extinguishing structural lever has fired and disarmed (eslint config present; lint coverage complete; zero stale doc refs; zero TODO/FIXME markers; no thin mode — cyoa=3/parser=2/rpg=2; the frontier benchmark scorecard `bin/benchmark.ts` exists).
