With content blind-exhausted and the assessor at the saturation floor, the highest-leverage move is hardening the project's CORE trust mechanism, the verifier, on which every future autonomous cycle's credibility rests.
- **Root cause (verifier blind spot, `scripts/verify-integrity.ts`):** the guard counted only `it()`/`test()` SHELLS — `countTestCases` + `TEST_COUNT_FLOOR` (static) + `TEST_COUNT_REGRESSION` (drift). An agent reward-hacking the bar can keep every shell intact (case count unchanged → both guards silent) while DELETING the `expect()` assertions inside a body, leaving a green test that asserts nothing. The behavioral suite can't catch it either (a body with no assertions can't fail). This is precisely the "swap a strict assert for a loose one / gut the body" semantic weakening the header (lines 24-26) admitted it did NOT catch — the standing "verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot" lever in project memory.
- **Fix (engine, trust-but-verify — deterministic, NO LLM-judge):** added a PARALLEL assertion-count guard mirroring the test-case guard exactly: `ASSERTION_RE`/`countAssertions` count `expect(` calls in every test body; `MIN_ASSERTIONS` static floor (`ASSERTION_COUNT_FLOOR` in runStatic, a mass-deletion tripwire set well below the current ~2950); and a NEW pure `detectCountRegressions(before, now)` that emits `TEST_COUNT_REGRESSION` on a case drop AND `ASSERTION_COUNT_REGRESSION` on an assertion drop. Because the two counts are independent, gutting a body (cases held, assertions dropped) trips the assertion guard even when the case guard stays silent. `countTestCasesAtRef` → `countTestArtifactsAtRef` returns `{cases, assertions}` in ONE git pass (no extra git cost); runDrift feeds before/now into the pure detector — which also makes the previously-inline, untested `TEST_COUNT_REGRESSION` unit-testable. **Honest limitation kept in the header:** a COUNT-PRESERVING strict→loose swap (`toBe`→`toBeDefined`) is still uncaught; catching it needs a semantic judge, which would forfeit this script's pure-determinism (no clock/RNG/network) — chosen against deliberately.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0129_assertion_gutting_blind_spot.yaml` + `tests/regression/verifier_assertion_gutting.test.ts` (4 cases: countAssertions independent of shells; gutted body → exactly `[ASSERTION_COUNT_REGRESSION]` while `TEST_COUNT_REGRESSION` stays silent; honest +tests/+assertions cycle trips nothing; real-repo above the floor). Extended `tests/unit/verifier_integrity.test.ts` with countAssertions, the three detectCountRegressions cases, and the real-repo `ASSERTION_COUNT_FLOOR` check. **No pack/content touched; no hash pin moved.**
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **EXIT 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **161 files / 870 tests pass** (+15, 0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008). Drift vs HEAD: 0 errors, 1 expected `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` warning (this cycle modified the guard itself — surfaced for review, by design, not a block).
- **Next suggested focus:** the verifier now guards both shells AND bodies; the only remaining semantic-weakening vector is the count-preserving strict→loose swap, deliberately left to a future LLM-judge lever. Content is blind-exhausted and the deterministic assessor is at the 0.5 floor with no structural lever left undisarmed — so the genuinely highest PRODUCT-value move next is **breadth**: author a new branching pack (the bug_0099/wreckers_light lever, re-confirmed 5/5 this cycle), or deepen a 4/5 linear puzzle pack (sealed_crypt is the thinnest, 2 endings) with one meaningful branch + alternate ending. Resist re-polishing blind-clean prose. The other standing structural item is the dormant-enemy engine feature (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss). Keep rotating the mandated blind pass — wreckers_light freshly attended (seed 41); cold_forge / sealed_crypt are now least-recently blind-played.

### Cycle result — engine/loop-brain (bug_0128): the AFK assessor's blind-pass RECENCY ROTATION was silently dead — it read a deprecated log marker and assumed the wrong file order, so it fell back to alphabetical and re-nominated clockwork_heist (the exact lock-in it was added to cure). Now it tracks true recency from the current log format.
- **Mandated blind pass ran on clockwork_heist (CYOA, seed 3 — the assessor's rank-1 target). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T13-31-14-318Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **ending_truth / ending_rich / ending_caught**, rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or broken state; conditional choices, consumed items, reactive prose, ticks/flags/journal all stayed in sync; determinism held (identical start state_hash across runs). Its only §4/§5 finding (the "toothless" clock/guard) is the **documented recurring false positive** — the teeth (`meta.deadline ticks>=10 → ending_overstayed`; `cross_to_vault_blind → ending_patrol` at ticks>=4 without the ledger) are real and test-locked (clockwork_deadline_enforced, clockwork_guard_patrol_deadline, clockwork_patrol_reachable_in_careful_play; bug_0121 exhaustive solver reaches all 5 endings), but efficient blind play reaches the vault at ticks≤3 and never lingers to ticks 10, so it never WITNESSES them. Per [[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]] the clock must NOT be re-fixed (10 prior prose cycles already did). The real signal was structural: WHY clockwork keeps being the rank-1 target for ~30 straight cycles.
- **Root cause (deterministic loop-brain bug, `src/afk/assessor.ts`):** the blind-pass recency ROTATION — added expressly to "cure the clockwork_heist lock-in that re-nominated one pack" — had two coupled defects that made it a no-op falling back to alphabetical (clockwork sorts first): (1) **STALE MARKER** — `parseAttendanceOffsets` matched only the legacy structured header `"Mandatory LLM playtest target this cycle: <path>"`, abandoned ~15 cycles ago for the prose `"Mandated blind pass ran on <pack>"`, so every recent attendance was invisible and the recency map was frozen; (2) **INVERTED FILE ORDER** — it did `map.set(target, offset)` top-to-bottom with "last write wins (most recent)", correct only for an oldest-first log, but AI_LOOP_STATE.md is NEWEST-FIRST (prepended), so it kept each pack's OLDEST mention and the downstream sort direction inverted. The existing unit test masked both: its fixture was oldest-first (opposite of reality) and its rotation assertion MIRRORED the production sort expression, so it could confirm only that the code matched itself — never that the direction was right.
- **Fix (engine, trust-but-verify):** `parseAttendanceOffsets` now (a) recognizes BOTH the current prose AND the legacy header, (b) accepts a path OR a bare id and keys by a new exported `packStem()` (path-or-id → stem), and (c) keeps each stem's FIRST (topmost = most recent) mention, honest about the newest-first log. `assess()`'s recency tiebreak resolves `c.target` via `packStem` and negates the offset so a less-recent (larger-offset) pack sorts EARLIER, never-attended (MIN_SAFE_INTEGER) earliest, non-playtest (MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) last — so it still only reorders the equal-scored 0.5 `playtest-*` stubs. Incidental captures (e.g. "…ran on the assessor") land under a stem no candidate queries; harmless. **No pack/content touched; no hash pin moved.**
- **Verified live:** the fixed rotation now nominates **wreckers_light first** (least-recently blind-attended), places clockwork_heist mid-list, and **watchtower_road last** (it was bug_0127, the most recent pass) — matching the actual cycle history. The pre-fix order was driven by each pack's oldest mention and collapsed to alphabetical when stale (clockwork first). The loop remains free to override the recommendation (this cycle did, completing the mandated clockwork pass while fixing the rotation).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0128_blind_pass_rotation_stale_marker.yaml` + `tests/regression/assessor_blind_pass_rotation.test.ts` (4 cases: current-prose recognition; newest-first topmost-is-most-recent; most-recently-attended sorts LAST / never-attended first; real-repo guard that the nominee is not the most-recently-attended pack). Rewrote the two `tests/unit/assessor.test.ts` attendance tests to assert the SEMANTIC property (newest-first, least-recently-attended first) instead of mirroring the implementation, and added `packStem` + legacy-marker cases.
- **Next suggested focus:** the rotation is honest again, so let the deterministic assessor drive the blind pass — **wreckers_light is now correctly surfaced as least-recently blind-attended** (rotate the next blind pass there). Content remains blind-exhausted across all packs; resist churning blind-clean prose (esp. clockwork's clock — false-positive magnet). Standing structural levers if rotating off content: (1) the **dormant-enemy engine feature**; (2) the **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge); (3) the **true-goal pivot** — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md; the assessor already raises `frontier-benchmark-scorecard` above the 0.5 floor once content saturates).

### Cycle result — content_fix/hint_text (bug_0127): The Watchtower Road's sealed-letter journal no longer reads as a SELF-affordance — "a knowing eye could break open and read" becomes "the right pair of eyes — not your own; the seal is set too hard for that" so the east-only player stops hunting for a self-break action that never existed
- **Mandated blind pass ran on watchtower_road (CYOA, seeds 31 / 77 / 12 — the assessor's rank-1 target; last attended at seed 11, bug_0120). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T13-20-51-530Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **ending_truth (win) and ending_captured ×2**, rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, broken state, or dup-take; conditional choices (carry-lantern/descend/light-beacon/show-papers) all appeared/disappeared correctly; paths converge cleanly; transcript fully consistent.
- **The one actionable finding, raised INDEPENDENTLY in §4 AND §5:** the sealed letter "heavily implies it can be read/opened ('a knowing eye could break open and read'), but on the east-only route I never found an action to do so … the hint over-promises … or it's a dangling clue." Root cause: bug_0108 rewrote this exact journal to foreshadow the letter's two real uses (checkpoint `show_papers`; the hermit's `show_letter`, west, which sets `learned_truth`+`seal_broken`), but "a knowing eye could break open and read" still parses as the PLAYER's own capability. A blind player who took the letter and went EAST (tower/beacon/checkpoint, never west to the hermit) read it as "I could crack this myself" and hunted for a self-break action that does not exist by design — only the hermit breaks the seal, and an unread letter is explicitly NOT proof at the checkpoint (`confront_smuggler`'s `not_flag:learned_truth` variant: "The seal is unbroken, and you've no idea what it says"). The hint pointed the east-only route at an affordance it cannot reach, framed as the player's own. This is the bug_0027/0119 honesty discipline (don't promise an affordance the player can't reach as written), applied as the direct descendant of bug_0108 with NEW blind evidence its wording didn't fully land.
- **Fix (hint_text only — one journal line on `take_letter`):** "…or that a knowing eye could break open and read" → "…or that the right pair of eyes — **not your own; the seal is set too hard for that** — could break open and read." The em-dash aside disclaims the self-action and reframes the knowing eye as SOMEONE ELSE the player must find (the hermit). bug_0108's two-use foreshadow is preserved verbatim in structure (papers/checkpoint AND break/read the seal). **NO choice/flag/route/ending touched** — pure signposting.
- **Why WITHIN the bar:** content-only; no engine/validator/schema change. Validator stays 0/0. **Deliberate, surfaced re-pin:** watchtower_road's compiled content_hash is pinned in TWO HASH_PIN_FILES (`tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts:93` + `traces/bugs/bug_0002_watchtower_blind_polish.yaml:3`); both re-pinned `cd749bd5…` → `f989028090…` alongside this real content change (verify:integrity static OK, 0/0).
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0127_watchtower_letter_self_affordance.yaml` + `tests/regression/watchtower_letter_self_affordance.test.ts` (3 cases: the journal disclaims the self-action and names "not your own"/"the right pair of eyes" while the bare ambiguous "a knowing eye" is GONE; bug_0108's two-use foreshadow intact — matches /papers|checkpoint/i AND /break|read|seal/i, single entry no stacking; the hermit's `show_letter` still breaks the seal — signposting-only). bug_0108's `watchtower_letter_signposting.test.ts` passes unchanged.
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **exit 0** — verify:integrity static OK (0/0), typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **159 files / 855 tests pass** (+3 new, 0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008 — and exactly the gap the bug_0121 exhaustive solver closes).
- **Next suggested focus:** watchtower_road is now blind-clean AND its sealed-letter signposting is honest end-to-end (no over-promised self-affordance). Per [[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]] the structural ending-reachability proof TRILOGY is complete across all 3 modes, so the remaining levers are **content/experience, not structural proof** — this cycle was exactly such a lever, surfaced by the blind pass. Standing deferred items if rotating off content: (1) the **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in mandatory-prep boss); (2) the **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge); (3) the **true-goal pivot** — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack — watchtower freshly attended (seeds 31/77/12); clockwork_heist / cold_forge are least-recently blind-played.

### Cycle result — content_fix (bug_0123): the alchemists_tower silver casket's base examine now telegraphs the betrayal fork as a TERMINAL greed choice, not a free bonus — so the moral fork is a deliberate choice, never an ambush
- **Mandated blind pass ran on alchemists_tower (parser, seed 19 — the assessor's rank-1 target). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T12-34-33-369Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes to **all THREE endings** (ending_betrayal 35/40, ending_poisoned death, ending_cured 40/40 win), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or broken state; gating, reactive prose, scoring, and the iron-key-serves-two-locks mechanic all held.
- **The ONE real friction (hit in Run 1, §5):** unlocking the silver casket immediately fires `ending_betrayal` with no warning, and the casket's examine framed it as treasure ("the Great Work… worth a hundred towers") — so a completionist holding the finished cure popped it "for the last 5 points" expecting a bonus and was railroaded into the dark ending, *accidentally* rather than as a deliberate choice.
- **Fix (content-only):** bug_0114 ADDED the moral fork; for it to MEAN something the player must understand they are CHOOSING betrayal. The `has_item:antidote` examine variant already carries that weight, but a player who examines the casket BEFORE brewing (as Run 1 did) sees only the BASE text. The base examine now telegraphs the act as a one-way greed choice — "to set the iron key to it is to turn from her pallet to her coffer … Lift this lid and there is no closing it after: it is a thing chosen once and for good." No engine confirmation prompt (that would break the Sierra-genre's deliberate, load-recoverable traps, §8.7). Keeps the bug_0114 base anchors ("masterwork" / "locked away within arm's reach"), no leak of the iron key's location, no antidote assumption. **Byte-identical behaviour:** the casket still fires `ending_betrayal` only via end_game; canonical solve still wins `ending_cured` 40/40; max_score stays 40 (bug_0104 intact); validator 0/0.
- **Locked (§15):** `traces/bugs/bug_0123_alchemists_casket_terminal_telegraph.yaml` + `tests/regression/alchemists_casket_terminal_telegraph.test.ts` (6 cases). Content hash cf894db… → 99e5986… (deliberate, surfaced; alchemists_tower pins no hash). `npm run health` green (EXIT=0). bug_0114's 7-case test still passes against the new base text.
- **Next suggested focus:** the remaining 5 assessor blind-playtest targets (sealed_crypt, cold_forge, sunken_barrow, watchtower_road, clockwork_heist) — but per project memory the content-polish backlog is exhausted; these packs are blind-clean and dock enjoyment only on shortness/linearity. Higher-value: **structural/breadth** work (the proven enjoyment lever is branching, bug_0099/0105/0114) or extending the bug_0121/0122 exhaustive ending-reachability solver to RPG packs (the last unproven mode; step-keyed combat RNG is the open hard problem — see [[cyoa-exhaustive-solver]]).

### Cycle result — structural/test: an exhaustive concrete SOLVER now proves every declared ending of every CYOA pack is dynamically reachable by real play — ground truth that closes the validator's "conservative" static-reachability gap AND the health bar's single-pack dynamic-coverage gap (bug_0121, test-layer, no production/content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on clockwork_heist (CYOA, seed 23 — the assessor's rank-1 target). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T11-46-12-813Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all FOUR endings** (Truth ×2 variants / Rich / Caught), rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional blockers** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or broken state; conditional choices, consumed items, reactive prose, ticks/flags/journal all stayed in sync; determinism held.
- **Both §5 "design flaws" it raised are FALSE POSITIVES already implemented AND test-locked.** (1) "Ledger gates nothing" — Run 4 crossed safely only at `ticks 2` (`approach_vault` gate is `any_of:[ticks<=3, read_ledger]`); at `ticks>=4` without the ledger the only vault-ward choice is `cross_to_vault_blind → ending_patrol`, locked by `clockwork_guard_patrol_deadline` + `clockwork_patrol_reachable_in_careful_play`. (2) "`ticks` is cosmetic" — `meta.deadline: ticks>=10 → ending_overstayed` is engine-enforced (checkWin), tuned generous so honest play tops at ticks 6, locked by `clockwork_deadline_enforced`. The tester reached only ticks 6 and never took the gamble, so it never witnessed the (correctly generous) teeth — a structural false-positive of blind play on this over-polished pack (25 traces + 25 tests).
- **Second blind pass (least-attended rotation) ran on sealed_crypt (parser, seed 19):** fresh source-blind subagent solved both endings (ending_victory 35/35, ending_plunder), **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs**. Both freshly-tested packs are blind-clean; both dock enjoyment to 4/5 for the SAME reason (shortness/linearity), confirming the content-polish backlog is exhausted (project memory) and the pivot is structural.
- **The real signal = the assessor's entire rank-list:** six identical "blind-playtest pack X — the coverage bot can't solve its puzzles, so quality is unverified" items. Root cause is a genuine BAR gap, not content: the CYOA validator's `ENDING_UNREACHABLE` is by its own header (`src/validate/cyoa_validator.ts:6`) a "documented, conservative approximation" over an over-approximated abstract graph — sound for "IS unreachable" but NOT a proof of "IS reachable"; and `npm run health`'s only DYNAMIC playthrough runs on 1 of 7 packs via the random coverage bot, which (demonstrated live this run) reaches just 2/3 watchtower endings.
- **Fix (test-layer, pure addition — no production/content code touched):** `tests/regression/cyoa_all_endings_reachable.test.ts` runs an exhaustive breadth-first search over the engine's OWN concrete action space (`buildObservation` legal choices fed back through `makeStep`, incl. the `meta.deadline` checkWin), keyed on full game state (scene + true flags + inventory + every var), and asserts {endings reached by play} == {endings declared}. Declared-but-unreachable (dead content the static check can miss) fails; reached-but-undeclared (dangling end target) fails; any future route-severing regression fails. Bounded by a 200k-state cap and **fails if the cap is hit**, so it can never silently pass by truncating — sound by construction. Packs are auto-discovered from `content/cyoa/pack`, so a new CYOA pack is covered the moment it ships (matches the bug_0096 health-covers-all-packs bar).
- **Verified live (the solver has teeth):** clockwork_heist exhausts **822** distinct states and reaches ALL 5 endings — including `ending_overstayed` (the deadline) and `ending_patrol`, the very endings the random coverage bot cannot plan to; watchtower_road **1930** states, all 3; wreckers_light **127** states, all 4. Every search completed well under the cap; missing-ending set empty for all three.
- **Locked:** `traces/bugs/bug_0121_cyoa_exhaustive_solvability_proof.yaml` + the test above (4 cases: discovery guard + one exhaustive proof per CYOA pack). No hash pin touched (no pack edited).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **exit 0** — verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **153 files / 832 tests pass** (+4 new, 0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008 — and now exactly the gap the new solver closes).
- **Next suggested focus:** extend the exhaustive solver to the **parser/RPG modes** (verb×object action space — bigger but the same BFS shape; would retire the "coverage bot can't solve puzzle packs" churn for ALL seven packs and let the assessor stop generating 0.5-value blind-playtest busywork on solved packs). Then the standing structural levers: (1) the **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss); (2) the **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge); (3) the **true-goal pivot** — run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack; clockwork_heist + sealed_crypt freshly attended this cycle.

### Cycle result — content: The Watchtower Road's tower-top beacon is now a ONE-SHOT whose scene text follows the flag — lighting it retires the choice and the parapet reads as a lit fire, instead of re-offering "Light the signal beacon" over a brazier still described as cold (bug_0120, content_fix, the bug_0098/0055 reactive-prose device)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on watchtower_road (CYOA, seed 11 — the assessor's rank-1 target; last attended at seed 53). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T11-34-06-334Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played FOUR routes to **all THREE endings** (truth / escape / captured), confirmed the bug_0098 reactive beacon epilogue adapts to `raised_alarm`, and rated the pack **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional blockers** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or broken state; the cart→cellar shortcut, lantern/letter/ledger gating, the hermit dialogue, and the proof-state-reactive `confront_smuggler` choices all stayed in sync; determinism held.
- **The §5 lead finding (a real state/text contradiction):** after lighting the beacon and setting `raised_alarm` (run 1), returning to **Tower Top** still showed "A cold brazier waits, begging for a flame" and **RE-OFFERED "Light the signal beacon."** The `light_beacon` choice gated only on `{ has_item: lantern }` — and the lantern is never consumed — so the choice stayed selectable and the player could "light" an already-blazing beacon a second time, while the scene's single static text contradicted the fire they had just kindled (which `signal_fire` and every ending's `raised_alarm` variant already describe as lit). A one-shot dramatic act was rendered as a repeatable, state-blind toggle. (The other §5 items are deferred: the `seal_broken`-vs-`confront_smuggler` prose is by-design — the pack treats `learned_truth`, not the seal, as the proof state per bug_0055 — and the retreat/geography notes are subjective polish.)
- **Fix (content only — the pack's standing reactive-prose + one-shot-gate device, bug_0098/0055/0099):** two changes to `tower_top`, both purely cosmetic to reachability: (1) `light_beacon`'s condition tightened from `{ has_item: lantern }` to `all_of [ has_item: lantern, not_flag: raised_alarm ]` — the beacon fires exactly once, then the choice retires; (2) added a `raised_alarm` scene variant (most-specific-first, first-match-wins) so the returning player reads the beacon they kindled "still blazes... and below, the horn that answered it has not yet fallen silent" instead of the un-lit base text. The un-alarmed player sees the original base text AND the original `light_beacon` choice verbatim. `raised_alarm` is still set exactly once, on the same first lighting, by the same effect — **no new flag, route, or ending.**
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar:** content-only; no engine/validator/schema change. The bug_0098 payoff (every ending renders its `raised_alarm` variant on the beacon route) is untouched and its test passes unchanged — lighting once still sets the flag and the route still reaches the same endings. **Deliberate, surfaced re-pin:** watchtower_road's compiled content_hash is pinned in TWO HASH_PIN_FILES (`tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts` line 88 + `traces/bugs/bug_0002_watchtower_blind_polish.yaml`); both re-pinned `862b33ad…` → `cd749bd5…` alongside this real content change (drift check: 0 errors, 2 expected HASH_PIN_REPINNED warnings — the legitimate snapshot-update workflow, not a launder).
- **Locked:** `traces/bugs/bug_0120_watchtower_beacon_one_shot.yaml` + `tests/regression/watchtower_beacon_relight.test.ts` (3 cases: before lighting, the base "begging for a flame" text shows and `light_beacon` is offered; after lighting and stepping back from `signal_fire`, the variant "still blazes" shows, the base prose is gone, and `light_beacon` is absent while `survey_road`/`descend` remain — with the lantern still in inventory, proving the GATE not item-loss retires the choice; and the beacon still fires once and the route still reaches `ending_escape` with `raised_alarm` set).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **exit 0** — verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean, full suite **152 files / 828 tests pass** (+3 new cases, 0 regressions), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached `ending_truth`/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008). Drift vs HEAD: 0 errors, 2 expected HASH_PIN_REPINNED warnings.
- **Next suggested focus:** watchtower_road is now blind-clean and its tower-top beat is state-honest end-to-end. Content polish remains exhausted across all packs — resist churning blind-clean packs. Pivot STRUCTURAL, highest value first: (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where prep is *mandatory*) — the largest deferred item; bug_0114's `combat_guaranteed` opt-in + bug_0097's reachable-stat proof are the groundwork. (2) The **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge). (3) The **true-goal pivot**: run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack — watchtower freshly attended (seed 11); clockwork_heist is now the least-recently blind-played cyoa.

### Cycle result — content/hint_text: the reaver's shade now tells the player its ward is the ONLY aid the barrow gives — so the cautious player who hits the wight fight's designed scare presses on instead of retreating to hunt for gear that doesn't exist (bug_0119, content_fix, the bug_0027 honesty-discipline lever)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sunken_barrow (rpg, seed 7) — the assessor's rank-1 target (coverage bot can't plan its multi-step puzzles). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T11-22-38-112Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played TWO routes: the **warded victory (50/50, ending_victory)** and the **warned-against doom fork (prise the sarcophagus → ending_woken, 25/50)**. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO hard bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or stale prose; reactive room text, the shade dialogue (+3 ward real & one-shot), persistent enemy/player HP across retreat, the lever might-check, the claim-not-arrival win, and both endings all stayed in sync; determinism held.
- **The one actionable finding (§4/§5):** mid-fight at 8 HP vs the 12-HP wight, "the deterministic math looked like a guaranteed loss, and I retreated thinking I was under-prepared," saved only by a high variance roll — and with **no heal/rest mechanic anywhere**, "a cautious player could reasonably conclude the encounter is unwinnable and quit," backing out to seek healing/gear that does not exist. This is the **SAME failure mode as bug_0027** (a player abandons a winnable game hunting a missing item), here on the wight rather than the slab. The fight's narrow, swingy difficulty is **INTENDED and verified** (bug_0102 gave it teeth, bug_0113 tuned the warded route to a reliable ~88% win with an HP cushion, both over seeds 1-40) — so the defect is **legibility, not balance**: the shade teaches the ward but never says the ward is ALL the aid the barrow offers.
- **Fix (content/hint_text only — the pack's standing honesty discipline, bug_0027/0029/0069, applied to the shade's spoken counsel):** the `ask_wight` npc_text now adds, after the existing ward instruction, that there is no balm in the barrow, no second blade, no charm laid by — "this ward is the whole of the help you will find, and it is help enough"; that the grim mid-fight moment "is the wight's way of wearing a man down, not the truth of the odds"; and to "set your feet and keep striking" rather than break off to "seek aid that is not here" (which "you only come again to the same fight, no fresher than you left it"). The prepared player who hits the designed scare now reads "press on", not "go find what you're missing." The under-armed player who skips the shade never hears this and stays in bug_0102's intended lethal gamble.
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar:** PURE spoken npc_text — no flag/var/journal/score/topic/ending/DC/stat/enemy change. The +3 ward (defense 2→5), its one-shot retire, the lore topic, and the canonical victory trace's recorded JOURNAL text are all untouched. **Deliberate, surfaced re-pin:** `traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json` content_hash `80ab4154…` → `7546c419…`; the trace's `expected_final_hash` is UNCHANGED (`aa5f8649…`) — only narration changed, not state, so the canonical seed-1 warded victory replays to the identical final hash (verified live before re-pinning; HASH_PIN_REPINNED, accompanied by a real content change — not a launder). No test SOURCE pins this pack's hash.
- **Locked:** `traces/bugs/bug_0119_barrow_ward_no_more_aid.yaml` + `tests/regression/rpg_barrow_ward_no_more_aid.test.ts` (3 cases: the live ward counsel frames the fight winnable-by-persistence and denies the find-more-gear/retreat inference while keeping the original ward instruction; the ward mechanic is untouched — defense 2→5, one-shot, journalled; the wight keeps bug_0102's stats, proving this is legibility not a balance retune).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` **exit 0** — verify:integrity static OK, typecheck, lint, format, full suite green (+3 new cases), all 7 packs validate 0/0, watchtower playtest ran (its unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit, bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** every linear pack now branches and both RPG packs make prep decisive+reliable+legible — content polish is exhausted across the board; resist churning blind-clean packs. Pivot STRUCTURAL, highest value first: (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where prep is *mandatory*) — the largest deferred item; bug_0114's `combat_guaranteed` opt-in + bug_0097's reachable-stat proof are the groundwork. (2) The **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge). (3) The **true-goal pivot**: run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack — sunken_barrow is now freshly attended (seed 7); rotate onto a less-recent pack next (clockwork_heist / watchtower_road are least-recently blind-played).

### Cycle result — content: cold_forge makes a carried-but-unworn plate legible at the fight (bug_0118, content_fix, the bug_0023/0094 reactive-prose device)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on cold_forge (rpg, seed 7) — the assessor's recommended target (coverage bot leaves its multi-step puzzles unsolved, a standing heuristic-planner limit). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T11-14-01-076Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played TWO routes: the **full geared victory (50/50, ending_victory)** and an **under-armed stress run**. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or stale prose; reactive room text, the lantern-spirit dialogue, combat/HP/score, the lever might-check, and item-gating all stayed in sync; the no-pry-bar recovery path and the intentional non-takeable hammer both confirmed as good design, not flaws.
- **The one actionable finding (§4): the founder's cold-iron plate is a TWO-STEP** — TAKE only carries it; a separate USE ("don") buckles it on for the +2 defense. A hasty player can walk into the slag-sentinel fight with the plate slung loose at **+0 defense and no nudge** they are under-armed; the only "+2 defense" feedback is the journal line that fires on donning. The Bellows Walk (the combat-approach scene) read identically whether the plate was buckled, carried loose, or never picked up — the one unreactive seam at the pack's single decision-under-pressure beat, in an otherwise fully-reactive pack (pry-bar bug_0094, plate/founder bug_0023, sentinel_stilled).
- **Fix (content-only):** added a second reactive variant to `bellows_walk`, declared AFTER `sentinel_stilled` so the broken-sentinel prose still wins post-combat (first matching variant in declared order wins — `src/parser/model.ts` `roomDescription`). The new variant fires on `[has_item cold_iron_plate, none_of plate_donned]` — only PRE-combat and only while the plate is held-but-unbuckled — and names the loose plate: "still hangs loose and unbuckled at your side — better to don it now than to meet that anvil-weight under-armed." Players with no plate, or who already donned it, see the original base threat verbatim. NO new choice/flag/exit/score/ending; routes untouched.
- **Locked:** `traces/bugs/bug_0118_cold_forge_unworn_plate_nudge.yaml` + `tests/regression/cold_forge_unworn_plate_nudge.test.ts` (5 cases: base text with no plate; nudge while carried-unworn; base text again once donned; declared-order precedence — sentinel_stilled wins over the nudge while still carried-unworn; pack validates green). No hash pin touched (cold_forge is not a HASH_PIN_FILE; the additive variant changes the content hash to `8590fefd…` but no test pins it).
- **Verify:** `npm run health` green (verify:integrity static OK, typecheck, lint, format, full suite 822/822, all 7 pack validations OK, watchtower playtest ran). The watchtower coverage bot's unreached ending_truth/hermit scenes are the standing heuristic-planner limit (bug_0008).
- **Next suggested focus:** rotate the blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack (clockwork_heist or watchtower_road were the prior targets; sunken_barrow/cold_forge just covered) — content stays blind-clean across packs, so once a rotation finds no actionable finding, pivot to the structural/breadth levers (more branching/endings, per bug_0099) rather than churning clean prose.

### Cycle result — validator: SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN now also covers `has_item` wins — claiming the winning relic is a climactic act the perfect score must coincide with, the same as setting the win flag (bug_0117, validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on sealed_crypt (parser, seed 29 — a FRESH seed; recent passes used 7/53): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T10-59-04-756Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played TWO routes: **WON ending_victory 35/35** (honest route) and reached **ending_plunder 10/35** (the bug_0105 moral fork). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or stale prose; reactive room/object text, the key dependency chain, scoring, and both endings stayed in sync; determinism held. The pack is blind-clean and content-complete. Its one substantive observation **CONFIRMED the bug_0116 design**: the score hits 35/35 one step before the `visited: catacombs` win but *"reads as intentional rather than a 'perfect-score-yet-stuck' trap … because the only remaining action (step north) is obvious and prompted"* — i.e. a navigation denouement rightly awards nothing, exactly why bug_0116 excludes `visited` wins. Per project memory (content polish is exhausted; pivot STRUCTURAL; resist churning blind-clean packs), the improvement is structural — and it sits right at the boundary the blind pass illuminated.
- **The improvement (validator, trust-but-verify — the bug_0116 generalization to possession-wins):** bug_0116's `SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN` fires only on a single `has_flag` win. But a `has_item: I` win turns on the SAME kind of deliberate climactic ACT — **CLAIMING the relic** — as a `has_flag` win turns on SETTING the flag; both are distinct from `visited` locomotion. I widened the check to cover `has_item`: a new **`requiredItems(conds)`** helper (the has_item twin of `requiredFlags`), and a refactor of the reachability math into a shared **`scoreWithout(winActLists)`** helper (perfect score reachable WITHOUT the win-trigger act = init + every award not co-located with a list in that set). For a required item I, the "granting acts" are **object I's `take_effects`** (taking I grants it) **plus any list with `add_item: I`**; if max_score is reachable without all of them, warn. The flag path is behaviour-identical (now expressed through the same helper). `visited` still never drives a finding.
- **Why this is SOUND (zero false positives; fires on no current pack):** a **purely IMPLICIT take** (a takeable item with NO take_effects and no add_item) has no scriptable act list to attach score to, so — like an unsettable flag — it is left to `WIN_UNREACHABLE` and NOT flagged (`granters.size === 0` continue, mirroring the existing flag guard). Excluding EVERY granting list (even a scored one) only LOWERS the without-act total, so the bar errs toward NOT warning. List exclusion is by REFERENCE identity, matching each declared act list exactly. The single-win guard + any_of/none_of opacity are unchanged. **Audit of all four scoring packs = 0 findings, no content changed, no hash moved:** the only has_item-winning pack is **sunken_barrow** (`visited: relic_chamber` + `has_item: circlet`) — its circlet's `take_effects` award **+25** (the deliberate CLAIM, bug_0107), so the score reachable WITHOUT the claim is 15 (might-check) + 10 = 25 < max_score 50 → no warning; sealed_crypt/cold_forge win on `visited`; alchemists' cure carries +5.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 149 files / 817 tests pass** (was 149/812; +5 new cases in the SAME file); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (no content edited — no hash moved anywhere). Extended `tests/regression/parser_score_peaks_before_win.test.ts` (6 → 11 cases): a has_item win whose winning relic is claimed by an UNSCORED act (take_effects present but no score) while max is reachable first IS flagged (bug-first, names "claiming"/the relic); moving the capstone onto the relic's `take_effects` clears it; a relic granted by a SCORED `add_item` act is not flagged while an UNSCORED `add_item` grant IS; and a purely-implicit take (no take_effects/add_item) is conservatively NOT flagged. The existing case (1) now also exercises the item path on sunken_barrow (still 0 findings). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0117_score_peaks_before_win_has_item.yaml`. No engine/runner/schema/content change.
- **Next suggested focus:** SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN now spans both climactic-act win shapes (`has_flag` SET + `has_item` CLAIM) while correctly leaving `visited` locomotion alone — the bug_0104/0116/0117 family is a complete enforced bar for the "perfect-score-before-the-win" smell across the closed win-condition DSL. Remaining STRUCTURAL items, highest value first: (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where prep is *mandatory*) — the largest deferred structural item; bug_0114's `combat_guaranteed` opt-in + bug_0097's reachable-stat proof are the groundwork. (2) The **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge). (3) The **true-goal pivot**: run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — sealed_crypt is now freshly attended (seed 29); rotate onto a less-recent pack next (watchtower_road / clockwork_heist / cold_forge are the least-recently blind-played).

### Cycle result — validator: the bug_0104 "perfect-score-yet-unfinished" smell is now caught STRUCTURALLY — a new SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN warning flags a max_score reachable before the win-trigger act (bug_0116, validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass rotated onto alchemists_tower (parser, seed 23 — a FRESH seed; recent passes used 41/89/53/101): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T10-44-32-963Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played THREE routes: **WON ending_cured 40/40** (full brew→cure), reached **ending_betrayal** (the bug_0114 non-death greed fork) and **ending_poisoned** (drank the black phial), and observed the fourth action (give black phial to master). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or stale prose; every reactive room/object text, the key/brew dependency chain, scoring, and all branches stayed in sync; determinism held. The pack is blind-clean and content-complete; its only knock (a short, low-resistance golden path) is the inherent brevity the bug_0114 fork already addressed as far as content polish reaches. Per project memory (content polish is exhausted; pivot STRUCTURAL; resist churning blind-clean packs), the improvement is structural — the standing, repeatedly-listed next-focus item.
- **The improvement (validator, trust-but-verify — the bug_0104 generalization, "caught structurally rather than by blind playtest"):** added **`SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN`** (a WARNING) to `validateParser` (so `validateRpg` inherits it via delegation), right after `SCORE_UNREACHABLE`. bug_0104 fixed a "perfect score yet unfinished" smell that ONLY a blind playtester (seed 89) caught — alchemists awarded 5+10+20 = 35 = old max_score ALL before the cure (the bug_0057 climax the win turns on), which awarded nothing. The fix moved the +5 onto the cure (max 35→40) but nothing stopped the class recurring. The new check: when a pack has **exactly ONE** win_condition that **requires a `has_flag` F** the player must set (a guaranteed conjunctive literal — top-level or in `all_of`; any_of/none_of are opaque), it computes the max score reachable by awards **none co-located** (same effect list) with a setter of F; if that already reaches `max_score`, the perfect score is attainable **before** firing the win-trigger act → warned. Two new helpers: `effectLists(pack)` (the grouped twin of `allEffects`, keeping each declared effect LIST intact so co-location is decidable) and `requiredFlags(conds)`. The RPG validator passes its runtime effect lists (each enemy `on_defeat`, each skill-check `on_success`/`on_failure`) via a new `extraEffectLists` option, so the check covers a future RPG pack whose win turns on a defeat flag.
- **Why this is SOUND (zero false positives; fires on no current pack):** it warns only when ALL guards hold, so it detects fewer cases but never wrong ones — a WARNING, never an error (a peaked-early score is a quality nit, not a broken game). The **single-win guard** avoids a false positive on a multi-win pack whose flagless second win is the real climax. Excluding EVERY F-setting list (even a scored one) only LOWERS the without-F total, so the bar errs toward NOT warning. any_of/none_of are skipped (same stance as the existing dead-content analysis). **Audit of all four scoring packs = 0 findings, no content changed, no hash moved:** sealed_crypt wins on `visited: catacombs`, cold_forge on `visited: ember_chamber`, sunken_barrow on `visited: relic_chamber` + `has_item: circlet` — navigation/possession, not a has_flag, so the check never engages; alchemists wins on `has_flag: cure_administered` whose setter act now carries the bug_0104 +5, so the without-F max is 35 < 40 — no warning. The pre-bug_0104 alchemists shape WOULD now be flagged (proven by the regression's synthetic bug-first case).
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 149 files / 812 tests pass** (was 148/806; +6 new, +1 file); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (no content edited — alchemists_tower hash unchanged at `cf894dbe…`, no hash moved anywhere). New regression `tests/regression/parser_score_peaks_before_win.test.ts` (6 cases): the four shipped scoring packs produce ZERO SCORE_PEAKS_BEFORE_WIN findings and stay green; the pre-bug_0104 shape (win turns on a flag set by an UNSCORED act, max reachable before it) IS flagged (bug-first, WARNING severity, names the flag); moving the capstone award ONTO the flag-setting act clears the finding; a navigation-only win is NEVER flagged even with full score reachable before the final step (the denouement-step shape); a multi-win pack is left alone; and an RPG pack reaches the check through `validateRpg`. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0116_score_peaks_before_win_check.yaml`. No engine/runner/schema/content change.
- **Next suggested focus:** the score/win-coincidence class is now an enforced bar (the bug_0104 + bug_0107 generalization, complementing SCORE_UNREACHABLE + INERT_FLAG in the parser validator's soundness/quality family). Remaining STRUCTURAL items, highest value first: (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where prep is *mandatory*) — the largest deferred structural item; bug_0114's `combat_guaranteed` opt-in + bug_0097's reachable-stat proof are the groundwork. (2) The **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge). (3) The **true-goal pivot**: run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — alchemists_tower is now freshly attended (seed 23); rotate onto a less-recent pack next.

### Cycle result — content_fix: The Wrecker's Light makes the knows_truth reframe LEGIBLE at the moment of choice — reading the keeper's journal now colors the save/wreck DECISION scenes, not only the epilogue (bug_0115, content only, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on wreckers_light (CYOA, seed 11): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T10-35-28-612Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent played 4 routes to **all FOUR endings** (saved / wrecker / mercy / drowned), rated it **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5 (the project's joint-highest), ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, or broken state; reactive scene text (bare mantel after taking the striker; the gallery bracket reframed once armed) and item-gating (`unlock_chest` needs the key, `hang_false_light` needs oil+striker, `tend_keeper` needs `met_keeper`) all verified; determinism held. I rotated the mandated pass onto wreckers_light per the protocol's "rotate the seed and the pack" clause and project memory (rotate onto the least-attended pack; it had one prior pass at seed 7, bug_0100). The assessor's rank-1 was wreckers_light too.
- **The finding (report §4 + §5 + §6, the ONE substantive observation, raised three times):** reading the keeper's journal (`knows_truth`) had a payoff that was *"invisible within a single playthrough"* — the truth reframed only the ENDING prose, never the decision the player was actually making, so *"the most investigative players get narrative-only payoff they could not perceive in one run."* Root cause: `knows_truth` fired reactive variants ONLY on the ending text (ending_saved/wrecker/mercy, locked by `wreckers_light_branching.test.ts`); the two scenes where the player COMMITS the moral act — `lantern_room` (save-beat) and `gallery` (wreck-beat) — read identically whether or not the Mourning Star truth had been uncovered. The pack's design is deliberately a REFRAME, not a route (the journal must NEVER unlock a new ending), so the gap is legibility, not missing mechanics — making "give the journal mechanical teeth / a new route" the WRONG fix (it would contradict the design); the right fix is to make the existing reframe visible at the moment of choice.
- **The improvement (content only — the proven reactive-prose lever, bug_0099/0051, applied at the decision point):** added a `knows_truth`-aware variant to each moral-decision scene, placed most-specific-first so it wins over the inventory-only variant (first-match-wins): `lantern_room` when `[lamp_oil, striker, knows_truth]` NAMES the Mourning Star and frames waking the lamp as *"a debt with a steady hand that a vengeful one never could"* (echoing the ending_saved knows_truth epilogue at the moment of choice); `gallery` when `[lamp_oil, striker, knows_truth]` makes the false light vengeance for the keeper's drowned son (*"walk him onto the teeth that took the boy"*), not bare greed. Uninformed players (no journal) still see the original inventory-only variants verbatim.
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar (content-only, no engine/validator/schema change):** NO new choices, conditions, flags, routes, or endings — the available actions at each decision scene are byte-identical informed vs uninformed (locked by the test), so the reframe changes only how the moment READS, never what the player can DO. knows_truth stays cosmetic; the four endings and every route are untouched, so `wreckers_light_branching.test.ts` (which exercises these exact routes to all four endings and locks the cosmetic-not-route invariant) passes unchanged.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 148 files / 806 tests pass** (was 803/147; +3 new, +1 file); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all packs validate 0/0** (wreckers_light compiled content_hash now `28e6eb0b…`). New regression `tests/regression/wreckers_light_decision_point_truth.test.ts` (3 cases, live engine + buildObservation): the informed save/wreck DECISION scenes name the Mourning Star (plus the distinctive "steers him clear" / "the teeth that took the boy" reframe phrases) while the uninformed ones do not and still render their original prompts ("lights up for her" / "ape a safe harbor"); and the available actions are identical informed vs uninformed at both beats (the reframe is cosmetic). **Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change** (wreckers_light is NOT a HASH_PIN_FILE and NO test pins its hash — confirmed in bug_0100 — so the hash moves with nothing to re-pin). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0115_wreckers_decision_point_truth.yaml`. No engine/validator/schema change; no other pack's hash moved.
- **Next suggested focus:** wreckers_light is now blind-clean AND its investigative payoff is legible end-to-end (decision + epilogue). With content polish exhausted across all packs (every linear pack branched: sealed_crypt bug_0105, alchemists_tower bug_0114; both RPG packs prep-decisive-and-reliable: bug_0101/0102/0113; reactive payoffs now legible: bug_0115), the next high-value moves are **STRUCTURAL**, not more pack tweaks — resist churning blind-clean packs. (1) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where prep is *mandatory*) is the largest deferred structural item. (2) The **scoring-coincidence validator WARNING** (flag "max_score reachable before any win_condition fires" — the bug_0104/0107 generalization, caught structurally not by blind playtest). (3) The **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge). (4) The true-goal pivot: run the loop once against a REAL frontier author model (docs/ULTRAPLAN-2026-06-02.md). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — wreckers_light is now freshly attended (seed 11).

### Cycle result — content_new: The Alchemist's Tower gains a NON-DEATH moral fork — the iron key now opens the cellar OR the master's Great Work, finally giving the last linear pack a real choice (bug_0114, content_new, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on alchemists_tower (parser, seed 41): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T10-21-17-693Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent ran two playthroughs: **WON ending_cured 40/40** (full brew→cure route) and reached **ending_poisoned** (drink the black phial). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks, or stale text; every reactive room/object text (cut herb bed, sprung locks, drained cauldron, master regaining colour), the key/brew dependency chain, and scoring all verified in sync; determinism held. I rotated the mandated pass onto the pack I intended to improve (the protocol's "let the findings inform the fix" + "rotate the pack"); the assessor's rank-1 was clockwork_heist, but it is ~30×-blind-clean — diminishing returns — while alchemists_tower was the open, evidence-backed content target.
- **The finding (report §3/§5/§6, the SAME knock every blind report on this pack raises, scored harder this seed):** *"competent and coherent but short and very linear; the puzzle solves itself once you read two descriptions, with no real choice or misdirection beyond the obvious poison trap … more of a guided vignette than a puzzle with branching … caps replay value."* Root cause: bug_0111 made the spire a "decision," but BOTH its branches are DEATHS (drink the phial yourself → ending_poisoned; dose the master with it → ending_master_poisoned), so the pack still had exactly ONE satisfying outcome. Across the loop the linear parser packs cap at enjoyment 3–4 while the branching packs (clockwork 4–5, wreckers_light 5) and the now-branched sealed_crypt (bug_0105: 3→4) rate higher. alchemists_tower was the LAST linear pack without a real, non-death choice — and the 09-08 pass had already declared intent to branch it, but the loop drifted onto other packs (bug_0112/0113) and never executed it.
- **The improvement (content_new — the proven bug_0099/bug_0105 lever, applied where the blind evidence demanded it):** the iron key the player already carries (it opened the cellar hatch) becomes a **FORK**, exactly the sealed_crypt bug_0105 device — one key, two locks. A locked **silver casket** holding the alchemist's **Great Work** now sits at the dying master's deathbed in the spire, keyed to that same iron: **GIVE the antidote to the master → ending_cured** (the UNCHANGED altruistic win) — or **UNLOCK the silver casket → ending_betrayal** (a new NON-death greed ending: take the masterwork, leave her to the fever you climbed to break). The casket fires `end_game` via `unlock_effects` (the bug_0077 first-class UNLOCK the cellar hatch + strongbox already use), so the two outcomes are **MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE** — a genuine terminal choice, not a detour. A reactive casket examine (the bug_0052/0065 leak discipline): the base text never assumes the antidote (the spire stair is unconditional and the iron key can be carried up without brewing — verified live, the greed speedrun), while the has_item:antidote variant sharpens the moral weight ("to crack it now, with her cure already in your hand, is to choose the Work over the maker"). The spire room description telegraphs the casket.
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar (content-only, no engine/validator/schema change):** the **victory route is byte-identical** — the casket adds no `inc_var` (`meta.max_score` stays 40, so the perfect score still coincides with the cure per bug_0104) and no exit/flag/gating to the win path, so every prior route/score test passes unchanged (alchemists_score_victory_coincide, administer_cure_agency, master_wrong_vial_branch, use_command_verbs, spire_inventory_truth …). Validator stays green: `END_GAME_UNDECLARED` satisfied (ending_betrayal declared); `WIN_IS_DEATH` cannot fire (no win_condition resolves to betrayal, ending_cured non-death); `NO_WINNABLE_ENDING` cannot fire; the casket adds no exit ⇒ no `SOFTLOCK` risk. ending_betrayal is NON-death — the player chooses greed and survives.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 803 tests / 147 files pass** (was 791/145; +7 new cases, +1 file, plus prior); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (alchemists_tower content_hash now `cf894dbe…`). Live-verified the fork end-to-end through the MCP server: the greed route (iron key → spire → unlock silver casket) fires **ending_betrayal**, non-death (`death:false`), score 0/40, mutually exclusive with the cure. New regression `tests/regression/alchemists_masterwork_fork.test.ts` (7 cases, live engine + buildObservation): the fork is REAL (at the spire after brewing, holding the iron key, BOTH `use_antidote_on_master` and `unlock_silver_casket` legal in the same state); unlocking the casket ends in ending_betrayal (non-death), master never cured, narration names the Great Work; ending_betrayal reached ONLY by end_game and ending_cured stays the sole winnable win; the casket needs the iron key (spire-without-key offers examine, not unlock); the casket examine is reactive (antidote bearer vs greed route); the canonical route still wins ending_cured 40/40. **Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change** (alchemists_tower is NOT protected and NO test pins its hash — confirmed, as bug_0093/0103/0104/0111 all recorded — so the hash moves with nothing to re-pin). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0114_alchemists_masterwork_fork.yaml`. No engine/validator/schema change; no existing content edited beyond the spire's telegraph text; no other pack's hash moved.
- **Next suggested focus:** with bug_0114, **every linear pack in the loop now offers a real, non-death choice at its climax** (sealed_crypt bug_0105, alchemists_tower bug_0114), and both RPG packs make prep decisive + prepared play reliable (bug_0101/0102/0113) — the recurring "linear / no real choice" and "prep is cosmetic" knocks are now resolved across the board. (1) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where prep is *mandatory*) is now the largest deferred STRUCTURAL item — the natural next step beyond content polish. (2) The **scoring-coincidence audit / validator WARNING** (flag "max_score reachable before any win_condition fires" — the bug_0104 generalization). (3) The **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge). (4) A second content_new branching pack to grow breadth. Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — alchemists_tower is now freshly attended and newly branching. NOTE: content polish is exhausted; the next high-value moves are STRUCTURAL (engine/validator), not more pack tweaks — resist churning blind-clean packs.

### Cycle result — content_fix/balance: The Sunken Barrow's shade ward is now +3 (was +2), so PREPARED play RELIABLY survives the wight — the complementary half of bug_0102 (bug_0113, content only, wight untouched)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on the assessor's rank-1 target — sunken_barrow (rpg, seed 11): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T09-48-19-753Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent ran the brief's explicit prepared-vs-under-armed comparison across 3 playthroughs and reached **all three endings** (ending_victory 50/50, ending_fallen, ending_woken 25/50). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, or soft-locks; reactive room/object text, the might-check retry loop, dialogue topic retirement, flee, and scoring all verified in sync; determinism held.
- **The finding (report §4 AND §5, raised twice — the marquee soft spot):** the COMBAT MARGIN on the *prepared* route. Even with the recommended preparation (the shade's ward, defense 4) the tester won at **exactly 3 HP on two separate seeded runs** and flagged the fight as razor-thin: *"an unlucky early run of high wight rolls can kill a fully-prepared, 'correct play' player … the fight outcome leans on luck more than tactics — there's no heal, no consumable, only attack or flee."* Its verdict: *"Tightening that margin would take it from a good short pack to an excellent one."* This is the COMPLEMENTARY edge to bug_0102: that cycle made the wight lethal so prep would *decide* the fight (under-armed ~43% win), but at the +2 ward the prepared route won only **~70%** of seeds — a third of correct-play runs DIED. Prep decided the fight but did not make doing everything right reliably survivable.
- **The fix (content only — raise the shade's ward +2 → +3 defense; NOTHING else):** the reaver-shade's `inc_var defense by 2 → by 3` (defense 2→5), and ONLY the ward — **the wight (hp22/atk5/def2) is untouched**, so the under-armed gamble stays exactly the bug_0102 ~43% lethal one and the prep gap WIDENS (43% vs 88%). The wight deals `d6+5−playerDef (min 1)`, so +1 defense shaves a point off every blow over the ~4-round fight. Verified live over seeds 1-40 on the REAL routes (`__probe3.ts`, throwaway, step-faithful ward modelling): **WARDED 28/40 → 35/40 (~70% → ~88%), avg survivor HP 5.4 → 7.5, the ≤3-HP nail-biters more than halved (10 → 6); UNDER-ARMED unchanged at 17/40 (~43%).** Still NO guaranteed win (~12% of warded seeds lose, recoverable via save §8.7), so the pack keeps the tension its "careful play isn't free" identity demands — this is the OPPOSITE of the old "trivially safe" complaint, not a return to it. Updated the journal line "(+2 defense)" → "(+3 defense)" and the reaver_shade + barrow_wight design comments.
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar (and is NOT undoing bug_0102):** the validator's `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE` proof credits the player's BEST REACHABLE stats; raising the ward only INCREASES reachable defense (now def5), so the fight is strictly MORE provably winnable (worst wight blow now `d6+5−5 ≤ 6 < 20` reachable HP). A bigger ward can only REMOVE the (already-absent) unwinnable risk, never add one. bug_0102 made under-armed lethal (kept, wight untouched); bug_0113 makes prepared reliable — the two halves of "prep is the difference between death and a confident win," each driven by a distinct blind finding (under-armed-was-cosmetic vs. prepared-was-razor-thin). Coherent progression, not churn.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 145 files / 791 tests pass** (was 785; +3 new, plus prior); verify:integrity static 0/0; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (sunken_barrow content_hash now `80ab4154…`). New regression `tests/regression/rpg_barrow_ward_reliable_survival.test.ts` (3 cases, live engine): the ward is +3 (defense 2→5, journals "+3 defense"); RELIABLE + DECISIVE over seeds 1-40 on the real routes (prepared wins ≥33/40 AND ≥2× under-armed, which stays ≤20/40); and **the +1 is LOAD-BEARING** — at seed 6 the warded route (def5) wins ending_victory 50/50 but the SAME seeded route with defense forced to 4 (the old +2 ward) is killed → ending_fallen. **Deliberate, surfaced re-pins** (accompanying the content change, the allowed-warning case): `traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json` re-recorded along the UNCHANGED warded action sequence (attacks-to-kill is defense-independent) — content_hash `c187324c…→80ab4154…`, expected_final_hash `e55b00eb…→aa5f8649…` (seed 1 still wins 50/50, larger HP cushion); `rpg_barrow_reaver_shade.test.ts` ward assertions repointed (defense 2→5, journal "+3 defense"); `rpg_barrow_wight_prep_matters.test.ts` case (4) post-ward `defense` 4→5 (the bug_0102 prep-decides property is unchanged — warded seed 2 still wins). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0113_barrow_ward_reliable_survival.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) BOTH RPG packs now make prep DECISIVE *and* prepared play RELIABLE (sunken_barrow bug_0102+0113; cold_forge bug_0101 — worth a confirming blind pass to check cold_forge's prepared margin isn't also razor-thin, applying this same +ward lever if so). (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where prep is *mandatory*) remains the largest deferred structural item — bug_0113 shows the winnability proof handles a hard, prep-gated, prepared-reliable fight. (3) **content_new / branching** retrofit onto a linear parser pack (alchemists_tower is the remaining enjoyment-below-branching pack). (4) the tester's §5 second note (the +3 ward is gated behind the *wight* dialogue topic, not the *lord* topic) is low-impact — the root re-offers ask_wight, so a careful player asks both — but a future cycle could surface the ward on either topic. Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — sunken_barrow is now freshly attended.

### Cycle result — engine_rule: a score change now NARRATES itself — the Zork "[Your score has gone up by N points; it is now M of T.]" line, completing the bug_0060 delta infrastructure (bug_0112, engine_rule/enhancement, content-free hook)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on the assessor's rank-1 target — sealed_crypt (parser, seed 7): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T09-21-43-791Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent ran TWO playthroughs: **WON `ending_victory` 35/35** (honorable route) AND exercised the bug_0105 moral fork to **`ending_plunder` 10/35** (rob the founder's coffin). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5** (the moral fork lifted it from the historical 3/5), **ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, or soft-locks; every reactive room/object text in sync; determinism held. Its single concrete, actionable finding (§4/§5) was **SCORE LEGIBILITY**: *"the HUD shows score/35 but never tells the player WHY numbers jumped or that the headstone is a missable bonus — a win at 35 vs. 30 is invisible to a first-timer mid-game."*
- **The improvement (engine_rule — complete the infrastructure bug_0060 deliberately built):** bug_0060 added a signed `delta` to `score` inc_var/dec_var events *"so a transcript narrator could surface 'points just earned'"* — but no narrator existed; the award surfaced only as a structured `state_change` event, never as prose. bug_0112 supplies the narrator. Milestone awards (read headstone +5, solve well +10, unlock gate +20) now each emit the classic IF line **"[Your score has gone up by N points; it is now M of T.]"** Two pieces: (1) a new **content-free engine hook `Rules.decorateEvents(events) → GameEvent[]`** (`src/core/engine.ts`), run LAST in `makeStep` against the step's full event list — it appends player-facing narration only and never mutates state, so determinism and the state hash are untouched; (2) **`scoreChangeNarrations(events, maxScore)`** (`src/parser/runner.ts`), the narrator: for each `score`-var change with non-zero delta it emits one line ("gone up"/"gone down", singular/plural point(s), running total "M of T"); guards — max_score 0 (CYOA/untracked) → silent, delta 0 (guardFinite reject) → silent, non-`score` var ignored. Both the **parser AND rpg runners** supply the hook, so feedback is uniform across modes; CYOA omits it.
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar (the §3 content-free engine invariant is PRESERVED):** `makeStep` never inspects which var is "score" — that knowledge lives entirely in the content layer (`parser/runner.ts`, where `SCORE_VAR` already lives) that supplies the hook. Engine CHROME derived generically from the score var + meta.max_score, exactly like the observation's existing **"Final score: X of Y." ending closure** — no per-pack authoring, NO pack content edited (every pack's bytes are byte-identical; no content hash moves). Additive/non-destructive: the structured inc_var event is unchanged and still rides alongside the new narration, so the bug_0060 delta consumers are untouched. No route/score/ending/softlock change.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 785 tests pass** (was 772; +13 new); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (no hash moved — engine-only change). New regression `tests/regression/score_change_narration.test.ts` (13 cases): the helper (gone-up/gone-down, singular "point", delta-0 silent, max_score-0 silent, non-score var ignored, multi-change → multi-line); **end-to-end through The Sealed Crypt** — each milestone (read +5 → "5 of 35", well +10 → "15 of 35", gate +20 → "35 of 35") emits exactly one score line with the inc_var event still alongside, and a non-scoring step emits none; **determinism** — two same-seed runs produce identical events and the final state hash is unchanged by decoration. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0112_score_change_narration.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** the `decorateEvents` hook is a general seam for per-turn engine chrome derived from a step's events — today it carries score feedback, future derived narration composes without further engine changes. (1) **alchemists_tower** remains the linear parser pack rating enjoyment below the branching packs — the bug_0105 fork retrofit (a climactic alternate ending / meaningful choice) is the natural next evidence-backed CONTENT move; it now also scores 40 and could exercise the new score narrator. (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss). (3) the **scoring-coincidence audit / validator WARNING** (flag "max_score reachable before any win_condition fires" — the bug_0104 generalization). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — sealed_crypt is freshly attended and now SHOWS its score awards.

### Cycle result — content_new: The Sealed Crypt gains a climactic MORAL FORK — the iron key now opens the catacombs gate OR the founder's own coffin, attacking the one enjoyment knock every blind report on this pack raised (bug_0105, content_new, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on the assessor's rank-1 target — sealed_crypt (parser, seed 53): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T07-50-34-062Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent: **WON ending_victory 35/35**, edge-probed the barred gate (no iron key → no unlock/north exit). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected actions, loops, or soft-locks; reactive room/object text all in sync. Its ONLY substantive note is the SAME signal every blind report on this pack (and on the linear packs generally) keeps raising: *"short and entirely linear with a single ending … its only real limitation is scope — it leaves you wanting more breadth (branching, a second puzzle, or a meaningful choice), which is why enjoyment sits at 3 despite flawless execution."* Across the loop the linear parser packs cap at enjoyment 3 while the branching packs (clockwork 4–5, wreckers_light 5) rate higher.
- **The improvement (content_new — the standing #1 next-focus, "retrofit a branch onto a linear pack like sealed_crypt"):** rather than re-polish an already-clean pack, I added a climactic **moral fork**. The founder's own coffin (`founders_coffin`) now sits in the crypt under an iron clasp keyed — like the catacombs gate — to **the iron key the player already carries**. The iron key becomes a FORK, not just a key, and the headstone's *"THE CRYPT YIELDS ONLY TO IRON"* gains a darker second reading: **UNLOCK the gate** → press on to the resting place → `ending_victory` (the unchanged canonical win); or **UNLOCK the founder's coffin** → take its silver reliquary and leave, the dead robbed → **`ending_plunder`** (a new non-death greed ending). Both fire through the engine's first-class UNLOCK (the **bug_0077** path the gate already uses): the coffin carries `unlock_narrate` + `unlock_effects: [end_game: ending_plunder]`. Because plunder fires by `end_game`, the two endings are **mutually exclusive** — a genuine terminal choice (the sunken_barrow `ending_woken` shape), not a detour. The crypt's base + open-gate `variant` text now telegraph the sealed founder's coffin; the coffin's own examine telegraphs the silver and the moral weight.
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar (content-only, no engine/validator/schema change):** the **victory route is byte-identical** — the coffin adds no `inc_var` (`meta.max_score` stays 35) and no exit/flag/gating to the win path, so **every prior route/score test passes unchanged** (sealed_crypt_scoring, ending_score_summary, parser_acceptance, sealed_crypt_final_step_legibility). Validator stays green: `END_GAME_UNDECLARED` satisfied (ending_plunder declared); `WIN_IS_DEATH` cannot fire (no win_condition resolves to plunder, ending_victory non-death); `NO_WINNABLE_ENDING` cannot fire; no new exit ⇒ no `SOFTLOCK` risk.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 738 tests / 137 files pass** (was 732/136; +6 new, +1 file); verify:integrity + typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (sealed_crypt content_hash now `0ff57a59…`). New regression `tests/regression/sealed_crypt_plunder_fork.test.ts` (6 cases, live engine + buildObservation): the fork is REAL (at the crypt with the iron key BOTH `unlock_crypt_gate` and `unlock_founders_coffin` are legal in the same state); unlocking the coffin ends in `ending_plunder` (non-death), never reaches the catacombs, distinct from victory, narration names the silver/reliquary; ending_plunder is reached ONLY by end_game and ending_victory stays the sole winnable win; the coffin needs the iron key (crypt-without-key offers examine, not unlock); the canonical route still wins ending_victory 35/35 (no regression); the pack validates clean. **Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change** (sealed_crypt is NOT protected and NO test pins its hash — confirmed, as bug_0034/0044/0054/0077 all recorded — so the hash moves with nothing to re-pin). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0105_sealed_crypt_plunder_fork.yaml`. No existing content edited beyond the crypt's reactive text; no other pack's hash moved.
- **Next suggested focus:** sealed_crypt is now the FIRST previously-linear parser pack with a real branch — the proven enjoyment lever applied where the blind evidence demanded it. (1) **alchemists_tower** is the remaining linear parser pack rating enjoyment below the branching packs — the same fork retrofit (a climactic alternate ending / meaningful choice) is the natural next evidence-backed content move. (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss) remains the largest deferred structural item. (3) the **scoring-coincidence audit / validator WARNING** idea from bug_0104 (flag "max_score reachable before any win_condition fires"). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — sealed_crypt is now freshly attended and newly branching.

### Cycle result — The Alchemist's Tower's perfect score now COINCIDES with the win: the bug_0057 climactic cure (previously unscored) carries the final +5, max_score 35→40 (bug_0104, content_fix/balance, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED onto the least-recently-attended pack: alchemists_tower (parser, seed 89): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T07-40-46-287Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was alchemists_tower itself (the coverage bot can't solve its puzzle chain, so quality is "unverified" by the heuristic). Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent: **WON ending_cured** on the brew route; reached **ending_poisoned** (drink the black phial); edge-probed gating (pour never offered before the herb is steeped). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — no rejected commands, loops, or soft-locks; every reactive room/object text stayed in sync. ONE concrete actionable finding, raised TWICE (report §4 **and** §5).
- **The finding (report §4/§5, a marquee design nit — score peaks before the win):** *"The score hits the maximum 35/35 when you brew the antidote — one full step before the game actually ends (giving it to the master). A perfect-score-yet-unfinished state is slightly odd."* The tester's explicit suggestion: *"award the final points on administering the cure so score and victory coincide."* Root cause: the chain awarded 5 (read recipe) + 10 (steep herb) + 20 (decant antidote) = 35 = old max_score, ALL before the spire; the **bug_0057 deliberate cure** — the USE-antidote-on-master act the win turns on, the literal point of the pack — awarded **nothing**, so the most important act was the one unscored move.
- **The fix (content only — reward the climax, the bug_0098/0101 "fire the gun" move applied to a dead award):** the master's USE-antidote-on-master interaction gains a final `inc_var { name: score, by: 5 }` alongside its existing set_flag/remove_item/narrate; `meta.max_score` raised **35 → 40**. The brew now tops out at **35/40** and the perfect **40/40** is reached ONLY on the cure — score and victory coincide. Every prior award (5/10/20) is unchanged; no flag/item/exit/gating/interaction-shape/reachable-ending change, no new action, no route change. Updated the pack header + interaction design comments.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 732 tests / 136 files pass** (was 731 pre-edit; +5 new, the 1 stale hardcode now fixed); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (alchemists_tower content_hash now `447ace72…`). New regression `tests/regression/alchemists_score_victory_coincide.test.ts` (5 cases, live engine): max_score==40; standing at the spire pre-cure the score is **35, strictly BELOW max** (the regression — it used to EQUAL max here); the cure awards **+5 → exactly 40/40** at the win; the interaction carries inc_var score by 5; the pack validates green. **Test updates (surfaced, not weakened):** `alchemists_administer_cure_agency.test.ts` — bug_0057's assertion "full score in hand BEFORE the cure" *encoded the very smell* this fixes, repointed to assert the pre-cure score is strictly below max (==max-5); its administer-wins-at-full-score case is unchanged (max is now 40). Three hardcoded `35`s in win-route assertions (`alchemists_use_command_verbs`, `parser_command_verb`) switched to `pack.meta.max_score` (+ explicit 40); two stale `35/35` comments corrected (`stage3_alchemist`, `ending_score_summary`). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0104_alchemists_score_victory_coincide.yaml`.
