NO protected/HASH_PIN file touched; alchemists is not hash-pinned by any test/trace, so the content hash moves with nothing to re-pin.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the **scoring-coincidence audit could generalize** — check whether the other scoring packs (sealed_crypt 35, cold_forge 50, sunken_barrow 50) also max out before their terminal act, or whether a validator WARNING could flag "max_score reachable before any win_condition fires" so this class is caught structurally rather than by blind playtest. (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss) remains the largest deferred structural item. (3) **content_new** — branching lifts enjoyment 3→5/4→5 (wreckers_light evidence); a second branching pack or retrofitting a branch onto a linear pack (alchemists/sealed_crypt both rate enjoyment below the branching packs). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — alchemists_tower is now freshly attended.

### Cycle result — The Sunken Barrow's +2 shade ward finally DECIDES the fight: the cosmetic lever both RPG packs kept flagging is now the survival lever, under-armed is genuinely lethal (bug_0102, content_fix/balance, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the ~30×-blind-clean clockwork_heist onto the least-recently-attended pack: sunken_barrow (rpg, seed 37): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T02-14-47-663Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork AGAIN, but by blind-pass recency sunken_barrow was the oldest target (last at bug_0095; clockwork bug_0097, watchtower bug_0098, sealed/wreckers bug_0099/0100, cold_forge bug_0101). Rotating onto it both followed the protocol's "rotate the seed and pack" + project memory AND directly served **bug_0101's #1 next-focus** (apply the proven "make prep matter" pattern to sunken_barrow's cosmetic +2 ward). Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent ran the brief's explicit **under-armed vs. fully-prepared** comparison: reached **both ending_victory (prepared) and ending_woken (the doom fork)**, **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5, ZERO functional bugs**. Its §5 finding converged on the same root cause.
- **The finding (report §5, the marquee defensive lever writing a check it never cashed):** against the old **hp12/atk3/def1** wight, the +2-defense ward was mechanically near-irrelevant — the tester found the wight "dies in 2 rounds either way," warded it hit for 3, under-armed for 4, so the ward **"saved a single HP"** (finished 17/20 vs 16/20): "mechanically genuine but the fight is so short and tilted you win trivially at near-full HP regardless." (It also flagged the shade's "the strength runs out of your arm" line as a chill-drain with no live mechanic — but that is the reaver's backstory account of his OWN defeat, not a promised player stat-drain.) This is the SAME "telegraphed prep is cosmetic / careful play is trivially safe" note both RPG packs keep raising (bug_0095, cold_forge bug_0092→0101), deferred pending author intent — and under the full-trust model (memory: [[full-trust-model]]) the agent IS the author.
- **The fix (content only — the bug_0101 "fire the gun" move, applied to a dead lever):** retuned the wight **hp12/atk3/def1 → hp22/atk5/def2** — the two-stat lift (more HP → longer fight; +2 attack → harder blows) that makes the +2 ward COMPOUND over several rounds instead of shaving one HP; defense 1→2 also slows the player's own blade (atk4 vs def2 = d6+2, not d6+3) so the fight has rounds to matter. Verified live over seeds 1-40 on the REAL canonical route (`__probe2.ts`, throwaway, the exact action sequence the trace records): **UNDER-ARMED (def2) now loses on a majority of seeds** (wins 17/40 ≈ 43%, survivors scrape through at ~4 HP of 20 — the shade's cold-blow danger is finally honest); **the shade's +2 ward (def4) is the DECISIVE survival lever** (wins 28/40 ≈ 70% — a likely-dead gamble becomes a clear favourite, exactly the ward the tester called one-HP-cosmetic); 15 seeds are clean "prep decides it" cases. Prose/flags/score/endings/exits untouched; updated the header + reaver_rest + reaver_shade design comments that called the ward an "even-ish" tweak / the canonical trace "skips the shade."
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar (the second pack to exercise bug_0097):** bug_0097 widened the RPG `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE` proof to credit the player's **best reachable** stats (init + the reachable +2 ward = def4) precisely to permit a sound "gear up first" design. With hp22/atk5/def2: bestPlayerDmg=8 (atk4 vs def2), roundsToKill=ceil(22/8)=3, worstCaseDamageTaken=4 < 20 reachable HP ⇒ **provably winnable**; sunken_barrow stays a clean **0-finding** pack while base-stat play is a lethal gamble the bar never required to be safe.
- **The canonical victory trace was RE-RECORDED through the warded route (per bug_0101's "switch helpers to the canonical buffed route"):** the old trace ran the under-armed route with exactly **2 ATTACK** actions — structurally impossible against a 22-HP wight (2 hits cap at 16 dmg), so it had to be re-recorded regardless. It now runs the intended **prepared (warded)** path at seed 1: ending_victory, score 50/50, finishing at **9 HP with defense 4** (the ward visibly applied). **Deliberate, surfaced re-pins** (HASH_PIN_FILE, the allowed-warning case — a re-pin accompanying a content change, documented in the bug artifact, never silent): `traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json` content_hash 952216…→54712ef3… and expected_final_hash e6de3a9c…→e55b00eb…. Four victory-driving tests had hard-coded the old 2-attack kill or used seed 2 (now lethal under-armed): `rpg_scoring` + `ending_score_summary` switched their fixed-2-attacks to a loop-until-wight_slain (seed 1 survives under-armed); `rpg_barrow_lever_legibility` + `rpg_barrow_lever_first_heave_honest` swapped their first-lever-FAILS seed 2→3 (survives under-armed AND first lever fails). The historical bug_0056/0075/0083 artifacts that cite the old e6de3a hash are left untouched (accurate history of those cycles).
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 700 tests / 131 files pass** (was 696/130); verify:integrity + typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0** (sunken_barrow content_hash 54712ef3… matches the re-recorded trace). New regression `tests/regression/rpg_barrow_wight_prep_matters.test.ts` (4 cases, live engine): validator green (no COMBAT_UNWINNABLE); the wight carries hp22/atk5/def2; **REAL FAILURE STATE** — under-armed (skips the shade) at seed 2 dies → ending_fallen (death:true); **PREP DECIDES IT** — at the SAME seed 2, the ONLY change being a detour to the shade for its +2 ward, the fight flips death→full ending_victory 50/50. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0102_barrow_wight_real_teeth.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) **BOTH RPG packs now make their telegraphed prep decisive** (cold_forge bug_0101, sunken_barrow bug_0102) — the recurring "prep is cosmetic" note is resolved across the board. ### Cycle result — The Cold Forge's slag-sentinel finally has the TEETH its prose always promised: under-armed is now LETHAL and the spirit's +2-attack counsel is the survival lever (bug_0101, content_fix/balance, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the ~30×-blind-clean clockwork_heist onto the least-recently-attended pack: cold_forge (rpg, seed 23): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T01-55-43-557Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork AGAIN, but by blind-pass recency cold_forge was the oldest target (last at bug_0092; alchemists bug_0093, sunken bug_0095, watchtower bug_0098, sealed/wreckers bug_0099/0100). Per the protocol's "rotate the seed and pack" + project memory, the pass rotated. Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent ran the brief's explicit **under-armed vs. fully-prepared** comparison: **both** routes reached ending_victory 50/50, **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5, ZERO functional bugs**. Its findings converged on ONE root cause.
- **The finding (report §4/§5, a marquee mechanic writing a check it never cashed):** the pack has **no real failure state** and its whole preparation arc is **cosmetic**. The tester won an UNDER-ARMED run "with 9 HP to spare", found the sentinel "dies in exactly 3 hits whether I had attack 4 or attack 6" (so the lantern-spirit's +2-attack counsel "felt pointless"), and flagged the room's own warning — *"better not to meet it under-armed — face it with every edge you can bring to bear"* — as text that "writes a check the mechanics don't cash." Root cause: the old hp16/atk4 sentinel was too soft for a 20-HP player. This is the SAME "prep is cosmetic / careful play is trivially safe" note both RPG packs keep raising (sunken_barrow bug_0095), deferred since bug_0092 "pending author intent" — and under the full-trust model (memory: [[full-trust-model]]) the agent IS the author.
- **The fix (content only — FIRE the gun; the bug_0098 "make the dead flag consequential" move, applied to a dead threat):** retuned the slag_sentinel **hp16/atk4 → hp18/atk7** (defense unchanged), the two-stat change that makes every tier of prep MATTER. Verified live over seeds 1-40 (`__probe.ts`, throwaway): **UNDER-ARMED (atk4/def2) now loses on a majority of seeds** (the "don't meet it under-armed" warning is finally honest); **the spirit's +2 ATTACK (atk6/def2, on the mandatory Outer-Forge path) is the decisive survival lever** (~45%→~90% win — the buff the tester called "pointless" is now what saves you); **the founder's plate (+2 def) on top** is a near-certain win with an HP cushion (atk6/def4 won every seed tested). Prose/flags/score/endings/exits untouched — the bellows_walk warning was always honest; only the numbers lied. Updated the pack's header + founder_cell design comments that called the fight "winnable from base stats" / the plate "optional grace" to read true to the new balance.
- **Why this is WITHIN the bar (and the first pack to exercise bug_0097):** last week's bug_0097 widened the RPG `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE` proof to credit the player's **best reachable** stats (init + every reachable +atk/+def buff = atk6/def4) **precisely to permit a sound "gear up first" design**. With hp18/atk7: bestPlayerDmg=10, roundsToKill=2, worstCaseDamageTaken=4 < 20 reachable HP ⇒ **provably winnable**; cold_forge stays a clean **0-finding** pack while base-stat play is a lethal gamble the bar never required to be safe. bug_0101 is the first pack to actually use that widening.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 696 tests / 130 files pass** (was 688); verify:integrity + typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 7 packs validate 0/0**. New regression `tests/regression/cold_forge_sentinel_prep_matters.test.ts` (4 cases, live engine): validator green (no COMBAT_UNWINNABLE); the sentinel carries hp18/atk7/def2; **REAL FAILURE STATE** — under-armed (base atk4) at seed 1 dies → ending_fallen (death:true); **PREP DECIDES IT** — at the SAME seed 1, the ONLY change being the spirit's +2 attack, the fight flips death→full ending_victory 50/50. **Deliberate, surfaced re-pins** (a stat retune, not a weakening — every other guard in each test still runs): `cold_forge_sentinel_threat_telegraph.test.ts` and `cold_forge_sentinel_warmup_fiction.test.ts` each pinned the sentinel as hp16/atk4 ("stats untouched") — re-pinned to hp18/atk7; `cold_forge_grate_journal.test.ts` and `parser_item_use_command_verb.test.ts` reached the grate via an under-armed seed-1 fight (now lethal) — switched their helpers to the canonical spirit-buffed route (their concern is grate journaling / the "lever" command verb, not the fight). Deliberate cold_forge content-hash change (NOT a protected file; **no test pins its hash**, no replay trace exists — confirmed, as prior cold_forge cycles bug_0029/0048/0059/0070 all recorded). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0101_cold_forge_sentinel_real_teeth.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) **sunken_barrow's identical "prep is cosmetic" note (bug_0095): its +2 shade ward is still cosmetic** (wight is a 2-hit kill either way) — give it the SAME bug_0101 treatment now that there's a proven, validator-blessed pattern (retune the wight so the ward matters, staying winnable via best-reachable). (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss) — the largest deferred structural item; bug_0101 just demonstrated the winnability proof handles a genuinely hard, prep-gated fight. (3) **content_new** — branching lifts enjoyment 3→5 (wreckers_light evidence). (4) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — cold_forge is now freshly attended.

### Cycle result — content_new: shipped a NEW branching pack, *The Wrecker's Light*, to attack the one enjoyment knock EVERY blind report raises — linearity (bug_0099, content_new, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the ~30×-blind-clean clockwork_heist onto the OLDEST blind target among the six: sealed_crypt (parser, seed 67): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T01-16-27-596Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork AGAIN, but a last-25-runs scan showed sealed_crypt was the least-recently-targeted pack (last at bug_0090, older than alchemists bug_0093 / cold_forge bug_0092 / sunken_barrow bug_0095 / watchtower bug_0098). Per the protocol's "rotate the seed and the pack" + project memory (rotate onto the least-attended pack), the pass rotated. Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent: solved 35/35, **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5, ZERO functional bugs** (its ~3rd straight clean pass; every gate holds, no softlock). Its substantive note is the SAME signal every blind report across the six packs keeps raising: *"short and entirely linear with a single ending; the puzzle never branches or surprises … replay value is essentially nil."* The linear packs (sealed_crypt 3/5, alchemists_tower 3/5) consistently rate BELOW the branching clockwork (4–5/5).
- **The improvement (content_new — the standing top next-focus, grow the game):** rather than re-polish a pack that is already clean, authored a NEW branching CYOA pack, **`content/cyoa/pack/wreckers_light.yaml` — The Wrecker's Light**. A storm night at a dark lighthouse, a ship foundering on the rocks, the keeper collapsed at the stair. Its THESIS is a MORAL fork, not a lock-and-key chain: the SAME two tools (lamp_oil from the store + the keeper's striker from the mantel) arm BOTH the honest act and its dark mirror, so it is WHERE you carry them that decides the night — **four distinct endings**: up the ladder relights the great lamp and saves the ship (`ending_saved`); out to the gallery hangs a false low light that wrecks her to plunder (`ending_wrecker`); staying at the dying keeper's side loses the ship but saves a life (`ending_mercy`); climbing down to the rocks too soon drowns you (`ending_drowned`, an informed risk telegraphed by the prose). A hidden truth REFRAMES rather than adds a route: the keeper's iron key opens the oil-store sea-chest, whose journal names the foundering ship as the cutter *Mourning Star* — the vessel that drove his own son onto these rocks, her master now at the wheel. Reading it sets `knows_truth`, firing reactive `endingText` variants (the clockwork ending_truth device, bug_0051) on the save, wreck AND mercy endings: saving becomes mercy past justice, wrecking becomes vengeance not greed, mercy CONFIRMS the grief instead of re-revealing it. No deadline — branching IS the feature.
- **The NEW pack's own blind playtest (protocol requires it for content_new — seed 71):** fresh source-blind MCP-only subagent, 5 sessions, all FOUR endings + the informed/uninformed variants. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5 — the project's highest enjoyment score to date** — ZERO functional bugs, no loops/rejected actions, reactive scene text (bare mantel, dipped cask, gaping chest) and gating all consistent. It flagged ONE polish gap (ending_mercy re-revealed the truth to an informed player); **fixed in-cycle** by adding the knows_truth-aware mercy variant ("he sees that you already know … He does not have to tell it"). The same blind evidence that diagnosed the linearity knock now confirms branching+reactive-prose lifts enjoyment 3→5.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 688 tests / 128 files pass** (was 681); verify:integrity + typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all SEVEN packs validate 0/0** (new pack hash `9302ddbe…`). Wired into the bar: added to the `health` validate chain in package.json, and the discovery guard `tests/regression/all_packs_validated_by_bar.test.ts` (bug_0096) auto-covers it (its sanity list updated six→seven) and would FAIL if it didn't validate clean or wasn't in the chain. New regression `tests/regression/wreckers_light_branching.test.ts` (6 cases, live engine + buildObservation→ending_id/endingText): all four endings reachable + distinct; the moral fork is real (identical oil+striker prefix reaches saved via the lantern, wrecker via the gallery); knows_truth fires a reactive variant on save/wreck/mercy while the uninformed route renders base text; and the variant is COSMETIC — informed/uninformed converge on the same endingId per ending. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0099_wreckers_light_branching_pack.yaml`. No engine/validator/schema change; no existing content edited (all prior hashes unchanged; no re-pin).
- **Next suggested focus:** the game now has **7 packs**, the first authored expressly against the linearity knock and the highest-rated to date. (1) **content_new again** — branching demonstrably raises enjoyment, so a second branching pack (or retrofitting a branch/alternate ending onto a linear one like sealed_crypt) is now an evidence-backed direction. (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where the RPG packs' telegraphed +2 prep finally matters, exercising the bug_0097 winnability proof) — the largest deferred structural item. (3) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle; wreckers_light is now itself in the rotation.

### Cycle result — The Watchtower Road's lit signal beacon finally PAYS OFF: its dead `raised_alarm` flag now fires reactive variants on every ending it reaches (bug_0098, content_fix/quest_structure, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the ~30×-blind-clean clockwork_heist onto the single most-neglected pack: watchtower_road (seed 19, + strategy runs 42/7/99): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T01-02-26-045Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork_heist AGAIN, but a scan of the last 40 ai-runs showed clockwork played ~30 times and **watchtower_road NOT ONCE** (last content touch bug_0062; we are at bug_0098). Per `docs/blind_playtest_protocol.md`'s "rotate the seed and the pack" and project memory (rotate onto the least-attended pack), the pass rotated onto The Watchtower Road — the original main CYOA, candidate #2 on the assessor's list. Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent: reached **all three endings** (truth/escape/captured), **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** (every gate holds: lantern→cellar/beacon, letter→checkpoint, proof→expose; no dead ends; turn-back routes back to gather proof). ONE concrete actionable finding.
- **The finding (report §1/§5, a Chekhov's-gun dead flag):** tower_top's `light_beacon` sets the flag `raised_alarm` and routes to `signal_fire`, whose prose narrates *"a horn answers. Help — or trouble — is coming."* But a whole-repo grep confirmed `raised_alarm` was set in exactly ONE place and **read in NONE** — a player who lit the beacon and one who never climbed the tower reached the same Edge of Town and the same epilogues, so the loud promise never cashed. The tester flagged it directly: the beacon "promises a consequence that never materializes differently from the un-alarmed path." Same over-promise / urgency-writes-a-check-the-engine-never-pays class the clockwork bug_0061 fought.
- **The fix (content only — FIRE the gun, don't defuse it):** rather than mute the signal_fire prose (the bug_0061 move, taken there only because that clock had no teeth yet), each of the three endings the beacon route can reach now carries a reactive `raised_alarm` variant (engine `endingText`, declared-order first-match-wins — the SAME reactive-ending machinery as clockwork's ending_truth, bug_0051), so the watch the player summoned actually shows up in the epilogue: as **help** (ending_truth — the watch is already converging on the cellar when you reach the magistrate), as **cover** (ending_escape — you slip the other way in the confusion), and as **the noose you tightened yourself** (ending_captured — the roused watch is why you can't force the gate). The base text is the un-alarmed fallback, unchanged; `signal_fire`'s own prose is untouched (it was always honest setup — only the payoff was missing). `raised_alarm` co-occurs with all three endings, so all three get a variant. No scene/choice/route/gating change and no new ending; the beacon-lighter reaches the SAME endingId as the un-alarmed player (a dead flag becomes a consequential choice).
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 681 tests / 127 files pass** (was 676); verifier-integrity static 0/0; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 6 packs validate 0/0**. New regression `tests/regression/watchtower_beacon_alarm_payoff.test.ts` (5 cases, live engine + `buildObservation`→`endingText` the way the MCP server renders): lighting the beacon sets `raised_alarm` (unset otherwise); each ending renders an alarm epilogue containing "beacon" on the beacon route and its base text (no "beacon") off it; and the variant is **cosmetic** — beacon and no-beacon routes converge on the same endingId for all three endings, proving no route/gate/reachable-ending changed. **Content-hash re-pin (deliberate, surfaced):** the YAML edit changed the watchtower pack hash, re-pinned in BOTH the canonical HASH_PIN_FILE `traces/bugs/bug_0002_watchtower_blind_polish.yaml` and the compile-pin assertion in `tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts` (4c971d43…→95cf3665…); this is the "re-pin ACCOMPANIED by a content change" case the drift machinery (verifier_integrity.test.ts) classifies as an allowed warning, not an error. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0098_watchtower_beacon_alarm_payoff.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** watchtower_road is now blind-clean and its last dead flag is consequential — **all six packs have current clean blind passes again**. The standing structural items remain the highest value: (1) the **dormant-enemy engine feature** (a woken Barrow-Lord opt-in optional boss where sunken_barrow's +2 shade ward / cold_forge's prep finally MATTERS for a genuinely hard fight, exercising the bug_0097-unified winnability proof and answering both RPG packs' "telegraphed prep is cosmetic" note); (2) **content_new** — author a 7th pack and blind-playtest it, now that the bar gates all 6 (bug_0096); (3) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle — clockwork is heavily over-attended; watchtower was the gap and is now closed.

### Cycle result — the RPG combat-winnability proof now credits the player's BEST REACHABLE stats, not init alone (bug_0097, engine/validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (seed 97, + 42/7/13): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T00-53-10-360Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only source-blind subagent. Reached **four endings** (The Truth via the hidden AND the displayed letter, Rich, Caught), **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — clockwork's ~29th straight clean blind pass. Its two notes (the watchman/ledger tension is cosmetic on the efficient path; the crawlspace route is a shorter, asymmetric experience) are the author-intentional "careful play is safe" non-defects the bug_0061..0084 reactive arc and prior cycles already settled. With the blind target clean and the content-polish backlog blind-exhausted (project memory), this cycle's improvement is **structural** (the protocol's "you MAY pick a higher-value candidate").
- **Audit note (a stale next-focus item is already DONE):** the recurring "DEFERRED climactic-agency / post-action win-evaluation hook (parser/rpg win_conditions evaluated only on room entry)" that prior cycles kept queuing is **implemented** — `src/core/engine.ts` has a §8.4.5 `checkWin` hook firing after ANY action's effects, and BOTH `src/parser/runner.ts` and `src/rpg/runner.ts` provide it (`checkWin → winningEnding`), complementary to `onEnter`. A pack already lands a climactic TAKE/administer ending by adding the act's post-condition to its win_condition. So I pursued the OTHER standing structural item instead.
- **The fix (engine/validator, trust-but-verify, no content change):** the RPG validator's `COMBAT_UNWINNABLE` proof ("only a TRULY impossible fight is an error") computed winnability from **init stats alone** (`playerHp/Atk/Def` read straight off `meta.vars_init`), while the sibling `SKILL_CHECK_IMPOSSIBLE` proof right below already used a **best-reachable ceiling** (init + every positive `inc_var` targeting the skill). Inconsistent: a fight winnable only after a reachable buff — cold_forge's lantern-spirit **+2 attack** & founder's-plate **+2 defense**, sunken_barrow's reaver-shade **+2 defense** ward — would be FALSELY flagged unwinnable, blocking a sound "gear up first" design. Routed BOTH proofs through one `statCeiling(name)` = init + every positive `inc_var` across `rpgRuntimeEffects` (enemy on_defeat + skill_check on_success/on_failure) + `allParserEffects` (room on_enter, object interactions, NPC dialogue). Over-approximating player power is the SOUND direction for "only flag a truly impossible fight": it can only REMOVE false positives, never add one. A negative `inc_var` (debuff) is ignored via `Math.max(0, by)`, so the bar is never lowered. Removed the now-redundant `skillCeiling` (identical body). No schema/runner/engine/content change.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` **exit 0; 676 tests / 126 files pass** (was 663); verify:integrity static OK; drift-vs-HEAD OK (1 surfaced warning: `VERIFIER_TOUCHED` for editing — not deleting — the validator, the legitimate workflow per AGENTS.md); typecheck + ESLint + Prettier clean; **all 6 packs validate 0/0** (no content edited, all content hashes unchanged). New regression `tests/regression/rpg_combat_winnable_with_reachable_buff.test.ts` (5 cases): bug-first — the SAME brute (hp30/atk3 vs player hp10/atk1) is flagged COMBAT_UNWINNABLE with no buff and NOT flagged (and validates fully green) once a reachable +6-attack on_enter buff is credited; soundness — a +1 buff (still impossible) stays flagged, a -5 debuff is never credited, and sunken_barrow/cold_forge stay 0-finding. The existing `rpg_unwinnable` unit fixture (no buffs → ceiling = init) still yields COMBAT_UNWINNABLE. Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0097_rpg_combat_winnability_reachable_stats.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord as an opt-in optional boss where sunken_barrow's +2 shade ward / cold_forge's prep finally MATTERS for a genuinely hard fight — directly exercising the winnability proof just unified and answering both RPG packs' "telegraphed prep is cosmetic" note); (2) **content_new** — author a 7th pack (a second parser or rpg adventure) and blind-playtest it, growing the game now that the bar gates all 6 (bug_0096); (3) the **verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot** (wants an LLM-judge). Keep rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle.

### Cycle result — every shipped pack is now inside the verification bar (bug_0096, engine_fix, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran ON the assessor's rank-1 target — clockwork_heist, seed 88 (a fresh seed): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T00-45-56-977Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose MCP-only subagent, no source access. Reached **4 distinct endings** (Rich, The Truth ×2 flag-driven prose variants `letter_hidden`/`letter_displayed`, Caught), **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO bugs** — clockwork's ~28th straight clean blind pass. Two minor observations, both explicitly NON-defects: the `ticks` time-pressure is atmospheric (no enforced turn-limit fail for a leisurely player), and the study ledger is effectively optional (you can cross+loot the vault pre-chime without it). Flavor-vs-mechanics quibbles, not bugs. With clockwork re-confirmed content-complete and the content-polish backlog blind-exhausted (project memory), this cycle's improvement is **structural**, per the protocol's "you MAY pick a higher-value candidate."
- **The defect (repo audit of the bar itself):** `npm run health` — the enforced verification bar (AGENTS.md trust-but-verify; the project memory's "automated verification stays the enforced bar") — ran content validation from a **hand-listed** `npm run validate --` chain that had drifted behind the content. It validated only **4 of the 6** shipped packs: watchtower_road, sealed_crypt, sunken_barrow, cold_forge. The other two — **clockwork_heist (cyoa) and alchemists_tower (parser) — were NEVER validated by health.** Both validate clean today (hashes 65cd79a6… / c12f7a7c…), but a future schema/soundness regression in either, or any new pack added without remembering to extend the string, would pass the bar undetected. Per-pack regression tests cover specific scenes; nothing asserted that EVERY pack still validates, nor that the bar even looks at it.
- **The fix (engine/repo, trust-but-verify):**
  1. `package.json` — added the two omitted packs to the `health` validate chain; all 6 shipped packs are now validated by the bar.
  2. `tests/regression/all_packs_validated_by_bar.test.ts` — a new guard that closes the hole by **discovery, not a hand list**, so it can't drift again: (1) every pack under `content/{cyoa,parser,rpg}/pack/*.yaml` loads and validates with **zero errors** (mode auto-detected exactly as `bin/validate.ts` does); (2) the `health` script contains a `validate --` step for every discovered pack. Adding a new pack now FAILS this guard until it both validates clean AND is wired into health.
  - Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0096_all_packs_in_verification_bar.yaml`.
- **Verification:** `npm run health` green (exit 0); the new guard is 8/8; **proved it has teeth** — removing clockwork from health makes the coverage assertion fail with "health is missing a 'validate -- …clockwork_heist.yaml' step", restoring it passes. Drift guard vs HEAD clean (no protected file deleted, no test disabled, no hash-pin changed; package.json is neither). No content or engine behavior changed — this only widens what the bar checks.
- **Next suggested focus:** the content backlog is blind-exhausted and the bar now gates all 6 packs. Two structural directions remain: (a) **content_new** — author a 7th pack (e.g. a second parser or rpg adventure) to grow the game, then blind-playtest it; or (b) **engine feature** — e.g. extend the RPG combat-winnability proof to account for the player's *best reachable* attack/defense (it currently uses init stats only), or add a turn-limit/deadline mechanic to the parser/rpg engines (the clockwork tester noted CYOA-style time pressure that the parser/rpg packs can't yet enforce). Continue rotating the mandated blind seed/pack each cycle.

### Cycle result — the Sunken Barrow's sarcophagus examine stops promising a might-check it never runs (bug_0095, content_fix/hint_text, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the ~27×-blind-clean clockwork_heist onto the never-blind-combat-tested RPG pack: sunken_barrow (seed 5): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T00-34-06-096Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork_heist AGAIN, but it has had ~27 straight clean blind passes while **sunken_barrow is the one pack that had NEVER had a blind LLM combat playtest** (cold_forge got its first in bug_0092; sunken_barrow had not appeared in the last 40 runs). Per `docs/blind_playtest_protocol.md`'s "rotate the seed and rotate the pack", the standing next-focus, and the project memory (rotate onto the least-attended packs), the pass was rotated. The coverage bot can't solve this pack at all — it stalls at `guard_crypt` (combat), reaching only 3/6 rooms — so its quality was genuinely unverified. Fresh general-purpose MCP-only subagent, no source access: reached **BOTH `ending_victory` (peaceful, 50/50) AND `ending_woken` (the doom fork)**, plus edge-case probing (retreat from the wight, drop/retake the bar — no softlock). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; ZERO functional bugs, no loops, no soft-locks.** Combat math, the +2-defense shade ward, the slab might-check, dialogue topic retirement, exit gating, scoring, and both endings all verified clean. ONE concrete actionable finding.
- **The finding (report §4/§5, content/hint_text):** the `sealed sarcophagus`'s examine read *"Prising the lid would want the iron bar and a stubborn back, **the same as the slab did**."* That explicitly promises the SLAB's mechanic — a might `skill_check` (DC 12) whose own prose says it "may give on the first heave or take several" — but the `prise` interaction carries **NO skill_check**: it fires `end_game: ending_woken` instantly on the first use, with no roll and no "stubborn back" struggle. The tester flagged the examine/action mismatch directly. Text writing a check the engine never cashes is exactly the over-promise this pack's honesty discipline fights (bug_0027/0029, and the slab's OWN bug_0069 reword that stopped over-selling a grind).
- **The fix (content only, examine-only):** reworded the `sarcophagus` `description` so it no longer claims parity with the slab. It STILL names the iron bar (genuinely required — `item: iron_bar` on the interaction) and repeats the don't-wake warning (so the act stays informed, never a gotcha), but now reads true to the terminal act: *"the iron bar would set to the leaded seams readily enough — but this is no patient slab to be worked loose by degrees: one wrench of the lid and the deed is done, past all undoing."* The `prise` interaction itself is UNCHANGED. No flag/score/item/exit/interaction/gating/reachable-ending change.
- **The OTHER finding deliberately NOT acted on:** the tester also noted the shade's +2 defense buff is effectively cosmetic (the wight is a 2-hit kill dealing ~4-5/turn either way). This is the SAME author-intentional "careful play is safe" balance stance that **last cycle (bug_0092) explicitly DEFERRED for cold_forge pending author intent** — a soft tuning observation, not a defect, and not this cycle's one focused change.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` exit 0 — verify:integrity + ESLint/Prettier OK, **663 tests / 124 files pass**, all 4 health-gated packs validate 0/0. New regression `tests/regression/rpg_barrow_sarcophagus_examine_mismatch.test.ts` (3 cases, live `rules.resolve` LOOK + `enumerateRpgActions`): examine names the bar + warning but NOT "the same as the slab"/"stubborn back" and signals the one-shot act; the prise interaction still has no skill_check and ends the run at `ending_woken` instantly; examine is cosmetic and the peaceful route still wins `ending_victory` 50/50. **Content-hash re-pin (deliberate, surfaced):** the YAML edit changed the pack hash, so `traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json` `content_hash` was re-pinned 7cadeb…→952216… (the `expected_final_hash` is unchanged — examine prose isn't part of end-state hashing, replay still green). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0095_barrow_sarcophagus_examine_mismatch.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** sunken_barrow is now blind-clean too — **all six packs have current clean blind passes**. The highest-value remaining items, per the standing backlog: (1) the recurring DEFERRED **climactic-agency / post-action win-evaluation hook** (parser/rpg win_conditions evaluated only on room entry — e.g. alchemists `use_antidote_on_master`, sunken_barrow's auto-claim circlet) — the top STRUCTURAL engine item; (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord as an opt-in optional boss where the shade's +2 ward finally pays off — directly addresses BOTH packs' "telegraphed prep is cosmetic" note by giving the prep a real fight to matter for); (3) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge).

### Cycle result — the cauldron now teaches the two-step brew ORDER in The Alchemist's Tower (bug_0093, content_fix/hint_text, no engine change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the ~27×-blind-clean clockwork_heist onto the least-attended parser pack: alchemists_tower (seeds 11/7/3): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T00-13-20-449Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork_heist AGAIN, but it has had 27+ independent clean blind passes in the last ~40 ai-runs while the parser packs are the least-attended — **alchemists_tower had NOT appeared in the last 40 runs**. Per `docs/blind_playtest_protocol.md`'s "rotate the seed and rotate the pack" and the project memory ("rotate the pass onto the least-attended packs"), the pass was rotated onto The Alchemist's Tower. Fresh general-purpose MCP-only subagent, no source access: **WON `ending_cured` 35/35** on the intended brew route; reached `ending_poisoned` on the trap route; **verified the wrong-item guard** (the poison cannot be delivered as the cure). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected commands, no loops, no soft-locks.** Key/lock chain, two-step brew gate, scoring (5/10/20→35), and both endings all verified clean. ONE concrete actionable finding.
- **The finding (report §4, legibility/hint_text):** standing in the lab with the pale herb but no water yet, the recipe reads *"the pale herb steeped in clear water"* as a SINGLE combined state, while the mechanic is two ORDERED steps — steep the herb FIRST, then pour in the cellar's clear spring water. The cauldron's pre-herb examine was the static *"A simmering cauldron, waiting for ingredients"*, giving no hint of the sequence, so a careful reader couldn't tell whether the cauldron's OWN simmering liquid already counted as the recipe's clear water or whether the cellar vial was still needed. The tester explicitly suggested the cauldron examine "could hint at the two-step sequence."
- **The fix (content only):** added ONE reactive `variant` to the `cauldron` object, gated on `read_recipe` (the flag the spellbook READ sets), listed AFTER the antidote_brewed/herb_added variants so it fires ONLY in the pre-herb decision moment (first match wins). It names the cauldron's simmer as NOT the recipe's clear water and spells out the two-step order. Gating on `read_recipe` keeps the **bug_0052 leak lesson** — a library-skipper sees the intrinsic static base, never a quoted recipe. Examine-only: no flag/item/score/exit/interaction/gating/reachable-ending change. The cauldron's reactive-variants feature (bug_0023/0039) already existed; the pre-herb state was simply the one tier it never covered.
- **Verify (the bar):** `npm run health` exit 0 — verifier-integrity OK, ESLint+Prettier OK, **656 tests / 122 files pass**, all 6 packs validate OK. New regression `tests/regression/alchemists_cauldron_brew_order_hint.test.ts` (4 cases, live `resolveParserAction` LOOK path): reader two-step hint + simmer-isn't-clear-water cue; non-reader keeps the static base and never says "recipe"; first-match `herb_added` beats the hint; brew route still wins `ending_cured` 35/35. UPDATED `alchemists_cauldron_reactive_brew.test.ts`: its pre-herb assertion used a recipe-reader route, now repointed to the two-step hint (three-state invariant preserved, static base now asserted by the bug_0093 non-reader case — surfaced, not weakened). Locked by `traces/bugs/bug_0093_alchemists_cauldron_brew_order_hint.yaml`.
- **Next suggested focus:** alchemists_tower is now blind-clean too. The two remaining least-attended packs to rotate the blind pass onto next are **sealed_crypt (parser)** and **sunken_barrow (rpg)** — both puzzle-heavy with low coverage-bot ending reach. Alternatively, the recurring DEFERRED climactic-agency note (alchemists `use_antidote_on_master` / sunken_barrow auto-taken circlet — parser/rpg win_conditions evaluated only on room entry) remains the highest-value STRUCTURAL engine item per the content-backlog-exhausted memory: a post-action win-evaluation hook would let several packs land a deliberate final "administer/claim" beat instead of an auto-fire on entry.

### Cycle result — provably-dead terminals no longer mask a SOFTLOCK: the soft-lock escape graph excludes them in BOTH validators (bug_0092, engine/validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the content-complete clockwork onto the never-blind-tested RPG mode: cold_forge (seeds 13 & 7): report `ai-runs/2026-06-02T00-03-55-507Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork_heist again (~27 clean passes), but the standing next-focus #3 + the project memory both call to rotate the pass onto the least-attended packs, and **RPG mode had NEVER had a blind LLM combat playtest**. Fresh general-purpose MCP-only subagent, no source access: BOTH runs reached the victory ending **50/50** — one optimal, one deliberately under-armed (no spirit buff, no plate) — **ZERO functional bugs, ZERO soft-locks, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5**. Combat math, items, flags, quest stages, scoring, and the bug_0088 smith's-hammer object all verified clean. Its one note (the sentinel is beatable under-armed, so the telegraphed prep is mechanically optional) is a soft balance/tuning observation, not a defect — the same author-intentional "careful play is safe" stance the clockwork bug_0061..0084 arc settled.
- **Why validator work (off-list, justified):** the blind target had no fault, the standing memory records the content-polish backlog exhausted, AND **last cycle's (bug_0091) next-focus #1 queued this verbatim**: *"EXCLUDE [unsatisfiable win_conditions] (and unfireable CYOA deadlines) from the soft-lock escape graph so a real SOFTLOCK behind a dead terminal is no longer masked (the unsoundness bug_0087/0091 only surface, not yet fix)."* This is the first cycle to ACT on the dead-terminal detection inside the soundness graph itself.
- **The bug (a real validator miss, the worst class):** both validators verify "every reachable scene/room can still reach a win/ending" via reverse-reachability over an escape graph — and both **polluted that graph with PROVABLY-DEAD terminals**, so a region whose only way out was a dead terminal read as escapable when it is in fact soft-locked. (1) **parser/RPG:** `winRooms` was populated from EVERY win_condition's `visited` room, even one whose `conditions` are internally contradictory (UNSATISFIABLE_CONDITION, bug_0091) and can never fire. (2) **CYOA:** a scene writing a deadline-watched var got an escape edge to `meta.deadline.ending` even when the deadline can PROVABLY never fire (DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE, bug_0087). **Both sites already carried an in-code comment admitting the gap** ("could mask a true SOFTLOCK" / "that edge is a phantom"); bug_0087/0091 only surfaced the dead terminal (a warning) — it still swallowed a real soft-lock behind it.
- **Improvement (engine/validator, no content change):** exclude provably-dead terminals from the escape graph in both validators. `parser_validator.ts`: skip a win_condition's `visited`-room contribution to `winRooms` when `isUnsatisfiable(whenProfile(wc.conditions))` (reusing the in-file bug_0085/0086 machinery; the independent ENDING_UNDECLARED check still runs). `cyoa_validator.ts`: `checkDeadlineFirability` now RETURNS whether the deadline is provably unfireable; that boolean is hoisted and gates the deadline escape edge in the successors loop (`&& !deadlineUnfireable`). The separate UNSATISFIABLE_CONDITION / DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE warning still names the dead terminal. Conditional & sound: a satisfiable win / fireable deadline stays a real escape edge → no spurious SOFTLOCK; the change only removes phantom edges a clean pack never has.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 652 tests pass (121 files)** (was 646); verify:integrity + typecheck + lint + format:check clean; all four health-gated packs (sealed_crypt, sunken_barrow, cold_forge, watchtower) validate 0/0, clockwork & alchemists_tower too. **No content pack edited — all content hashes unchanged.** **Bug-first verified by stashing the two source edits and re-running:** WITHOUT the fix, a parser one-way trap wing whose only "win" is contradictory, and a CYOA self-looping scene whose only escape is an unfireable deadline, are NOT flagged SOFTLOCK (masked); WITH the fix both ARE (alongside the pre-existing warning). Soundness no-false-positive: making the win satisfiable / the deadline fireable restores the edge → NO SOFTLOCK either way; the shipped packs gain NO new SOFTLOCK.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0092_softlock_dead_terminal_masking.yaml` + `tests/regression/softlock_dead_terminal.test.ts` (6 cases: parser masked→flagged + satisfiable-soundness + shipped-clean; CYOA masked→flagged + fireable-soundness + shipped-clean). This closes the soft-lock-graph half of the global-terminal family: bug_0087/0089/0090/0091 built the static detection of dead terminals; this acts on it inside the soundness graph.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss; parallels meta.deadline) — now the clear top deferred structural item, since the deadline/win-condition global-terminal family is fully bracketed AND its soft-lock unsoundness is now fixed. (2) the cold_forge **combat-difficulty tuning** the blind pass flagged — the sentinel is beatable fully under-armed, so the telegraphed spirit-buff/plate prep is mechanically optional; bumping the sentinel's HP/attack would make the prep actually matter (a content tuning, audit whether the author wants the "careful play is safe" stance here too before touching). (3) keep rotating the mandated blind pass onto the still-least-attended pack — **sunken_barrow RPG has now never had a blind combat playtest** (cold_forge just got its first). (4) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge).

### Cycle result — parser/RPG reactive variants & guards get the shadowing + unsatisfiable checks the CYOA validator already has (bug_0091, engine/validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (seed 23): report `ai-runs/2026-06-01T23-49-45-026Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; reached all four endings (Truth ×2 prose variants, Rich, Caught). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; ZERO functional bugs.** Its two notes ("deadline feels cosmetic", "ledger gate cosmetic") are NOT defects: the deadline fires at ticks>=10 (generous by design) and the patrol gate is real (ticks>=4 ∧ not read_ledger → ending_patrol); the tester just played efficiently / crossed before the hour (ticks 2). Same perennial perception the bug_0061..0084 reactive arc already answered. Clockwork remains content-complete (~27th clean pass).
- **Why validator work (off-list, justified):** the blind target had no fault, the standing memory records the content-polish backlog exhausted, AND **last cycle's (bug_0090) next-focus #1 queued this verbatim**: *"the symmetric parser/RPG check still open — a `win_condition` that is INTERNALLY CONTRADICTORY (unsatisfiable…) … Audit + port `isUnsatisfiable`/`whenProfile` to the parser validator."* Auditing it surfaced a **larger adjacent gap**: parser rooms and objects carry reactive `variants` with the EXACT first-match-wins semantics CYOA scenes/endings have (`src/parser/model.ts` roomDescription/objectDescription), yet the parser validator checked NEITHER their ordering NOR their satisfiability — and exit/interaction conditions (the analogue of CYOA choice conditions) were never contradiction-checked either.
- **Improvement (engine/validator, no content change):** ported the CYOA validator's `WhenProfile`/`whenProfile`/`entails`/`isUnsatisfiable`/`checkVariantShadowing`/`checkUnsatisfiable` machinery into `parser_validator.ts` (following the repo precedent that each validator carries its own copy — cf. the existing "mirrors the CYOA validator's deadline machinery" `collectFalsifiers`). In `validateParser` (so RPG inherits via delegation): **`checkVariantShadowing`** over every room.variants + object.variants → **warning `UNREACHABLE_VARIANT`** when a later variant is entailed by an earlier sibling; and **`checkUnsatisfiable`** over every variant `when`, every exit `conditions`, every interaction `conditions`, and every **`win_condition` `conditions`** (the parser/RPG analogue of `meta.deadline`, whose contradiction case = CYOA's `DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE`) → **warning `UNSATISFIABLE_CONDITION`**. An unsatisfiable win is also a latent soft-lock unsoundness (its `visited` room is still added to `winRooms` as a phantom escape, masking a true SOFTLOCK — the pollution bug_0087 cited for CYOA); surfacing it points at the root cause. Severity WARNING matches the CYOA siblings; helpers are pure & additive, absent `variants` untouched.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 646 tests pass (120 files)** (was 635); verify:integrity + typecheck + lint + format:check clean; all four health-gated packs (sealed_crypt, sunken_barrow, cold_forge, watchtower) validate 0/0, alchemists_tower too. **No content pack edited — all content hashes unchanged.** Bug-first verified: general-before-specific (flag superset, var threshold, duplicate `when`) and a shadowed OBJECT variant all yield UNREACHABLE_VARIANT; a flag-pinned-both-ways variant, a crossed-var-bound exit condition, and a contradictory win_condition all yield UNSATISFIABLE_CONDITION. Soundness no-false-positive: specific-first ordering, incomparable (distinct-flag) variants, and an `any_of` (opaque) earlier sibling are NOT flagged; the shipped parser packs (via validateParser) and RPG packs (via validateRpg) produce ZERO new findings.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0091_parser_variant_shadowing_unsatisfiable.yaml` + `tests/regression/parser_variant_shadowing.test.ts` (9 cases) + new negative fixtures `content/broken-fixtures/parser_unreachable_variant.yaml` & `parser_unsatisfiable_condition.yaml` wired into `tests/unit/parser_validator.test.ts` (a new WARNING_FIXTURES block, since these are warnings — report.ok stays true). This closes the **parser/RPG half of the reactive-variant + guard-satisfiability audit**: bug_0085/0086 made the CYOA reactive machinery sound; every reactive and gating surface in every mode is now ordering- and satisfiability-checked.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the **soft-lock pollution itself** — now that unsatisfiable win_conditions are *flagged*, EXCLUDE them (and unfireable CYOA deadlines) from the soft-lock escape graph (`winRooms` / deadline edge) so a real SOFTLOCK behind a dead terminal is no longer masked (the unsoundness bug_0087/0091 only surface, not yet fix). (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss; parallels meta.deadline) — the largest deferred structural item. (3) keep rotating the mandated blind pass onto the least-attended packs — **RPG mode (cold_forge / sunken_barrow) has never had a blind LLM combat playtest**. (4) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge).

### Cycle result — the parser/RPG global-terminal gets the fires-at-start guard the CYOA deadline already has (bug_0090, engine/validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off clockwork onto the least-attended pack (parser/sealed_crypt, seeds 7/19): report `ai-runs/2026-06-01T23-33-30-413Z/playtest.md`.** The assessor's rank-1 was clockwork_heist again, but it is content-complete (~26 consecutive clean passes) and the last several cycles' next-focus #2 + the standing memory both call to rotate the blind pass onto the never-blind-tested parser/RPG packs. Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access: WON 35/35 (perfect score), reached `ending_victory`, **ZERO functional bugs, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5** (only knock = scope: short, linear, one ending). Room-text refresh, scoring, and gating all verified clean.
- **Why validator work (off-list, justified):** the rotated blind target had no fault (as clockwork has had for ~26 passes), the standing memory records the content-polish backlog exhausted, AND **last cycle's own next-focus #4 explicitly queued this**: *"a possible parser/RPG deadline-analog audit — check whether those modes have any global-terminal mechanic with the same firability/fires-at-start degeneracies the CYOA validator now guards."* I ran the audit and it is real: the parser/RPG analogue of CYOA's `meta.deadline` is `win_conditions` (a list of `{conditions, ending}` evaluated by the SAME §8.4.5 `checkWin`). Its never-fires end IS already guarded (IMPOSSIBLE_GATE / ITEM_REQUIRED_UNOBTAINABLE / WIN_UNREACHABLE); its **fires-at-start end was not**.
- **Improvement (engine/validator, no content change):** added **`checkWinFiresAtStart`** to the parser validator (RPG delegates to it, so both modes are covered by one check). A `win_condition` whose conditions ALREADY HOLD in the engine's own initial state (`initStateForParserPack(index, 0)` — start `on_enter` applied, start room marked visited by `initState`) AND can never be falsified fires on the player's **first action on every path** (engine §8.4.5 `checkWin` runs post-action, never at game start) → the game is won at turn 1 with no room past the start ever played → **unplayable → ERROR `WIN_FIRES_AT_START`** (mirrors START_MISSING and CYOA's `DEADLINE_FIRES_AT_START`). Realistic footguns: `{ visited: <start_room> }`, a flag in `flags_init`, `score >= 0`. Soundness mirrors bug_0089: ground (b) un-falsifiability is proven only for a flat conjunction of monotone-stable atoms via a parser `collectFalsifiers` + `winStaysTrueForever` (sign-significant var arithmetic; any disjunction/negation/not_visited/object/quest bails to falsifiable). **RPG soundness:** the RPG validator injects (i) its declared runtime effects (enemy `on_defeat`, skill `on_success`/`on_failure`) as extra falsifiers, and (ii) the combat-volatile vars (player hp/attack/defense + every `enemyHpVar`) — combat mutates HP via a DYNAMIC `set_var` the static scan never sees, so a win on a volatile var bails to falsifiable. Two new `ValidateParserOptions` (`extraFalsifierEffects`, `extraVolatileVars`) default empty → Stage-2 parser behaviour byte-identical.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 635 tests pass (119 files)** (was 617); verify:integrity + typecheck + lint + format:check clean; all four health-gated packs (sealed_crypt, sunken_barrow, cold_forge + watchtower) validate 0/0, and alchemists_tower (non-gated) too. **No content pack edited — all content hashes unchanged.** Bug-first verified: a `{ visited: start_room }` win and a monotone-increasing-var-at-bound win both yield the ERROR; soundness no-false-positive — a var-true-at-init-but-decrementable win, a not-yet-met (room-not-visited) win, and (RPG) a combat-volatile `hp>=1` win are all NOT flagged, while the SAME `hp>=1` shape in a pure parser pack (where `hp` is inert) IS flagged.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0090_parser_win_fires_at_start.yaml` + `tests/regression/parser_win_fires_at_start.test.ts` (6 cases) + new negative fixture `content/broken-fixtures/parser_win_fires_at_start.yaml` wired into `tests/unit/parser_validator.test.ts`. This closes the **parser/RPG half** of the §8.4.5 global-terminal audit: bug_0087/bug_0089 bracketed the CYOA deadline on both degenerate ends; the existing infeasibility checks + this new guard now bracket parser/RPG `win_conditions` the same way.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the **symmetric parser/RPG check still open** — a `win_condition` that is INTERNALLY CONTRADICTORY (unsatisfiable: crossed var bounds / `has_flag x ∧ not_flag x`), the direct analogue of CYOA's `DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE`/`UNSATISFIABLE_CONDITION` (isUnsatisfiable). It is also a latent soft-lock unsoundness: the parser validator adds a win_condition's `visited` room to `winRooms` as a real escape target even if the condition can never fire, so an unsatisfiable win referencing a room could mask a true SOFTLOCK (the exact pollution bug_0087 cited for CYOA). Audit + port `isUnsatisfiable`/`whenProfile` to the parser validator. (2) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss; parallels meta.deadline) — the largest deferred structural item. (3) keep rotating the mandated blind pass onto the still-least-attended packs (alchemists_tower parser; sunken_barrow / cold_forge RPG — RPG mode has never had a blind LLM combat playtest). (4) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge).

### Cycle result — the deadline's OTHER degenerate tuning gets its guard: fires-at-start (bug_0089, engine/validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (seed 11, + 23/37/41): report `ai-runs/2026-06-01T23-16-02-571Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; reached three distinct endings (Rich, Truth ×2 prose variants, Caught). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; ZERO functional bugs.** Its one substantive note: the namesake deadline "feels cosmetic" — careful/efficient play never lingers to the ticks>=10 overstay. **I verified live (drove study↔gallery to ticks 10) that `ending_overstayed` DOES fire cleanly** — the deadline has real teeth; the note is a discoverability observation about the author-intentional "careful play is safe" tuning (the bug_0061→0084 arc), not a defect, and not worth relitigating.
- **Why validator work again (off-list, justified):** the blind target had no fault, the standing memory records the content-polish backlog exhausted, AND **last cycle's own next-focus #2 explicitly queued this**: *"extend the deadline soundness guard with an 'instant deadline' check (a `when` already satisfied in the INITIAL state … the dual of unfireable; needs an init-state evaluator, audit value first)."* I audited it and it is real and sound — so I built it.
- **Improvement (engine/validator, no content change):** bug_0087 flags a `meta.deadline` that can PROVABLY NEVER fire (`DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE`, warn). The **symmetric opposite went unchecked**: a deadline whose `when` ALREADY HOLDS in the initial state and can never be falsified. The engine's §8.4.5 `checkWin` runs **post-action, not at game start** (`src/core/engine.ts`), so such a deadline ends the game on the player's **first action, on every path** → no scene past the start is ever playable → the pack is **unplayable** (strictly worse than unfireable, which leaves the game completable). Added **`checkDeadlineFiresAtStart`**: ground (a) `when` holds in the engine's own initial state (`initStateForPack`, start `on_enter` applied, evaluated by the engine's own `evalConditions` — zero drift); ground (b) `when` can never be falsified (`deadlineStaysTrueForever`: a flat AND of monotone-stable atoms — flags not cleared/set against, items not removed/added against, `var_gte`/`var_lte`/`var_eq` whose var never moves the wrong way with **sign-significant** `by` arithmetic since `effects.ts` allows a negative `by`; `visited` is monotone; any_of/none_of/not_visited/object/quest bail to *falsifiable*). Both true → **ERROR `DEADLINE_FIRES_AT_START`** (mirrors START_NOT_SCENE). Conservative: never claims un-falsifiability it can't prove → no false positives.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0** (verify:integrity + typecheck + lint + format + full test suite + 4 pack validations + watchtower playtest); +6 regression cases (`cyoa_deadline_fires_at_start.test.ts`) +1 unit fixture row, 0 regressions; all four health-gated packs validate 0/0. **No content pack edited — all content hashes unchanged.** Shipped clockwork (`ticks>=10`, init 0, foyer start advances no ticks) is **NOT flagged** and stays green; watchtower has no deadline. Bug-first verified: a `ticks>=0`/init-0/`inc_var +1` deadline yields the ERROR; soundness no-false-positive — `ticks>=5` (above init), a `ticks>=0` deadline whose scene can `dec_var ticks` (escapable first move), and a `has_flag doomed` deadline whose choice clears `doomed` are all NOT flagged.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0089_cyoa_deadline_fires_at_start.yaml` + `tests/regression/cyoa_deadline_fires_at_start.test.ts` (6 cases) + new negative fixture `content/broken-fixtures/deadline_fires_at_start.yaml` wired into `tests/unit/cyoa_validator.test.ts`. Together bug_0087 (never fires) and bug_0089 (always fires at start) **bracket the `meta.deadline` mechanic on both degenerate ends.**
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord as an opt-in optional boss where the shade's +2 ward pays off; parallels `meta.deadline`) — the largest deferred structural item, now the clear top candidate since the deadline family is fully bracketed; (2) keep **rotating the mandated blind pass onto the least-attended packs** (sealed_crypt / cold_forge / sunken_barrow / alchemists_tower) — clockwork is content-complete (~26th clean pass), keep touches to engine/validator only; (3) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge); (4) a possible **parser/RPG deadline-analog audit** — check whether those modes have any global-terminal mechanic with the same firability/fires-at-start degeneracies the CYOA validator now guards.

### Cycle result — the namesake deadline gets its own soundness guard (bug_0087, engine/validator, no content change)
- **Mandated blind pass ran on the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (seed 23): report `ai-runs/2026-06-01T22-53-38-839Z/playtest.md`.** Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; 6 sessions reaching FIVE endings (Truth ×2 prose variants, Rich, Caught, Caught-on-the-Hour, and the ticks>=10 Overstay). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; ZERO functional bugs.** It specifically looped past ticks 7/8/9 and confirmed the deadline lands cleanly at 10 — the "threat that never lands" suspicion of earlier cycles is disproven. Its only two notes (free-vs-costly move asymmetry; the short strongbox route to the Truth ending) are author-intentional design, not defects. Clockwork is content-complete (~25th clean pass).
- **Why I pivoted to validator work (per the cycle's "you MAY pick something off-list" clause):** the blind target had no functional fault and the standing memory records the content-polish backlog as exhausted across all six packs. So I closed the soundness gap the variant family left open, the direct companion to last cycle's checks — and explicitly the kind of "sibling soundness guard … extending bug_0085's profile machinery" the last two cycles' next-focus notes pointed at. I AUDITED the literal next-focus #3 ("a variant whose `when` is a strict superset of the base / always-on") and rejected it as low-value: `SceneVariantSchema.when` is `.min(1)`, and over flags/items/vars with no domain knowledge there is no SOUND always-true detection — so that check could never fire. The deadline-firability gap is concrete, sound, and guards the pack's namesake mechanic.
- **Improvement (engine/validator, no content change):** bug_0085 (shadowed variant) + bug_0086 (vacuous guard) made the reactive-variant machinery sound, but **`meta.deadline.when` (the §8.4.5 global terminal from bug_0079/0080) was never analysed for firability**. A `meta.deadline` that can PROVABLY never fire is two bad things at once: (1) a Chekhov's gun — the declared urgency mechanic is dead, the exact "looks like a timer but never bites" sin the whole clockwork arc fought, shippable by renaming the watched var / dropping the only `inc_var` / fat-fingering a crossed bound; and (2) a **latent unsoundness in the validator's OWN soft-lock graph**, which adds the deadline ending as a real escape edge from any scene that writes a watched var — if the `when` also requires a flag/item nothing provides, that escape is a phantom and a true soft-lock would be masked. Added **`checkDeadlineFirability`**: two sound grounds reusing existing machinery — (a) `isUnsatisfiable(whenProfile(when))` (contradictory `when`), and (b) the `when` requires (AND-context, via `collectRequired`) a flag/item/var nothing provides (the IMPOSSIBLE_GATE feasibility logic, applied to the deadline) → **warning `DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE`**. Hoisted `writes = collectWrites(pack)` to the deadline section and reused it in the later feasibility loop (no double scan).
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 617 tests pass (116 files; +5 bug_0087 regression cases +1 unit fixture row; 0 regressions)** (was 611); verify:integrity OK; typecheck/lint/format:check clean; all four health-gated packs validate 0/0. **No content pack edited — all content hashes unchanged.** The shipped clockwork deadline (`ticks>=10`, fed by real `inc_var ticks`) produces **ZERO `DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE`**; watchtower has no deadline. Bug-first verified: a synthetic deadline firing at `doom>=5` while `doom` is never written (only red-herring `ticks` is), one with crossed bounds (`doom>=5 ∧ doom<=3`), and one requiring an unset flag all yield the finding; a fireable `doom>=1`-with-`inc_var doom` deadline does NOT (soundness, no false positive).
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0087_cyoa_deadline_unfireable.yaml` + `tests/regression/cyoa_deadline_unfireable.test.ts` (5 cases) + new negative fixture `content/broken-fixtures/deadline_unfireable.yaml` wired into `tests/unit/cyoa_validator.test.ts`.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord as an opt-in optional boss where the shade's +2 ward pays off; parallels `meta.deadline`) — the largest deferred structural item; (2) extend the deadline soundness guard with an **"instant deadline" check** (a `when` already satisfied in the INITIAL state ends the game at turn 0 — the dual of unfireable; needs an init-state evaluator, audit value first); (3) keep **rotating the mandated blind pass onto the least-attended packs** (sealed_crypt / cold_forge / sunken_barrow / alchemists_tower) — clockwork stays at rest (content complete, keep touches to engine/validator); (4) the verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge).

- Current objective: Make AdventureForge safe for bounded, MCP-driven AFK improvement loops.
- Last completed improvement: Added this durable state file as the handoff point for autonomous cycles.
- Evidence summary: Baseline `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run validate -- content/cyoa/pack/watchtower_road.yaml` passed before AFK loop changes. `npm run health` was missing and is now a required gate.
- MCP playtest notes: The shipped MCP server already exposed core AdventureForge tools; the AFK pass adds goal-compatible aliases and transcript/playtest summaries.
- What improved: Future agents have explicit MCP play requirements, ignored scratch evidence, and a bounded loop entry point.
- What still feels weak: The loop can gather evidence and verify routes, but it intentionally keeps code/content edits conservative and does not invent broad autonomous rewrites.
- Highest-priority next task: Run the bounded loop and use its evidence to pick one small discoverability or transcript-quality improvement.
- Risks/blockers: Repo-local `CODEX_HOME=$PWD/.codex` does not include user auth by default; unattended Codex invocation may need the operator's configured auth or an external runner. Plain `npm run mcp` writes npm banner output, so repo-local MCP launch uses `npm --silent run mcp`.
- Repeated agent mistake to avoid: Do not claim playtesting from validator output alone; actual route decisions must go through MCP tools.

## Current OpenAI/Codex Notes

- Codex CLI inspected locally: `codex-cli 0.135.0`.
- Official OpenAI docs say Codex supports non-interactive execution through `codex exec`, MCP server configuration, workspace-write sandboxing, and goal mode across Codex surfaces.
- Official ChatGPT help says GPT-5.5 is current in ChatGPT as of May 2026; GPT-5.5 Thinking is the deeper reasoning option, GPT-5.5 Pro is for hardest long-running work, and GPT-5.5 is available in Codex for eligible users. GPT-5.5 is not necessarily available through API deployments.

## Cycle 2026-06-01 — blind playtest loop closed (bug_0002)

- Ran the blind-playtest step per docs/blind_playtest_protocol.md (fresh subagent, MCP-only, no source access) twice on watchtower_road seed 7.
- Findings fixed (content/hint, all re-validated green, locked by tests/regression/watchtower_blind_fixes.test.ts + traces/bugs/bug_0002): stale cart text, stale cellar-door text, duplicate cellar journal (on_enter→scene text), ledger now a carried item + path-agnostic ending_truth, ungated the visible cellar "gap in the wall", de-misleading decision_point framing.
- Second blind run confirmed duplicate_journal and ledger issues resolved; the good ending now reads as earned.
- Deferred (structural, next cycle — see bug_0002 deferred_findings): the decision_point↔road_north↔checkpoint orbit and the payoff-less confront_smuggler dead-end. These are flow/design tightenings, not faults; gating is correct.
- Protocol is now wired into AGENTS.md and the ai-loop generated agent-prompt, so future cycles run the blind subagent step automatically.
- Pack content hash: e83eef5a3e12d55df5df576f9b893dc7134d13897f5a99dfdf2db2790ebe1c5e.

## Cycle 2026-06-01b — deferred structural findings resolved (bug_0002)

- Tackled the two deferred structural findings from the blind playtest.
- confront_smuggler dead-end → real stakes: press_bluff (no proof) → ending_captured; reveal_evidence (proof) → win path; back_off reframed as retreat-to-gather-proof. No node is a no-op dead-end now.
- decision_point↔road_north↔checkpoint orbit → broken for the prepared player: turn_back gated on not_flag learned_truth, so with proof you must commit (expose/slip_away); without proof a signposted back-path to find proof remains, slip_away always exits (no soft-lock).
- Locked by tests/regression/watchtower_blind_fixes.test.ts (describe "bug_0002 deferred"); content hash 4188f7de58079146e00af8c5505094df19540af9d137a3736e26332d197820f0. 174 tests + health green.

### Verification (blind run, seed 7) — structural findings CONFIRMED resolved
- Blind playtester confirmed the checkpoint/town-edge region "is NOT pointless orbiting" and the guard confrontation "produces clearly different, meaningful outcomes" with vs. without evidence. Both deferred findings closed.
- New findings for a future cycle (logged, not yet fixed — narrative payoff/continuity, not structural):
  1. reveal_evidence (confront_smuggler) → decision_point is a silent teleport; the standoff's most dramatic beat has no on-screen payoff. Add a resolving narration/journal line.
  2. Ledger is never presentable at the checkpoint (only the sealed letter is); the letter/ledger relationship is muddled.
  3. "broken seal" is referenced (press_bluff text + ending_truth) but no action ever opens the letter — dangling mechanic / continuity slip.
  4. Hermit tower-lore (hermit_about_tower) sets no flag/journal — optional flavor with no mechanical hook.
  5. Cosmetic: search_rubble / search_cache remain offered after the cart/cache are emptied (re-entry shows an empty room).

## Cycle 2026-06-01c — verifier-integrity guard (trust-but-verify keystone)

- Reviewed sibling AFK loops (zork-unlimited-2: bash outer/inner cycle, self-healing autopilot, multi-persona, seed+hash determinism, but unconditional `git add .`; zork-unlimited-3: evidence-driven prompts, MCP evidence, health gate, hardcoded true-ending canary, post-agent verify-before-commit/push) and web-researched 2025–26 AFK techniques (Ralph loop, four-stage gather→act→verify→repeat, Stop-hook gates, EvilGenie reward-hacking + "forbid touching the verifier", externalized state, iteration/cost caps, worktree-per-task).
- Decision (aligned to our trust-but-verify philosophy): implemented the research's #2 technique — enforce "don't route around the verifier" — which neither sibling has. scripts/verify-integrity.ts: static mode (protected assets present, no .skip/.only/.todo/xit, test-count floor 120) wired into health + CI + npm test; drift mode (--against <ref>) wired into loop.sh as refuse-and-surface (halt + leave uncommitted for review) on protected-file edits or silent hash re-pins, overridable via AI_LOOP_ALLOW_VERIFIER_EDITS=1.
- Honest limit: catches mechanical tampering (skip/delete/empty/re-pin), not semantic weakening (needs LLM-judge, future). 195 tests + health green.
- Next AFK candidates from the research, ranked: (1) Milestone 2 multi-mode loop generalization (rotate packs/modes — M1 unblocked it); (2) fresh-context adversarial /code-review of the diff before "done"; (3) iteration/budget + cost caps; (4) worktree-per-task isolation.
## Cycle 2026-06-01d — best AFK loop implemented

- Built the loop's brain: src/afk/assessor.ts ranks the next-best improvement across content_fix / content_new / engine / repo from real signals (per-pack coverage playtests, validator warnings, mode thinness, engine TODOs, tooling gaps). `npm run assess` prints the backlog; deterministic + unit-tested.
- Rewrote src/ai-loop.ts to drive off the assessor (dropped the brittle hardcoded watchtower route), pick a playtest target, emit a cycle prompt that MANDATES a blind LLM playtest each cycle, and record artifacts under ai-runs/.
- loop.sh enforces the mandate: require_playtest_record refuses to commit a cycle with no blind-playtest report; health + verify:integrity drift gate already block red/verifier-tampering work. Full doc: docs/afk_loop.md.
- A separate transient run produced the bug_0003 cellar-discoverability fix on watchtower concurrently (see the cycle note below); I kept my AFK-loop commit scoped to its own files and reconciled bug_0003 separately. Its re-pin (watchtower hash → c49b4424…, rpg_validator.test.ts) is deliberate and surfaced, not laundered.

## AFK Cycle 2026-06-01 — cellar discoverability (bug_0003)

Deliberately does NOT grant learned_truth — the knowledge-vs-proof gate fencing ending_truth is preserved.

### Cycle result — lantern-less cellar door now legible (bug_0005)
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, seed 31, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T05-06-44-014Z/playtest.md. Reached all 3 endings across 3 runs; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions, no soft-locks. Verdict: "solid, shippable short CYOA."
- Top friction the playtester surfaced (§4): arriving at the **cellar door with no lantern** offered only "Step back from the door." The watchtower scene explicitly invites "Circle around to the cellar door," so a curious first-timer plausibly does so BEFORE looting the cart — and then the clearly-described, descendable stair gives no forward action and no in-fiction reason why. The block read as a dead end, not a puzzle.
- Improvement (one, content layer): added a `peer_into_dark` choice to `cellar_door`, shown only when the player holds no lantern (`not_item lantern`). It narrates the darkness ("…you'd break your neck going down blind, so you back up the steps — better find a light first") and routes the player back out to `ruined_watchtower`. This reinforces the lantern→cellar chain (bug_0003) WITHOUT spoiling where the lantern is. The nudge vanishes the moment a lantern is carried (when `light_lantern` takes over).
- Verification gotcha caught + fixed mid-cycle: my first cut looped `peer_into_dark` back to `cellar_door` with only a narrate effect. The playtester's sound loop-detector (agents/playtester.ts) correctly flagged that same-scene no-state-change step as a stuck self-loop — the structural playtest started reporting "fell into a loop near cellar_door" (the very thing bug_0003 de-looped). Re-routing the nudge OUT to the watchtower (a real scene change = progress) removed all loop findings. Lesson: a pure-narration choice must make progress, never self-loop.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0; 217 tests pass (+3 new); zero loop findings in the coverage playtest. Deliberate re-pin: watchtower content hash 8094e553… → 7f322e4cdbf1df48460c76df56fb8bdfd379fc1b755d804fb390542a3b9e71a6 (tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts comment updated, surfaced not laundered).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0005_watchtower_cellar_dark_feedback.yaml + tests/regression/watchtower_cellar_dark_feedback.test.ts (3 cases: nudge offered when lantern-less, narrates + makes progress / no self-loop, vanishes once equipped).
- Next suggested focus: the still-deferred narrative-payoff findings on watchtower (the "broken seal" continuity slip — ending_truth references a broken seal the ledger route never opens; the muddled letter↔ledger relationship at the checkpoint), then clockwork_heist_v1 (assessor rank 2) and the unverified parser packs (alchemists_tower, sealed_crypt). The deterministic coverage bot still cannot reach ending_truth / the hermit cluster on seeds 1-3 — its puzzle-solving, not pack discoverability, is the limiter there.
