### Cycle result — sealed-letter proof beat made coherent (bug_0006)
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, seed 42, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T05-41-02-671Z/playtest.md. Reached all 3 endings across 3 runs; clarity 4/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions, no soft-locks. Verdict: "a compact, well-built branching mystery... a real player would finish satisfied."
- Findings the playtest surfaced in the sealed-letter thread (§5.1 + §5.3), combined with the deferred bug_0005 next-focus, formed one coherent improvement: (1) "Show him the sealed letter" stayed available after use, re-narrating "The hermit breaks the seal" on an already-broken seal and re-setting learned_truth (same class as bug_0004); (2) hermit_about_letter set learned_truth but journaled NOTHING while every comparable reveal journals; (3) ending_truth claimed "the oil in the cellar, the names, the broken seal" — but the seal is broken only on the hermit route, and the cellar oil is seen only on the cellar route, so every single-route win narrated evidence the player never gathered.
- Improvement (one, content layer): show_letter now gated on not_flag seal_broken (can't re-break an opened seal); hermit_about_letter.on_enter now also sets seal_broken + adds a journal entry recording the reveal (single gated entry ⇒ no stacking); ending_truth reworded path-agnostic ("the smugglers' scheme, the arson, and the proof that damns them") so it reads truthfully whether you won via the carried ledger or the hermit-opened letter. learned_truth remains the proof gate; which routes can win is unchanged.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0; 225 tests pass (+4 new); zero new coverage findings (the standing ending_truth/hermit-cluster warnings are the deterministic bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack discoverability). Deliberate re-pin: watchtower content hash 7f322e4c… → 46ac614226e8b6e478ea4b3bfeedb92a1cb693646301ec5bae8cec3be3c956d3 (tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts comment updated, surfaced not laundered).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0006_watchtower_letter_proof_coherence.yaml + tests/regression/watchtower_letter_proof_coherence.test.ts (4 cases: reveal journals + sets seal_broken/learned_truth, seal can't re-break, hermit/letter route still reaches ending_truth, ending text no longer presumes broken seal / cellar oil).
- Next suggested focus (logged this cycle, not yet fixed): (a) presumptive ledger text at hidden_cache — "a seal you recognize from the letter" reads even when the player never picked up the letter (playtest §5.2); (b) the muddled compass geography around the ford/north-road loop (playtest §4) — east/west/north labels don't form a consistent mental map; (c) the beacon's raised_alarm flag never pays off in any ending. Then clockwork_heist_v1 (assessor rank 2) and the unverified parser packs (alchemists_tower, sealed_crypt).

### Cycle result — stale ledger text at hidden_cache fixed (bug_0007)
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, seed 55 (+77/12/5 for alt routes), report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T05-51-01-531Z/playtest.md. Reached 3 endings + the no-proof guard branch; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "a complete, fair, and enjoyable short adventure" — all remaining flaws cosmetic polish.
- Top concrete finding (playtest §5, first item — also the bug_0006 deferred next-focus (a)): at hidden_cache, take_ledger routed back to hidden_cache, so after pocketing the ledger the scene re-rendered "A ledger lies forgotten in the dust... a seal you recognize from the letter" with only "Slip back out" — a taken item still described as present, AND a description presuming the player took the sealed letter (false on the dominant cellar-only route).
- Improvement (one, content layer): (1) take_ledger.next is now `cellar` (not hidden_cache); the player exits cleanly to the cleared cellar (only climb_out remains) and the cache is unreachable again (search_cache requires not_item ledger), so the stale view is eliminated, not just skipped once — mirroring the bug_0003 "exits cleanly" design. (2) cache text reworded path-agnostic: "a wax seal pressed into the cover" replaces "a seal you recognize from the letter". No flags/items/gating/reachable-endings change.
- Verification gotcha: routing take_ledger straight to the cellar made the old `..take_ledger -> leave_cache..` step illegal in pre-existing routes (bug_0002 LEDGER_ROUTE asserts every step legal; bug_0002 climax path; two bug_0003 paths via the no-op-tolerant play() helper). Updated all four routes to drop the now-redundant leave_cache — legitimate flow updates, not weakening.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0; 229 tests pass (+4 new). Deliberate re-pin: watchtower content hash 46ac6142... -> 7e3392b6bbf675855db289b57fceea6fc4dc878b9cd7305ae6276748598347bb (tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts comment updated, surfaced not laundered).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0007_watchtower_cache_stale_ledger.yaml + tests/regression/watchtower_cache_stale_ledger.test.ts (4 cases: clean exit to cellar / no stale re-render, cache unreachable after pickup, cellar-only route still reaches ending_truth, description no longer presumes the letter).

- Why: An LLM playtest can pinpoint why these are hard to reach (signposting, clue legibility, pacing) and the fix raises player-facing quality.
- Mandatory LLM playtest target this cycle: content/cyoa/pack/watchtower_road.yaml.
- Process: assessor ranks → blind LLM playtest for quality → one improvement → health + verify:integrity green → commit (trust-but-verify).

### Cycle result — rotated to clockwork_heist; vault-door signposting fixed (bug_0008)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its remaining "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws — the prior cycle's note explicitly recommended rotating). Chose the assessor's rank-2 candidate, clockwork_heist_v1, which has a GENUINELY unreached ending (ending_rich) and an unvisited vault — a real, unverified quality gap on a fresh target. Justified higher-value than another watchtower micro-polish.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 19, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T06-01-12-651Z/playtest.md. Reached all 3 endings (Truth, Caught, Rich) across 4 routes; clarity 4/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: a short, atmospheric, mechanically clean CYOA; main risk is anticlimax/legibility.
- Top friction the playtest surfaced (§4 + §5, scene vault_door): a player who climbs the stairs first — a natural opening — reaches the vault door with only "Force the door" (which the study's brass plate AND the door text both say fails, and which leads to ending_caught) or "Back away". The intended non-force solution (pick_lock) is invisible without the lockpick (found off in the kitchen). So a player who HEEDS the "never to force" clue has no action that honours it and no hint that a tool found elsewhere is the answer — the vault, and the Rich ending behind it, read as a dead end. This is exactly why both the coverage bot and a first-timer miss ending_rich/vault.
- Improvement (one, content layer): added a `study_lock` choice to vault_door, shown only when the player holds no lockpick (not_item lockpick, not_flag alarm). It narrates "a lock to coax, not to break … force will only wake the house … you need a slender tool and a steady hand … search the manor for the right instrument before you try the door again" and routes the player OUT to the gallery (next: landing). Mirrors the proven bug_0005 peer_into_dark shape: a pure-narration nudge must MAKE PROGRESS (route to a real scene), never self-loop — a same-scene no-state-change step is flagged by the playtester's loop-detector. Routing to the gallery steers the player toward the study (clue) and back toward the kitchen (picks) WITHOUT naming where the picks are, preserving the lock-and-key puzzle. The nudge vanishes the moment a lockpick is carried (pick_lock takes over).
- Verification: validate green 0/0; coverage playtest is net-neutral vs. the committed pack (confirmed by stash-and-compare — the one pre-existing study↔landing oscillation is the coverage bot's planning limit, not introduced and not a pack flaw, so reachable endings/coverage are unchanged); full `npm run health` exit 0; 232 tests pass (+3 new). No content-hash pin exists for clockwork in the test suite, so no re-pin was needed (hash recorded in the bug artifact only): 2001b7b6… → 79dead145bee8ac72df040fe68d6fdc801a39bbdf66be9476e4c2c08b8f08f29.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0008_clockwork_vault_door_signposting.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_vault_door_signposting.test.ts (3 cases: lockpick-less door offers the nudge + losing force/exit but NOT pick_lock; studying narrates a 'find a tool' hint, takes no item/flag/journal, moves to landing without tripping the alarm / no self-loop; once a pick is carried the nudge is gone and pick_lock takes over).
- Next suggested focus (logged this cycle, not yet fixed — all on clockwork_heist, from the playtest §5): (a) crawlspace continuity — "that same sealed letter" presumes the player already saw the letter, false on the clock-first route; (b) ending_truth text ("You leave the gold") presumes the player stood in the vault and saw gold, false on the strongbox/crawlspace route; (c) the study's hourly-guard ledger clue pays off nowhere (clue pointing nowhere). Then the unverified parser packs (alchemists_tower, sealed_crypt) and sunken_barrow (rpg). Keep rotating the playtest target rather than re-polishing one pack.

### Cycle result — rotated to a parser pack; self-targeted USE made legible (bug_0009)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws) AND off clockwork_heist (bug_0008 last cycle). Chose a GENUINELY UNVERIFIED target: the two parser packs are unsolved by the deterministic coverage bot (alchemists_tower 0/60 ended, sealed_crypt 0/60), so their quality had never been confirmed by actual play — the highest value-of-information per the assessor's own candidates 5 & 6 and the standing "keep rotating" guidance. Picked alchemists_tower_v1.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 13, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T06-09-34-002Z/playtest.md. Solved the pack to FULL score (ending_cured, 35/35) and reached the clued death (ending_poisoned, recovered via save/load). Clarity 4/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "solid, coherent, and bug-light" — the puzzle chain is fully clue-legible and mechanically clean. This CONFIRMS the previously-unverified parser pack is genuinely good, which is itself valuable signal.
- Top friction the playtest surfaced (§5, scene laboratory / black_phial): after taking the black phial, the only action to consume the clued death-trap rendered as the command "use black phial on black phial" (id use_black_phial_on_black_phial) — a USE whose item AND target are the same object. It reads like an authoring bug and contradicts the recipe's own warning ("Do not DRINK it"): the warning says drink, the action said "use X on X".
- Improvement (one, ENGINE layer — generic, pack-agnostic): a self-targeted USE (item === target) is the legitimate "consume this thing" pattern (drink the phial, eat the bread) and is now surfaced as id `use_<obj>` / command "use <obj>" (src/parser/legal_actions.ts), with the human command parser accepting the bare "use <obj>" as a self-USE ONLY when the object carries a self-interaction (src/parser/command_map.ts, gated via useInteraction) — so non-self-use items like the crypt's rope still require a target and every other USE in every pack is unchanged. NO pack YAML edited ⇒ no content hash changed, no re-pin needed. Only other touched test: tests/acceptance/stage3_alchemist.test.ts death-path step id updated use_black_phial_on_black_phial → use_black_phial (deliberate, surfaced flow update, not a weakening).
- Evidence: full `npm run health` exit 0; 235 tests pass (+3 new). Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0009_parser_self_use_command.yaml + tests/regression/parser_self_use_command.test.ts (3 cases: held phial offered as use_black_phial / "use black phial" with the old self-on-self forms gone; human parser maps "use black phial"→USE(black_phial,black_phial) which still fires ending_poisoned; a non-self-use item — crypt rope — still needs a target, "use rope on old well" unchanged).
- Next suggested focus (logged this cycle, not yet fixed — all on alchemists_tower, from the playtest §4/§5, all cosmetic): (a) stale room text after state change — garden still "a pale herb still grows" after you take it, study still "a LOCKED iron strongbox" after unlocking (a broader parser description-reactivity feature, not a one-line fix); (b) the win auto-fires on spire entry with no "administer the antidote" agency — a flat finale. Then blind-playtest sealed_crypt_v1 (still 0/60 unverified) and sunken_barrow (rpg, also unverified). Keep rotating the playtest target rather than re-polishing one pack.

### Cycle result — reactive room descriptions; sealed_crypt stale text fixed (bug_0010)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007) and the already-verified clockwork_heist (bug_0008) / alchemists_tower (bug_0009) onto the LAST genuinely UNVERIFIED pack: The Sealed Crypt. The deterministic coverage bot is stuck at the entrance (0/60 ended; only forest_path/chapel_yard/old_well visited, never the crypt), so this pack's quality had never been confirmed by actual play — highest value-of-information per the standing "keep rotating" guidance and the assessor's candidate 6.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 7, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T06-21-08-533Z/playtest.md. Solved to ending_victory and probed the no-key failure branch; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "a tight, fair, fully-solvable little parser puzzle." This CONFIRMS the previously-unverified pack is genuinely good — valuable signal in itself.
- Top finding (playtest §5, hit twice; also the bug_0009 deferred alchemists §5(a) item): stale room text contradicting changed state — Old Well still read "too far to climb down without a rope" after the rope was tied and the down exit opened; Crypt still read "an iron catacombs gate bars the way" after the gate swung inward.
- Improvement (one, ENGINE feature — generic, pack-agnostic): **reactive room descriptions.** RoomSchema gains an optional `variants: [{ when: Condition[], text }]` (src/parser/schema.ts); a pure helper roomDescription(room, state) returns the first variant whose `when` holds (declared order, first-match-wins) else the base `description` (src/parser/model.ts); the observation builder and the LOOK action both route through it so `look` and the observation read identically (src/parser/observation.ts, src/parser/legal_actions.ts). Applied to sealed_crypt's old_well (rope-tied variant) and crypt (gate-open variant). No flags/items/exits/gating/reachable-endings change — only narrated text reacts.
- Verification gotcha caught + fixed mid-cycle: my first cut used `.default([])`, which materialized `variants: []` on EVERY room in every parser/RPG pack at compile time and drifted the sunken_barrow RPG content hash (rpg_barrow_trace.test.ts failed: trace 39f9551e… vs recompiled cb871d6a…). Switched to `.optional()` so an absent field stays absent ⇒ packs that don't use it compile byte-identically and their hashes are unchanged (the same backward-compat rule Stage-4 skill_check uses). Lesson: an optional schema addition must be `.optional()`, never `.default([])`, or it silently rewrites every pack's content hash.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0; 238 tests pass (+3 new). No content-hash pin exists for sealed_crypt (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin was needed; sealed_crypt hash recorded in the bug artifact for the record.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0010_parser_reactive_room_text.yaml + tests/regression/parser_reactive_room_text.test.ts (3 cases: Old Well flips rope-less→rope-tied and never re-claims no rope; Crypt flips gate-barred→gate-open mid-crypt without ending; a variant-less room returns its base description byte-identically — backward-compat).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) APPLY the new reactive-text feature to alchemists_tower's stale rooms (bug_0009 §5(a): garden "a pale herb still grows" after taking it, study "a LOCKED iron strongbox" after unlocking) — now a one-variant-each content fix; (b) sealed_crypt's dialogue topic-menu re-ask (sexton: "crypt" reappears after asking "well"); (c) sealed_crypt unimplemented score field (always 0 at win). Then blind-playtest sunken_barrow (rpg, still unverified). Keep rotating the playtest target.

- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws) and off the already-verified clockwork_heist (bug_0008) / alchemists_tower (bug_0009) / sealed_crypt (bug_0010) onto the LAST genuinely UNVERIFIED pack: the RPG pack **The Sunken Barrow**. - Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 11, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T06-31-55-193Z/playtest.md. Solved to ending_victory and probed the no-iron-bar failure gate (recovery clean). Clarity 4/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "a clean, bug-light, well-clued micro-dungeon" — the iron-bar-as-lever clue pairing and the transparent skill-check math are highlights. This CONFIRMS the previously-unverified RPG pack is genuinely good — valuable signal in itself. Every playable pack has now been blind-verified at least once.
- Top finding (playtest §5, hit TWICE; same class as bug_0010): stale room text contradicting changed state — Guard Crypt still read "An archway east is choked with the cold the thing gives off" after the wight was slain (journal says the cold lifted; east exit opened); Slab Passage still read "a great stone slab set flush with the floor" after the might check levered it aside (stair-down exit opened).
- Improvement (one, **content** layer — NOT engine): the generic reactive-room `variants` feature already exists from bug_0010 (RoomSchema.variants + the pure roomDescription helper, routed through the observation builder and the LOOK action), so this is a one-variant-each content fix. guard_crypt gains a `when: [{ has_flag: wight_slain }]` variant (cold lifted, arch open); slab_passage gains a `when: [{ quest_stage: { quest: barrow, stage: slab_moved } }]` variant (slab levered aside, stair bared). No flags/items/exits/gating/reachable-endings change — only narrated text reacts. Verified LIVE through the MCP tools (seed 11): both variants flip at the right moment and ending_victory still fires.
- Deliberate, surfaced re-pin (not a weakening): adding the variants genuinely changed sunken_barrow's content hash 39f9551e… → ca34eeb6…, so traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json content_hash was updated to match. The trace's expected_final_hash is UNCHANGED because variants alter only narrated text, never game state — the Stage-4 victory replays to the identical final hash (rpg_barrow_trace.test.ts green). No test source pinned the old hash.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0; 241 tests pass (+3 new). Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0011_rpg_reactive_room_text.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_reactive_room_text.test.ts (3 cases: Guard Crypt flips cold-choked→cold-lifted on wight_slain and never re-claims the cold; Slab Passage flips flush→levered-aside on slab_moved and never re-claims the slab flush; a variant-less room — barrow_mouth — returns its base description byte-identically, backward-compat).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): with every pack now blind-verified at least once, the deferred polish items are the backlog — (a) APPLY the reactive-text feature to alchemists_tower's stale rooms (bug_0009 §5(a): garden "a pale herb still grows" after taking it, study "a LOCKED iron strongbox" after unlocking) — now a one-variant-each content fix; (b) sunken_barrow's implicit goal / no stated quest and its toothless combat balance (playtest §3/§4); (c) sunken_barrow's repeatable lever action after success; (d) sealed_crypt dialogue topic-menu re-ask + unimplemented score field. Also consider the assessor's content_new candidate (author a new rpg pack) to broaden the game, and the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap. Keep rotating the playtest target.

The playtester's secondary "score doesn't sum" worry was a misread — +5/+10/+20 accumulate correctly to 35 = max_score; no defect.
- Improvement (one, **content** layer — NOT engine): the generic reactive-room `variants` feature already exists from bug_0010, so this is a content-only application. garden gets three variants over its two takeable things (herb-gone = any_of[has_item herb, has_flag herb_added]; brass-gone = has_item brass_key, never consumed so a durable signal) covering all three live combinations; great_hall gets `when:[{has_flag: cellar_unlocked}]`; study gets `when:[{has_item: iron_key}]` (holding the iron key ⟺ the box was unlocked + emptied); cellar gets `when:[{any_of:[{has_item: water_vial},{has_flag: antidote_brewed}]}]`. No flags/items/exits/gating/reachable-endings change — only narrated text reacts. Verified LIVE through the engine (seed 29): all four flip at the right moment and ending_cured still fires.
- Note on the study signal: the strongbox open-state lives in state.objectState, which the closed condition vocabulary (flags/items/visited/vars/questStage) can't read — so has_item: iron_key is the earliest reliable flag-free "the box was dealt with" signal. Never wrong (you can't hold the iron key without having opened the box).
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0; 246 tests pass (+5 new). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): alchemists_tower d689ff3d… → 8302bfdf…. ### Cycle result — Sealed Crypt scoring brought to life (bug_0013)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws — the standing guidance is "keep rotating") onto **The Sealed Crypt** to cash in a deferred finding logged across bug_0010 / bug_0012: the sexton dialogue topic re-ask and the unimplemented score field.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 73, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T06-51-15-149Z/playtest.md. Solved to ending_victory and probed the no-key failure branch; clarity 4/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "a tight, fair, fully solvable little parser puzzle." Surfaced TWO concrete flaws (§5): (1) score is 0 at start and STILL 0 at the victory ending despite solving every puzzle — the score field reads as dead weight; (2) the sexton's "crypt"/"well" dialogue topics never exhaust and toggle forever.
- Improvement (one, **content** layer — NOT engine): opted the pack into the EXISTING, validator-checked Stage-3 scoring feature (ParserMetaSchema.max_score + the conventional `score` var + inc_var awards + the SCORE_UNREACHABLE check, already used by alchemists_tower). Set meta.max_score: 35 plus three one-time gated milestone awards mirroring alchemists' 5/10/20 shape: +5 reading the headstone clue (new READ interaction on `headstone`, read_text preserved, gated not_flag read_headstone), +10 solving the well (added to the existing USE rope→old_well interaction), +20 unlocking the catacombs gate (added to the existing USE iron_key→crypt_gate interaction). Each award rides a condition that flips on first use, so the score can't be farmed; the intended path reaches the full 35. No flags' meaning / items / exits / gating / reachable endings change — only the score var now accrues.
- Verified LIVE through the MCP tools (seed 73, full solution walk): score 0 → 5 (read headstone) → 15 (tie rope) → 35 (open gate) → ending_victory at 35/35; the headstone READ correctly drops out of the legal-action set after the first read (no farming).
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0; 250 tests pass (+4 new). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): sealed_crypt content hash → 11dab666f3826615b99ecf8c97ce502fa0f02f16d0454e420737144acba06252. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no test edit was needed.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0013_sealed_crypt_scoring.yaml + tests/regression/sealed_crypt_scoring.test.ts (4 cases: score climbs 0→5→15→35 and is 35/35 at ending_victory; the headstone +5 is one-time / READ drops out, no farming; re-tying the well does not re-award; meta.max_score is 35 and the pack validates so SCORE_UNREACHABLE cannot fire).
- DEFERRED this cycle (logged in the bug artifact): the sexton dialogue topic-menu re-ask — "crypt"/"well" toggle forever instead of exhausting. A clean fix needs a NEW engine field `conditions` on DialogueTopicSchema (filter topics in enumerateActions + guard the ASK resolution) PLUS validator awareness that a gated topic must not make a dialogue node non-terminating (cf. broken-fixtures/parser_dialogue_nonterminating.yaml) — a generic, reusable engine feature worth its own dedicated cycle, deliberately out of scope for this one focused content change.
- Next suggested focus: (a) the deferred dialogue-topic-conditions engine feature (above) — fixes the sexton loop here and is reusable across every NPC in every pack; (b) sunken_barrow's implicit goal / no stated quest, toothless combat balance, and repeatable lever action after success; (c) the assessor's content_new candidate (author a new rpg pack, 1/2) to broaden the game; (d) the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap. Keep rotating the playtest target.

### Cycle result — conditional dialogue topics; sexton ping-pong fixed (bug_0014)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws) onto **The Sealed Crypt** to cash in the engine feature deferred at the end of bug_0013 (and logged across bug_0010 / bug_0012): the sexton dialogue topic re-ask. This was the highest-value standing item — a generic, reusable engine capability, not another micro-polish.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 91, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T07-03-45-157Z/playtest.md. Solved to ending_victory (score 35/35); clarity 4/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Re-confirmed the deferred flaw exactly (report §A/§B): (A) the sexton's two info topics mutually re-enable, so __dlg_sexton oscillates 2↔3 and you can re-ask the same questions forever, each re-firing the same set_flag + identical narration; (B) the heard_*_rumor flags those topics set gate nothing — dead payload.
- Improvement (one, **engine_rule + content**): generic conditional dialogue topics. (1) src/parser/schema.ts: DialogueTopicSchema gains optional `conditions: ConditionSchema[]` — `.optional()` not `.default([])`, so a topic without a gate compiles byte-identically and no other pack's content hash drifts (bug_0010's lesson). (2) src/parser/legal_actions.ts: the ASK resolution now returns `topic.conditions`, so a gated topic is filtered from the legal set (via the `option` helper) AND re-checked by the engine — a told-once info topic retires itself. (3) src/validate/parser_validator.ts: the dialogue TERMINATION analysis now counts only UNCONDITIONAL topics as guaranteed escape routes (gated gotos excluded from the reachability graph; only an ungated `end` topic marks an end node), so a node whose every exit is gated is flagged DIALOGUE_NONTERMINATING — content must always keep an ungated way out. CONTENT: sealed_crypt's two sexton info topics gated `not_flag heard_crypt_rumor` / `not_flag heard_well_rumor` (each retires after one telling; ungated `bye` keeps every node terminating; the previously-inert rumor flags now drive the gating, fixing B too).
- Verified LIVE through the engine (the running MCP server process predates the schema edit, so the authoritative live check is the regression test, which exercises the same enumerateActions/makeStep/resolveParserAction path the server uses): greeting offers {crypt, well, bye}; after ask_crypt only {well, bye}; after ask_well only {bye}; a retired topic forced via step() is rejected by the engine re-check; bye always ends the talk.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 254 tests pass (+4: 3 regression cases + 1 new validator fixture). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): sealed_crypt 11dab666… → e03a1622d5966f06702db0e13d2f7c612721a3ed62adc8b31ebaa8cd66f11451. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no test edit was needed.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0014_dialogue_topic_conditions.yaml + tests/regression/dialogue_topic_conditions.test.ts + the new negative fixture content/broken-fixtures/parser_dialogue_gated_nonterminating.yaml (wired into tests/unit/parser_validator.test.ts).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) The Sealed Crypt's design thinness — short, linear, single-solution, one ending, no failure states, hint-giver positioned past the point of usefulness (move the sexton earlier, or add a branch/failure state); (b) sunken_barrow's implicit goal / no stated quest, toothless combat balance, and repeatable lever action after success; (c) the assessor's content_new candidate (author a new rpg pack, 1/2) to broaden the game; (d) the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap. The dialogue-topic-conditions feature is now available to every NPC in every pack. Keep rotating the playtest target.

### Cycle result — one-shot skill-check lever retires after success (bug_0015)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws) onto the RPG pack **The Sunken Barrow** to cash in the long-deferred backlog item logged across bug_0011/bug_0014: the "repeatable lever action after success".
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 17, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T07-13-47-984Z/playtest.md. Solved to ending_victory and probed the no-iron-bar gate (correctly hidden, no soft-lock); clarity 4/5, enjoyment 2/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "a clean, bug-light mechanics demo" — very short. Its #1 concrete finding (report §5, items 1 & 4): after a successful might check levers the slab aside (quest_stage barrow/slab_moved, stair open), `use iron bar on stone slab` STAYS available, re-rolls the check, re-narrates "the slab grinds aside" on an already-open slab, AND — because the re-roll can FAIL — can narrate "the slab does not give" while the bared stair stands open. A genuine player-facing contradiction, not mere polish — the only finding this cycle that produces an actual contradiction, so the highest-value real-defect fix (the rest are framing/scoring polish, deferred).
- Improvement (one, **content + engine_rule** — coherent, both layers of the same defect): retire the lever after success at BOTH the enumeration and resolution layers. (1) CONTENT: the stone_slab USE interaction is gated `conditions: [ { none_of: [ { quest_stage: { quest: barrow, stage: slab_moved } } ] } ]`, so once the slab is moved the action drops out of the legal set (enumeration already honours interaction conditions via resolveParserAction). (2) ENGINE (generic, pack-agnostic): src/rpg/runner.ts's skill-check resolve branch now calls evalConditions(it.conditions, state) before resolving — its own comment always CLAIMED it enforced "meeting conditions" but the code only checked the item was held, so a forced/stale step could still re-roll a gated check. Now a gated skill-check interaction is enforced even against a forced step; the fix applies to any gated skill check in any current or future pack. No flags' meaning / items / exits / gating of other actions / reachable endings change — only the now-solved lever retires.
- Verified LIVE through the engine (seed 17): lever offered before success → leaves the legal set after slab_moved → a forced re-lever is rejected by resolve() (engine guard) → ending_victory still fires.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 258 tests pass (+4). Deliberate, surfaced re-pin (NOT a weakening): the gate genuinely changed sunken_barrow's content hash ca34eeb6… → 018c6df5…, so traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json content_hash was updated to match. The trace's expected_final_hash is UNCHANGED — the canonical victory levers the slab exactly once (the gate only blocks redundant re-levers, never the first), so the Stage-4 victory replays to the identical final hash (rpg_barrow_trace.test.ts green). No test source pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0015_rpg_skillcheck_retires.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_skillcheck_retires.test.ts (4 cases: lever legal pre-success / retires from the legal set after slab_moved; a forced re-lever is rejected by resolve() so it can never re-roll; pre-success the gate is inert / the first lever still resolves to the skill check; the canonical victory still reaches ending_victory).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed — all on sunken_barrow, from the playtest §3/§5): (a) Entry Hall stale prose — "A rusted iron bar lies among fallen rubble" persists after the bar is taken (now a one-variant reactive-room content fix, same shape as bug_0011); (b) no combat reward / scoring — max_score is 0 and stays 0 through a kill and a win (wire Stage-3 scoring like sealed_crypt bug_0013); (c) auto-win on entering the relic chamber with no "take the circlet" agency, and no opening quest/objective line (framing). Then the assessor's content_new candidate (author a new rpg pack, 1/2) to broaden the game, and the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap. Keep rotating the playtest target.

### Cycle result — Sunken Barrow scoring brought to life (bug_0016)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws — the standing guidance is "keep rotating") onto the RPG pack **The Sunken Barrow** to cash in the long-deferred backlog item logged across bug_0011/bug_0015: no combat reward / scoring (max_score 0, stays 0 through a kill and a win).
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 43 (+seed 7 probe), report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T07-23-12-130Z/playtest.md. Solved to ending_victory and probed the no-bar gate (correctly hidden, no soft-lock); clarity 4/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "mechanically trustworthy; just thin and missing its scoring payoff." Its #1 concrete finding, hit TWICE (report §4 + §5): the score stays 0 the entire game including the victory screen despite a relic-grab finale — a "missing reward hook" / "vestigial field" that "undercuts the payoff."
- Improvement (one, **content + validator** — the content fix plus the one validator-correctness fix the RPG layer needs): wired Stage-3 scoring exactly as bug_0013 did for sealed_crypt. CONTENT: meta.max_score 0 → 50 plus three one-time milestone awards on the real beats — +10 in barrow_wight on_defeat (slay the guard-wight), +15 in the stone_slab skill_check on_success (lever the slab), +25 in relic_chamber on_enter (claim the circlet). Each fires once: the wight dies once; the lever retires after success (bug_0015 gate + engine guard); the relic chamber's win ends the game on entry so its on_enter can't re-fire. VALIDATOR (generic, RPG-wide): the SCORE_UNREACHABLE upper bound is computed in validateParser from allEffects(pack), which scans only room on_enter / object-interaction effects / npc dialogue — NOT the RPG-only award branches (enemy on_defeat, skill_check on_success/on_failure). So +10 and +15 were invisible to the bound and the pack false-flagged SCORE_UNREACHABLE (confirmed live: the stale MCP server reported "max_score is 50 but at most 25 point(s) can ever be awarded"). Fix: ValidateParserOptions gains optional `extraScoreAwards?: number`; validateRpg sums the inc_var-score awards in rpgRuntimeEffects and folds them into the bound. These sites are genuinely reachable (COMBAT_UNWINNABLE / SKILL_CHECK_IMPOSSIBLE already guard them), so this SHARPENS the conservative bound — it can only raise totalAwards — never weakens a check. Default 0 ⇒ every non-RPG validation byte-identical.
- Verified LIVE through the engine source path (seed 1 victory route, the running MCP server predates the validator edit so it is NOT authoritative): score 0 → 10 (wight) → 25 (slab) → 50 (relic) → ending_victory at 50/50. The headstone-style one-time gating holds (wight dead ⇒ no re-attack; lever retired ⇒ no USE; relic chamber ends the game).
- Evidence: validate green 0/0 (source CLI); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 261 tests pass (+3). Deliberate, surfaced re-pin (NOT a weakening): scoring genuinely changes game state, so BOTH fields of traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json changed — content_hash 018c6df5… → ba69fcdd… AND (unlike the bug_0011 text-only variants) expected_final_hash 007fdea0… → 871ddf3e… (the canonical victory now ends with score 50 in state; rpg_barrow_trace.test.ts green). No test source pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0016_rpg_scoring.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_scoring.test.ts (3 cases: score climbs 0→10→25→50 and is 50/50 at ending_victory; each award one-time — wight dead / lever retired / relic ends the game; the validator fold-in is real — validateParser(pack) under-counts to 25 and fires SCORE_UNREACHABLE while validateRpg(pack) passes clean).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed — all on sunken_barrow, from the playtest): (a) Entry Hall stale prose — "A rusted iron bar lies among fallen rubble" persists after the bar is taken (a one-variant reactive-room content fix, same shape as bug_0011; the bug_0015 deferred item, not re-surfaced this run only because the playtester did not re-LOOK after taking the bar); (b) skill-check grindiness — DC 12 vs might 3 ≈ 55%/attempt with no failure cost (balance/framing); (c) auto-win on entering the relic chamber with no "take the circlet" agency and no opening quest/objective line (framing). Then the assessor's content_new candidate (author a new rpg pack, 1/2) to broaden the game, and the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap. Keep rotating the playtest target.

### Cycle result — Clockwork Heist "Truth" thread made path-agnostic (bug_0017)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws — the standing guidance is "keep rotating") onto the assessor's rank-2 candidate **The Clockwork Heist** — last blind-played at bug_0008 and carrying a logged backlog of path-presumptive text (the bug_0008 deferred next-focus items).
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 37, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T07-35-13-159Z/playtest.md. Reached all three endings cleanly (Truth via the crawlspace, Rich and Caught via the vault); clarity 4/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "solid short piece with seams." Its top friction (§4 + §5) confirmed the bug_0008 deferred items exactly: the "Truth" thread was written assuming the player had already walked the vault route, so it reads as a contradiction on the crawlspace route — the very route a curious first-timer hits by examining the clock first.
- Top finding (the only set producing actual on-screen contradictions, so the highest-value real-defect fix; the rest — dangling guard clue, stale kitchen text, clock-route shortcut — are framing/structural, deferred): two route-blind narration lines. (1) crawlspace: "...that same sealed letter" presumes a vault visit the player has never made — and the crawlspace is ONLY ever reached before/instead of the vault (entering the vault ends the game on the next choice), so "that same" is ALWAYS wrong. (2) ending_truth: "You leave the gold" presumes the player stood in the vault and saw gold, false on the crawlspace route.
- Improvement (one, **content** layer — same class as watchtower bug_0006/bug_0007): made the Truth thread path-agnostic. crawlspace "that same sealed letter" → "a single sealed letter, addressed to no one" (matching the vault's own description of the letter). ending_truth "You leave the gold and take the truth" → "There was gold enough in this house to vanish on, but you leave it untouched and carry the truth out instead" — the gold is now framed as a property of the manor (true on every route) rather than something the player physically stood over. No flags / items / choices / gating / reachable endings change — text only.
- Verified LIVE through the engine (seed 37): crawlspace route → ending_truth; vault take-letter route → ending_truth; vault grab-gold → ending_rich; force-door → ending_caught — all four endings still fire; crawlspace text no longer says "that same" / still introduces the sealed letter; ending_truth no longer says "leave the gold" / keeps the truth-over-riches moral.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 266 tests pass (+5 new). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): clockwork_heist 79dead14… → 1b121b1a67e3b7aee6d1cd53c10964f5de9b5eb8184eccfdfe74a9dfdf46b27a. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no test edit was needed.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0017_clockwork_truth_path_coherence.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_truth_path_coherence.test.ts (5 cases: crawlspace text stands alone / still introduces the letter; crawlspace route still reaches ending_truth; ending_truth drops "leave the gold" but keeps its moral; the vault take-letter route also reaches ending_truth; reachability unchanged — ending_rich + ending_caught still fire).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed — all on clockwork_heist, from the playtest §5): (a) the Steward's Study ledger clue ("a guard walks the gallery on the hour") sets read_ledger but pays off nowhere — no turn/time counter exists, so the guard never appears (wire a time-pressure mechanic, or reframe as atmosphere); (b) stale kitchen text after take_pick — CYOA scenes have no reactive-text `variants` feature (that lives on the parser/RPG RoomSchema, bug_0010), so this would need an engine feature to bring reactive room text to CYOA — a generic capability worth its own cycle; (c) structural: examining the clock first fast-tracks the richest ending in four clicks, bypassing the puzzle (a design choice, not a defect). Then the assessor's content_new candidate (author a new rpg pack, 1/2) to broaden the game, and the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap. Keep rotating the playtest target.

CONTENT: clockwork_heist kitchen gains a `when: [{ has_item: lockpick }]` variant reporting the bare spot where the picks lay. No flags / items / choices / gating / reachable endings change — only narrated text reacts. The feature is now available to every scene in every CYOA pack.

### Cycle result — the "guard on the hour" clue finally pays off (bug_0019)
- ROTATED THE PLAYTEST TARGET off the assessor's rank-1 watchtower_road (heavily polished bug_0002–0007; its "unvisited" scenes are the deterministic coverage bot's known puzzle-solving limit, not pack flaws — the standing guidance is "keep rotating") onto the assessor's rank-2 candidate **The Clockwork Heist**, to cash in THE single most-repeatedly-logged standing gap (bug_0008 §5(a) / bug_0017 §5(a) / bug_0018 §5(a)): the Steward's Study ledger clue "a guard walks the gallery on the hour" pays off nowhere — a Chekhov's gun that implies a stealth/timing mechanic the game lacked.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 61, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T07-53-15-619Z/playtest.md. Reached all 3 endings (Truth via crawlspace, Rich + Caught via vault); clarity 4/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: polished and bug-free, but its #1 design flaw (report §5, item 1) is exactly the standing item — "I paced Gallery↔Foyer and lingered; the guard never appears on any route. A Chekhov's gun that points nowhere." Its closing line: "Giving the guard an actual consequence ... would take this from good to genuinely tight."
- Improvement (one, **content** layer — NO engine change; the core inc_var / var_gte / var_lte DSL already exists): wired a time-pressure deadline that makes the clue real. meta.vars_init seeds `ticks: 0`; the upstairs rooms (kitchen, gallery, study) inc_var ticks on_enter so moving through the manor advances the clock (the ground-floor foyer is safe and does not tick; in-room self-goto actions like inspect_clock/take_pick/read_ledger do NOT re-tick, because the engine fires on_enter only on a genuine location change). The gallery (`landing`) gains reactive `variants` (the bug_0018 CYOA feature): rising tension at ticks≥4, the watchman's patrol at ticks≥6. On the hour (ticks≥6) the safe `approach_vault` crossing is gated `any_of:[var_lte ticks 5, has_flag read_ledger]` — available before the hour OR once you've read the ledger and know his rounds (THE PAYOFF for the clue) — while a clearly-labelled gamble `cross_to_vault_blind` (var_gte ticks 6 + not_flag read_ledger) appears and leads to a NEW fourth ending `ending_patrol` ("Caught on the Hour"). The safe `enter_study`/`back_down` exits always remain (no soft-lock); the gamble is an informed risk (the prose shows the patrol), not a gotcha. The read_ledger journal now states the mechanical payoff outright. Efficient/clue-reading play is never punished (ticks stay low); only dawdling triggers the guard, and reading the optional clue is what lets you beat it.
- Verified LIVE through the MCP tools (seed 61; content-only change ⇒ live server authoritative): ticks surfaces and increments on room moves only; gallery prose flips base→tension(t4-5)→patrol(t6+); at t7 with no ledger read, approach_vault gated out + cross_to_vault_blind offered + safe exits remain → ending_patrol; at t7 WITH the ledger read, approach_vault still safe + the gamble absent.
- Coverage net-neutrality (run_playtest, 100 runs each): COVERAGE strategy byte-identical to the committed pack (ended 99/unfinished 1; ending_truth 98, ending_caught 1; vault unvisited; the lone study↔landing max_steps oscillation is the coverage heuristic's pre-existing planning limit, documented since bug_0008, NOT introduced here). RANDOM strategy now reaches all FOUR endings (caught 37, truth 43, patrol 16, rich 4) with zero suspicious paths — the new deadline ending is genuinely reachable and the vault endings still are.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 275 tests pass (+5). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): clockwork_heist 587116384afa… → 6c8e8e4583d003c1b8a629bef5be8ca31343203850caedb496fecfbc901d1698. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no test edit was needed.

### Cycle result — the foyer's namesake clock now feels the deadline too (bug_0020)
- KEPT the playtest target on the assessor's rank-1 candidate **The Clockwork Heist** (its 2 coverage-"unreached" endings + 1 unvisited location are the deterministic bot's planning limit — random strategy reaches all 4 endings, documented since bug_0019 — not pack flaws). With watchtower heavily polished (bug_0002–0007) and every pack already blind-verified, clockwork is the live target carrying the freshest backlog.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 67, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T08-10-22-369Z/playtest.md. Reached ALL FOUR endings (Truth, Rich, Caught, Caught on the Hour); clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Verdict: "mechanically clean, narratively coherent, and genuinely replayable"; the bug_0019 deadline "lands perfectly." Its ONLY concrete finding (report §4 + §5): the bug_0019 time-pressure urgency text is scoped to the **gallery** only — the **foyer**, which the opening prose says houses the namesake great clock ("vast as a coffin") right against its far wall, shows no urgency at any tick count, so a player who climbs up, burns the clock, and retreats to the foyer gets no cue the hour is upon them. "Slightly undercuts the omnipresent-clock motif."
- Improvement (one, **content** layer — NO engine change; uses the existing CYOA `variants` feature from bug_0018): the foyer gains two reactive variants, higher threshold first (first-match-wins), mirroring the gallery's escalation — a tension variant at `var_gte ticks 4` ("the great hall clock grinds toward the hour ... your time in the manor is running short") and an on-the-hour variant at `var_gte ticks 6` ("the great hall clock has come round to the hour ... and somewhere up the stair, on the gallery landing, a watchman's tread answers it"). The foyer stays safe ground: it still never advances `ticks` and its choices are unchanged; only narrated text reacts, and the on-the-hour variant points the danger UP the stair (where the patrol is) without falsely making the foyer itself perilous. No flags / items / choices / gating / reachable endings change.
- Verified LIVE through the engine source path (seed 67, the regression drives the same initStateForPack/makeStep/buildObservation path the MCP server uses): a quick out-and-back keeps the foyer at base text; dawdling to ticks 5 then retreating shows the "running short" tension (not the hour cue); to ticks 7 then retreating shows the on-the-hour watchman variant; all four endings still fire.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 279 tests pass (+4 new). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): clockwork_heist 6c8e8e4583d0… → 626c00d93cc3730937afd2eb2a9d2f048915206c483c8f822851bf7e3da5ba87. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin was needed. One surfaced test-source update: the bug_0018 backward-compat case asserted `foyer.variants` is undefined — repointed onto `vault_door` (still variant-less); the assertion is unchanged, just on a still-variant-less scene.

### Cycle result — BROADENED the game: authored the 2nd RPG pack, The Cold Forge (bug_0021)
- Ran the MANDATED blind playtest of the assessor's rank-1 target, the CYOA pack The Clockwork Heist (fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 83, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T08-17-46-152Z/playtest.md). It came back PRISTINE: all four endings reached, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO concrete on-screen defects — the only note was the long-deferred STRUCTURAL pacing item (the clock route reaches the "Truth" twist in 3 clicks), which the playtester explicitly called "isn't a bug." This confirms clockwork is polished past diminishing returns (six cycles: bug_0008/0017/0018/0019/0020).
- DECISION — rotated to content_new (justified per protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value"): the bug_0020 durable note "strongly considers the assessor's content_new candidate (author a new rpg pack) to broaden the game (only 1 rpg pack exists)." With clockwork clean and every existing pack already blind-verified, broadening the game is higher value than a 6th clockwork micro-change. The game's genuine weak point was breadth: only ONE RPG pack (The Sunken Barrow), repeatedly judged thin/short with "toothless" combat and an implicit goal.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, content_new): authored content/rpg/pack/cold_forge.yaml ("The Cold Forge"), a tight Stage-4 micro-dungeon on the proven core (one seeded fight, one seeded might check, Stage-3 milestone scoring to max_score 50, reactive room text, a save-recoverable death ending, retire-after-success lever — bug_0015). Auto-discovered by list_stories' directory scan (no registry edit). It deliberately BROADENS beyond the barrow and answers the barrow's standing critiques: (a) a TALKING NPC with CONDITIONAL one-shot dialogue (the bug_0014 feature no existing RPG pack used) whose counsel is MECHANICAL — asking how the sentinel fails grants a one-time +2 attack blessing that genuinely eases the fight (no dead-payload rumor flags); (b) combat with REAL TEETH (barrow's was "toothless") — without the buff the slag-sentinel CAN kill the player (death ending ending_fallen genuinely reachable; engine sweep: seeds 2 & 4 no-buff → death), yet the fight is provably winnable from base stats (validator COMBAT check) and a fall is save-recoverable; (c) an EXPLICIT goal stated in the opening room.
- Design balance (authoring engine sweep, seeds 1-20): WITH the spirit's buff every seed wins comfortably (2-3 rounds, healthy HP); WITHOUT it the fight is a genuine gamble (seeds 2 & 4 kill the player). The optional NPC counsel turns a coin-flip-ish fight into a reliable win — exploration and clue-following are mechanically rewarded. The DC-12-vs-might-3 check is retryable with no penalty so a bad roll never dead-ends.
- VERIFICATION (two independent blind reads + the engine + the validator): (1) validate green (0/0) under the full RPG validator — the AMBIGUOUS_ALIAS check caught a shared "iron" alias on first pass; fixed by narrowing the grate's aliases, re-validated clean. (2) A SECOND blind LLM playtest of the NEW pack (fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, seed 5, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T08-17-46-152Z/playtest_cold_forge.md): clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, no functional bugs, victory on both the talk-to-NPC and skip-NPC routes; confirmed the NPC buff is mechanically meaningful and combat is tighter without it. (3) full `npm run health` exit 0; 283 tests pass (+4).
- SURFACED, NOT GAMED — the assessor tests: adding the 2nd RPG pack brought every mode to TARGET_PER_MODE (cyoa/parser/rpg = 2/2/2), so the assessor CORRECTLY stopped raising any content_new candidate — the success condition of this work. I deliberately did NOT inflate TARGET_PER_MODE to keep the stale tests green (that would game the check). Instead I updated two tests/unit/assessor.test.ts cases to encode the new truth: "spans content and non-content work" now asserts content_fix + repo (content_new exhausted); the former "flags the thin rpg mode" case became "raises no content_new candidate once every mode meets its breadth target" (locks that meeting the target legitimately disarms the nag; raising TARGET_PER_MODE re-arms it). No production threshold weakened.
- New pack content hash: e5e280296d6dfdfd229a191ecb009ed7a7f18f0e242b2e56c1b9dced11b81a63 (unpinned; only watchtower_road is pinned). Wired into `npm run health` (added `validate -- content/rpg/pack/cold_forge.yaml`, mirroring sunken_barrow; not coverage-gated since the deterministic bot can't solve seeded combat + skill check + dialogue, the same known limit as the barrow).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0021_cold_forge_new_pack.yaml + tests/regression/cold_forge_pack.test.ts (4 cases: validates green; buffed seed-1 route → ending_victory 50/50 with attack raised 4→6 and score climbing at each beat; the +2 topic is one-shot — ask_sentinel retires, can't be farmed; no-buff seed-2 fight kills the player → ending_fallen, locking combat teeth + death-ending reachability).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): the game now has 2 packs per mode (breadth target met), so content_new is disarmed until an operator raises TARGET_PER_MODE. Remaining levers: (a) the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap (lint is still just tsc — the assessor's standing repo candidate); (b) reactive OBJECT descriptions — a generic engine feature (cold_forge's grate, like alchemists' strongbox, keeps a static object name/examine after its state changes; the ROOM text already reacts via variants); (c) clockwork's remaining STRUCTURAL/pacing item (the 3-click clock→crawlspace shortcut to "Truth" bypasses the heist) — gate/re-flavor so it complements rather than pre-empts the vault. Keep rotating the playtest target; consider giving the new Cold Forge a second blind pass on a lethal seed in a later cycle.

### Cycle result — the 3-click crawlspace shortcut closed; the "Truth" is now earned (bug_0022)
- KEPT the playtest target on the assessor's rank-1 candidate **The Clockwork Heist** (its 2 coverage-"unreached" endings + 1 unvisited location are the deterministic bot's planning limit — random strategy reaches all 4 endings, documented since bug_0019 — not pack flaws). With watchtower heavily polished and every pack already blind-verified, clockwork is the live target carrying the freshest backlog.
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 29, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T08-38-02-056Z/playtest.md. Reached all four endings; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. Its ONLY enjoyment-docking finding (report §4 + §5) was the long-deferred STRUCTURAL item (logged across bug_0019 §4(a) / bug_0020 / bug_0021 note (c)): "The crawlspace route ends the whole game in 3 actions. A first-timer who examines the clock first (a natural choice, and the leftmost option) is routed straight to an ending before discovering the vault, lockpicks, ledger, or moral choice — the game's best content... Reachable in one click too few, that secret ending undersells an otherwise polished and genuinely satisfying piece." This cycle that finding was the SOLE reason enjoyment wasn't 5/5 — so it is the highest-value improvement, not a deferrable nicety.
- Improvement (one, **content** layer — NO engine change): made the crawlspace strongbox a true lockbox requiring the **lockpick** (the heist's signature item, found in the kitchen), so the secret route now COMPLEMENTS rather than pre-empts the vault. (1) Removed the brute-force `pry_strongbox` ("Lever the rusted strongbox open by hand") — it was both the path of least resistance AND a latent coherence crack: the only place in the manor where forcing worked, contradicting the brass plate's central motif "yields only to a steady hand — never to force." `open_strongbox` (has_item lockpick) is now the only way in. (2) Added a no-pick nudge `study_strongbox` (not_item lockpick) that narrates the locked box + the never-to-force rule, then routes OUT to the foyer — a real scene change is progress, never a self-loop (the bug_0005/bug_0008 lesson). (3) Added a foyer `enter_panel` re-entry, gated has_flag found_passage AND has_item lockpick, so the crawlspace stays reachable after you leave to fetch the pick without giving a tool-less player a way to bounce into a locked box. (4) Reworded the crawlspace base text so the strongbox reads "locked fast" (justifying the pick) while still introducing the "single sealed letter" (preserving bug_0017's coherence assertion). The truth, on EITHER route, now sits behind a picked lock — elegant symmetry. The 3-click freebie becomes a real mini-heist: discover the panel → realize you need a tool → traverse to the kitchen → return → crack it. No reachable endings change (ending_truth still reachable via crawlspace AND vault take_letter); no soft-lock (lockpick always in the kitchen; foyer is safe ground).
- Verified LIVE through the MCP tools (content-only change ⇒ the server reloads the pack on new_game, authoritative; seed 29): discovering the panel pick-less shows only study_strongbox + back_crawl (pry_strongbox gone); study_strongbox narrates + moves crawlspace→foyer (no self-loop); after fetching the pick, enter_panel re-entry appears and open_strongbox → ending_truth.
- Coverage net-neutrality (run_playtest, 100 runs each): RANDOM now ended 100/100 reaching ALL FOUR endings (caught 47, patrol 32, truth 14, rich 7) with ALL scenes visited and ZERO suspicious paths — strictly BETTER than the prior baseline (vault now visited). A first cut (re-entry gated on found_passage alone) introduced a transient foyer↔crawlspace coverage-bot oscillation; the run_playtest coverage pass caught it and the enter_panel lockpick gate removed it BEFORE commit, leaving only the PRE-EXISTING study↔landing oscillation + vault-unvisited (the coverage heuristic's documented planning limit since bug_0008/bug_0019, NOT introduced here).
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 288 tests pass (+5). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): clockwork_heist 626c00d93cc3… → a5f76f8a3943cdbdc7568b677ef56a493a0899334890b4fdd2d4c1c05637250d. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin was needed. Four surfaced test-route updates (legitimate flow updates, mirroring bug_0007): the old brute-force route ["inspect_clock","pry_panel","pry_strongbox"] served as an ending_truth reachability check in clockwork_truth_path_coherence / clockwork_guard_patrol_deadline / clockwork_foyer_reactive_text / cyoa_reactive_scene_text — each repointed to the new lockpick-gated route.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0022_clockwork_crawlspace_lockpick_gate.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_crawlspace_lockpick_gate.test.ts (5 cases: pick-less discovery offers no brute-force / no-key way in; the nudge narrates + MOVES to the foyer taking no item/flag; re-entry appears only once the pick is held — no tool-less bounce; with the pick the crawlspace offers open_strongbox and the route reaches ending_truth; reachability unchanged — vault take-letter truth + rich + caught all still fire).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): clockwork is now polished bug_0008/0017/0018/0019/0020/0022 with NO remaining concrete on-screen defects AND the last STRUCTURAL pacing item closed. Remaining levers, per-pack polish having reached deep diminishing returns: (a) the repo/ESLint+Prettier tooling gap (lint is still just tsc — the assessor's standing rank-3 repo candidate); (b) reactive OBJECT descriptions — a generic engine feature (clockwork has none left, but cold_forge's grate / alchemists' strongbox keep a static object name/examine after state changes; the ROOM text already reacts via variants); (c) the minor continuity quirk of the sealed letter appearing in BOTH the crawlspace strongbox and the vault cushion. Strongly consider the repo tooling gap or the reactive-object engine feature next — both broaden value beyond a 7th clockwork micro-change. Keep rotating the playtest target.

### Cycle result — reactive OBJECT descriptions come to the engine (bug_0023)
- Ran the MANDATED blind playtest of the assessor's rank-1 target, the CYOA pack **The Clockwork Heist** (fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 97, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T08-53-07-908Z/playtest.md). It came back PRISTINE for the SEVENTH straight cycle: all five endings reached (Truth via vault AND via the crawlspace strongbox, Rich, Caught, Caught on the Hour), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken state. Its only two notes were NOT concrete defects: (a) a stylistic residual of the already-applied bug_0017 path-agnostic ending text; (b) a legibility uncertainty about whether reading the ledger grants a safe crossing — the mechanic IS present and correct (bug_0019 `any_of:[ticks<=5, has_flag read_ledger]` gate; locked by clockwork_guard_patrol_deadline.test.ts), the player just never read it. Clockwork is polished past diminishing returns (bug_0008/0017/0018/0019/0020/0022 + this run).
- DECISION — rotated to an engine_feature (justified per protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value" + the standing bug_0020/bug_0022 guidance "strongly consider … the reactive-object engine feature"): closed the generic engine feature the **bug_0021 artifact EXPLICITLY deferred for "its own cycle"** — reactive OBJECT descriptions. Higher value than a 7th clockwork micro-change: a cross-pack capability that fixes a real on-screen contradiction.
- THE GAP: RoomSchema gained reactive `variants` (bug_0010/0011) so a ROOM narrates state it changed, but the OBJECT examine path ("look at <thing>" → LOOK target) still fell back to the static `description`. In **The Cold Forge**, after the slag grate is levered open (quest_stage forge/grate_open) the ROOM reads "stands open" yet examining the grate ITSELF still said "welded shut by cooled slag" — contradicting both the room and the player's own levering action. Same static-examine gap affects alchemists_tower's strongbox (deferred).
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **engine + content**): (1) ENGINE, generic/pack-agnostic — src/parser/schema.ts adds ObjectVariantSchema (object analogue of RoomVariantSchema) + ObjectSchema.variants declared `.optional()` (NOT `.default([])`) so variant-less objects compile byte-identically and keep their content hashes (the bug_0010 backward-compat rule); src/parser/model.ts adds pure helper `objectDescription(object, state)` mirroring roomDescription (first matching `when` wins, else base); src/parser/legal_actions.ts routes the single LOOK render site through it. The RPG runner reuses resolveParserAction, so the feature reaches BOTH parser and RPG packs from one site. NO validator change — object variants carry no effects, so allEffects() and every bound built on it are unchanged. (2) CONTENT — cold_forge's stone_grate gains a `variants` entry gated on grate_open ("levered up off its lip and standing open … a low way drops down"). Examine-only: no flag/item/score/exit/gating/reachable-ending change.
- Verified LIVE through the engine render site the regression exercises (resolveParserAction LOOK→stone_grate via initStateForRpgPack/enumerateRpgActions/makeStep — the MCP server's path): examining the grate narrates "welded shut" before levering, "standing open" (no "welded shut") after grate_open; the canonical victory route still reaches ending_victory at 50/50.
- Evidence: lint green; validate green 0/0 (all four packs); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 291 tests pass (+3 new). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): cold_forge e5e28029… → c8ac35e220c9d88cc64962bcd7eb7d0700b68d231e2215d79329f1837e0f5e54. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin was needed.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0023_reactive_object_descriptions.yaml + tests/regression/reactive_object_descriptions.test.ts (3 cases: variant-less object → base text under arbitrary states + `variants` undefined / backward-compat; varianted object → first-match-wins variant text else base; LIVE on cold_forge — grate examine flips welded-shut→standing-open at grate_open, victory still 50/50). Closes bug_0021 deferred[0].
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) APPLY the now-available object `variants` to alchemists_tower's strongbox (still examines as a brass-locked box after being unlocked + emptied; the ROOM already reacts via bug_0012) — low-risk content polish; (b) the repo/ESLint + Prettier tooling gap (lint is still just tsc — the assessor's standing rank-3 repo candidate); (c) the minor continuity quirk of clockwork's sealed letter appearing in BOTH the crawlspace strongbox and the vault cushion. Per-pack content polish has reached deep diminishing returns across all packs — favour the repo tooling gap or another generic engine/UX capability next. Keep rotating the playtest target.

This fixes a REAL on-screen contradiction, higher value than a 9th clockwork micro-note.
- THE GAP (confirmed LIVE via MCP, sess_3): bug_0012 made the Cluttered Study ROOM react (after the iron key is held it reads "open and empty ... its brass lock sprung"), and bug_0023 shipped reactive OBJECT descriptions — but DEFERRED applying it here. So the strongbox OBJECT still examined as the static "A squat iron strongbox with a brass lock" after being unlocked, opened, and emptied — contradicting the room's own prose and the player's actions. I navigated to the study, unlocked + opened the box, and `look at iron strongbox` still returned the brass-locked text.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — NO engine/validator/schema change; the bug_0023 engine feature already exists): strongbox gains a `variants` entry gated on `has_item: iron_key` — the SAME durable "unlocked and emptied" proxy the study room uses (the closed condition DSL, src/core/conditions.ts, has no object-open predicate), so the examine flips in lockstep with the room rather than introducing a new lag. New text: "The squat iron strongbox sits open and empty, its brass lock sprung and its lid thrown back — nothing left inside but the faint smell of old iron." Examine-only: no flag / item / score / exit / interaction / gating / reachable-ending change.
- Verified LIVE through the engine render site the regression drives (resolveParserAction LOOK→strongbox via initStateForParserPack / enumerateActions / makeStep — the MCP server's path): the examine reads the base box before the iron key is held (even after unlock+open, mirroring the room), flips to "open and empty / brass lock sprung" once the key is taken, and never again reads "with a brass lock"; the canonical brew route still wins at 35/35.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 294 tests pass (+3 new). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): alchemists_tower 8302bfdfd0e9… → 04d4e8be75dec47d8b5a92a36d2606b6b0458df116bf8a9ee2d5f8dcc99dcd1e. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin was needed.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0024_alchemists_strongbox_reactive_examine.yaml + tests/regression/alchemists_strongbox_reactive_examine.test.ts (3 cases: examine flips locked→open-and-empty in lockstep with the iron-key proxy via the live LOOK render path; a variant-less object — the spellbook — returns base text under arbitrary states + `variants` undefined for backward-compat; reachability — canonical brew route still reaches ending_cured at full score). Closes bug_0023 deferred[0].
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the repo/ESLint + Prettier tooling gap — lint is still just tsc, the assessor's standing rank-3 repo candidate; now the strongest remaining lever since every pack is blind-verified and per-pack content polish has reached deep diminishing returns. (b) clockwork's ending_truth prose presumes the vault route even when reached via the crawlspace strongbox (cosmetic continuity; surfaced again in this cycle's playtest §5). Keep rotating the playtest target.

### Cycle result — the "Truth" ending no longer presumes the vault/gold route (bug_0025)
- Ran the MANDATED blind playtest of the assessor's rank-1 target, the CYOA pack **The Clockwork Heist** (fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seeds 101/202/303/404/505, report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T09-10-35-256Z/playtest.md). NINTH straight strong run: reached three endings across four routes (Truth via the vault AND via the crawlspace strongbox, Rich, Caught), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken state; independently re-confirmed the bug_0022 lockpick-gated strongbox and the bug_0019 deadline framing. The playtester's ONE concrete on-screen defect (report §5, item 3, hit on the crawlspace route at seed 303): the shared ending_truth prose presumes the vault/gold route even when the Truth is reached via the crawlspace strongbox.
- DECISION — closed that concrete playtest-surfaced defect rather than the standing rank-3 ESLint+Prettier lever (justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value"). ESLint+Prettier is a large, unbounded "repo/L" change (src ~4.6k LOC / 41 files; 107 TS files total) with real risk of a mid-cycle red health gate, and it is orthogonal to player experience — whereas the loop's north star is player-facing quality and the playtest is mandatory precisely so its findings drive the fix. This defect is small, safe, and has direct precedent (bug_0017). ESLint+Prettier re-logged as the next lever.
- THE GAP: bug_0017 already reworded this ending once ("You leave the gold" → "There was gold enough in this house to vanish on, but you leave it untouched...") to frame the gold as a property of the manor — but "you leave IT untouched" STILL presumes the player was physically beside the gold and chose not to take it. True on the vault take-letter route; FALSE on the crawlspace/strongbox route, which is only ever reached BEFORE/INSTEAD of the vault, so the player never sees a single coin. The "leave it untouched" beat narrated an act that route's player never performed.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — text-only, no engine/validator/schema change): ending_truth reworded to frame the forgone riches as the heist's PURPOSE — "You came to rob this house and could have vanished rich on its hoard; instead you slip out into the dark carrying nothing but the truth." True on BOTH routes (the player is a thief; the manor demonstrably holds a fortune) without presuming a physical gold encounter. The truth-over-riches moral ("Some things are worth more than escaping rich") is kept verbatim. No flags / items / choices / gating / reachable endings change.
- Verified through the engine route path the regression drives (initStateForPack/makeStep over CHOOSE): the crawlspace/strongbox route (never sees the vault or gold) still reaches ending_truth; the vault take-letter route still reaches ending_truth; grab-gold → ending_rich; force → ending_caught.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); 297 tests pass (+3 new). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (NOT a re-pin): clockwork_heist a5f76f8a3943… → 85b5dda6998e19c7d55a27484ffb2368febcacde145889508d134ab03b6e2f66. No test pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin was needed.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0025_clockwork_truth_ending_route_agnostic.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_truth_ending_route_agnostic.test.ts (3 cases: ending_truth no longer presumes a physical gold encounter — no "untouched"/"leave it"/"leave the gold" — yet keeps the moral; the crawlspace/strongbox route still reaches ending_truth; reachability unchanged — vault take-letter → truth, grab-gold → rich, force → caught). Closes bug_0024 deferred[1].
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the repo/ESLint + Prettier tooling gap — lint is still just tsc, the assessor's standing rank-3 repo candidate; now the strongest remaining lever (every pack is blind-verified and per-pack content polish is at deep diminishing returns), but budget a full cycle as it is "repo/L" and unbounded. (b) Deadline legibility on clockwork is a balance wrinkle, not a defect — a sensible player keeps ticks low and never experiences the ticks≥6 guard patrol (which DOES fire → ending_patrol, locked since bug_0019); tightening it would punish efficient play, which bug_0019 deliberately avoids. Keep rotating the playtest target.
