### Cycle result — endings finally show the player their final score (bug_0026)
- ROTATED THE MANDATORY PLAYTEST off the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (blind-playtested PRISTINE for NINE straight cycles: bug_0008/0017/0018/0019/0020/0022/0023/0024/0025 — its coverage-"unreached" endings are the deterministic bot's documented planning limit, not pack flaws). Per blind_playtest_protocol.md's explicit "rotate the seed and ... rotate the pack" and the standing "keep rotating" guidance, played the MOST under-verified pack: **The Sealed Crypt** (parser; the coverage bot can't plan its multi-step lock-and-key chain, leaving 7/10 rooms unvisited; last focused blind read around bug_0013). Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 7; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T09-19-40-916Z/playtest.md.
- Blind playtest: solved to ending_victory first try; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken/stale state; all 10 rooms + full sexton dialogue. TWO findings, one dismissed + one genuine: (DISMISSED) the playtester saw running scores 5→15→35 and suspected inc_var behaved like `set` — verified against the pack the `by` values are 5/10/20, so additive gives exactly 0→5→15→35; the score is CORRECT, the playtester misread the totals as deltas (I did NOT chase the phantom). (GENUINE, report §5) "No closure/score summary at the win" — ending_victory is one line with no final-score tally despite a scoring system (meta.max_score 35); enjoyment was docked for "a score ... never shown to the player."
- DECISION — the genuine finding is GENERIC, not pack-specific: every scoring pack has it (sealed_crypt 35, sunken_barrow 50, cold_forge 50, alchemists_tower 35), so the right fix is a cross-pack engine/UX capability, higher value than a one-pack content patch. Justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value." Deliberately chose this over the standing rank-3 ESLint+Prettier lever (still the strongest remaining repo lever, re-logged) because it directly closes a mandatory-playtest finding and is small/bounded, whereas ESLint+Prettier is "repo/L" and unbounded.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **engine** layer — generic, pack-agnostic; NO YAML edited ⇒ no content hash changed, no re-pin): src/parser/observation.ts (1) surfaces `meta.max_score` as a new `max_score` observation field (the denominator, previously absent), and (2) when ended AND max_score>0 appends "\n\nFinal score: X of Y." to the player-facing `description`, where X is the ACTUAL accrued score (so a partial-credit win reads honestly, e.g. "30 of 35", never a hardcoded max). The canonical `ending.text` and every pack YAML stay PURE — only the rendered `description` carries the tally, so all renderers (the CLI play bins which print obs.description, the MCP observation JSON the playtester reads, the UI) surface closure with zero per-pack edit. RPG inherits it for free (RpgObservation spreads buildParserObservation's result). CYOA packs use a separate observation and are untouched. NO validator change (rendering carries no effects).
- Verified LIVE through the engine render path the regression drives (buildParserObservation / buildRpgObservation over initStateForParserPack/initStateForRpgPack + makeStep — the MCP server + CLI bins' path): sealed_crypt full route → ending_victory with description containing "Final score: 35 of 35." and ending.text pure; headstone-skipping route wins at 30/35 reading "Final score: 30 of 35." (proves the tally tracks real score, not max); mid-game shows no tally but already surfaces max_score; sunken_barrow seed-1 victory inherits "Final score: 50 of 50." via buildRpgObservation.
- Evidence: lint green; validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity OK, format:check + lint pass); **301 tests pass (+4 new)**. No content-hash change (engine-only).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0026_ending_score_summary.yaml + tests/regression/ending_score_summary.test.ts (4 cases: full route → "35 of 35." appended + max_score surfaced + ending.text pure; partial-credit win → "30 of 35." tracks real score not max; mid-game → no tally but max_score surfaced; RPG inheritance → sunken_barrow victory "50 of 50." via buildRpgObservation).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the repo/ESLint + Prettier tooling gap — lint is still just tsc, the assessor's standing rank-3 repo candidate; now the strongest remaining lever (every pack blind-verified, per-pack content polish at deep diminishing returns), but budget a full cycle as it is "repo/L" and unbounded. (b) Optional richer denouement on the new score tally (a classic-IF rank or "in N turns" line) only if a future playtest asks for it — kept minimal here. Keep rotating the playtest target.

### Cycle result — the Sunken Barrow's slab lever now reads as a retryable attempt, not a strength wall (bug_0027)
- ROTATED THE MANDATORY PLAYTEST off the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (blind-playtested PRISTINE for NINE straight cycles: bug_0008/0017/0018/0019/0020/0022/0023/0024/0025 — its coverage-"unreached" endings are the deterministic bot's documented planning limit, not pack flaws). Per blind_playtest_protocol.md's explicit "rotate the seed and ... rotate the pack" and the standing "keep rotating" guidance, played the LEAST-recently blind-verified, repeatedly-"thin"-judged pack: **The Sunken Barrow** (rpg). Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 88; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T09-31-14-694Z/playtest.md.
- Blind playtest result: clarity 3/5, **enjoyment 2/5**, verdict **"would NOT finish satisfied — and a meaningful fraction would not finish at all."** In Run 1 the tester cleared the only combat, solved the only puzzle correctly, exhausted the whole map, and STILL hit a wall it couldn't pass and QUIT a winnable game — the slab might-check. This is the strongest "real player abandons" signal the loop has surfaced in many cycles, and the clear highest-value target this cycle.
- TWO findings, one MISREAD + dismissed, one GENUINE + fixed:
  - (DISMISSED) "broken dice — rolls of 3, 6, 18 on a d6": investigated against the engine — the SKILL check rolls a d20 (`resolveSkillCheck`); only COMBAT damage uses a d6 (`resolveAttack`). 18 is a valid d20. Confirmed LIVE via MCP (seed 88 levered on a d20 roll of 19). The RPG validator independently proves the check is PASSABLE (DC 12 ≤ 20 + might-ceiling 3). I did NOT chase a phantom RNG bug or touch balance.
  - (GENUINE, FIXED) The slab is mechanically a fair, penalty-free, **infinitely retryable** ~60%/try d20+might(=3) vs DC 12 — but its FICTION framed it as a fixed STRENGTH THRESHOLD ("with a lever and real might"; "the slab does not give"). `might` is fixed and NO effect raises it, so there is no strength item to find. A player who fails a couple of rolls infers "I'm missing an item", searches, finds none, and abandons — exactly Run 1.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — NO engine/validator/DC/skill/effect change; the check stays the identical fair d20+might vs 12): reframed the slab puzzle so a failed attempt reads as REPEATABLE PROGRESS via leverage/persistence, not a strength wall. (1) on_failure narration rewritten — signals the slab IS shifting ("grinds a hand's breadth — then grates back into its bed"), denies the missing-item inference outright ("No greater strength or hidden tool is wanting here — only leverage and a stubborn back"), and invites another attempt plainly ("Reset your feet on the iron lip and heave again; keep at it and the stone will come"). (2) stone_slab examine description reframed from "with a lever and real might, it might shift" → "...it can be levered — not by one heroic effort, but by setting your feet and heaving again and again until the old stone gives". No flags / items / exits / gating / scoring / reachable endings change; the bug_0015 one-shot retire is untouched.
- Verified LIVE through the engine the regression drives (initStateForRpgPack/makeStep/resolve — the MCP server's path): a failing roll (seed 2, "rolled 8 + 3 = 11 vs 12 — failure") now surfaces the new persistence guidance and changes no state (penalty-free, lever stays legal/retryable); a first-lever success (seed 1, the canonical trace seed) still levers the slab and reaches ending_victory at 50/50.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity static OK; format:check + lint pass); **305 tests pass (+4 new)**. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: exit 0 with the single expected HASH_PIN_REPINNED *warning* (accompanied by an intentional content change = the legitimate snapshot-update workflow, not a launder). Deliberate, surfaced re-pin: traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json content_hash ba69fcdd… → 4e185710…; its expected_final_hash is UNCHANGED (only prose changed — no mechanic/effect/DC/roll moved — so the seed-1 victory replays to the identical final state hash; rpg_barrow_trace.test.ts green). No test SOURCE pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0027_barrow_lever_legibility.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_barrow_lever_legibility.test.ts (4 cases: on_failure feedback signals repeatable progress and never a strength threshold / missing item, with the check still d20+might vs 12; examine description reframed to persistence; a LIVE failed check surfaces the retry guidance and changes no state / stays retryable; reachability+balance intact — first-lever success still reaches ending_victory at full score).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) Entry Hall stale prose — after the iron bar is taken the room still reads "A rusted iron bar lies among fallen rubble" (confirmed live this cycle; also bug_0015 deferred[0]); a one-variant reactive-room fix (bug_0011) or object variant (bug_0023) — small, clean, high-precedent. (b) No opening objective line in barrow_mouth (the circlet goal is revealed only in the final room; bug_0015 deferred[2]). (c) The repo/ESLint + Prettier tooling gap — lint is still just tsc; the npm registry IS reachable (verified eslint 10.4.1 this cycle), so it is now feasible, but budget a full cycle as it is "repo/L" and unbounded. Keep rotating the playtest target/seed.

- ROTATED THE MANDATORY PLAYTEST off the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (blind-playtested PRISTINE for TEN straight cycles — its coverage-"unreached" endings are the deterministic bot's documented planning limit, not pack flaws). Per blind_playtest_protocol.md's explicit "rotate the seed and ... rotate the pack" + the standing "keep rotating" guidance, played the weakest-scoring pack, the rpg **The Sunken Barrow**, seed 13. Its only critique was SCOPE, not quality ("a tidy ~10-step vertical slice"). BUT: the tester took the iron bar and moved straight on, so it never re-looked at the Entry Hall WHILE HOLDING the bar and did not re-trigger the standing deferred stale-prose defect.
- DECISION — closed the standing deferred item **bug_0027 deferred[0]** (also bug_0015 deferred[0]), justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value": a confirmed on-screen continuity defect outranks a tenth clockwork micro-note, and it is a small, high-precedent (bug_0011) content fix. - IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — NO engine/validator/schema change; the reactive room `variants` feature already exists, bug_0010/bug_0011): added a single variant to `entry_hall`, gated on `has_item: iron_bar`, that drops the "lies among fallen rubble" clause and reads "The rubble is scuffed bare where the rusted iron bar lay until you took it up." Gating on has_item is the durable proxy for "the bar is no longer on this floor" — the closed condition DSL (src/core/conditions.ts) has no object-in-room predicate, so this mirrors guard_crypt's own `wight_slain` variant and bug_0024's has_item proxy. Examine/look-only: no flag / item / exit / quest gating / scoring / reachable-ending change.
- Verified LIVE through the engine render path the regression drives (buildRpgObservation over initStateForRpgPack/makeStep — the MCP server's path): base text shows the bar before it is taken; once taken the variant reads "scuffed bare" and never again "lies among fallen rubble"; the post-take state_hash is IDENTICAL to the pre-fix run (50df2bca…) — the variant carries no effects; and dropping the bar back in the Entry Hall flips has_item false and restores the base text (the bar IS present again, matching visible_objects), so prose tracks state in both directions.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity static OK; format:check + lint pass); **309 tests pass (+4 new)**. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: exit 0 with the single expected HASH_PIN_REPINNED *warning* (accompanied by an intentional content change = the legitimate snapshot-update workflow, not a launder). No test SOURCE pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0028_barrow_entry_hall_stale_bar.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_barrow_entry_hall_stale_bar.test.ts (4 cases: base text narrates the bar before it is taken; the variant replaces the stale prose once the bar is in hand and never again says "lies among fallen rubble"; dropping the bar back in the Entry Hall restores the base text — prose tracks state both ways; the variant is text-only — taking the bar changes no flag/var/journal/quest state). ### Cycle result — the Cold Forge sentinel stops promising a warm-up clock it never delivers (bug_0029)
- ROTATED THE MANDATORY PLAYTEST off the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (blind-playtested PRISTINE for ~ten straight cycles — its coverage-"unreached" endings are the deterministic bot's documented planning limit, not pack flaws). Per blind_playtest_protocol.md's explicit "rotate the seed and ... rotate the pack" + the standing "keep rotating" guidance, played the assessor's OWN "quality unverified" candidate, the rpg **The Cold Forge** — a pack that had never had a full blind QUALITY playtest (only an engine-demo touch in bug_0023) and whose coverage bot ends 0/100, reaches 3/5 rooms (never ember_chamber/forge_heart), and loops on bellows_walk/forge_steps. Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 31 (+ seed 7 no-buff probe); report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T09-54-41-679Z/playtest.md.
- Blind playtest result: WON ending_victory 50/50; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; NO mechanical bugs (zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken/stale state); it independently re-confirmed bug_0026 ("Final score: 50 of 50.") and bug_0023 (reactive grate examine) hold in play. ONE on-screen legibility finding (report §4 + §5, scenes Bellows Walk + the inscription): the sentinel's "wakes to heat / sleeps in cold" inscription + its "cold now but grinding slowly to life as you enter" framing strongly imply a TIME/TEMPERATURE mechanic (dawdle → it warms up/strengthens) that DOES NOT EXIST — the fight is a static 16-HP encounter regardless of timing. "The flavor over-promises a system that isn't there." The report's other two findings (infinitely-retryable might-check; light NPC buff) are BALANCE wrinkles, deliberately NOT chased: the retryable "set-your-feet, try again" check framing is exactly what bug_0027 EMBRACED for the barrow slab, and re-tuning DCs/buffs would risk the combat-winnability + death-ending proofs in cold_forge_pack.test.ts.
- DECISION — fixed the on-screen legibility defect (the bug_0027 class: fiction misleading the player about mechanics), justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value": a real, confirmed first-player friction on a previously-unverified pack outranks an ~11th clockwork micro-note, and it is a small, content-only, high-precedent fix.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — NO engine/validator/stat/effect/DC change; the fight stays the identical static 16-HP encounter): reframed the FOUR sentinel-prose sites so the cold reads as a SETTLED, PERMANENT advantage and the warm-up clock is denied outright. (1) bellows_walk room: "cold now but grinding slowly to life as you enter" → "The dead hall holds no fire to rouse it; the deep cold keeps it slow and stiff ... It will be no swifter than it is now." (2) inscription read_text: adds "; AND THIS FORGE-FIRE IS LONG DEAD AND WILL NOT KINDLE. SO IT SLEEPS, AND STRUCK TRUE IN THE CHILL IT WILL NOT RISE AGAIN." — keeps the "wakes to heat" lore but makes clear no heat is coming. (3) enemy description: "grinding to cold life" → "holding a slow, cold half-life ... as stiff and clumsy as it will ever be". (4) spirit_sentinel npc_text: now states "this hall has been bitter cold for an age and will never be warm again. ### Cycle result — the "Truth" ending stops breaking the seal "by moonlight" in a windowless crawlspace (bug_0030)
- Ran the MANDATED blind playtest on the assessor's rank-1 target, the CYOA pack **The Clockwork Heist** (fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 113; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T10-04-47-010Z/playtest.md). Reached all three endings across four routes (Truth via the vault AND via the crawlspace strongbox, Caught, Rich); clarity 4/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken state. Unlike the prior ~10 straight pristine clockwork runs, this one surfaced an actionable concrete defect.
- THREE findings — two dismissed on inspection, one genuine + fixed:
  - (DISMISSED) "Dangling time-pressure / guard mechanic" — NOT a defect: the guard patrol DOES fire (ticks>=6 gallery variant + `cross_to_vault_blind` -> ending_patrol; reading the ledger grants the safe crossing via the `any_of:[ticks<=5, read_ledger]` gate). The "running short" line is honest foreshadowing; an efficient player who keeps ticks low simply never needs the ledger. Deliberate since bug_0019/bug_0025 — tightening it would punish efficient play. Not chased.
  - (DISMISSED) "No final score shown" — NOT a defect: clockwork is CYOA with no `meta.max_score`/scoring system, so there is nothing to total; bug_0026 added the score summary to scoring parser/RPG packs only (CYOA uses a separate observation, untouched). The playtester misread the bug_0026 commit. Showing a score would invent one. Not chased.
  - (GENUINE, FIXED) Truth-via-strongbox flavor mismatch (report §5 item 3): ending_truth opened "You break the seal by moonlight ..." but on the crawlspace/strongbox route the player breaks the seal in a windowless dusty crawlspace behind the clock — nowhere near the foyer's moonlit glass dome. Shared ending prose presuming the (lit) vault route. Same class as bug_0017/bug_0025.
- DECISION — closed the genuine playtest-surfaced defect rather than the standing rank-3 ESLint+Prettier lever (justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value", and the Hard constraint "prefer a small, verified change over a broad rewrite"). ESLint+Prettier is a large unbounded "repo/L" change with real red-gate risk and orthogonal to player experience; this fix is small, safe, and directly playtest-driven. ESLint+Prettier re-logged as the next lever.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — text-only, no engine/validator/schema change): ending_truth reworded so the seal is broken AFTER the escape — "You slip out into the dark with the letter still sealed, and break it open by moonlight only once the manor's walls are well behind you. ... instead you carry away nothing but the truth." Breaking the seal outdoors after leaving the manor is true on BOTH the vault and the crawlspace/strongbox routes (each ends with the thief slipping into the night), so the evocative moonlight is kept without presuming an interior lit setting at the reveal. The bug_0025 route-agnostic gold framing and the truth-over-riches moral are preserved. No flags / items / choices / gating / reachable endings change.
- Verified through the engine route path the regression drives (initStateForPack/makeStep over CHOOSE, seed 113): the crawlspace/strongbox route (never sees the lit vault) still reaches ending_truth; the vault take-letter route still reaches ending_truth; grab-gold -> ending_rich; force -> ending_caught.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity static OK, format:check + lint pass); **316 tests pass (+3 new)**. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: exit 0, 0 warnings — no protected-file edit, NO re-pin (no test SOURCE pins this pack's hash; only watchtower_road is pinned). Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (recorded in the bug artifact only): clockwork_heist 85b5dda6998e… -> 833822baa1065fc4e01a4851f99397e87033fb9cd23ba35df4fabfe080fdc3e3.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0030_clockwork_truth_ending_moonlight.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_truth_ending_moonlight.test.ts (3 cases: the seal is broken after the escape not "by moonlight" indoors, keeping the moral + bug_0025 gold framing; the windowless crawlspace/strongbox route still reaches ending_truth; reachability unchanged — vault take-letter -> truth, grab-gold -> rich, force -> caught).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the repo/ESLint + Prettier tooling gap — lint is still just tsc, the assessor's standing rank-3 repo candidate; now the strongest remaining lever (every pack blind-verified; per-pack content polish at deep diminishing returns), but budget a full "repo/L" cycle. (b) No opening objective line in the Sunken Barrow's barrow_mouth — the circlet goal is revealed only in the final room (bug_0027 deferred[1]; bug_0015 deferred[2]); a small, content-only orientation line. Keep rotating the playtest target/seed.

### Cycle result — ESLint + Prettier finally added; lint is no longer just tsc (bug_0031)
- Ran the MANDATED blind playtest on the assessor's rank-1 target, the CYOA pack **The Clockwork Heist** (fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 137; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T10-14-11-953Z/playtest.md). STRONG for the ~12th straight cycle: all three endings across four routes, clarity 4/5, enjoyment 4/5, zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken state. Its three §4/§5 notes were ALL previously surfaced and adjudicated, none new/actionable: (a) "phantom time / dead ledger" — DELIBERATE per bug_0019/0025/0030 (the deadline DOES fire at ticks>=6 → ending_patrol; the ledger grants the safe crossing; an efficient player keeps ticks low and tightening would punish efficient play); (b) duplicated-letter continuity quirk — minor, logged since bug_0022/0023; (c) "no final score" — NOT a defect (clockwork is CYOA with no meta.max_score; bug_0026 added the tally to scoring parser/RPG packs only). Clockwork is polished past diminishing returns.
- DECISION — took the standing strongest lever, the **ESLint + Prettier tooling gap** (assessor rank-3, id repo-eslint), deferred since bug_0024 and re-logged as the next lever every cycle since (bug_0024/0025/0026/0027/0028/0029/0030). Justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value": with every pack blind-verified and content polish at deep diminishing returns, a cross-codebase tooling capability beats a ~12th clockwork micro-note. Until now `npm run lint` == `format:check` == `tsc --noEmit` (a redundant double typecheck) — no real static-analysis or formatting gate existed.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **repo/tooling** layer): (1) **eslint.config.js** — flat config (ESLint 9): @eslint/js recommended + typescript-eslint recommended (non-type-checked) + eslint-config-prettier (Prettier owns formatting), scoped to first-party engine/tooling TS (src, bin, scripts, agents); deliberately NOT tests/ (own conventions; verify-integrity guards it), content/ (hash-sensitive YAML), traces/, or ui/. (2) **.prettierrc.json + .prettierignore** — printWidth 100 / 2-space / double-quote / semi / trailing-comma all (matches existing style); the ignore + the explicitly-scoped format scripts are a belt-and-suspenders guard so Prettier NEVER rewrites content/ or traces/ hash assets. (3) **package.json** — split the tsc aliases into honest roles: `typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, the typecheck gate preserved), `lint` (eslint), `format:check` (prettier --check), `format` (prettier --write); health now runs verify:integrity && typecheck && lint && format:check && test && validate(all) && playtest. (4) **ci.yml** — Typecheck step now runs `npm run typecheck`; added explicit Lint + Format-check steps. (5) **AGENTS.md / README.md** — updated the lint-is-typecheck descriptions. (6) **8 genuine behavior-preserving code fixes** ESLint surfaced: comma-sequence statements → blocks (bin/inspect.ts, parser_validator.ts ×3), `let scripted`→`const` (bin/play.ts), dropped a useless `= null` init (assessor.ts), removed an unused import (legal_actions.ts) and an unused local (parser_validator.ts). (7) **`prettier --write src bin scripts agents`** reformatted 43 files (formatting only).
- Verified: `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier check + 320 tests + every pack validate 0/0 + watchtower coverage). **320 tests pass (+4** — new tests/regression/lint_tooling_wired.test.ts; the assessor-test case modified IN PLACE, no count drop). The full suite + tsc prove the 43-file reformat is behavior-neutral. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0 with 6 expected non-blocking VERIFIER_TOUCHED warnings** — the protected files Prettier reformatted / I lint-fixed (src/core/sha256.ts, src/persist/save_load.ts, src/validate/{cyoa,parser,rpg}_validator.ts, scripts/verify-integrity.ts). These are honest, surfaced edits; the static + count-regression checks remain the hard gate and the suite is green. **NO HASH_PIN_FILE touched** (format scripts exclude tests/ + traces/) ⇒ no re-pin, no HASH_PIN error. **No content/ YAML edited ⇒ no content hash moved.**
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0031_eslint_prettier_tooling.yaml + tests/regression/lint_tooling_wired.test.ts (4 cases: scripts invoke eslint/prettier/tsc and lint is no longer bare tsc; health runs typecheck+lint+format:check; config files ship; ESLint actually FUNCTIONS — flags an unused var, passes clean code). tests/unit/assessor.test.ts's "spans content and non-content" case repurposed to document the repo-eslint disarm (eslint.config.js now exists ⇒ no repo-eslint candidate), mirroring the existing content_new disarm precedent; it re-arms (and the test catches it) if the tooling is removed. Closes the repo/ESLint deferred item from bug_0024–0030.
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): **the assessor now has NO non-content lever left** (both content_new and repo-eslint are disarmed), so it will monotonically rank content_fix until a new detector is added. Restore the cross-category radar via one of: (a) extend ESLint/Prettier coverage to tests/ (excluded here to keep this cycle bounded); (b) give the assessor a real engine detector (its header promises TODO/FIXME-marker scanning the code doesn't yet emit); (c) lint the ui/ package. ### Cycle result — the assessor stops chasing the coverage bot's PLANNING limit on gated CYOA (bug_0032)
- Ran the MANDATED blind playtest on the assessor's rank-1 target, the CYOA pack **The Clockwork Heist** (fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 163; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T10-31-33-025Z/playtest.md). ~13th straight STRONG run: all three endings across four routes (Truth via vault AND crawlspace strongbox, Caught, Rich), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken state. Its two findings were BOTH previously surfaced and adjudicated: (a) the "ticks countdown bluff" — DELIBERATE per bug_0019/0025/0030 (the patrol DOES fire at ticks>=6 → ending_patrol; the ledger grants the safe crossing; an efficient player keeps ticks low, and tightening would punish efficient play); (b) the duplicated-letter quirk — cosmetic, logged since bug_0022/0023. No NEW actionable content defect; clockwork is polished past diminishing returns.
- DECISION — fixed the LOOP'S OWN BRAIN rather than a ~13th clockwork micro-note (justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value", and the standing bug_0031 deferred lever "the assessor has NO non-content lever left … restore the cross-category radar"). THE FALSE POSITIVE: the assessor ranked clockwork_heist the #1 improvement EVERY cycle for ~13 cycles ("3 unreached ending(s), 1 unvisited location(s)", score 2.0) because its planning-free coverage bot reaches only 1/4 endings — yet the blind LLM playtest reaches ALL of them every cycle. The other three sit behind PLANNING (the lockpick chain for ending_rich/ending_truth; a deliberate ledger-skip for ending_patrol), a planning limit identical in kind to the parser/RPG puzzle packs the assessor ALREADY refuses to drive content_fix from. The evaluator was lying to the loop; making it honest is the trust-but-verify keystone applied to the loop itself.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **engine/loop-brain** layer — src/afk/assessor.ts; NO content/YAML edited ⇒ no content hash moved): generalized the dividing line from the MODE LABEL to PLANNING-GATING. Added a pure, exported predicate `cyoaPackIsGated(pack)` (true iff any choice carries a precondition — a non-empty `conditions` array) + a disk wrapper `isPlanningGatedCyoa`. `botCoverageIsMeaningful` is now `s.mode === "cyoa" && !isPlanningGatedCyoa(root, s.path)`. So GATED CYOA (clockwork, watchtower) gets the parser/RPG treatment — an unreached ending/scene becomes a LOW-priority blind-playtest review (impact 1, score 0.5, "review prompt, not a known flaw"), the fair judge being the mandatory blind playtest + validator warnings — while UNGATED / pure-branching CYOA is UNCHANGED (the bot reaches every node, so a coverage gap stays a real high-impact signal). Validator-warning-driven fixes untouched.
- EFFECT ON RANKING: clockwork was rank-1 "Improve … 3 unreached ending(s)" 2.0 → now a "Blind-playtest … quality is unverified" review 0.5; watchtower 1.5 → 0.5. All six packs are now uniformly honest 0.5 blind-playtest reviews — the TRUE state of the repo (every pack blind-verified; the only standing work is rotating playtests, which is what the loop does). The assessor no longer asserts phantom unreached-ending defects. `ai-loop.ts`'s playtestTarget() still returns the top content_fix target, so its behavior is unaffected.
- Evidence: typecheck green; validate green 0/0 (all packs, none touched); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier + tests + every pack validate + watchtower coverage). verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings** — assessor.ts is not a protected file, no content hash moved, no traces/ pin touched (no re-pin). Tests: tests/unit/assessor.test.ts 10/10 + new tests/regression/assessor_gated_cyoa_coverage.test.ts 6/6 (net +6 cases).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0032_assessor_gated_cyoa_coverage.yaml + tests/regression/assessor_gated_cyoa_coverage.test.ts (6 cases: cyoaPackIsGated true on a gated synthetic pack / false on a pure-branching one; clockwork is gated [premise guard]; assess() raises NO `fix-` for clockwork; clockwork is a `playtest-` review score<1 with "not a known flaw" evidence; no gated-CYOA pack yields a `fix-` phantom). Updated tests/unit/assessor.test.ts in place (refreshed the parser/rpg comment to note the CYOA generalization; removed a now-coincidental "top targets cyoa" assertion — true only by id tiebreak now, not a meaningful invariant). Closes bug_0031 deferred[a].
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): the assessor now honestly surfaces no high-impact lever (the repo is genuinely healthy — every pack blind-verified, codebase clean, Grep confirms ZERO TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers so the existing engine detector is correctly inert). To restore a real cross-category radar, ADD a detector that FIRES on currently-invisible work: (a) extend ESLint/Prettier + the assessor's view to tests/ and ui/ (a genuine lint-coverage gap, deliberately excluded in bug_0031); (b) a docs/staleness detector. Otherwise keep rotating the blind playtest target/seed; clockwork's duplicated-letter quirk is cosmetic-only.

### Cycle result — reactive variants can finally see a container's lock; the Study stops reading "LOCKED" once it's unlocked (bug_0033)
- ROTATED the mandated playtest off the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (blind-verified PRISTINE ~13 straight cycles; bug_0032 made the assessor honest, so all six packs now rank as uniform LOW-priority 0.5 blind-playtest reviews — none carries a known flaw). Per blind_playtest_protocol.md's explicit "rotate the seed and rotate the pack", played the LEAST-recently blind-QUALITY-tested pack: **The Alchemist's Tower** (parser). Its last dedicated quality playtest was ~bug_0012; since then it had only had deferred-item closes (bug_0024's playtest target was clockwork). Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 51 (+52 death route); report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T10-45-49-483Z/playtest.md.
- Blind playtest result: WON ending_cured 35/35 + the warned ending_poisoned; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken state. ONE on-screen defect (report §5.1) + two non-defects deliberately NOT chased (the water-add step is unsignposted until the herb steeps — intentional sequencing; back-loaded scoring — a taste note, not a bug).
- THE DEFECT (report §5.1): in the Cluttered Study, immediately after "You unlock the iron strongbox," the ROOM still read "A locked iron strongbox sits on the desk" — the text only corrected itself once the iron key was TAKEN. A direct on-screen contradiction of the player's own unlocking action.
- ROOT CAUSE (architectural, called out BY NAME in bug_0024): the reactive-`variants` system evaluates `when` through the PURE condition DSL (src/core/conditions.ts), which had NO predicate for a container's runtime open/locked state. So every prior reactive fix (bug_0012 room, bug_0023/0024 examine) used a `has_item iron_key` PROXY that flips only on EMPTYING, leaving the lockbox lifecycle locked → unlocked → opened → emptied with a stale "locked" middle.
- DECISION — fixed the architectural gap (a generic engine capability) rather than papering over one pack, justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value" and the standing preference for "a generic engine/UX capability over per-pack polish." This both closes the playtest-surfaced defect AND removes the reason four prior fixes needed proxy hacks.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **engine + content**): (1) **engine** — src/core/conditions.ts gains two pure, closed-DSL runtime object-state predicates: `{ is_open: <id> }` (objectState[id].open === true) and `{ is_unlocked: <id> }` (objectState[id].locked === false, i.e. the lock was cleared at runtime). Both default false until open_object / set_object_locked fires, so a reactive room/object variant can track a container's whole lifecycle directly. Schema + type + eval branch; pure over GameState (no index/pack). (2) **content** — alchemists_tower's Study room adds an `is_unlocked: strongbox` variant ("sits unlocked … brass lock sprung") above the emptied (has_item iron_key) variant; the strongbox examine adds `is_open` (opened, key still inside) + `is_unlocked` (unlocked, lid shut) variants ahead of the emptied one. First-match-wins keeps emptied primary. Variant-only: no flag/item/score/exit/interaction/gating/reachable-ending change.
- VALIDATOR-SAFE: the validator never analyzes variant `when` clauses (checkConds runs only on exit/interaction/win conditions) and its flag/item walkers ignore unrecognized kinds; the cyoa validator's condition walk is likewise non-exhaustive. New predicates used only in variants ⇒ zero static-analysis impact.
- Verified LIVE through the engine render path the regression drives (roomDescription + resolveParserAction LOOK over initStateForParserPack/enumerateActions/makeStep — the MCP server's path): after unlock the room reads "sits unlocked … brass lock sprung" and never "locked iron strongbox"; after open the examine reads "stands open … key still rests"; after take both flip to "open and empty"; a fresh box (no objectState) reads base prose; the canonical brew route (seed 1) still reaches ending_cured at 35/35.
- Evidence: typecheck green; validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier + tests + every pack validate + watchtower coverage). **331 tests pass.** verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings** — conditions.ts is not a protected file, no test SOURCE pins this pack's hash (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin. Deliberate, surfaced content-hash change (recorded in the bug artifact only): alchemists_tower 04d4e8be… → f73b97fb4e0edf5513621140807c33a225b7205ce66ab916c6c7dd01ab0a6869.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0033_alchemists_strongbox_unlocked_state.yaml + tests/regression/alchemists_strongbox_unlock_state.test.ts (4 cases: room tracks locked→unlocked→emptied and never re-asserts "locked" once sprung; examine tracks the same lifecycle, never "with a brass lock" once unlocked; predicates inert until their effect fires; reachability/score intact). Plus tests/unit/conditions.test.ts (+2 cases: eval of is_open/is_unlocked incl. defaults; schema accepts the new kinds / rejects empty id). Updated tests/regression/alchemists_strongbox_reactive_examine.test.ts in place: bug_0024's case (1) had ASSERTED the intermediate examine still read the base "with a brass lock" (documenting the very gap it deferred); changed to assert the new reactive intermediate ("stands open" / not "with a brass lock") — a surfaced behavior IMPROVEMENT, not a weakening. Closes the bug_0024 deferred limitation "the closed condition DSL has no object-open predicate."
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) restore the assessor's cross-category radar (bug_0032 deferred) — extend ESLint/Prettier + the assessor's view to tests/ and ui/, or add a docs/staleness detector; the assessor now surfaces only uniform 0.5 blind-playtest reviews because the repo is genuinely healthy. (b) The new `is_open`/`is_unlocked` predicates are now available to EVERY reactive pack — a future cycle could retire other `has_item` proxies (e.g. sealed_crypt's gate, cold_forge's grate) where the lock/open transition is the truer signal. Keep rotating the blind playtest target/seed.

- Blind playtest result: WON ending_victory at a perfect 35/35 on the FIRST run; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no dead ends, no soft-locks. Gating verified both directions (no rope ⇒ no well descent; no iron key ⇒ no gate use / north exit). The gate's reactive text flips correctly ("bars the way" → "swung inward") — no stale-"locked" bug. ONE concrete continuity finding (§5.1) + 3 cosmetic nits not chased.
- THE DEFECT (report §5, finding 1): after USE rope ON well the world reads "The hemp rope is knotted fast to the iron ring and trails down into the dark" and the tie narration says it "uncoils into the dark below" — yet the player still CARRIED a "coil of rope". The same rope tied to the well AND in your pocket (the bug_0007/bug_0028 stale-state class).
- ROOT CAUSE: the honest fix (remove the rope from inventory when tied off) was made ILLEGAL by a VALIDATOR FALSE POSITIVE. parser_validator's SOFTLOCK_QUEST_ITEM "consumed with no re-grant" branch rejected ANY consumed quest_critical item, conflating SPENT with LOST. The rope is spent for permanent progress: it sets `rope_attached_to_well` + unlock_exits the descent, and that descent stays open by the FLAG, never by `has_item rope` — nothing downstream needs the rope again. Same theme as bug_0032/0033: make the verifier honest about a false positive.
- DECISION — fixed the validator's PRECISION (a generic engine capability that unblocks correct item-consumption authoring for every parser pack) rather than papering the pack, justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value" and the standing preference for a generic engine capability over per-pack polish.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **engine + content**): (1) **engine** — src/validate/parser_validator.ts: the consume-branch now fires iff `removed && !re-granted && neededWhileHeld`. `neededWhileHeld` is built from every must-hold-this-item requirement (an interaction's `item:` field + `has_item` conditions on exits/interactions/win/dialogue), tagging whether that same site also consumes the item (a site's own consuming `item:` is in hand when spent, so it doesn't count). Sound over-approximation: an item required ANYWHERE it isn't spent still trips it; an item whose every hold-requirement coincides with its consumption is correctly SPENT. Header doc updated. (2) **content** — sealed_crypt's old_well USE-rope interaction adds `remove_item: rope` + reworded narration ("you knot the rope fast … your way down, and your way back up"). No flag/exit/score/gating/reachable-ending change.
- VERIFIED LIVE through the engine replay path the regression drives: the rope leaves inventory exactly at use_rope_on_old_well; the full seed-88 route still reaches catacombs and scores 35; the descent stays available with the rope GONE (flag-gated, not item-gated).
- Evidence: typecheck green; validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` **exit 0** (verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier + tests + every pack validate + watchtower coverage). verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 1 WARNING** — VERIFIER_TOUCHED on parser_validator.ts (a protected file; surfaced-for-review, NOT blocking — the designed trust-but-verify path for legitimate engine work). No HASH_PIN file moved (only watchtower_road's hash is pinned, not sealed_crypt's), so no re-pin. Deliberate, surfaced sealed_crypt content-hash change (recorded in the bug artifact): now 415be64bef26c3f179f655fb85b1d04a43478dcf92708d8c88ac9071c84949c6.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0034_sealed_crypt_rope_spent.yaml + tests/regression/sealed_crypt_rope_spent.test.ts (5 cases: rope leaves inventory when tied; full route wins 35/35; descent stays open by flag with rope gone; shipped pack validates clean; the refined check STILL bites a consumed item needed-while-held elsewhere — no weakening) + a new MUST-FAIL fixture content/broken-fixtures/parser_softlock_quest_item_consumed.yaml wired into tests/unit/parser_validator.test.ts (the consume-branch still rejects a genuinely-lost quest item). Net +6 test cases.
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) restore the assessor's cross-category radar (bug_0032/0033 deferred) — extend ESLint/Prettier + the assessor's view to tests/ and ui/, or add a docs/staleness detector; the assessor still surfaces only uniform 0.5 blind-playtest reviews. (b) The new `neededWhileHeld` precision now lets other packs spend quest items honestly — a future cycle could audit whether any pack leaves a consumed-or-spent item awkwardly in inventory. (c) sealed_crypt's remaining nits are cosmetic (intermediary brass key with no flavor; single ending). Keep rotating the blind playtest target/seed (cold_forge / sunken_barrow are now the least-recently quality-tested).

Its only §4/§5 findings were the two ALREADY-adjudicated items, NOT new defects: (a) the "heat wakes the sentinel / waiting doesn't make it worse" lore reads as flavor for mechanics that never materialize — this is the **bug_0029** fix WORKING (bug_0029 deliberately reframed those four sites to DENY a warm-up clock the static 16-HP fight never delivers); (b) the low-stakes optional +2 buff — the **bug_0029 deferred** balance wrinkle, to act on "only if a future playtest reports a genuine abandonment risk", which this playtest explicitly does NOT ("fine for a gentle game"). cold_forge is blind-verified clean; no content defect to fix.

Justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value": with cold_forge (and every pack) blind-verified and per-pack polish at deep diminishing returns, a real cross-category signal beats an ~Nth review of a pristine pack. Post-bug_0032 the assessor surfaced ONLY uniform 0.5 reviews because its two non-content detectors are both inert (repo-eslint disarmed since the config ships; engine-todos inert at zero markers) — yet there IS real, currently-invisible work: tests/ (63 files) and ui/ hold first-party TS with NO static-analysis or format gate. The bug_0032 keystone (make the loop's brain honest) applied to its BLIND SPOT.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **engine/loop-brain** layer — src/afk/assessor.ts; NO content/YAML edited ⇒ no content hash moved): added a deterministic lint-COVERAGE detector to the repo section. It runs only when an ESLint config exists (the bug_0031 success state) and emits a `repo-lint-coverage` candidate listing every first-party code dir (LINT_DIRS = src/bin/scripts/agents/tests/ui) that (1) exists and holds lintable .ts/.tsx (dirHasLintableTs — skips node_modules/dist/coverage and .d.ts) yet (2) is NOT under the gate (eslintCovers — true iff a `files:` glob names the dir AND no `ignores:` glob excludes it; parses the flat-config text, same lightweight style as the existing TODO/marker scan). impact = 1 + uncovered-dir count, effort L, repo weight 0.6. eslintCovers exported + pure for unit testing.
- EFFECT ON RANKING: the assessor now surfaces "Extend ESLint/Prettier coverage to 2 first-party code dir(s) outside the lint gate (tests, ui)" at score 0.6 as the honest rank-1 lever — ABOVE the uniform 0.5 blind-playtest reviews. It DISARMS automatically the moment a future cycle adds tests/ + ui/ to the ESLint `files` globs and drops them from `ignores` (proven by the eslintCovers DISARM test case). ai-loop.ts is unaffected: playtestTarget() falls back to the main story for an engine/repo top, and the mandatory blind playtest still runs every cycle regardless.
- Evidence: typecheck green; validate green 0/0 (all packs, none touched); full `npm run health` **exit 0** (verify:integrity static OK, typecheck + ESLint + Prettier + tests + every pack validate + watchtower coverage). **345 tests pass (+9 new).** verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings** — assessor.ts is not a protected file, no content hash moved, no traces/ pin touched (no re-pin). (Caught + fixed mid-cycle: ESLint flagged an irregular-whitespace char I'd used in a JSDoc to dodge `*/`; reworded the comment — the gate working as intended.)
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0035_assessor_lint_coverage_radar.yaml + tests/regression/assessor_lint_coverage.test.ts (9 cases: eslintCovers TRUE for files-named/un-ignored dirs, FALSE for ignored/absent dirs, DISARMS when a dir is added to files & dropped from ignores, ignore-wins on conflict, matches the shipped config; assess() raises repo-lint-coverage naming tests+ui, ranks it above the 0.5 reviews as the assessor top, does NOT re-arm repo-eslint). tests/unit/assessor.test.ts unchanged (distinct candidate id, no invariant disturbed). Closes the cross-category-radar deferred item from bug_0031/0032/0033/0034.
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the DETECTOR now queues the real work — a future "repo/L" cycle should ACTUALLY extend the gate: add tests/**/*.ts to the ESLint files globs (drop "tests/**" from ignores) and fix what it flags; ui/ additionally needs its own React/TSX lint setup (eslint-plugin-react/react-hooks + the ui tsconfig) as a separate Vite package — budget bounded-per-dir (tests/ has 63 files). The playtester reached 3/4 endings and never pushed ticks high WITHOUT the ledger, so the patrol fail simply wasn't on their routes — the mechanic is the deliberate "read the ledger or chance it late" beat, not a dead clue. - ROTATED the mandated playtest off the assessor's rank-1 clockwork_heist (blind-verified pristine ~15 straight cycles; since bug_0032 every pack ranks as a uniform LOW-priority 0.5 review, none carrying a known flaw) onto the LEAST-recently full-blind-quality-tested pack, the parser pack **The Alchemist's Tower** (its last full quality playtest predates the bug_0024/0033 from-source examine fixes). - Blind playtest result: WON ending_cured **35/35** across the intended route; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no broken state; the key-chain, recipe-order brewing, 5/15/35 scoring, the poison-trap death ending, and the antidote-gated win all behaved correctly; gating verified (spire with empty inventory does NOT false-win). Its ONE concrete finding (section 5): after the antidote is brewed the Laboratory ROOM still reads "A great cauldron simmers over a low flame" and the cauldron OBJECT still examines as "waiting for ingredients" — stale text contradicting the state the player just made (herb_added / antidote_brewed). Two further notes were taste/parser-verb niceties, not defects (the carry-able black phial's low stakes; "use black phial" vs "drink").
- DECISION — fixed the concrete finding rather than building the speculative doc/staleness assessor radar (the standing cross-category lever logged since bug_0032/0035, now that both repo levers are disarmed per bug_0038). The doc/staleness radar remains the logged next-best meta-improvement.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — text-only, no engine/validator/schema/flag/item/score/exit/interaction/gating/reachable-ending change): the Laboratory room + cauldron were the ONE stale-prone pair in this pack the reactive-text features were never applied to (rooms: bug_0012 covered garden/great_hall/study/cellar; object examine: bug_0023/0024/0033 covered the strongbox). ### Cycle result — the watchman deadline now bites in careful play, not just by dawdling (bug_0040)
- Mandated blind playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, on the assessor's rank-1 candidate **clockwork_heist_v1**, seed 101 (+202/303/404/505/606 for alt routes); report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T12-11-45-401Z/playtest.md. Reached ALL FOUR endings across six runs; clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**; mechanically flawless (zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks, correct state-gated action swaps).
- HEADLINE finding (report §4, §5, AND verdict — the one actionable item, the rest were "no bugs"/cosmetic): the pack's cleverest system, the ledger-gated watchman crossing (bug_0019), is BURIED. The patrol only began at ticks ≥ 6, but a thorough truth-via-vault route — kitchen for the pick(1), gallery(2), study to read the ledger(3), back to cross(4) — arrives at only ~tick 4. So the guard "never appears on a direct route" and the ledger reads as dead flavor; its payoff "is only discoverable by dawdling." The playtester's explicit ask: "surface the time/guard tension a little earlier so the ledger's value is felt on a first, careful playthrough." (This is a genuine NEW actionable finding — note it disproves the prior cycle's "clockwork blind-verified pristine" framing; a fresh blind LLM with a careful-player strategy caught what ~15 prior random-leaning runs did not.)
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — text/threshold only; NO engine/validator/schema change, NO new scene/flag/item, reachable endings UNCHANGED): retuned bug_0019's deadline DOWN so the hour falls exactly on the thorough vault route's natural arrival. Gallery (`landing`) variants: tension ticks ≥ 2 (was 4), patrol ticks ≥ 4 (was 6). Safe `approach_vault` gated `any_of:[var_lte ticks 3, has_flag read_ledger]` (was var_lte 5). Gamble `cross_to_vault_blind` `var_gte ticks 4 + not read_ledger` (was 6). Foyer's mirroring variants (bug_0020) tracked down to ≥2/≥4. Net: a player who reads the ledger meets the patrol AT the crossing they were heading to and crosses safely on his rounds (clue pays off, felt, first careful playthrough); a player who visits the study but skips the ledger, or who dawdles, hits the same hour and faces the labelled gamble or must retreat to read it; straight-shot routes (pick→vault ≤ tick 2, no-tick crawlspace truth) stay safely under the hour — no gotcha, no soft-lock (study/foyer exits always remain).
- VERIFIED LIVE (MCP, seed 101): the thorough read-ledger route lands at the gallery on **ticks 4 with the watchman's-patrol prose AND approach_vault offered** (read_ledger gates it in), cross_to_vault_blind absent — the payoff at the natural crossing; tension prose already showed on first arrival at tick 2. run_playtest random 100 now reaches all four endings (caught 48, **patrol 36** — up from bug_0019's 16, truth 11, rich 4), every scene visited.
- Evidence: typecheck + ESLint + Prettier green; validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify-integrity static OK; **365 tests pass (+4 new).** clockwork content hash is UNPINNED in tests (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no hash re-pin was needed.
- DELIBERATE, surfaced test retunes (not laundered, documented in the bug_0040 artifact): bug_0019's `clockwork_guard_patrol_deadline.test.ts` and bug_0020's `clockwork_foyer_reactive_text.test.ts` each had a tension-prose sample taken at ticks 5 — which the moved band boundary now places in the patrol band — retargeted to a ticks-3 sample; bug_0020's hour-variant threshold assertion 6→4. All original bug_0019/bug_0020 invariants are still asserted and still hold under the new tuning.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0040_clockwork_patrol_reachable_in_careful_play.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_patrol_reachable_in_careful_play.test.ts (4 cases: thorough read-ledger route meets patrol at tick-4 natural crossing + ledger payoff + reaches ending_truth with no dawdling; skipping the ledger on the same route loses the safe crossing [clue is load-bearing] with exits intact; band boundary moved to tick 4 [tick 3 safe + tension-only]; straight-shot stays under the hour + all four endings still fire).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the duplicate-letter world-consistency note this playtest raised (report §5, cosmetic): the crawlspace strongbox and the vault each independently contain "a single sealed letter addressed to no one", both yielding ending_truth — two literal copies of the unique MacGuffin; a small text reframe (e.g. the crawlspace box as a steward's private duplicate/draft) would tidy it. (b) The still-logged doc/staleness assessor radar (since bug_0032/0035) remains the next-best cross-category meta-improvement now that both repo levers are disarmed (bug_0038). Keep rotating the blind playtest target/seed.

### Cycle result — the duplicate sealed-letter world-consistency double-take resolved (bug_0041)
- Mandated blind playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, on the assessor's rank-1 candidate **clockwork_heist_v1**, seed 314; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T12-27-24-550Z/playtest.md. Reached ALL FOUR endings across four runs; clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**; mechanically flawless (zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks, correct state-gated action swaps). Confirmed bug_0040's patrol retune holds.
- HEADLINE finding (report §4 + §5 — the one concrete actionable item; the time-pressure note is the adjudicated-deliberate bug_0019/0040 mechanic, and the playtester's "strongbox dangling thread" worry was UNVERIFIED and is in fact a complete alternate truth route, verified from the engine this cycle): the "truth" letter is the pack's UNIQUE MacGuffin (the steward's confession) reachable two ways — vault `take_letter` and crawlspace `open_strongbox` — but BOTH scenes introduced it with the identical phrasing "a single sealed letter, addressed to no one." A player who saw the crawlspace first and then took the vault route read it as **two literal copies of a one-of-a-kind item** ("initially made the player think there were two letters to track"). Independently corroborated last cycle (seed 101, logged as bug_0040 deferred next-focus (a)) — so two blind LLM playtests on different seeds flagged the same world-consistency slip.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — text-only; NO engine/validator/schema change, NO new scene/flag/item, reachable endings UNCHANGED, both truth routes intact): differentiated the two descriptions so they read as the steward's deliberately-PAIRED set rather than a copy-paste duplicate. (1) The **vault** letter is now the DISPLAYED copy — "laid out in plain sight, as though its writer meant whoever cracked the vault to find it here among the gold." (2) The **crawlspace strongbox** letter is now the HIDDEN copy in the steward's own hand — "not laid out for show like a thing meant to be found, but hidden away here in the dark, apart from the gold, where he meant no eye ever to fall on it." Each scene describes its own letter on its own terms; the crawlspace text deliberately does NOT presume the player ever saw the vault, preserving the bug_0017/0025 stands-alone discipline (still contains "sealed letter", still no "that same"). Anyone who sees both now reads the fair copy set out with the fortune + the private copy hidden behind the clock — both carrying the same confession the shared ending_truth narrates.
- VALIDATOR-SAFE: scene descriptions are pure prose the CYOA validator never analyses for reachability/gating; validate green 0/0.
- VERIFIED LIVE (MCP, seed 314): the crawlspace scene renders the new hidden-copy prose and the vault scene renders the new displayed-copy prose; both still introduce a takeable sealed letter and both routes still reach ending_truth (vault take_letter and crawlspace open_strongbox). The existing clockwork truth/crawlspace regression suites (bug_0017/0022/0025 etc.) stay green unchanged.
- Evidence: typecheck + ESLint + Prettier green; validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify-integrity static OK; **370 tests pass (+5 new).** clockwork content hash is UNPINNED in tests (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no hash re-pin was needed; NO prior test required retuning. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings, 0 errors** — no protected source touched, no test hash-pin moved, no override used.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0041_clockwork_duplicate_sealed_letter.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_duplicate_sealed_letter.test.ts (5 cases: the two letter descriptions are no longer identical / no bare "single sealed letter, addressed to no one" in the crawlspace; vault frames its copy as displayed-among-the-gold; crawlspace frames its copy as hidden-apart-from-the-gold while preserving the bug_0017 stands-alone invariants; both truth routes still reach ending_truth; grab-gold→rich + force→caught unchanged). Closes bug_0040 deferred next-focus (a).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the still-logged **doc/staleness assessor radar** (since bug_0032/0035) remains the next-best cross-category meta-improvement now that both repo levers are disarmed (bug_0038) and every pack is blind-verified — a deterministic detector that surfaces stale/contradictory docs (AGENTS.md / docs/ referencing renamed files or retired ceremonies). (b) clockwork is now polished bug_0019–0041 with two independent blind passes confirming pristine bar the now-fixed letter slip; STRONGLY rotate the mandated playtest target/seed off it next cycle — sealed_crypt and cold_forge are the least-recently full-blind-quality-tested packs. ### Cycle result — the timed-crossing payoff beat (bug_0042)
- Mandated blind playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, on the assessor's rank-1 candidate **clockwork_heist_v1**, seed 91; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T12-36-56-864Z/playtest.md. Reached ALL FOUR endings across four runs; clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**; mechanically flawless (zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks, correct state-gated action swaps). **No bugs found** — a third independent blind pass (after seeds 101, 314) confirms the pack is pristine.
- HEADLINE finding (report §4 — the one substantive, actionable signal): "the repeated 'time is running short' warnings may slightly over-promise a ticking-clock threat that a careful player never feels — the only time-gated danger is the guard, which is itself gated behind the gallery." I adjudicated the obvious "fix" (a dawdling timeout) as OUT OF BOUNDS: bug_0019 deliberately LOCKS the deadline as escapable ("safe study/foyer exits always remain — no soft-lock", asserted even at ticks≥7), so adding teeth would overturn a deliberately-locked invariant, not close a gap. Instead I found the invariant-safe root cause: the ledger's signature payoff — *timing a crossing past the active patrol* — was a SILENT non-event (`approach_vault` routed straight to `vault_door`, whose base text never acknowledged the player had just threaded a live patrol with stolen knowledge). The heist's tensest, most skillful beat read as an ordinary stroll, which is precisely why the careful player never "feels" the clock.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — additive reactive-text only; NO choice/effect/flag/item/exit/gating/reachable-ending change): `vault_door` gains ONE `variants` entry, `when: [var_gte ticks 4, has_flag read_ledger]`, narrating the timed crossing ("You wait, pressed to the automata, until the watchman's lantern sweeps to the gallery's far end — then cross in its wake and crouch at the vault while his tread recedes, just as the ledger swore you could…"). By construction `vault_door` is reachable at ticks≥4 ONLY via the ledger-gated `approach_vault` (a no-ledger player past the hour gets the blind gamble → ending_patrol, never this door), so the beat shows EXACTLY to the player who earned it and never to a pre-hour crosser (calm base text). Same mechanism as the existing foyer/kitchen/gallery clock prose (bug_0018/0020). The bug_0019/0040 no-soft-lock / escapable-deadline invariants are untouched.
- VERIFIED LIVE (MCP, seed 91): the thorough kitchen-pick / study-ledger route arrives at the gallery at ticks 4 with the patrol active, and `approach_vault` → `vault_door` now renders the timed-crossing prose; pick_lock remains available; pre-hour straight-shot crossers still see the calm base text.
- Evidence: typecheck + ESLint + Prettier green; validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify-integrity static OK; **374 tests pass (+4 new in the new regression file).** clockwork content hash is UNPINNED in tests (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no hash re-pin was needed. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings, 0 errors** — no protected source touched, no test hash-pin moved, no override used.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0042_clockwork_timed_crossing_payoff.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_timed_crossing_payoff.test.ts (4 cases: reader on the hour sees the timed-crossing prose; pre-hour crosser sees the calm base text — no leak; all four endings still fire incl. the no-ledger gamble→patrol; the bug_0008 lockpick-less nudge unaffected). One pre-existing test repointed, not weakened: cyoa_reactive_scene_text.test.ts (bug_0018) used `vault_door` as its variant-less control; since vault_door now has a variant, the control moved to the still-variant-free `crawlspace` (intent preserved; logged in the bug artifact's also_touched).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) **ROTATE the mandated playtest target off clockwork** — it is now polished across bug_0019–0042 with three independent blind passes (seeds 101/314/91) all rating it 5/5 and finding no remaining real flaw; **sealed_crypt** (7 bot-unvisited locations, parser) and **cold_forge** (rpg) are the least-recently full-blind-quality-tested packs and the highest-value next targets. (b) the still-logged **doc/staleness assessor radar** (since bug_0032/0035) remains the next-best cross-category meta-improvement now that both repo lint levers are disarmed (bug_0038). (c) cosmetic watchtower stale re-offers (tower_base/forest_crossroads) only if a future cycle already edits those scenes.

### Cycle result — the loot-room deadline payoff (bug_0043)
- Mandated blind playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, on the assessor's rank-1 candidate **clockwork_heist_v1**, seed 91; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T12-51-55-985Z/playtest.md. Reached ALL FIVE endings across four runs; clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**; mechanically flawless (zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks). **No bugs found** — a fourth independent blind pass (after seeds 101/314/91) again rates the pack pristine.
- HEADLINE finding (report §5/§6 — the one substantive signal, repeated in both sections): "the headline timed-crossing setpiece is avoidable, and a player who beelines may never see it… worth considering whether the patrol should be guaranteed at least once on the vault route." The watchman walks only at the hour (ticks≥4); an efficient thief reaches the vault at ticks 2 and never meets him, so the marquee deadline is **wholly absent from the heist's most obvious route** (grab the gold) — felt only in the gallery crossing, never in the loot room where the defining gold-vs-truth choice is made.
- ADJUDICATION: I rejected the literal reading ("force the patrol on the vault route"). The patrol is an early-vs-late *crossing* gate; forcing it would mean forcing the approach for everyone (flattening the deliberate ticks-2→4 prose build-up of bug_0019/0020) or removing the speed-reward the playtester explicitly praised as a feature — overturning locked invariants, not closing a gap. The invariant-safe root cause is that the gold route reached its climactic room in clock-SILENCE. So I made the room itself feel the clock.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — additive reactive-text only; NO choice/effect/flag/item/exit/gating/reachable-ending change): the `vault` scene (previously variant-free) gains TWO `variants` (first-match-wins, higher threshold first): `var_gte ticks 4` → the careful ledger-reader (by construction the ONLY player who can be inside at/after the hour, via the ledger-gated `approach_vault`) feels the watchman patrolling the one way out, framed around the rounds the ledger gave them so it never lies about the clean getaway they earned; `var_gte ticks 2` → the fast/normal thief (every gold-route player is at ticks≥2 — kitchen tick + landing tick) is told the clock grinds toward the hour and to be gone before the chime. The base `text` is UNCHANGED, so the bug_0041 paired-letter framing it carries ("in plain sight … among the gold", asserted on `.text`) stays byte-identical. Same mechanism as the foyer/gallery/vault_door clock prose (bug_0018/0020/0042).
- VERIFIED LIVE (through the engine, seed 91): a fast pre-hour thief at the vault (ticks 2) now reads the running-short clock prose and not the watchman beat; a ledger reader on the hour (ticks 4, provably read_ledger) reads the watchman-beyond-the-door beat and not the running-short variant; a fresh ticks-0 state renders the unchanged base. All endings still fire on both the fast and careful routes (gold→rich, letter→truth), force→caught, blind-crossing→patrol; the crawlspace truth route (never enters the vault) is unaffected.
- Evidence: typecheck + ESLint + Prettier green; validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify-integrity static OK; **378 tests pass (+4 new in tests/regression/clockwork_vault_deadline_payoff.test.ts).** clockwork content hash is UNPINNED in tests (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no hash re-pin was needed; new hash 080e8005f155ebd818fdd4d86700813599df9c9589dc45f84b0a4a149aff7d18. NO pre-existing test needed repointing (the cyoa_reactive_scene_text control already moved to `crawlspace` in bug_0042, and all vault-text assertions read the unchanged base `.text`).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0043_clockwork_vault_deadline_payoff.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_vault_deadline_payoff.test.ts (4 cases: fast pre-hour thief sees the running-short prose / not the watchman beat; ledger reader on the hour sees the watchman beat / not the running-short variant / is provably a reader; base text unchanged + bug_0041 framing intact + no clock prose at ticks 0; all endings fire on both routes incl. force→caught, blind→patrol, crawlspace→truth).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): **ROTATE the mandated playtest target OFF clockwork.** It is now polished across bug_0008–0043 with FOUR independent blind passes all rating 5/5 and the gold-route clock gap now closed — further polish is diminishing returns. The highest-value next targets are the never-fully-blind-tested packs the assessor still flags: **sealed_crypt** (parser, 7 bot-unvisited locations), **alchemists_tower** (parser), **cold_forge** and **sunken_barrow** (rpg). Reached ALL FOUR endings, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless. Its ONE "real flaw" — that the shared `ending_truth` text says the letter was "laid out in plain sight … among the gold," contradicting the crawlspace's hidden-copy framing — I VERIFIED FALSE by replaying the crawlspace→ending_truth route via MCP: the actual ending text is path-agnostic ("…nothing but the truth. Some things are worth more than escaping rich."), with no "plain sight" phrasing. That phrasing lives only in the vault SCENE (bug_0041's deliberate paired-copy framing); the playtester attributed a scene description to the ending. clockwork is pristine on its 5th independent blind pass (seeds 101/314/91/211/103); its other notes (toothless deadline, tick up/down asymmetry) are the adjudicated-deliberate bug_0019/0040/0042/0043 mechanic.
- DECISION — ROTATED the actual improvement off clockwork (the standing next-focus since bug_0042; justified by the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value"). Per blind_playtest_protocol.md's "rotate the seed and rotate the pack", ran a fresh blind playtest on the least-recently full-blind-quality-tested pack, the parser pack **The Sealed Crypt**, seed 47 (+seed 99 gating probe); report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T13-07-14-415Z/playtest_sealed_crypt.md. WON ending_victory 35/35; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks; gating clean both ways.
- HEADLINE finding (sealed_crypt report §5): the **Bell Tower** kept reading "its great hemp pull-rope still hangs coiled on a peg" AFTER `take rope` — stale "still-present" text contradicting the just-emptied room (visible_objects correctly dropped the rope; it was in inventory). Same stale-state class as bug_0007/0010/0039, but the ONE takeable-object room never given a reactive `variant` (old_well/crypt already had theirs). Verified live before fixing.
- Also CONFABULATED in the sealed_crypt report (verified false from source AND live): a claimed score over-count (crypt gate "+35" past a 15 total, hidden by clamp). Real awards are headstone +5, rope-on-well +10, crypt gate +20 = exactly 35 = max_score; live score events read +10/+20. No clamp, no over-count. No fix.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — reactive room text only; NO engine/validator/schema/flag/item/score/exit/gating/reachable-ending change): bell_tower gains ONE `variant`, `when: [{ has_item: rope }]` → "the peg where its great hemp pull-rope hung is bare now — the coil is in your hands." Keyed on **has_item rope, NOT the rope_attached_to_well flag**, on purpose: `remove_item` at the well (bug_0034) only empties your hands — it does NOT place the rope in any room — so the object falls back to its home room and is shown present in the Bell Tower again (a latent bug_0034 artifact, left untouched). Keying on the flag would make the text read "bare" while visible_objects still lists the rope (a NEW contradiction); keying on has_item shows "bare" exactly when the rope is in hand (visible_objects empty) and otherwise (never-taken / dropped-back / returned-home-after-tying) falls back to the base text while the rope IS present — so room text and visible_objects never disagree.
- VERIFIED LIVE (MCP, seed 47): before-take shows coiled + rope present; after-take shows "bare now" + rope gone with a state_hash byte-identical to the pre-fix run (prose-only); tie-then-return-to-tower reverts to base text + rope present (self-consistent). validate green 0/0 (variants carry no effects).
- Evidence: typecheck + ESLint + Prettier green; validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify:integrity static OK; **382 tests pass (+4 new).** sealed_crypt content hash is UNPINNED in tests (only watchtower_road is pinned), so no re-pin needed; new hash 2c23dd6ead82718d936ecb1fe901e2bd4a1917fff894160f993781ff3e2e8f03. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings, 0 errors** — no protected source touched, no test hash-pin moved, no override used. Caught + fixed mid-cycle: Prettier flagged the new test file's formatting; ran `prettier --write` on it (the gate working).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0044_sealed_crypt_bell_tower_rope_text.yaml + tests/regression/sealed_crypt_bell_tower_rope_text.test.ts (4 cases: coiled+present before take; bare+absent after take with look matching obs; tie→return reverts to base text+present with no text/object contradiction; full route still wins 35/35).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the latent **bug_0034 object-placement artifact** — `remove_item: rope` at the well returns the rope to its home room (bell_tower), so a player who climbs back up after tying can re-take and re-tie it; harmless (well stays open by flag, no soft-lock) but fiction-odd. A future cycle could add a `place_object` to keep the spent rope "at the well" (or destroy it), but that is bug_0034 engine territory and should re-verify its no-soft-lock invariants. (b) The still-logged **doc/staleness assessor radar** (since bug_0032/0035) remains the next cross-category meta-improvement (both repo lint levers disarmed, bug_0038). (c) Keep rotating the blind playtest seed/pack — cold_forge and sunken_barrow are now the least-recently full-blind-quality-tested.
