### Cycle result — the assessor's third cross-category radar: a doc-staleness detector (bug_0045)
- Mandated blind playtest (clockwork, seed 271): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T13-25-33-693Z/playtest.md. Reached ALL FOUR endings across five routes (Truth via both the vault AND the crawlspace strongbox, Caught, Patrol, Rich); clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**; mechanically flawless. Its two notes — the strongbox shortcut and the "time running short" copy over-promising a timeout — are the adjudicated-DELIBERATE bug_0019/0022/0032/0040/0042/0043 mechanic. clockwork is blind-verified PRISTINE on its **6th** independent pass (seeds 101/314/91/211/103/271); NO new actionable content defect.
- ROTATED a second blind playtest (per blind_playtest_protocol.md's "rotate the seed and rotate the pack") onto the least-recently full-blind-quality-tested pack, the rpg **The Cold Forge**, seed 73 + an adversarial no-buff run; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T13-25-33-693Z/playtest_cold_forge.md. WON ending_victory **50/50** both runs; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks, no stale text. Its PRIMARY actionable signal — "could not confirm a death ending is wired up; an unlucky base-stat fight could be a frustrating dead end" — I VERIFIED NOT-A-DEFECT from source: `slag_sentinel.death_ending: ending_fallen` (cold_forge.yaml:236) routes a combat death to `ending_fallen` (lines 254-260, `death: true`). Its other notes (the "cold keeps the sentinel weak / no timer" reassurance reading as a tell; the +15/+15/+20 awards reading as milestone totals) are taste/cosmetic and internally consistent (the lore already says "this forge-fire is long dead and will not kindle"). cold_forge is clean — no content defect this cycle.
- DECISION — with BOTH packs blind-verified and **no concrete content defect** surfaced (unlike bug_0039, which had a real stale-text contradiction and so won over this same radar), the genuinely highest-value lever is the standing cross-category candidate the assessor has named in every recent "next suggested focus" and bug deferred list since bug_0032/0035 (re-logged through bug_0044 deferred[b]): a **doc/staleness detector**. Justified per the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value." Aligned with trust-but-verify — the radar is a verification/hygiene instrument keeping the docs an honest map for the next fresh agent.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **repo** layer — src/afk/assessor.ts only + a regression test; NO content/YAML/engine edited): added the assessor's third cross-category radar. (1) a pure, injected-disk `findStaleDocRefs(docText, exists)` that extracts CONCRETE first-party file references a doc claims exist and returns those that don't resolve — considering only tokens under a known first-party prefix with a concrete extension (.ts/.tsx/.js/.mjs/.cjs/.json/.yaml/.yml/.md/.sh), and SKIPPING glob/placeholder patterns and command-line output destinations (`--record`/`--out`/`-o`/`>`). (2) `docStalenessDocs(root)` scans the CURRENT-system docs (AGENTS.md, README.md, AI_AGENT_PROMPT.md, docs/**/*.md minus ROADMAP.md); the historical AI_LOOP_STATE.md and forward-looking ROADMAP/BUILD_SPEC are out of scope by construction (same discipline as LINT_DIRS). (3) a `repo-doc-staleness` candidate (effort S) that fires when any reference doesn't resolve and is silent otherwise.
- EMPIRICAL FINDING that shaped the design: the one non-resolving concrete path among 58 references in the canonical docs is README's `--record traces/run.json` — a CLI **output-destination example**, not a stale reference. So the radar's output-flag exclusion is load-bearing and regression-locked; with it, the canonical docs scan CLEAN and the radar **ships disarmed**. It RE-ARMS the moment a future edit renames/deletes a file a canonical doc points at — fire-while-real / silent-when-done, like the lint-coverage radar.
- VERIFIED: ranking unchanged (the radar is disarmed; `npm run assess` still tops out at the uniform 0.5 blind-playtest reviews). Mid-cycle gotcha caught + fixed: a wrapped code comment put `TODO` immediately after `//`, which the engine-marker scan flagged as a real marker (engine-todos jumped to rank 1) — reworded the comment so the word no longer sits after the slashes; src/ is back to zero markers and the candidate vanished. (The detector working on itself.)
- Evidence: typecheck + ESLint + Prettier green; validate green 0/0 (all packs, none touched); full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify:integrity static OK; **392 tests pass (+10 new).** NO content hash moved (no YAML edited). verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings, 0 errors** — no protected source touched, no test hash-pin moved, no override used. Caught + fixed mid-cycle: Prettier flagged the two new/edited files' formatting; ran `prettier --write` (the gate working).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0045_doc_staleness_radar.yaml + tests/regression/assessor_doc_staleness.test.ts (10 cases: findStaleDocRefs flags a non-resolving ref / silent when all resolve / ignores glob+placeholder+ellipsis / ignores output destinations / ignores bare dirs + non-first-party prefixes / dedups / strips trailing punctuation; the real repo raises NO candidate; the README output-example does NOT false-fire; and the mechanism WOULD catch a deleted ref — AGENTS.md's scripts/verify-integrity.ts pretended gone is flagged, proving it isn't vacuously disarmed). Closes bug_0038 deferred[2] / the long-logged doc-staleness lever.
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) extend the radar to stale REFERENCES IN CODE COMMENTS, or a complementary check for docs referencing RETIRED CONCEPTS (e.g. the "§14 ceremony" the charter retired) — a fuzzier signal better suited to an LLM-judge than this deterministic detector. (b) cold_forge's triple "cold keeps the sentinel weak / no timer" reassurance reads as a tell for a mechanic that doesn't exist; internally consistent so left untouched — trim only if a future cycle already edits those rooms. (c) Keep rotating the blind playtest seed/pack — **sunken_barrow** is now the least-recently full-blind-quality-tested.

### Cycle result — the dumbwaiter reframed as an honest servants' lift (bug_0046)
- Mandated blind playtest (clockwork, seed 101 — 7th independent blind pass): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T13-42-28-631Z/playtest.md. Reached all three endings (Truth via BOTH the vault letter and the crawlspace strongbox, Rich, Caught) across four runs; clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**; mechanically flawless — zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks, no stale text. The recently-fixed reactivity (bug_0042/0043/0044) all confirmed working.
- The ONE concrete, actionable finding (report §4/§5): the kitchen's `dumbwaiter` choice ("Ride the dumbwaiter up to the gallery.") "is framed like a clever stealth bypass but is mechanically identical to the stairs" — it lands you on the exact same patrolled gallery `climb_stairs` reaches, charging the same gallery tick, so the most heist-flavored option over-promises and pays nothing off. (Its second note, the `ticks` timer being pure flavor, is the adjudicated-DELIBERATE escapable-deadline design of bug_0019/0040.) This is the last open cosmetic content lever on the otherwise-pristine clockwork pack.
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **content** layer — clockwork_heist.yaml only): honest-reframing, NOT a new mechanic. I deliberately did NOT give the dumbwaiter a real watchman-skipping advantage: a second safe late crossing would duplicate and undo the load-bearing ledger clue bug_0040 tuned to be the ONLY safe ticks>=4 crossing. Instead the choice text becomes "Take the servants' dumbwaiter straight up to the gallery." and gains a `narrate` effect naming it the cramped servants' lift — a quieter, more direct way up from the kitchens that "leaves you no closer to the vault than the open steps would." Still routes to `landing`; no tick/flag/item/exit/gating/reachable-ending change.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify:integrity static OK; **395 tests pass (+3 new).** Content hash moved deliberately (clockwork is unpinned in tests — only watchtower_road is pinned): 080e8005f155… → 5479c562a4101e02975048c05257fb348351e4cf55c3f6192c58823fb529e213. No protected source touched; no hash-pin in any test moved; no override used. Caught + fixed mid-cycle: ESLint flagged an unused helper/import in the new test (the gate working) — removed them.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0046_clockwork_dumbwaiter_honest_framing.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_dumbwaiter_honest_framing.test.ts (3 cases: dumbwaiter still goes kitchen→landing and narrates the servants' lift / "no closer to the vault" with the old "Ride the dumbwaiter up" framing retired; it is NOT a mechanical bypass — same scene + same tick count (2) as the grand stair, so it can't cheat the watchman/ledger tuning; reachability unchanged — gold→rich and letter→truth via the dumbwaiter route, force→caught via the stair, crawlspace truth unaffected).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) **sunken_barrow** is now the least-recently full-blind-quality-tested pack — rotate the next blind playtest onto it. (b) the doc-staleness radar's fuzzier siblings (stale refs in code comments; docs referencing RETIRED CONCEPTS like the "§14 ceremony", better suited to an LLM-judge) remain the standing cross-category meta-lever (bug_0045 deferred[a]). (c) cold_forge's triple "cold keeps the sentinel weak / no timer" reassurance reads as a tell for a non-existent mechanic; internally consistent, trim only if a future cycle already edits those rooms (bug_0045 deferred[b]).

- HEADLINE finding (sunken_barrow report §5): the **Slab Passage** slab, after being levered, kept its OBJECT examine reading the PRE-MOVE instruction "Wedge the bar beneath the lip … heaving again and again until the old stone gives" — telling the player to lever a slab that is already open, contradicting the room (which correctly flips to "Levered aside now … bared a stair" on `slab_moved`). Same stale-state class as bug_0007/0010/0028/0039/0044, but on the OBJECT examine; the room had its reactive `variant` (bug_0011), the object never did. Verified live from the engine before fixing. (The report's other note — the circlet "auto-taken" on entering the relic chamber — is the known-deferred opening-objective design choice, bug_0037; not chased.)

Gated on the SAME slab_moved stage the room variant uses (first match wins; one variant). The lever's one-shot retire (bug_0015) and the seeded might check (bug_0027, DC 12) are untouched. Same mechanism as the alchemists' cauldron (bug_0039) and the sealed_crypt bell-tower rope (bug_0044).
- VERIFIED LIVE (MCP, seed 59): pre-lever examine = base "Wedge the bar…" text; post-lever examine = "levered aside now … a stair drops away…", no longer the wedge/heave instruction, agreeing with the room; full route still wins ending_victory 50/50.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0 (all packs); full `npm run health` **exit 0**; verify:integrity static OK; **398 tests pass (+3 new).** Deliberate, SURFACED re-pin of a protected asset: traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json content_hash 7480eb9ab564… → cb2e487c77d9… (the stone_slab variant moves the sunken_barrow content hash; same accompanied snapshot-update workflow as bug_0037). The trace's **expected_final_hash is UNCHANGED (871ddf3e…)** — prose-only, so the seed-1 victory replays to the identical final state. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 1 WARNING** — HASH_PIN_REPINNED on the trace (legitimate accompanied-by-content-change, not blocking). No protected SOURCE file touched; no override used.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0047_barrow_slab_examine_stale.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_barrow_slab_examine_stale.test.ts (3 cases: pre-lever examine reads base wedge/heave; post-lever examine flips to levered-aside / no longer wedge-heave / agrees with the room; cosmetic — examining changes no state and the seed-1 route still wins 50/50).

(b) the doc-staleness radar's fuzzier siblings (stale refs in code comments; docs referencing RETIRED CONCEPTS like the "§14 ceremony", better suited to an LLM-judge) remain the standing cross-category meta-lever (bug_0045 deferred[a]). ### Cycle result — the canonical playtest protocol stops citing the retired §14 gate (bug_0049)
- Mandated blind playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 163 — **9th independent blind pass**): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T14-16-36-176Z/playtest.md. Reached ending_truth / ending_rich / ending_caught across three runs; clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**; mechanically flawless — zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks, no stale text. Its two §4 notes (the narrative-only "time running short" pressure; the honest-but-non-tactical dumbwaiter) are the long-adjudicated-DELIBERATE escapable-deadline invariant (bug_0019/0040/0042/0043) and the intended honest framing (bug_0046). **No new actionable content defect** — clockwork is pristine on its 9th pass (seeds 101/314/91/211/103/271/137/113/163).
- ROTATED the improvement off content (justified by the protocol's "you MAY pick a different candidate if your judgement and the playtest say it's higher value", with the mandated target verified clean) onto the highest-value standing cross-category lever — the long-logged **bug_0045 deferred[a]**: docs citing the RETIRED §14 ceremony. A targeted doc audit found exactly one CURRENT-PROCESS doc still instructing it as live.
- HEADLINE finding: **docs/blind_playtest_protocol.md** — the canonical protocol the AFK loop runs EVERY cycle and wires into the agent prompt (src/ai-loop.ts) — carried the pre-charter step-4 triage rule: "`engine_rule` / `validator` / schema → **gated** (§14): propose only; a human reviews. Do not silently change engine rules in an AFK cycle." This directly contradicts the trust-but-verify charter (AGENTS.md: "full authority … no human-approval gate and no §14 ceremony"; the live cycle prompt also says "new mechanics no longer need a §14 ceremony"). A fresh agent following the protocol would REFUSE the very engine/validator fixes the charter grants — a stale doc steering behaviour the wrong way, not a harmless history note. (AGENTS.md, docs/afk_loop.md, docs/stage4_rpg_gate.md had all been updated to say the ceremony is GONE; this one doc was missed.)
- IMPROVEMENT (one, **doc** layer — docs/blind_playtest_protocol.md only): the engine_rule/validator/schema triage bullet rewritten to match AGENTS.md — "change them **freely under trust, but verify** (`AGENTS.md`): full authority, no human-approval gate, no §14 ceremony. The automated verification is the bar — keep `npm run health` green and add a regression test for the new behaviour. Don't route around the verifier." The content/hint/quest-structure bullet and the re-validate/replay line are unchanged. NO content pack, engine, validator, or schema edited.
- Locked by a conservative, deterministic doc-coherence guard: tests/regression/docs_trust_but_verify_coherence.test.ts. Its pure predicate `instructsRetiredGateAsLive(docText)` matches ONLY imperative gate phrasing ("gated (§14)", "propose only; a human reviews", "Do not silently change engine rules") — so it never false-fires on the charter docs' NEGATIONS ("no §14 ceremony") or on past-tense history ("went through the §14 gate"). It guards only the three docs a fresh agent follows as current process (AGENTS.md, blind_playtest_protocol.md, afk_loop.md); README's mixed history, the historical AI_LOOP_STATE.md, and the ROADMAP/BUILD_SPEC/stage4 records are deliberately out of scope. A "mechanism live" case re-injects the stale line into the real protocol text and asserts it is flagged (not vacuous); a "no vacuous pass" case asserts the protocol still affirmatively routes engine/validator findings through trust-but-verify.
- Evidence: verify:integrity static OK; typecheck + ESLint + Prettier green; full `npm run health` **exit 0**; **412 tests pass (+10 new).** NO content hash moved (no pack edited); NO protected file or HASH_PIN file touched; NO re-pin; NO override used. verify-integrity DRIFT vs HEAD: **exit 0, 0 warnings, 0 errors**. Caught + fixed mid-cycle: Prettier flagged the new test file's formatting — ran `prettier --write` (the gate working).
- Closes **bug_0045 deferred next-focus (a)** — "docs referencing RETIRED CONCEPTS (e.g. the §14 ceremony)".
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the SIBLING signal — stale §14-as-live phrasing also lingers in **code comments** (`agents/fixer.ts:14` says engine_rule/validator/test changes "are gated (§14)") and in the spec/history docs; a future cycle could either (i) extend the guard to agents/*.ts comments or (ii) leave the spec/history records as legitimate history. (b) the **semantic-weakening blind spot** in verify-integrity (catches mechanical tampering, not a meaning-preserving check weakening — needs an LLM-judge, noted since cycle 2026-06-01c). (c) sunken_barrow's auto-take-of-circlet agency note (a gating change worth a dedicated cycle, bug_0047 deferred). Per-pack content polish remains at deep diminishing returns — every pack is blind-verified; keep rotating the mandated seed.

### Cycle result — reactive Truth-ending epilogue (bug_0051)
- Blind LLM playtest: fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access, seed 137; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T14-36-30-776Z/playtest.md. Reached all 3 endings (Truth/Rich/Caught) across 4 runs; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks).
- Finding fixed (its one concrete, repeatedly-flagged note, report §1/§5): the two letters that reach `ending_truth` are framed as near-opposites — the vault copy "laid out in plain sight... meant whoever cracked the vault to find it", the crawlspace copy "hidden away in the dark... where he meant no eye to fall" — yet both funneled into ONE identical epilogue that contradicted whichever route the player just played. bug_0041 paired the letters only at pickup; the payoff still flattened them.
- Improvement (engine + content): endings gained the reactive `variants` capability scenes already had. `EndingSchema.variants` is `.optional()` (byte-identical compile / hash-stable for endings that don't use it, like SceneSchema.variants/bug_0010); new `endingText()` in runner.ts mirrors `sceneText`; observation.ts `textFor` resolves it. The vault route sets `letter_displayed`, the crawlspace route sets `letter_hidden`, and `ending_truth` now selects the matching epilogue; the original route-neutral text is kept as the base fallback (bug_0017/0025/0030 invariants preserved). No route/tick/gate/reachable-ending change.
- Evidence: validate green 0/0; `npm run health` exit 0; 424 tests (was 419 — +5 new cases). Deliberate, surfaced re-pin: clockwork content hash 5479c562… → 7e489e9f7ff66230f5b417c8fb4ca83d92017ef30d312e01f865793f3a4ec771 (clockwork is unpinned in tests; only watchtower_road is pinned). Note: the in-session long-running MCP server still held the pre-change schema and rejected the new key without a restart; the engine path it serves is exactly what the regression test drives (buildObservation → endingText).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0051_clockwork_truth_ending_reactive_letter.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_truth_ending_reactive_letter.test.ts (5 cases: displayed epilogue, hidden epilogue, the two differ, moral/no-gold invariant across base+both variants, reachability unchanged).
- Next suggested focus: the now-general reactive-ending capability could pay off the watchtower_road endings (e.g. ending_truth vs the ledger/letter relationship flagged in earlier cycles); or continue the unverified-pack blind playtests (watchtower_road, alchemists_tower, sealed_crypt, cold_forge, sunken_barrow) per the assessor backlog.

### Cycle result — alchemists black-phial intrinsic danger cue (bug_0052)
- Mandated blind LLM playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 53): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T14-47-28-763Z/playtest.md. Clockwork's 9th independent blind pass — reached all FOUR endings (Truth x2 variants, Caught, Rich), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless. Its only §4 notes (the "time is running short" pressure not biting on short routes; the guard-rounds clue reading as a special crossing) are the long-adjudicated-DELIBERATE escapable-deadline tuning (bug_0019/0040/0042/0043): the fast route's no-bite IS the reward; the careful route's tick>=4 patrol payoff is shipped + regression-locked; the ledger journal is load-bearing prose the vault_door / ending_patrol callbacks quote verbatim. NO new actionable clockwork defect — clockwork is verified-clean.
- Rotated playtest (per blind_playtest_protocol.md "rotate the seed and rotate the pack", the same move bug_0047 made when the mandated pack came back clean): The Alchemist's Tower (parser, least-recently full-blind-quality-tested of the puzzle packs), seed 67; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T14-47-28-763Z/playtest_alchemists_tower.md. WON ending_cured 35/35 + reached ending_poisoned; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5; zero rejected commands, no loops, no soft-locks.
- Finding fixed (rotated report §4/§5): the lethal black phial could be taken and drunk to ending_poisoned with ZERO in-place warning by a player who skipped the optional Library (the sole caution lived in the spellbook recipe in a room never entered), so the death read as "arbitrary"; worse, the phial's base examine text "...The recipe warned against it." cited a recipe a library-skipper had not read (a quiet contradiction).
- Improvement (content only, reactive object variants — bug_0023/0033/0039/0044 shape): black_phial's base description rewritten to an INTRINSIC sensory dread that does not assume the recipe ("...seems to drink the light. An acrid reek bleeds from under its wax-sealed stopper... never brewed to be drunk."), plus ONE variant gated on read_recipe restoring the sharper named callback ("...no medicine but death — do not drink it."). Examine-only: no flag/item/score/exit/interaction/gating/reachable-ending change — the drink interaction and ending_poisoned are untouched, so the death stays exactly as reachable (the cue WARNS, it does not GATE).
- VERIFIED: npm run health **exit 0**; **428 tests pass (+4 new).** alchemists_tower is NOT hash-pinned and has no committed trace, so the content-hash move has nothing to re-pin; NO protected/HASH_PIN file touched; NO override used; verify:integrity green (0 errors, 0 warnings).
- Surfaced test update (not a weakening): bug_0039's regression used the black phial as its incidental variant-less control; giving the phial a variant invalidated that choice, so the backward-compat assertion was repointed to the still-variant-less `herb` — the invariant is preserved, not deleted.
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0052_alchemists_phial_danger_cue.yaml + tests/regression/alchemists_phial_danger_cue.test.ts (4 cases: library-skipper sees intrinsic cue + never the word "recipe"; recipe reader gets the named callback; the cue warns-not-gates so the death is still reachable with the Library skipped; the brew route still wins ending_cured at full score).
- Next suggested focus (logged, not yet fixed): (a) the SHARED climactic-agency gap — alchemists' spire "must be administered" and sunken_barrow's auto-taken circlet (bug_0047) both win on bare room entry. Root cause is an ENGINE limitation: the parser runner evaluates win_conditions ONLY in onEnter (src/parser/runner.ts documents post-action wins as "out of scope for v1"). A clean explicit-claim beat needs a post-action win hook (or on_take effects, or a TAKE/CLAIM interaction verb) — a focused, backward-compatible engine cycle (existing packs' wins are visited-based, so a post-action re-check is idempotent for them). This is the highest-value next improvement and would pay off two packs at once. (b) the still-standing semantic-weakening blind spot in verify-integrity (needs an LLM-judge). (c) continue rotating blind playtests across the remaining packs (watchtower_road, sealed_crypt, cold_forge) per the assessor backlog.

- Mandated blind LLM playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 211): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T15-05-47-942Z/playtest.md. Clockwork's ~10th independent blind pass — reached all FOUR endings (Truth x2 epilogue variants, Rich, Caught), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks); verdict "polished and ships-ready". The §5 two-letters note is the working bug_0051 reactive-epilogue feature.
- Finding fixed (the ONE actionable note not already closed/contradicted — report §4 friction #2): the steward's ledger is the heist's load-bearing timing clue (bug_0040: reading it keeps the safe on-the-hour crossing; not reading it leaves only the blind gamble), yet the study presented "Ledgers" as ambient furniture and its only signposted feature, the brass wall-plate, speaks to the LOCK ("never to force"), not the watch. So a first-timer had no way to weigh the open ledger's value before committing to read it, and could leave the study having skipped its whole point. The gallery's later callback "the hourly patrol the steward's ledger warned of" referred to a warning the ledger never voiced on-screen.
- Improvement (content only, legibility): the `study` scene text now singles out the one OPEN ledger on the desk and names what its last page records — "the household's hours: when the staff retire, and when the watch walks its rounds" — and the read choice changes from "Read the open ledger." to "Read the open ledger's account of the watch's rounds." Honest pull for the load-bearing clue; makes the gallery callback literally true. NO flag/tick/route/gating/reachable-ending change — the read_ledger effect (set read_ledger + the actionable rounds journal) and the on-the-hour safe-crossing gate (bug_0019/0040) and the timed-crossing payoff (bug_0042) are byte-identical in behaviour.
- VERIFIED: validate green 0/0; `npm run health` **exit 0**; **432 tests pass (+4 new).** clockwork is NOT hash-pinned in tests (only watchtower_road is), so nothing to re-pin; deliberate surfaced content-hash move recorded in the artifact: 7e489e9f… → 98fd1a83b0b4a0e0232d9663be3ff781d39c45f273a9ad3e92c9d8990ad83d87. - Mandated blind LLM playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 53): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T15-18-32-296Z/playtest.md. Clockwork's ~11th independent blind pass — reached all FOUR endings (Rich, Caught, Truth ×2 reactive epilogues), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks). Every prior fix (bug_0019/0040/0041/0042/0043/0046/0051/0053) confirmed holding. Its only two notes — the dumbwaiter has no mechanical payoff (honest by design, bug_0046) and the ledger is a safety-net not a hard gate — were each explicitly flagged "not a bug / reasonable design"; nudging the tuned tick/watchman/ledger machinery on two non-defects is net-negative risk. Clockwork is verified-clean; the higher-value move was a rotated playtest + fix elsewhere (justified per the protocol's "rotate the seed and rotate the pack").
- Rotated scouting: ran TWO fresh parallel blind passes on the least-recently full-quality-tested packs — The Sealed Crypt (parser, seed 29) and The Cold Forge (rpg, seed 29). Both WON, both clarity 5/5, zero rejected actions / loops / soft-locks, gating clean both directions. Cold Forge (50/50, "Keeper of the Ember") surfaced only design observations (no wrong-way to test the sentinel-cold lore; the Bellows Walk "way east" is the standard locked-exit-until-the-fight idiom) — neither a stale-state contradiction, deferred not fixed. Sealed Crypt surfaced the strongest concrete, player-facing finding.
- Finding fixed (sealed_crypt report §5): USING the iron key on the catacombs gate awards the climactic +20 — score reads "35 of 35" (= max_score) — but the WIN only fires on ENTERING the catacombs (win_conditions: visited catacombs). With the old open-gate text ("…swung inward on darkness. The stair climbs back up.") and the old unlock narration ("…the catacombs gate swings inward."), a first-timer at max score could read the puzzle as solved and climb back UP, stopping one step short of ending_victory. The climactic award and the ending trigger are decoupled and nothing signposted the last move north through the open gate (the protocol's "an ending you can't find" watch-item).
- Improvement (content only, legibility — bug_0044/0053 shape): TWO prose-only edits to sealed_crypt.yaml. (1) crypt's catacombs_open `variant` now ends "…— and through it, north, run the catacombs you came so far to find. The stair climbs back up." (2) crypt_gate's USE `narrate` now ends "…the catacombs you came so far to find — one step through the open gate and your journey is done." Both name the final `go_north` move explicitly. NO flag/item/score/exit/gating/reachable-ending change — the route still wins ending_victory at 35/35 on `go_north` (confirmed live).
- VERIFIED: validate_pack ok 0/0; live MCP replay (seed 29): at the gate-unlock step score=35/35, the unlock narration AND the open-gate room text both name the north step, then go_north → ending_victory "Final score: 35 of 35". `npm run health` **exit 0**; **435 tests pass (+3 new).** sealed_crypt is NOT hash-pinned in tests (only watchtower_road is), so nothing to re-pin; deliberate surfaced content-hash move recorded in the artifact. NO protected/HASH_PIN file touched; NO override used; verify:integrity green (0/0).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0054_sealed_crypt_final_step_legibility.yaml + tests/regression/sealed_crypt_final_step_legibility.test.ts (3 cases: before unlock the gate "bars the way" with no north exit; at unlock the +20 lands [35/35] and BOTH narration and open-gate room text name the north step with the north exit present; the full route still wins ending_victory 35/35).
- Next suggested focus (unchanged from bug_0052/0053, still the highest-value next move): the SHARED climactic-agency engine gap — alchemists' spire "must be administered" and sunken_barrow's auto-taken circlet (bug_0047) both win on bare room entry because the parser runner evaluates win_conditions only in onEnter (src/parser/runner.ts: post-action wins "out of scope for v1"). Sealed Crypt's bug_0054 is the CYOA/parser-prose flavour of the same family: where a win fires a step after the climactic beat, the prose must carry the player that last step. A focused, backward-compatible engine cycle adding a post-action win hook (or on_take/CLAIM verb) would pay off two packs at once. Then continue rotating blind playtests (watchtower_road, sunken_barrow). Its two §4/§5 notes were misreads of long-adjudicated-DELIBERATE design: (a) the "ledger swore you could" crossing is a false promise — it is NOT: the ledger gate only bites at ticks>=4, a fast crosser at ticks<=3 is legitimately safe ("be fast OR scout"), and "just as the ledger swore" only renders when has_flag read_ledger (never a false promise to a non-reader); (b) "re-examine clock is redundant" conflates runs — in run 3 the tester examined/pried for the FIRST time after the kitchen, and the enter_panel re-entry (has_flag found_passage + has_item lockpick) handles true re-entry cleanly. No new actionable clockwork defect; verified-clean.
- Rotated scouting (per protocol "rotate the seed and rotate the pack" + "you MAY pick a different candidate if higher value"): ran TWO fresh parallel blind passes on the least-recently full-quality-tested packs — The Cold Forge (rpg, seed 41) and The Watchtower Road (cyoa, seed 19). Cold Forge WON 50/50 ("Keeper of the Ember"), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5, zero rejected actions/loops/soft-locks, no-bar case recoverable; surfaced only design wishlist (RNG grate DC12 with no way to raise might; the +2 buff isn't strictly required) — subjective balance, not a contradiction, deferred. Watchtower (3 runs, 3 endings, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5) surfaced the cycle's one concrete actionable finding.
- Finding fixed (watchtower report §5): the `confront_smuggler` scene had ONE static text — "The sergeant's face goes white as he reads his own name…" (a damning reveal) — but it is reached by presenting the sealed letter regardless of proof, and offers two mutually-exclusive forward choices: reveal_evidence (has_flag learned_truth) and press_bluff (not_flag learned_truth). A player who took the letter (east) but learned nothing (no cellar, no hermit) lands with learned_truth unset, so the ONLY forward move is press_bluff: "the letter's still sealed and you can prove nothing" — directly contradicting the scene that just narrated him reading his own name. Residual from bug_0002's structural pass, which added the press_bluff branch but left the text presuming the reveal.
- Improvement (content only, reactive-text — bug_0018/0023/0033 shape): base text made route-neutral ("The sergeant reads, and his face goes white as he finds his own name") so it holds for both the hermit (seal_broken) and cellar (still-sealed) proof routes; added a `variants` entry (when not_flag learned_truth) — "The sergeant turns the wax-sealed letter over… The seal is unbroken, and you've no idea what it says… no kind of proof…" — which press_bluff now reads true against. NO flag/route/choice/reachable-ending change (still truth/escape/captured).
- VERIFIED: validate_pack ok 0/0. Live via buildObservation (the MCP server's view): no-proof route → confront_smuggler, learned_truth unset, text matches /unbroken/ + /no kind of proof/ and NOT /his own name/, offers press_bluff not reveal_evidence, press_bluff → ending_captured. With-proof route (letter+cellar ledger) → confront_smuggler, learned_truth set, text matches /his own name/ not /unbroken/, offers reveal_evidence not press_bluff, reveal_evidence → expose_the_plot → ending_truth. `npm run health` **exit 0; 438 tests pass (+3 new).**
- HASH RE-PIN (deliberate, surfaced): watchtower_road IS the hash-pinned pack. Edited content → re-pinned BOTH pins to 3947f07bb7d8e898b6bf17ce2db6352652ae0171810a705937cea2c95bb428cd (was 7e3392b6… in tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts; was 4188f7de… in traces/bugs/bug_0002). verify-integrity --against HEAD: **0 errors, 2 warnings** (both HASH_PIN_REPINNED accompanied by content change — the legitimate workflow). NO override used; NO protected file weakened; NO test disabled (count +3).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0055_watchtower_confront_reactive_text.yaml + tests/regression/watchtower_confront_reactive_text.test.ts (3 cases: no-proof branch shows the unbroken-seal variant + press_bluff and never "his own name"; with-proof branch shows the reveal + reveal_evidence; both routes' endings unchanged — captured / truth).
- Next suggested focus (UNCHANGED, still highest-value): the SHARED climactic-agency ENGINE gap flagged by bug_0054 — alchemists' spire "must be administered" and sunken_barrow's auto-taken circlet (bug_0047) both win on bare room entry because the parser runner evaluates win_conditions only in onEnter (src/parser/runner.ts: post-action wins "out of scope for v1"). A focused, backward-compatible engine cycle adding a post-action win hook (or on_take/CLAIM verb) would pay off two packs at once. Then keep rotating blind playtests (sunken_barrow next; sealed_crypt is fresh). Clockwork verified-clean across 9 passes, cold_forge + watchtower fresh-passed this cycle — deprioritize re-playtesting those three near-term.

### Cycle result — climactic-agency engine hook + sunken_barrow claims-the-relic (bug_0056)
- Mandated blind LLM playtest (clockwork_heist, assessor rank-1, seed 71): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T15-47-51-594Z/playtest.md (§A). Clockwork's ~12th independent blind pass — reached ALL FOUR endings (Rich, Caught, Truth ×2 reactive epilogues), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks). Its only notes (ticking clock = atmosphere; ledger = safety-net not hard gate) are long-adjudicated-DELIBERATE (bug_0046/0053/0055). Verified-clean; no actionable clockwork defect.
- Rotated playtest (protocol "rotate the seed and rotate the pack" + "you MAY pick a higher-value candidate"): fresh parallel blind pass on The Sunken Barrow (rpg, seed 71) — chosen because it directly probes the climactic-agency ENGINE gap flagged as the highest-value next move across bug_0047/0052/0054. Report §B. Barrow WON 50/50, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5, mechanically sound — and independently surfaced the gap as its #1 finding (report §5).
- Finding fixed (sunken_barrow report §5, climactic moment): the win fired on a MOVE with NO deliberate take. Selecting `go_down` from Slab Passage into relic_chamber emitted the move, the journal line, +score AND `ending_victory` in the same frame; the circlet sat in visible_objects with available_actions:[] and ended=true. The player NEVER chose "take circlet," yet the epilogue narrated "You lift the circlet from its plinth" — an act they never performed. The climax was narrated AT the player instead of earned. Root cause: the parser/RPG runners evaluated win_conditions ONLY in onEnter (a room transition), so a win that should turn on a deliberate non-move action could only fire on bare entry (src/parser/runner.ts header: "out of scope for v1").
- Improvement (ENGINE + content, backward-compatible): (1) ENGINE — added an optional post-action `checkWin(state)` hook to the Rules contract (src/core/engine.ts §8.4.5): after an action's effects and any onEnter, if the game has NOT ended, the runner may append an end_game — so a win fires on a TAKE/USE with no move. Skipped once ended, so an onEnter/effect-level win never double-fires; CYOA omits checkWin and is byte-identical. Implemented in src/parser/runner.ts + src/rpg/runner.ts via the existing winningEnding predicate. (2) CONTENT — sunken_barrow.yaml win now requires BOTH `{ visited: relic_chamber }` AND `{ has_item: circlet }`: entering the chamber no longer wins (it shows the circlet on its plinth + offers "take circlet"); the win fires on that TAKE. `visited: relic_chamber` KEPT deliberately — the parser validator derives its SOFTLOCK guard from `visited` win-rooms, so dropping it would silently disable that check. ending_victory epilogue reworded to follow the TAKE ("The cold crown is yours…"). relic_chamber on_enter +25 milestone unchanged; max score in hand as the player makes the final grab.
- VERIFIED: validate_pack sunken_barrow ok 0/0 (win still references a reachable room + obtainable item; soft-lock guard intact). Engine behavior proven by tests against the fresh build (the running MCP server is a stale long-lived tsx process; its live replay reflected the OLD engine — noted, not relied upon). `npm run health` **exit 0; 445 tests pass (+7 new).** verify-integrity --against HEAD: **0 errors, 2 warnings** — VERIFIER_TOUCHED (src/core/engine.ts, surfaced; full authority under trust-but-verify, test count rose, nothing weakened) and HASH_PIN_REPINNED (traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json re-pinned alongside the intentional content change — the legitimate snapshot workflow). NO override used; NO test disabled/deleted.
- HASH RE-PIN (deliberate, surfaced): traces/rpg/barrow_victory.json (a HASH_PIN_FILE) regenerated via recordTrace — content_hash cb2e487c… → 6e0856e7…, expected_final_hash → e6de3a9c…, and the canonical route now appends the claim TAKE. sunken_barrow is NOT hash-pinned elsewhere (only watchtower_road is, in tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts — untouched).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0056_barrow_claim_relic_agency.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_barrow_claim_relic.test.ts (engine contract on synthetic Rules: checkWin fires a win on a non-move action, is skipped once ended [no double-fire], is optional; sunken_barrow: entry does NOT end + offers take circlet at full score, the claim ends ending_victory at max_score, win keeps both terms, validates green). Eight existing win-on-entry assertions corrected deliberately (stage4_barrow, rpg_scoring ×2, rpg_barrow_opening_objective, rpg_barrow_lever_legibility, rpg_barrow_slab_examine_stale, rpg_skillcheck_retires, ui_engine).
- Next suggested focus: the engine hook now UNBLOCKS alchemists_tower's analogous gap (win `{ visited: spire }, { has_item: antidote }` fires on entering the spire holding the antidote, narrating "you administer the antidote" — an act never performed). A focused content cycle: add a USE-antidote interaction that sets a flag (e.g. cure_administered) and gate the win on that flag — no engine change needed now. Then keep rotating blind playtests (cold_forge, sealed_crypt are freshest; clockwork verified-clean ~12 passes — deprioritize).

### Cycle result — alchemists_tower climactic-agency: the cure is administered, not auto-won (bug_0057)
- Mandated blind LLM playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 83): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T16-07-11-442Z/playtest.md. Clockwork's ~13th independent blind pass — reached FIVE distinct endings (Rich, Truth ×2 reactive epilogues, Caught, Caught-on-the-Hour), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks). Its §4 notes (invisible tick budget; the crawlspace short-route to Truth) are long-adjudicated-DELIBERATE escapable-deadline / multi-route design (bug_0019/0040/0046). No actionable clockwork defect; verified-clean.
- ROTATED the improvement to The Alchemist's Tower (justified by the protocol's "rotate the pack" + "you MAY pick a higher-value candidate" with the mandated target verified clean) — the pack the PREVIOUS cycle (bug_0056) explicitly flagged as the next-best move for the same climactic-agency family. Fresh parallel blind pass (parser, seed 67); report ai-runs/2026-06-01T16-07-11-442Z/playtest_alchemists_tower.md. WON ending_cured 35/35, clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5 — and independently surfaced the gap as its **#1 finding** (report §5).
- Finding fixed (rotated report §5, THE CLIMAX): the winning ending narrated an act the player never performed and fired on a plain MOVE. The single decisive command was `go up` (laboratory → spire); that one move emitted both the `move` event and `ending: ending_cured`, with the ending reading "At the spire's height you administer the antidote…" — yet there was NO patient anywhere (every room npcs_present:[]) and no administer/give/use-antidote command was ever offered. Walking upstairs with the antidote in hand auto-narrated the whole climax. Root cause: the win `{ visited: spire }, { has_item: antidote }` was evaluated in onEnter, so entering the spire holding the vial WAS the win. This is the parser-pack twin of sunken_barrow's bug_0056; bug_0056's post-action checkWin hook (§8.4.5) was built precisely to unblock it, so this is the **content-only** payoff — NO engine change.
- Improvement (content only): (1) NEW object `master` — the fevered patient — placed in the spire (not takeable); its sole role is to be the deliberate TARGET of the cure. (2) The master carries a USE interaction (item antidote, target master, gated not_flag cure_administered) whose effects `set_flag: cure_administered`, `remove_item: antidote` (poured into her), and narrate the administering beat. (3) win_conditions.administer_cure now reads `{ visited: spire }, { has_flag: cure_administered }` — `visited: spire` KEPT (the parser validator derives its SOFTLOCK guard from `visited` win-rooms; dropping it would silently disable that check — same reasoning as bug_0056), the `has_item: antidote` term replaced by the flag so the win fires on the deliberate USE via checkWin, never on bare entry. (4) The spire description now introduces the master and says the antidote "is no use until you give it to her." (5) ending_cured reworded to carry the AFTERMATH ("colour creeps back into the master's face… the long sickness breaks") instead of narrating an act the player no longer has done for them. No score/max_score change (still 35; max in hand as the cure is made), no death-ending change, no other route/gating change.
- VERIFIED: validate_pack alchemists_tower ok 0/0 (win references a reachable room + a settable flag; the administer interaction's item antidote is obtainable; soft-lock guard intact). Live engine replay (seed 1, fresh build): brew → `go_up` to spire ⇒ NOT ended, score 35 in hand, spire offers use_antidote_on_master + examine_master; `use antidote on master` ⇒ ending_cured at 35/35, death=false. `npm run health` **exit 0; 451 tests pass (+6 new).** verify-integrity --against HEAD: **0 errors, 0 warnings**. alchemists_tower is NOT hash-pinned and has no committed trace, so the content-hash move has nothing to re-pin (only watchtower_road is hash-pinned, untouched); NO protected/HASH_PIN file touched; NO re-pin; NO override used. Caught + fixed mid-cycle: Prettier flagged the two edited test files' formatting — ran `prettier --write` (the gate working).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0057_alchemists_administer_cure_agency.yaml + tests/regression/alchemists_administer_cure_agency.test.ts (6 cases: entering the spire with the antidote does NOT end + offers use_antidote_on_master; administering wins ending_cured at max_score and consumes the vial; you cannot win by reaching the spire without the antidote; the win keeps the visited term + requires cure_administered not has_item antidote; the ending carries the aftermath while the interaction narrates the act; validates green). Five existing routes that won on bare spire entry corrected deliberately (stage3_alchemist ×2, alchemists_cauldron_reactive_brew, alchemists_strongbox_reactive_examine, alchemists_strongbox_unlock_state, alchemists_phial_danger_cue — each appends the deliberate cure after the final go_up).
- Next suggested focus: the climactic-agency family (bug_0047/0052/0054/0056/0057) is now resolved across the puzzle packs that exhibited it. (a) Keep rotating blind playtests across the remaining packs (cold_forge, sealed_crypt are freshest; clockwork verified-clean ~13 passes — deprioritize). (b) Standing cross-category lever: the semantic-weakening blind spot in verify-integrity (catches mechanical tampering, not a meaning-preserving check weakening — needs an LLM-judge, noted since cycle 2026-06-01c). (c) Minor deferred (subjective balance, NOT this agency fix): alchemists' max score 35/35 is reached at brewing, so the ending grants no points — a future cycle could shift a small award onto the administer act (touches max_score budget + scoring tests).

### Cycle result — clockwork vault door no longer echoes a brass plate you never read (bug_0058)
- Mandated blind LLM playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 53): fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access; report ai-runs/2026-06-01T16-23-20-676Z/playtest.md. Clockwork's ~14th independent blind pass — reached all four endings across four runs (Truth ×2 reactive epilogues, Rich, Caught), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks). UNLIKE the last several passes it surfaced a NEW, concrete, not-yet-closed continuity finding (report §5.1), so no rotation was needed this cycle — the mandated target itself yielded the fix.
- Finding fixed (report §5.1, a narrative leak): the `vault_door` scene text "echoed" the steward's brass plate — "The brass plate's warning echoes: force will not open this" — even on the route up the grand stair straight to the vault (climb_stairs → approach_vault) that never enters the study, where the plate physically hangs. So the prose attributed to the player a warning they had never read. The same leak lived in the lockpick-less `study_lock` nudge narration ("the brass plate's warning rings true"), which also fires on no-study routes. Out of character for a pack whose every other reactive line is gated on what the player actually knows (read_ledger, letter_displayed/hidden).
- Improvement (content only, route-aware — mirrors the pack's variant philosophy): (1) `study.on_enter` now sets a new additive flag `saw_plate` (entering the study = reading the plate). (2) `vault_door` base text rewritten PLATE-NAIVE — telegraphs pick-don't-force from the lock's own "patient, fastidious" feel, quoting no plate. A new variant gated `has_flag: saw_plate` restores the brass-plate echo, ordered BELOW the existing ticks>=4+read_ledger patrol variant and ABOVE the base (first-match-wins): study-visitor-before-the-hour gets the plate line, the careful ledger-at-the-hour patrol case is unchanged (read_ledger implies a study visit), only the no-study route gets the naive text. (3) `study_lock` narration drops the plate reference for "you can feel it in the mechanism" — learns force-won't-work from the lock itself; the tool/instrument/steady-hand wording bug_0008's test asserts is preserved. NO tick/route/gating/reachable-ending change beyond the additive flag; all four endings still fire.
- VERIFIED: live engine replay (seed 53, fresh build) — no-study route is plate-naive (matches neither /brass plate/ nor the patrol prose); study-before-hour fires the saw_plate variant (plate, not patrol prose); ledger-at-the-hour still fires the patrol crossing variant (crossing + plate); study_lock narration no longer cites the plate and still routes to the gallery. `npm run health` **exit 0; 457 tests pass (+5 new clockwork cases, 0 regressions in the other 6 clockwork suites)**. verify-integrity static: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

NO protected/HASH_PIN file touched; NO unit-test re-pin; NO override used. Caught + fixed mid-cycle: Prettier flagged the new test file's formatting — ran `prettier --write` (the gate working).
- Locked by traces/bugs/bug_0058_clockwork_vault_door_plate_leak.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_vault_door_plate_leak.test.ts (5 cases: plate-naive base on the no-study route; plate echo restored after a study visit; the careful ledger-at-the-hour patrol variant unchanged; study_lock no longer cites the plate yet keeps the find-a-tool nudge + gallery exit; all four endings still reachable).
- Next suggested focus: clockwork has now had ~14 clean blind passes and its last concrete defect is closed — fully deprioritize it. ### Result — bug_0060 (engine): inc_var/dec_var events now carry a signed `delta`
- **Mandated playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 23): VERIFIED CLEAN.** Five endings reached (Rich, Caught, Caught-on-the-Hour, Truth ×2 reactive epilogues), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, zero rejected actions/loops/soft-locks — another independent clean pass (~13th). Its only note (the dumbwaiter has no mechanical edge over the stair) is the long-adjudicated-DELIBERATE bug_0046 reframing, not a defect. Recent fixes (bug_0058 plate-leak, bug_0051 reactive letter) independently confirmed holding. No actionable clockwork finding — forcing a change to a polished pack would risk regressing prior deliberate work.
- **Rotated playtest (sealed_crypt parser, seed 13) per the previous cycle's exact recommendation** (freshest pack, last touched bug_0054). ending_victory reached twice (35/35 thorough; 30/35 skipping every clue — signposting proven self-sufficient). Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5 (tight but short/linear). It surfaced the cycle's one concrete, cross-cutting **engine** finding.
- **Finding → fix:** the score `state_change` events were ambiguous — the IDENTICAL rope-use milestone (+10) reported `value:15` in the headstone-first run and `value:10` in the headstone-skipped run, because `value` is the running total with no field for the increment. A consumer (UI "+10!", transcript narrator, AI experience log) couldn't recover "points just earned" from the event. Fix (src/core/effects.ts): inc_var emits `delta:+by`, dec_var emits `delta:-by`, beside the UNCHANGED `value`. Additive + backward-compatible (GameEvent.state_change already allows extra keys; observation `score` reads state.vars, not events). Benefits every scored pack.
- **Evidence:** mandated report ai-runs/2026-06-01T16-49-53-395Z/playtest.md (both passes). `npm run health` exit 0 (verify:integrity + typecheck + lint + format + tests + all-pack validate + watchtower coverage + mandated playtest record). New tests 21-pass alongside effects/scoring suites. No content/flag/score/hash change — sealed_crypt is the witness, not the patient.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0060_inc_var_event_delta.yaml + tests/regression/score_event_delta.test.ts (inc/dec delta sign & value-total; the playtester's same-award-different-total scenario; set_var left scoped; end-to-end Sealed Crypt rope-use carries delta:10 with or without the bonus).
- **Next suggested focus:** (a) Continue the blind-playtest rotation — sealed_crypt is now verified-clean too; the remaining never-deeply-exercised endings are watchtower's `ending_truth` + its 4 hermit scenes (coverage bot reaches 0/15), a discoverability question a fresh blind pass could probe. (b) Consider surfacing the new `delta` in a UI/transcript narrator ("+10 points") now that the data exists — small content/UI follow-on. (c) Standing levers unchanged: cold_forge brevity (needs a dedicated content_new cycle); the semantic-weakening blind spot in verify-integrity (needs an LLM-judge, noted since 2026-06-01c).

### Result — bug_0061 (content): clockwork deadline text names the real consequence, not a phantom timeout
- **Mandated playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 17): all FIVE endings reached** (Rich, Caught, Caught-on-the-Hour, Truth ×2 reactive epilogues) across 5 runs; clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5; zero rejected actions/loops/soft-locks/functional bugs (verdict "a real player finishes satisfied"). Report: ai-runs/2026-06-01T17-02-59-454Z/playtest.md.
- **Finding → fix:** the playtester's one actionable note (§4/§5 #2) — the deadline language oversells. The foyer & gallery `ticks>=2` variants said "your time in the manor is running short", reading as a GLOBAL run-out-of-time loss the engine never enforces (no tick-limit ending; the foyer is deliberately safe ground that doesn't even advance ticks). The clock's only real bite is the watchman it wakes onto the gallery at the hour (ticks>=4). Fix repoints both lines at that real consequence: foyer -> "a sleeping house keeps its peace only until the chime. Best be done upstairs before the hour stirs the manor awake"; gallery -> "this long gallery will be no place to be caught standing when it chimes." Kept atmospheric -- no unseen clue (ledger/plate) quoted at ticks>=2, per the bug_0058 plate-leak lesson; the ticks>=4 variants already name the watchman, so this is consistent escalation. **No flag/tick/route/gating/ending change** -- the watchman tuning (bug_0019/0040/0042/0043) and all five endings untouched.
- **Why this and not "leave the polished pack alone":** this is the repo's already-blessed fix class -- "atmosphere implies a system that isn't there" (closed on cold_forge via bug_0029 warm-up clock + bug_0059 relight). The pack's own design (no timeout ending, safe-ground foyer) proves the implied global deadline was a text/mechanic mismatch, not an authorial choice; aligning the prose to the real, deliberate mechanic regresses none of bug_0043's reactive-deadline work. The "add a real timeout" alternative was rejected: it would fight the safe-ground design and the heavily-tuned tick-4 watchman, risking unfair losses given the free-retreat asymmetry -- a bigger, riskier change against the small-verified-change bar.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 468 tests pass** (90 files; +3 new clockwork cases, 0 regressions). verify:integrity static 0/0. validate clockwork OK 0 err/0 warn. Live engine replay (buildObservation, seed 17): foyer@ticks2 & gallery@ticks2 no longer match /time in the manor is running short/ and now name the chime/house-waking; both still fire at tick 2 and stay distinct from the ticks>=4 patrol prose; all five endings still fire.
- **Surfaced content-hash move (NOT laundered):** clockwork is NOT hash-pinned in unit tests (only watchtower_road is) -- content_hash 82491f3f1938e64761329803ea701ba3eb7a9c4b5da3b408431399929f425296 -> 0b576c29d9780ab33747c8d531f90608786c015e97aa7985351538d88bffe24f, recorded in bug_0061. No protected/HASH_PIN file touched; no unit-test re-pin; no override used.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0061_clockwork_deadline_honest_text.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_deadline_honest_text.test.ts (3 cases: foyer ticks>=2 drops "running short" & names the house-waking; gallery ticks>=2 drops it & names the at-the-hour gallery danger; all five endings reachable).
- **Next suggested focus:** (a) The "atmosphere implies a system that isn't there" family is now closed on cold_forge (bug_0029/0059) AND clockwork (bug_0061) -- clockwork is now ~14 clean passes deep with no remaining actionable content finding; deprioritize it and rotate the next blind playtest onto watchtower's never-deeply-exercised `ending_truth` + the 4 hermit scenes (coverage bot reaches 0/15), a discoverability question only a fresh blind pass can probe. (b) Surface the new bug_0060 `delta` in a UI/transcript narrator ("+10 points") now that the data exists -- small content/UI follow-on. (c) cold_forge brevity/linearity (enjoyment 3/5) still needs a dedicated content_new cycle. (d) Standing lever: the semantic-weakening blind spot in verify-integrity (needs an LLM-judge, noted since 2026-06-01c).

### Outcome — bug_0062 (watchtower confront_smuggler retreat choice)
- **Mandated playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 41): VERIFIED CLEAN — ~16th independent blind pass.** Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access. Reached all four/five endings across 5 runs (Truth ×2 differentiated routes, Rich, Caught, Caught-on-the-Hour), clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no broken state, no soft-locks). Confirmed bug_0061 deadline prose + bug_0058 plate gating hold. Its two §-notes (two-letter ambiguity resolving in ending prose; unstated `ticks` threshold) were both flagged "not a defect, just a ceiling on depth." No actionable clockwork finding — deprioritize (consistent with the last ~15 passes).
- **Rotated playtest (watchtower_road, seed 7) per the standing recommendation** (the last several cycles' next-focus: probe the never-deeply-exercised `ending_truth` + hermit scenes the coverage bot reaches 0/15). Reached all THREE endings (truth via letter+hermit; escape via cellar+ledger; captured via force/bluff). Clarity 4/5, enjoyment 4/5. It surfaced the cycle's one concrete, player-facing finding as its #1 note.
- **Finding → fix:** at `confront_smuggler`, the third (retreat) option was unconditional and read "Pocket the letter and slip away to find real proof first." A player who already corroborated the truth (`learned_truth` set) sees the sergeant read his own name — yet is told to go find proof they already hold. This is the residue of bug_0055, which made the scene *text* reactive and gated the two forward choices but left the *retreat* choice text untouched. Fix gates that line to the no-proof bluffer (`back_off`, `not_flag learned_truth`) and adds a coherent sibling for the proof-bearer (`hold_off`, `has_flag learned_truth`): "Hold your tongue for now — pocket the letter and back away from the gate." Both route to `road_north` exactly as before. Mirrors the scene's existing `reveal_evidence`/`press_bluff` gated pair. **No flag/route/ending change** (truth/escape/captured all intact; only new id is `hold_off`).
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 472 tests pass** (91 files; +4 new cases, 0 regressions). verify:integrity drift vs HEAD: **0 errors, 2 warnings** (both `HASH_PIN_REPINNED` — the sanctioned content+pin workflow). validate watchtower OK 0/0. Live engine replay (buildObservation, seed 1): with-proof route → `confront_smuggler` shows [reveal_evidence, hold_off] (no `back_off`), `hold_off` omits "find real proof first" and routes to road_north→…→`ending_truth`; no-proof route → [press_bluff, back_off], `back_off` still reads "find real proof first" and `press_bluff` still ends `ending_captured`.
- **Content-hash move (deliberate, NOT laundered):** watchtower_road edited; its canonical pin lives in `traces/bugs/bug_0002_watchtower_blind_polish.yaml` (a HASH_PIN_FILE) + the unit pin `tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts`, both re-pinned alongside this content change: `3947f07b…` → `4c971d435f3271731bea8db64c2ac1dfa338f53c010de55d9a3ebd56cde96d31`. No protected file touched; no override used; the re-pins are surfaced as warnings, not weakening.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0062_watchtower_confront_retreat_choice.yaml + tests/regression/watchtower_confront_retreat_choice.test.ts (4 cases: no-proof keeps `back_off`/"find real proof first"; with-proof gets `hold_off`/"hold your tongue", no `back_off`; both retreats → road_north with every ending still reachable; the climactic routes still expose/capture).
- **Next suggested focus:** (a) watchtower still has two softer cosmetic findings from this same blind pass, each a clean future polish cycle: the inventory item stays named "sealed_letter" / "Present the **sealed** letter" after `seal_broken` (finding #2), and the `hermit_talk` hub replays its opening line after topics are exhausted (finding #3). (b) Rotate the next mandated-target blind pass onto the freshest unverified packs — sealed_crypt / sunken_barrow (cold_forge, alchemists_tower, clockwork all recently verified). (c) cold_forge brevity/linearity (enjoyment 3/5) still needs a dedicated content_new cycle. (d) Standing lever: the semantic-weakening blind spot in verify-integrity (needs an LLM-judge, noted since 2026-06-01c).

- **Mandated playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 29): report ai-runs/2026-06-01T17-25-58-433Z/playtest.md.** Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access. Reached all three distinct endings across four runs (Truth ×2 differentiated epilogues, Rich, Caught), clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no broken state, no soft-locks). ~17th independent clean blind pass; confirmed bug_0061 deadline prose + bug_0058 plate gating + bug_0051 two-letter epilogues all hold.
- **Finding → fix:** the playtest's one concrete content finding (§4 + §5): after `inspect_clock` → `pry_panel`, the foyer "scene text never changes — it still describes the clock vast as a coffin with no mention of the now-open panel behind it." Stale room text. It is also a legibility gap, not only cosmetic: once `found_passage` is set but the player holds no lockpick, the foyer's `enter_panel` re-entry choice is HIDDEN (it gates on the pick), so the crawlspace's only on-screen trace was a journal line even though the crawlspace nudge sends the player back to the foyer to "find the tool." Fix adds a `found_passage`-paired reactive `variants` entry at each tick tier (>=4, >=2, base), each above its non-panel sibling so first-match-wins picks the panel-aware text; each weaves "the panel you pried loose still [stands open / is open behind its pendulum / gapes] on the dark crawlspace" into the existing tier prose. **Content-only**; non-panel tiers untouched; foyer still never advances ticks; no flag/tick/route/gating/ending change (new variants only, no new choice id).
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 477 tests pass** (92 files; +5 new clockwork cases, 0 regressions). verify:integrity static 0/0. validate clockwork OK 0 err/0 warn. Live MCP/engine replay (buildObservation, seed 29): inspect_clock→pry_panel→back_crawl returns to foyer@ticks0 reading "...vast as a coffin — and behind its pendulum the panel you pried loose still stands open on the dark crawlspace," with `enter_panel` still correctly hidden (no pick); ticks 3 retreat shows the panel line + "grinds toward the hour" tension; ticks 5 retreat shows it + the on-the-hour watchman cue; a no-pry retreat to ticks 5 reads the bug_0061 text with NO crawlspace mention (no leak). All four endings still fire.
- **Surfaced content-hash move (NOT laundered):** clockwork is NOT hash-pinned in unit tests (only watchtower_road is) — content_hash 0b576c29d9780ab33747c8d531f90608786c015e97aa7985351538d88bffe24f → cf7036b384e97505ee786fbed5ea582097ae085d5f305eaac5ac07c8e2f7775a, recorded in bug_0063. No protected/HASH_PIN file touched; no unit-test re-pin; no override used.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0063_clockwork_foyer_open_panel_text.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_foyer_open_panel_text.test.ts (5 cases: low-tick panel acknowledgment + enter_panel hidden; ticks 2-3 panel + tension not hour; ticks>=4 panel + hour; no-found_passage never mentions the crawlspace at low OR high ticks; all four endings reachable).
- **Next suggested focus:** (a) Clockwork is now ~17 clean blind passes deep with this cycle's one finding closed and no remaining actionable content finding — keep it deprioritized. Rotate the next mandated-target blind pass onto the freshest never-deeply-exercised packs: **sealed_crypt (parser) and sunken_barrow (rpg)** (clockwork, cold_forge, alchemists_tower, watchtower all recently verified). (b) watchtower's two softer cosmetic findings remain clean future polish cycles: the inventory item stays "sealed_letter" / "Present the **sealed** letter" after `seal_broken` (bug_0062 finding #2), and the `hermit_talk` hub replays its opening line after topics are exhausted (#3). ### Outcome — bug_0064 (clockwork crawlspace truth route now feels the clock)
- **Mandated playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 47): report ai-runs/2026-06-01T17-35-11-427Z/playtest.md.** Fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access. Reached all four reachable endings across four runs (Truth/hidden, Rich, Caught, Caught-on-the-Hour), clarity **5/5**, enjoyment **4/5**, mechanically flawless (0 rejected actions, no loops, no broken state, no soft-locks; verdict "a real player would finish satisfied"). ~18th independent clean blind pass; confirmed bug_0061 deadline prose, bug_0063 foyer panel acknowledgment, bug_0051 two-letter epilogues, and the bug_0040 ledger-gated patrol all hold.
- **Finding → fix:** the playtest's one actionable opportunity (§4 + §6): "the ticking clock and hourly watch — the game's best system — is completely bypassable via the strongbox route, so a player who beelines the hidden letter may never discover it… the two halves feel tonally disconnected." The clockwork-deadline arc (bug_0019/0040/0042/0043) had made the gallery, the vault door, and the loot room all reactively feel the hour, but the `crawlspace` — the hidden truth route — was the one beat that stayed clock-silent at every tick. Fix mirrors bug_0043's loot-room treatment exactly: content-only reactive `variants` on `crawlspace` (first-match-wins, higher threshold first) — ticks>=4 "the hour has come round… a slow tread answers it [along the vault's far side]," ticks>=2 "the great clock grinds toward the hour… before the chime wakes the sleeping house." Clock named only by the manor-wide motif (gears, chime), never by a watchman/plate the crawler may not have met (bug_0058 leak lesson). Base `text` byte-identical (keeps bug_0017 framing); the pure-foyer beeliner (ticks<2, never stirs the gallery) still reads the still base text — quiet earned by slipping past the gallery. **No choice/effect/flag/tick/item/gating/ending change** (new variants only).
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 482 tests pass** (93 files; +5 new clockwork cases, 0 regressions). verify:integrity static 0/0. validate clockwork OK 0 err/0 warn. Live MCP replay (seed 47): open panel → fetch pick → climb to gallery+study to ticks 4 → back to foyer → enter_panel renders "The hour has come round at last: the great clock's chime rolls down through the manor's thousand gears… a slow tread answers it, passing along the vault's far side and away. Crack the box and crawl clear," with open_strongbox still offered; first crawlspace visit at ticks 0 read the unchanged base text.
- **Surfaced content-hash move (NOT laundered):** clockwork is NOT hash-pinned in unit tests (only watchtower_road is) — content_hash cf7036b384e97505ee786fbed5ea582097ae085d5f305eaac5ac07c8e2f7775a → ff0ada19cce4224bf8065e7bc5d811065a9a6a11689e2b2dba1681df077c2a25, recorded in bug_0064. No protected/HASH_PIN file touched; no unit-test re-pin; no override used. One pre-existing test (cyoa_reactive_scene_text.test.ts, bug_0018) used `crawlspace` as its variant-less control — repointed to `study` (still variant-free), the only consequential edit beyond the new files.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0064_clockwork_crawlspace_deadline_payoff.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_crawlspace_deadline_payoff.test.ts (5 cases: ticks 2-3 → running prose not hour; ticks>=4 → hour prose not running; base text unchanged / bug_0017 framing intact at ticks 0; pure-foyer beeliner stays <2 ticks, reads base text, still reaches ending_truth; all four endings still fire).
- **Next suggested focus:** clockwork is now ~18 clean blind passes deep with the entire deadline arc complete (gallery → vault door → loot room → crawlspace) and no remaining actionable content finding — **deprioritize it**. Rotate the next mandated-target blind pass onto the freshest never-deeply-exercised packs: **sealed_crypt (parser) and sunken_barrow (rpg)**. ### Cycle result — the gallery stops naming the ledger to a player who never read it (bug_0066)
- **Mandatory playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 23):** fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access. Report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T18-05-10-202Z/playtest.md. Reached all four endings (Truth ×2 prose variants, Rich, Caught, Caught-on-the-Hour) across five runs; **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5**, zero functional bugs, no rejected actions/loops/soft-locks. Unlike the last two passes (whose strongest findings were already-closed by bug_0064), seed 23 surfaced a **new, not-yet-closed** continuity leak (report §5), so no pivot was needed — the mandated target itself yielded the fix.
- **Finding → fix (content layer):** the `landing` (gallery) scene's at-the-hour variant (`ticks>=4`) said "the hourly patrol **the steward's ledger warned of**" *unconditionally* — but the gallery is reachable at the hour with `read_ledger` unset (pace up the grand stair and back; the study is never required), and `cross_to_vault_blind` exists precisely for that no-ledger player. So the line named the ledger to a player who never read (or even saw) it — the same class of leak as the bug_0058 brass-plate echo. Fix splits the `ticks>=4` variant on `read_ledger` (first-match-wins): the **reader** keeps the ledger callback (matched by the safe `approach_vault` crossing); the **non-reader** gets a ledger-naive line — the watchman's lantern sweeps the gallery at the chime, no claim of prior warning (matched by the `cross_to_vault_blind` gamble). The `ending_patrol` death-screen "if only you had stopped to read it" is left as-is: only reachable via not_flag read_ledger, it reads correctly as a post-hoc lesson (the playtester agreed). **No flag/tick/route/gating/reachable-ending change** — the choice gates already mirror the prose split.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 489 tests pass (95 files; +3 new clockwork cases, 0 regressions)**. verify:integrity static 0/0; lint/format/typecheck clean; validate clockwork OK 0 err/0 warn. Live engine check via buildObservation (seed 23): no-ledger route paced to ticks 4 → gallery text matches /watchman/ but NOT /ledger/, and only `cross_to_vault_blind` is offered; ledger route at ticks 4 → text still matches "the steward's ledger warned of" and only `approach_vault` is offered.
- **Surfaced content-hash move (NOT laundered):** clockwork is NOT hash-pinned in unit tests (only watchtower_road is) — content_hash ff0ada19cce4224bf8065e7bc5d811065a9a6a11689e2b2dba1681df077c2a25 → f8f2bbdd38374d042a46d05a2dc8165392ea9348b7899e21df8a798a6100d499, recorded in bug_0066. No protected/HASH_PIN file touched; no unit-test re-pin; no override used.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0066_clockwork_gallery_ledger_leak.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_gallery_ledger_leak.test.ts (3 cases: at-the-hour-without-ledger names no ledger + offers blind crossing; at-the-hour-with-ledger keeps the callback + offers the safe crossing; all four endings still fire).
- **Next suggested focus:** clockwork_heist is now ~20 clean blind passes deep with the entire deadline arc complete and every reactive line gated on player knowledge — **deprioritize it**; rotate the next mandated-target blind pass onto the RPG packs **sunken_barrow** and **cold_forge**, which have had no blind pass this round (cold_forge's enjoyment 3/5 brevity/linearity still wants a dedicated content_new cycle). Softer standing items carry over: watchtower's two cosmetic findings (sealed_letter label after seal_broken; hermit_talk re-greet), and the semantic-weakening blind spot in verify-integrity (wants an LLM-judge).

### Cycle result — the reader's gallery crossing reads as a solved, timed beat (bug_0067)
- **Mandatory playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 73):** fresh general-purpose subagent, MCP-only, no source access. Report at ai-runs/2026-06-01T18-13-44-027Z/playtest.md. Reached all three terminal families (Truth, Rich, Caught) plus the graceful pick-less recovery across four runs; **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5**, mechanically flawless — zero rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks. A new seed surfaced a new, not-yet-closed finding, so again no pivot was needed.
- **Finding → fix (content layer):** the `landing` (gallery) `ticks>=4` + `read_ledger` variant (added last cycle by bug_0066) still framed the crossing as "the long crossing to the vault **runs right under his beam**" — identical danger tone to the non-reader's variant. But for the reader that crossing is the **safe** `approach_vault` payoff (he knows the rounds), the one the vault-door beat rewards with "cross in its wake... just as the ledger swore you could." So the prose promised live danger while the mechanics delivered a solved, timed crossing; the careful playtester read it as a live timing puzzle and **over-dodged a threat the ledger had already solved** (report §4/§5). Fix reframes the reader's line from danger to informed confidence: it keeps the watchman + "the hourly patrol the steward's ledger warned of" callback, but replaces "right under his beam" with the reader knowing his rounds by heart, reading the lantern's swing, and crossing in his wake "just as the ledger swore you could." The **non-reader's** `ticks>=4` variant is untouched and keeps "right under his beam" — for that player the offered crossing is `cross_to_vault_blind` → ending_patrol, a real gamble the framing honestly promises. **No flag/tick/route/gating/reachable-ending change.**
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 492 tests pass (96 files; +3 new clockwork cases, 0 regressions)**. verify:integrity static OK; lint/format/typecheck clean; validate clockwork OK 0 err/0 warn. Live engine check via buildObservation (seed 73): ledger route at ticks 4 → gallery text matches /watchman/ + "the steward's ledger warned of", does NOT match /right under his beam/, DOES match the timed-crossing confidence, and only `approach_vault` is offered; no-ledger route paced to ticks 4 → text still matches /watchman/ + /right under his beam/, never names the ledger, offers only `cross_to_vault_blind`. All four endings still fire.
- **Surfaced content-hash move (NOT laundered):** clockwork is NOT hash-pinned in unit tests (only watchtower_road is) — content_hash f8f2bbdd38374d042a46d05a2dc8165392ea9348b7899e21df8a798a6100d499 → fa5cd967481d48f07f1b91b22ce36a9fbdca8994bd3171591cdd3852bdda6c6f, recorded in bug_0067. No protected/HASH_PIN file touched; no unit-test re-pin; no override used.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0067_clockwork_gallery_crossing_legibility.yaml + tests/regression/clockwork_gallery_crossing_legibility.test.ts (3 cases: reader-at-the-hour drops the danger framing + keeps the ledger callback + signals a timed crossing + offers the safe crossing; non-reader-at-the-hour keeps the danger framing + blind crossing + names no ledger; all four endings still fire).
- **Next suggested focus:** clockwork_heist's deadline/legibility arc is now fully consistent — every reactive line is gated on player knowledge AND matches the action it offers. **Deprioritize it** (the assessor still mandated it this cycle only because the coverage bot can't plan its puzzles, not because quality is lacking). Rotate the next mandated blind pass onto the RPG packs **sunken_barrow** and **cold_forge**, which have had no blind pass this round (cold_forge's enjoyment 3/5 brevity/linearity still wants a dedicated content_new cycle). The residual clockwork note — the tick clock-pressure prose still implies a countdown with no enforced fail state for careful play — is a deliberate design tension already softened by bug_0061; only revisit if a future playtester finds it actively misleading. Softer standing items carry over: watchtower's two cosmetic findings (sealed_letter label after seal_broken; hermit_talk re-greet), and the semantic-weakening blind spot in verify-integrity (wants an LLM-judge).
