### Cycle result — the Cold Forge's stale pry-bar prose goes reactive (bug_0094, content_fix)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the over-fitted clockwork target** (its ~25th straight nomination; content-complete per bug_0079→0085, ~24 clean passes) **onto cold_forge** — the least-attended rpg pack AND the one carrying the prior cycle's (bug_0088) open #1 deferred content finding (stale pry-bar prose), so the pass both gives current quality feedback and independently re-tests that finding. Fresh MCP-only, source-blind general-purpose subagent, **seed 53** (a NEW seed — prior cold_forge passes were 23/37 — to avoid re-fitting). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T00-23-22-281Z/playtest.md`.
  - Reached `ending_victory` 50/50 on BOTH a full prepared route and an under-armed rush; **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5**; mechanically flawless (no rejected actions, no loops, no soft-locks). It **independently re-surfaced the stale pry-bar line as the pack's LONE concrete defect (§5)** — "the one inconsistent room" — confirming the bug_0088 seed-37 deferral on a second blind agent / different seed.
- **The fix (content_fix, cold_forge):** the Outer Forge room description statically read "By a cold trough lies a stout iron pry-bar"; once the bar is taken it is correctly dropped from `visible_objects`, but the prose kept narrating it lying there on every revisit of this hub room — the ONE non-reactive seam in a pack whose Founder's Cell (plate taken), Bellows Walk (sentinel killed) and stone_grate examine all already update (bug_0023). Added a reactive room `variant` on `outer_forge` gated on `has_item: pry_bar` that drops the pry-bar clause and names the trough now bare ("The cold trough stands bare now — the stout iron pry-bar that lay beside it is in your own hands."), mirroring the founder/grate reactive treatment. The base description (bar present) is the unchanged first-arrival fallback. **Content-only:** no object/exit/flag/quest/score(50)/win/ending touched; taking the bar is inert beyond inventory + the variant swap (verified live), so it cannot affect any route, and the canonical buffed route still wins `ending_victory` 50/50.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 660 tests pass (123 files; +4 bug_0094 regression cases; 0 regressions)** (was 622 at bug_0088); typecheck/lint/format:check clean; verify:integrity static OK; every health-gated pack validates 0/0 (cold_forge `content_hash` re-pins deliberately to `bb7018f2…`, a reactive-text-only edit — NO test pins cold_forge's hash, so nothing weakened). Live MCP (seed 53): the Outer Forge reads the base pry-bar line on arrival; after `take iron pry-bar` it reads the bare-trough variant and drops `pry_bar` from visible_objects, flags/vars/score untouched.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0094_cold_forge_stale_prybar_prose.yaml` + `tests/regression/cold_forge_stale_prybar_prose.test.ts` (4 cases: validates green; base line before take; bare-trough variant + bar gone from objects after take; take is inert beyond inventory).
- **Next suggested focus:** cold_forge is now blind-clean with NO open content findings (phantom hammer bug_0088 + stale pry-bar bug_0094 both closed). (1) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord as an opt-in optional boss where the shade's +2 ward pays off; parallels `meta.deadline`) — structural, higher-leverage than remaining polish. (2) The variant-soundness machinery (bug_0085/0086) could grow a *choice-shadowing* check or detect a variant whose `when` is a strict superset of the base (always-on) — audit value first. (3) The verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge). (4) Keep ROTATING the mandated blind pass across the least-attended packs (next: a parser pack — alchemists_tower or sealed_crypt — or sunken_barrow); clockwork & cold_forge are both at rest (content complete).

### Cycle result — the Wrecker's Light gets its FIRST blind pass; the gallery death is now a chosen fate (bug_0100, content_fix)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the over-fitted clockwork target** (its ~26th straight nomination; content-complete per bug_0079→0085, ~24 clean passes) **onto wreckers_light** — the NEWEST pack (shipped bug_0099, the branching/reactive enjoyment lever) and the **ONLY pack that had never received a dedicated blind LLM pass** (zero mentions across all prior ai-runs). Per the protocol's pack-rotation clause + project memory (rotate onto the least-attended pack), a never-blind-tested branching pack is the highest-value target. Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent, **seed 7**. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T01-45-25-898Z/playtest.md`.
  - Played 4 routes to all **FOUR** endings (saved/wrecker/mercy/drowned); **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5**; **ZERO functional bugs** (no rejected actions, loops, or broken state; reactive scene text — bare mantel after taking the striker, dipped cask, gaping chest — and all action-gating verified consistent with state). Surfaced ONE design flaw (§5).
- **The fix (content_fix, wreckers_light):** the Storm Gallery's only death route, `climb_to_wreck`, read as an "unsignposted instant-death trap" — its label "Climb down the cliff toward the rocks now" read as PURPOSEFUL exploration, so a curious player drowned instantly with no warning the descent was lethal, and the instant fail landed as a punish for curiosity rather than a fate the player elects. This missed the pack's OWN deliberate-terminal discipline (the tend_keeper comment: terminal beats must be "Deliberate and terminal ... never an accidental dead end"); the gallery prose carried the whole warning while the choice label — the thing the player commits to — did not. Reframed the label to **"Climb over the rail and down the storm-slick rungs into the breaking sea below"** so the descent foreshadows its own lethality (consistent with the gallery prose's drop/white water and the ending_drowned epilogue's "a dozen rungs down the cliff"). **Honest choice-text reframe ONLY** (mirrors clockwork's bug_0046 dumbwaiter fix): SAME choice id, SAME ending_drowned target, NO conditions added (the death is not newly gated — it is the same chosen act, now legible), no flag/var/route/ending change.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 692 tests pass (129 files; +4 bug_0100 regression cases; 0 regressions)**; verifier-integrity static OK (0/0); typecheck/lint/format:check clean; every health-gated pack validates 0/0. wreckers_light `content_hash` changes to `365ff499…` (text-only reframe; wreckers_light is NOT a HASH_PIN_FILE and no test pins it — nothing weakened). Live MCP (seed 7): the gallery now offers the reframed climb label and choosing it fires ending_drowned exactly as before; the existing bug_0099 branching test (which exercises this exact climb_to_wreck route in its DROWNED fixture) passes unchanged, confirming the route/ending are untouched.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0100_wreckers_drowned_signposting.yaml` + `tests/regression/wreckers_light_drowned_signposting.test.ts` (4 cases: the climb label names the lethal descent / breaking-sea+rungs and drops the old "toward the rocks now" framing; the route still reaches ending_drowned; the climb carries NO conditions — the fix is label-only, not a new gate; the label is consistent with the ending's rungs-down-the-cliff prose).
- **Next suggested focus:** wreckers_light is now blind-verified (its first pass) with this lone signposting flaw closed — its other three endings and reactive epilogues are clean. (1) Keep ROTATING the mandated blind pass across the least-attended packs — wreckers_light is now attended; next least-attended are **alchemists_tower** (parser) and **sunken_barrow** (rpg). (2) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord as an opt-in optional boss where the shade's +2 ward pays off; parallels `meta.deadline`) — structural, higher-leverage than polish. (3) The variant-soundness machinery (bug_0085/0086) could grow a *choice-shadowing* check or detect a variant whose `when` is a strict superset of the base — audit value first. (4) The verify-integrity semantic-weakening blind spot (wants an LLM-judge). clockwork & cold_forge remain at rest (content complete).

### Cycle result — the cellar hatch now signposts that the brass key won't fit (bug_0103, content_fix)
- **Mandated blind pass ROTATED off the over-fitted clockwork target** (its ~27th straight nomination; content-complete per bug_0079→0085, ~24 clean passes) **onto alchemists_tower** — the LEAST-attended pack by dedicated blind-target count across all 99 prior runs (4 targets vs clockwork's 37), a parser pack. Per the protocol's pack-rotation clause + project memory (rotate onto the least-attended pack). Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent, **seed 53**. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T02-40-31-708Z/playtest.md`.
  - Played 3 routes: full solve to **ending_cured (35/35)**, the death route (**ending_poisoned**), and a wrong-item-to-NPC edge case. **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 3/5**; **ZERO functional bugs** (no rejected actions, loops, or broken state; key-chain, context-sensitive actions, and all reactive room/examine text verified). Surfaced ONE signposting friction (§4) + the known structural enjoyment note (short/linear — a separate, larger lever).
- **The fix (content_fix, alchemists_tower):** in the Great Hall before the iron key, the cellar hatch read only as a "great iron lock" with no unlock action and no hint the **brass key won't fit** — but on the canonical route the player has JUST taken the brass key in the garden (the prior room), so they arrive with a key and "wonder why they can't even try the key they have." Added ONE reactive examine `variant` to `cellar_door`, gated on `has_item brass_key` AND `not_item iron_key`: it names the iron lock as far too big for the little brass key (cut for the strongbox) and foreshadows a "key of iron to match it" — disambiguating the wrong key and pointing at the iron key without naming where it is. Mirrors the pack's bug_0033/0073 reactive-examine discipline and the strongbox's own wrong-key idiom. Gated on `has_item brass_key` so it never leaks to a garden-skipper (bug_0052 leak lesson); the `not_item iron_key` guard retires it the moment the matching key is in hand; listed AFTER the `is_unlocked` variant so the sprung text wins once unlocked. **Examine-only:** no flag/item/score/exit/route/gating/ending change; the down exit still gates on `is_unlocked` and the canonical solve is untouched.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 705 tests pass (132 files; +5 bug_0103 regression cases; 0 regressions)**; verify:integrity static OK; typecheck/lint/format:check clean; every health-gated pack validates 0/0. alchemists_tower `content_hash` changes to `12f6429b…` (examine-text-only; alchemists_tower is NOT a HASH_PIN_FILE and no test pins it — nothing weakened). Live MCP (seed 53): garden→take brass key→Great Hall→examine hatch now reads the wrong-key hint; the base "great iron lock" still shows for a player without the brass key.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0103_alchemists_cellar_wrong_key_hint.yaml` + `tests/regression/alchemists_cellar_wrong_key_hint.test.ts` (5 cases: brass-key-but-no-iron-key fires the wrong-key + key-of-iron hint; no-brass-key keeps the intrinsic base (no leak); the not_item iron_key guard returns the base once the matching key is held; first-match-wins so the sprung variant beats the hint once unlocked; canonical brew route still reaches ending_cured at 35/35).
- **Next suggested focus:** alchemists_tower is now blind-verified with this lone signposting friction closed; its remaining open knock is the structural one (short, fully linear, single solution path) — a branching/second-approach lever, NOT polish. (1) Keep ROTATING the mandated blind pass across the least-attended packs — next least-attended after alchemists_tower (now attended) are **watchtower_road** (cyoa, 6 targets) and **cold_forge** (rpg, 4 targets). (2) Raise enjoyment via BRANCHING on a linear parser pack (alchemists_tower or sealed_crypt), the evidence-backed enjoyment lever (cf. bug_0099 wreckers_light 3→5). (3) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss). (4) The variant-soundness machinery (bug_0085/0086) could grow a choice-shadowing / superset-`when` check — audit value first. clockwork, cold_forge & wreckers_light remain at rest (content complete).

### Cycle result — the validator now flags INERT FLAGS; four dead flags scrubbed (bug_0104, validator)
- **Mandated blind pass on wreckers_light** (the assessor's rank-1, its FIRST pass was last cycle bug_0100 at seed 7). Per the protocol's rotate-the-seed clause it ran on a **NEW seed (23)** to avoid re-fitting. Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T07-27-13-080Z/playtest.md`.
  - Played 4 routes to **ALL FOUR endings** (saved/wrecker/mercy/drowned); **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5**; **ZERO functional bugs** (no rejected actions, loops, or broken state; gating + reactive prose all verified). The pack is now **blind-clean across two seeds** (7 / bug_0100 and 23 / this pass). The one recurring signal (§4/§5) was NOT a content defect but a **validator gap**: the playtester could not tell whether `knows_truth` was inert ("worth an author check") — because the question *does a SET flag ever get READ?* is invisible from inside the game. (`knows_truth` IS consumed by the save/wreck/mercy ending variants; the worry was the general class.) Since the pack needs no fix, the cycle's improvement is the structural lever that turns that manual worry into the enforced bar.
- **The fix (validator, trust-but-verify):** added an **INERT_FLAG** check to `validateCyoa` — the flag-side sibling of `DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE` and the newest member of the soundness family (UNREACHABLE_VARIANT bug_0085, UNSATISFIABLE_CONDITION bug_0086): for every flag in (`set_flag` writers ∪ `flags_init`) that NO condition anywhere reads (has_flag/not_flag, descending all_of/any_of/none_of across all choice `conditions`, scene/ending variant `when`s, and the deadline `when`), emit a **warning**. New helper `collectFlagReads`. **Sound — no false positives:** a flag is flagged ONLY when it has provably zero readers (a flag consulted only via not_flag, or only inside a disjunction, is correctly NOT flagged). Advisory warning, never error (an inert flag is a no-op, never a soft-lock). The check **immediately surfaced FOUR genuinely dead flags** that had accumulated as vestigial bookkeeping: `clockwork.vault_open` (the `next: vault` edge already carries it), `clockwork.has_letter` (redundant with the `letter` item + the `letter_displayed`/`letter_hidden` flags the truth ending actually reads, bug_0051), `watchtower.saw_watchtower` + `watchtower.met_hermit` (the scene transition is the state). **Scrubbed all four** (5 `set_flag` lines removed: 2 watchtower, 3 clockwork) — each a pure gameplay no-op since nothing read them, so routes/gates/variants/endings/journals are unchanged.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 727 tests pass (135 files; +6 bug_0104 regression cases; 0 regressions)** (was 705 at bug_0103); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck/lint/format:check clean; every health-gated pack validates **0 error / 0 warning**. clockwork + watchtower `content_hash` change (effect-only no-op edits): watchtower is the **one deliberately-pinned** cyoa hash (`tests/unit/rpg_validator.test.ts`) — **re-pinned deliberately** to `f6b64fd9…` (was `95cf3665…`) with a documented changelog entry naming the two removed dead flags; clockwork is NOT a HASH_PIN_FILE and no test pins it. The clockwork truth-ending letter tests (reactive-letter, duplicate-sealed-letter) and the watchtower hermit-lore test all pass unchanged, confirming the removed flags were inert. wreckers_light untouched this cycle (hash `365ff499…`).
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0104_cyoa_inert_flag_check.yaml` + `tests/regression/cyoa_inert_flag.test.ts` (6 cases: the three shipped packs are NOT flagged — the scrub held; a flag set-but-never-read IS flagged as a warning naming the flag; a flag read only via not_flag is NOT flagged; a flag read only inside any_of is NOT flagged; a flag read by an ending variant `when` is NOT flagged; an unread `flags_init` flag IS flagged).
- **Next suggested focus:** the soundness family now covers dead variants (bug_0085/0086), dead mechanics (DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE), and dead state (bug_0104) for cyoa packs. (1) **Port the INERT_FLAG check to the parser + rpg validators** — those packs also use flags but have no such check; an audit there is the natural follow-on. (2) Keep ROTATING the mandated blind pass onto the least-attended packs — next are **cold_forge** (rpg) and **sealed_crypt** (parser); wreckers_light is now twice-attended. (3) Raise enjoyment via BRANCHING on a linear parser pack (alchemists_tower or sealed_crypt), the evidence-backed lever (bug_0099). (4) The standing dormant-enemy engine feature (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss). All packs remain content-complete and at rest.

### Cycle result — INERT_FLAG ported to the parser + RPG validators (bug_0106, validator)
- **Mandated blind pass on cold_forge** (the assessor's rank-1 this cycle, rpg). Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent, **seed 7**. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T08-06-40-885Z/playtest.md`.
  - Played the optimal route to **ending_victory (50/50)** plus an under-armed stress route; **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5**; **ZERO functional bugs** (no rejected actions, loops, soft-locks; the bug_0023/0094 reactive seams all held). Its one substantive note was a **balance** observation (the cold-iron plate's +2 defense feels small; even fully geared you finish ~2 HP on seed 7) — but that combat was **deliberately retuned only last-ish cycle (bug_0101)** to make the prepared player a real, winnable gamble (spirit's +2 attack = decisive lever, plate = optional grace). Re-tuning it would churn against a fresh intentional decision, and the playtest called the pack content-complete. So the cycle's improvement is the documented **#1 next step from bug_0104**: port the new INERT_FLAG soundness check to the parser + RPG validators.
- **The fix (validator, trust-but-verify):** added the **INERT_FLAG** check to `validateParser` — the flag-side member of the soundness family already mirrored there (UNREACHABLE_VARIANT shadowing + UNSATISFIABLE_CONDITION, bug_0091), porting bug_0104's CYOA check. It collects every flag the pack **writes** (`set_flag` across room on_enter / object interactions / object unlock_effects / NPC dialogue nodes; `flags_init`; and — via the existing `extraSettableFlags` option the RPG validator already fills — enemy `defeat_flag` + on_defeat / skill-check `set_flag`) and every flag it **reads** (new `collectFlagReads`: has_flag/not_flag, descending all_of/any_of/none_of, across exit/interaction/win conditions, room & object variant `when`s, and dialogue-topic gates). A written flag with NO reader is **warned** (never errored — an inert flag is a no-op, not a soft-lock). **Sound — no false positives:** a flag read only via not_flag (the one-shot dialogue/interaction idiom these packs lean on heavily) or only inside a disjunction still counts as read. Because the RPG validator delegates to the parser one, the check covers **both** parser and rpg packs.
- **Audit result:** the four shipped parser/RPG packs have **ZERO inert flags** — unlike the CYOA audit (bug_0104 scrubbed four). Every set_flag/defeat_flag is read: alchemists_tower + sealed_crypt (catacombs_open / rope_attached_to_well via has_flag; heard_*_rumor / read_* via not_flag), cold_forge (plate_donned via variant+interaction; sentinel_stilled via room variant+exit; heard_* via not_flag), sunken_barrow (wight_slain + heard_* the same way). **So NO content pack changed and NO content_hash moved** — the check is purely an enforced bar against future inert flags.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 745 tests pass (138 files; +7 bug_0106 regression cases; 0 regressions)** (was 727 at bug_0104); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck/lint/format:check clean; every health-gated pack validates **0 error / 0 warning** (no false positives). No HASH_PIN re-pin needed — no pack content was edited.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0106_parser_rpg_inert_flag_check.yaml` + `tests/regression/parser_inert_flag.test.ts` (7 cases: shipped parser+rpg packs NOT flagged & error-free; a flag set by an interaction / by a room on_enter / by flags_init but unread IS flagged; a not_flag-only reader is NOT flagged; an any_of reader is NOT flagged; an RPG enemy defeat_flag with no reader IS flagged via validateRpg — proving runtime-written flags are covered).
- **Next suggested focus:** the soundness family (dead variants bug_0085/0086/0091, dead mechanics DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE/WIN_FIRES_AT_START, dead state INERT_FLAG bug_0104/0106) now covers **all three** validators consistently — that port is complete. (1) Raise enjoyment via **BRANCHING on a linear parser pack** (alchemists_tower or sealed_crypt), the evidence-backed lever (bug_0099 lifted wreckers_light 3→5). (2) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss). (3) The variant-soundness machinery could grow a *choice-shadowing* / superset-`when` check — audit value first. (4) Keep ROTATING the mandated blind pass onto the least-attended packs — cold_forge is now freshly attended; next least-attended are **watchtower_road** (cyoa) and **sunken_barrow** (rpg). All packs remain content-complete and at rest.
## AFK Cycle 2026-06-02T08-19-08-656Z
- Assessment: packs cyoa=3 parser=2 rpg=2; 7 candidate(s) ranked.
- Next best improvement (recommended): [content_fix] Blind-playtest "sunken_barrow_v1" — the coverage bot can't solve its puzzles, so quality is unverified.
- Why: A heuristic bot can't plan multi-step puzzles; only a fresh blind LLM playtest reveals real signposting/clarity issues in this pack.
- Mandatory LLM playtest target this cycle: content/rpg/pack/sunken_barrow.yaml.
- Process: assessor ranks → blind LLM playtest for quality → one improvement → health + verify:integrity green → commit (trust-but-verify).
- **Blind playtest (sunken_barrow, seeds 23 good / 7 doom):** clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, both endings reached, mechanics flawless. Report: ai-runs/2026-06-02T08-19-08-656Z/playtest.md. The tester raised ONE finding TWICE (§4 confusion + §5 design flaw): the score reached the full 50/50 the instant you ENTER the Relic Chamber — *before* the final crown-vs-coffin choice — because the +25 final beat rode the chamber's on_enter. So the irreversible DOOM ending (prising the sarcophagus → ending_woken, death) also read "Final score: 50 of 50," the SAME tally as the true victory, undercutting score as a win signal. Its own suggested fix: "not awarding the final score chunk until the crown is actually taken."
- **Improvement (bug_0107, content_fix + a general engine feature):** added `take_effects` to the parser object schema — the symmetric twin of `unlock_effects` (bug_0077): a takeable object can carry effects that fire AFTER pickup, so a goal item can award its climactic points on the deliberate CLAIM rather than on bare room entry. Fired in the TAKE resolver (covers parser AND rpg — rpg delegates TAKE to resolveParserAction); folded into the parser validator's allEffects() so SCORE_UNREACHABLE / INERT_FLAG / WIN_FIRES_AT_START all count it; schema superRefine rejects take_effects without `takeable: true`; `.optional()` so every other pack compiles byte-identically (no hash drift). Then moved the barrow's final +25 off relic_chamber.on_enter onto the circlet's take_effects. Result: entering the chamber leaves score at 25; only TAKING the crown reaches 50; the doom fork (prise the sarcophagus, never take the crown) tops out at 25/50 — the score now distinguishes the true win from the irreversible doom.
- **Why:** highest-value finding this cycle (raised twice by a fresh blind tester); same honesty discipline the pack already enforces elsewhere (bug_0027/0029/0069 — don't let the surfaced signal say something the mechanics don't). take_effects is a general, reusable hook, not a barrow-only patch.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` exit 0; **752 tests pass (139 files; +7 bug_0107 cases; 0 regressions)** (was 745 at bug_0106); verify:integrity static OK; drift vs HEAD = 0 errors / 2 expected warnings (VERIFIER_TOUCHED on the one-line parser_validator allEffects add; HASH_PIN_REPINNED on barrow_victory.json accompanying the content change). Every health-gated pack validates 0/0. Canonical victory route's FINAL state is byte-identical (score still 50, only the increment turn moved), so the trace's expected_final_hash is UNCHANGED (e55b00eb…); only the content_hash pin moved (54712ef3… → c187324c…).
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0107_barrow_claim_award_not_arrival.yaml` + `tests/regression/rpg_take_effects.test.ts` (7 cases: take_effects fire on pickup; one-shot; schema rejects without takeable; validator fold-in counts/under-counts; barrow chamber-entry 25, victory 50/50, doom 25/50). Existing barrow tests updated to the moved award (rpg_scoring, rpg_barrow_claim_relic, rpg_barrow_lord_woken_fork).
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) Raise enjoyment via **BRANCHING on a linear parser pack** (alchemists_tower or sealed_crypt) — the evidence-backed lever (bug_0099 lifted wreckers_light 3→5); still the standing top breadth lever. (2) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord as an opt-in boss where the shade's +2 ward finally pays off, bug_0083 deferred). (3) The blind tester's secondary note: the wight fight is heal-less with no fallback — an unlucky/shade-skipping player can die with no recourse; consider a recovery option (milder than the score fix; deferred). (4) Keep ROTATING the mandated blind pass onto least-attended packs — sunken_barrow now freshly attended; next least-attended are **watchtower_road** (cyoa) and **sealed_crypt** (parser).

## AFK Cycle 2026-06-02T08-47-43-611Z
- Assessment: packs cyoa=3 parser=2 rpg=2; 7 candidate(s) ranked.
- Next best improvement (recommended): [content_fix] Blind-playtest "clockwork_heist_v1" — the coverage bot can't solve its puzzles, so quality is unverified.
- Why: A heuristic bot can't plan multi-step puzzles; only a fresh blind LLM playtest reveals real signposting/clarity issues in this pack.
- Mandatory LLM playtest target this cycle: content/cyoa/pack/clockwork_heist.yaml.
- Process: assessor ranks → blind LLM playtest for quality → one improvement → health + verify:integrity green → commit (trust-but-verify).
- **Blind playtest (clockwork_heist, seed 5):** fresh source-blind general-purpose subagent, MCP-only. Reached ALL FOUR endings (Truth ×2 prose variants, Rich, Caught), ZERO mechanical bugs, **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5**. Report: ai-runs/2026-06-02T08-47-43-611Z/playtest.md. The ~26th clean pass on this heavily over-fitted target — content backlog remains exhausted (memory: content-polish-backlog-exhausted). The tester's one substantive note (§4): it actively tried and could NOT find a tick threshold that fires a bad ending — "the looming-clock tension is atmospheric flavor rather than a live mechanic ... the prose slightly oversells a constraint that isn't enforced." (The deadline DOES exist at ticks>=10 → ending_overstayed; tuned generous on purpose, so the tester never lingered that long — not a defect.)
- **Decision — NO content churn; generalize the lesson to a validator soundness fix.** Re-tuning the deadline tighter would punish honest play (the whole point of bug_0079's generous tuning) and the pack is content-complete. Instead I took the generalizable lesson — "a declared deadline can read as toothless / be a Chekhov's gun" — and audited the deadline-firability machinery (bug_0087), finding a real soundness gap.
- **The fix (bug_0109, validator, trust-but-verify):** made `checkDeadlineFirability`'s `var_gte` test **direction-aware**. bug_0087 only asked "is the watched var ever written?"; a `var_gte: {value: V}` deadline whose var IS written but only ever DROPS (every write a dec_var / inc by<=0 / set below V) starts below V and can NEVER reach it — as dead as an unwritten var, but the coarse test passed it. New helper `varCanReachGte(value, writes)` (reuses the directional VarWrite data collectFalsifiers already builds, now computed once and shared with checkDeadlineFiresAtStart): a bound is reachable only if some write can RAISE the var there (a positive inc, repeatable round a loop, or a set landing at/above the bound). `var_eq` left on the coarse test (an inc/dec/set could land on the value — not provably dead). Strictly strengthens an existing WARNING; no schema/runner/engine/content change.
- **Why:** highest-value move this cycle that respects "don't churn content." The blind playtest is a weak backstop for a dead deadline (it simply never fires, reading as normal), so this belongs in the static verifier. Generalizes the exact "clock with no teeth" class the playtester described into an enforced bar for all current and future packs.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 759 tests pass (140 files; +4 bug_0109 regression cases; 0 regressions)** (was 752 at bug_0107 / 755 at bug_0108); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck/lint/format:check clean; all SEVEN health-gated packs validate **0 error / 0 warning**. **No content pack edited → no content_hash moved, no HASH_PIN re-pin.** Clockwork's own deadline (ticks>=10, inc_var by:1) still produces ZERO DEADLINE_UNFIREABLE and the full bug_0087 suite stays green (soundness — no false positive on a live deadline).
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0109_deadline_firability_direction_aware.yaml` + `tests/regression/cyoa_deadline_unfireable.test.ts` (new bug_0109 describe block, 4 cases: decrement-only var flagged; set-below-bound flagged; positive-inc NOT flagged; set-at/above-bound NOT flagged — plus the pre-existing bug_0087 suite still green).
- **Next suggested focus:** the deadline mechanic is now machine-checked against the full common family of "declared but silently dead" timers (bug_0079/0080 made-real + reachable-via-choice-effects; bug_0087 initial firability; bug_0109 direction-aware). Remaining levers, unchanged in priority: (1) **BRANCHING on a linear parser pack** (alchemists_tower or sealed_crypt) — the evidence-backed enjoyment lever (bug_0099 lifted wreckers_light 3→5), still the top breadth move. (2) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss, bug_0083 deferred). (3) A possible analogue: port the same direction-aware var check to the choice-gate feasibility logic (IMPOSSIBLE_GATE, cyoa_validator.ts ~275) — audit value first, it shares the coarse `writtenVars.has` test. (4) Keep ROTATING the blind pass onto least-attended packs — clockwork now freshly attended; least-attended remaining are **wreckers_light** (cyoa) and **alchemists_tower** (parser).

- Process: assessor ranks → blind LLM playtest for quality → one improvement → health + verify:integrity green → commit (trust-but-verify).

### Cycle result — IMPOSSIBLE_GATE made direction-aware for var_gte (bug_0110, validator)
- **Mandated blind pass on wreckers_light** (the assessor's rank-1). Per the rotate-the-seed clause this ran on a **NEW seed (42)** after seed 7 (bug_0100) and seed 23 — the THIRD dedicated blind pass on this pack. Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T09-00-04-216Z/playtest.md`.
  - Played 4 routes to **ALL FOUR endings** (saved/wrecker/mercy/drowned); **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5**; **ZERO functional bugs** (no rejected actions, loops, or broken state; gating + reactive prose all verified — bare mantel after striker, dipped cask, sprung chest, knows_truth-reframed save ending). The pack is now **blind-clean across three seeds** (7/23/42). The tester's only notes were taste-level (the truth is optional/skippable; the drown hatch is the weakest of four endings) — no content defect. Content backlog remains exhausted (memory: content-polish-backlog-exhausted).
- **The fix (validator, trust-but-verify):** with no content fix warranted, the cycle's improvement is the documented **#3 next-step from bug_0109**: port the direction-aware var-reachability reasoning to the **choice-gate feasibility check** (IMPOSSIBLE_GATE), which still shared the coarse `writes.writtenVars.has` test bug_0109 retired for deadlines. A choice gated on `var_gte: V` (needing a raise above init) whose watched var IS written but only ever DROPS (every write a dec_var, an inc_var by<=0, or a set_var landing BELOW V) can never reach V — the choice is never offerable, as dead as an unwritten var, but the coarse test passed it (invisible to a blind playtest: the option simply never appears). **The fix:** hoisted `collectFalsifiers` to the top of `validateCyoa` (computed ONCE, now shared by both deadline checks AND the choice loop; removed the inner `const falsifiers` in the deadline block) and split the var-feasibility branch — `var_gte && value>init` now errors IMPOSSIBLE_GATE unless `varCanReachGte(value, varWrites)` holds (some write is a positive inc, repeatable round a loop, or a set at/above the bound). `var_eq && value!=init` is LEFT on the coarse "ever written?" test (an inc/dec/set could land on an `==` value — not provably dead). **Sound — no false positives:** strictly strengthens the existing ERROR only for the provably-impossible case.
- **Audit result:** the only shipped pack with a var-gated CHOICE is clockwork (`ticks>=4`, fed by `inc_var ticks by:1`) — `varCanReachGte` returns true, so it is **NOT flagged**. **No content pack edited → no content_hash moved, no HASH_PIN re-pin.** The check is purely an enforced bar against future down-only / sub-bound var gates.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 767 tests pass (141 files; +8 bug_0110 regression cases; 0 regressions)** (was 759 at bug_0109); verify:integrity static OK; typecheck/lint/format:check clean; all SEVEN health-gated packs validate **0 error / 0 warning** (no false positives). All content hashes unchanged.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0110_impossible_gate_direction_aware.yaml` + `tests/regression/cyoa_impossible_gate_direction_aware.test.ts` (8 cases: shipped clockwork var-gated choice NOT flagged; decrement-only / sub-bound-set / non-positive-inc gates ARE flagged; positive-inc and set-at/above-bound NOT flagged (soundness); a var_eq gate on a written var NOT flagged but on an entirely unwritten var IS — eq's coarse test retained).
- **Next suggested focus:** the two var-bound consumers in the CYOA validator (deadline firability bug_0109, choice feasibility bug_0110) are now consistently direction-aware — that port is complete. Remaining levers, unchanged in priority: (1) **BRANCHING on a linear parser pack** (alchemists_tower or sealed_crypt) — the evidence-backed enjoyment lever (bug_0099 lifted wreckers_light 3→5), still the top breadth move. (2) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss, bug_0083 deferred). (3) Consider porting the same direction-aware var-gate reasoning to the parser/RPG validators' feasibility checks (audit value first — those packs may not use var gates). (4) Keep ROTATING the blind pass onto least-attended packs — wreckers_light is now thrice-attended; least-attended remaining are **alchemists_tower** (parser) and **sealed_crypt** (parser). All packs remain content-complete and at rest.

### Cycle result — alchemists_tower BRANCHED at the climax (bug_0111, content_fix / quest_structure)
- **Mandated blind pass on alchemists_tower** (the assessor's rank-1; the least-attended parser pack). Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent, **NEW seed 101** (win) + 202 (trap). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T09-08-43-513Z/playtest.md`. WON ending_cured 40/40 and reached ending_poisoned (drink the phial). **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — reactive prose + gating all verified. The pack is content-complete and blind-clean across many seeds (memory: content-polish-backlog-exhausted).
- **Two complementary signals (both -> the SAME fix):** (§3/§6) the lost enjoyment point is attributed to the pack being "short and fully **linear** with a single non-trivial branch (the trap)" — recurring across this pack's prior passes and exactly the standing **#1 evidence-backed lever** (BRANCH a linear parser pack, cf. bug_0099 which lifted wreckers_light 3->5). (§5) the tester (and an earlier seed-67 tester) flagged that a player carrying the black phial to the spire had **no "give black phial to master" action** — the trap only ever punished self-experimentation (drink it), never the more natural mistake of dosing the patient with the wrong vial. The climax offered no decision.
- **The fix (bug_0111, content / quest_structure):** made the spire climax a real **DECISION** — give the master the antidote (the WIN) or the black phial (a distinct new death, **ending_master_poisoned** "The Wrong Vial", the wrong-fork twin of drinking it yourself, recoverable via load §8.7). Added (1) a USE-black_phial-on-master interaction (command_verb `give`, mirroring the antidote grammar; **no score, no flag** -> no INERT_FLAG), (2) the new declared death ending, (3) a reactive `master` examine variant (gated has_item black_phial + not_item antidote, listed AFTER the antidote variant so the success state wins first-match) showing her recoil from the dark phial — telegraphing the wrong move. Heavily foreshadowed (recipe + phial examine + recoil) and OPTIONAL (the canonical player leaves the phial on the bench, never sees it), so it adds replay depth without complicating the main route. In-genre (the header calls it "Sierra-Quest-style"; deadly-but-recoverable wrong choices are the genre's signature). Canonical win + self-drink trap untouched.
- **Verified live** through the MCP tools: carrying the phial to the spire surfaces `give black phial to fevered master`; firing it ends at ending_master_poisoned (death, 0/40); the recoil examine variant renders.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 772 tests pass (142 files; +5 bug_0111 cases; 0 regressions)** (was 767 at bug_0110); verify:integrity static OK; all SEVEN packs validate **0 error / 0 warning**. alchemists_tower content_hash moves to 9003feae... (new interaction + ending + variant) — **NOT hash-pinned by any live test** (only watchtower_road is), so nothing to re-pin; NO protected source / HASH_PIN file touched, no override.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0111_alchemists_master_wrong_vial_branch.yaml` + `tests/regression/alchemists_master_wrong_vial_branch.test.ts` (5 cases: branch surfaces + ends in death/0; gated on the phial (absent on the antidote route); reactive examine reflects what you hold base/recoil/pale-vial; new ending is death & ending_cured still a non-death win, pack 0/0; canonical brew still wins 40/40). **Updated** `alchemists_spire_inventory_truth.test.ts` (bug_0065): the phial-bearer master examine now hits the more-specific bug_0111 recoil variant — its assertion repointed to the recoil text while KEEPING bug_0065's core invariant (never claims an antidote/"pale vial in your hand"); the truly-bare base text is now covered by the new bug_0111 test. No check weakened.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) the standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss, bug_0083 deferred) is now the top untouched structural lever. (2) Port the bug_0111 branch pattern to the OTHER linear parser pack, **sealed_crypt** — the enjoyment lever is now demonstrated on alchemists_tower as well as wreckers_light. (3) Port the direction-aware var-gate reasoning (bug_0109/0110) to the parser/RPG feasibility checks if those packs grow var gates (audit value first). (4) Keep ROTATING the blind pass onto least-attended packs — alchemists_tower now freshly attended; least-attended remaining is **sealed_crypt** (parser).

- Assessment: packs cyoa=3 parser=2 rpg=2; 7 candidate(s) ranked.
- Next best improvement (recommended): [content_fix] Blind-playtest "cold_forge_v1" — the coverage bot can't solve its puzzles, so quality is unverified.
- Why: A heuristic bot can't plan multi-step puzzles; only a fresh blind LLM playtest reveals real signposting/clarity issues in this pack.
- Mandatory LLM playtest target this cycle: content/rpg/pack/cold_forge.yaml.
- Process: assessor ranks → blind LLM playtest for quality → one improvement → health + verify:integrity green → commit (trust-but-verify).

### Cycle result — the RPG combat-winnability proof's CONTRACT is now honest (bug_0113, validator)
- **Mandated blind pass on cold_forge** (the assessor's rank-1, rpg). Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent, **NEW seed 31** (prior cold_forge pass was seed 7 / bug_0106), per the rotate-the-seed clause. Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T09-35-33-314Z/playtest.md`.
  - WON ending_victory **50/50** (full prep, finished ~4 HP) + reached ending_fallen **0/50** (under-armed). **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — every reactive seam (bare trough, plate-stripped founder, broken-sentinel walk, levered grate), one-shot dialogue, score (15/15/20=50), and the retreat valve all verified. The pack is content-complete and blind-clean across seeds (memory: content-polish-backlog-exhausted).
- **The recurring signal (raised §4/§5/§6, three ways):** combat damage RNG can fell a CORRECTLY-prepared player on bad rolls (the buffed run finished ~4 HP) and there is no way to restore HP — "a fully-prepped player who gets unlucky has no recourse." This is the SAME note every prior cold_forge/sunken_barrow pass raised. It is **TRUE and INTENTIONAL** (bug_0101/0102 deliberately made preparation a real, winnable *gamble*, honestly prosed — "a lethal gamble", never "guaranteed"), so **no content fix is warranted** — the prior cold_forge cycle (bug_0106) faced the identical balance note and correctly declined to churn the fresh bug_0101 tuning.
- **The fix (validator, trust-but-verify):** the generalizable lesson — "a fully-prepared player can still LOSE to luck" — pointed at a genuine defect not in the pack but in the **verifier's stated contract**. The RPG combat-winnability proof (`COMBAT_UNWINNABLE`, `src/validate/rpg_validator.ts`) is a deliberately CONSERVATIVE check: best reachable stats × LUCKIEST rolls (player max d6=6, enemy min d6=1), erroring only when even that best case loses — only a TRULY impossible fight is flagged. But the docstring (lines 10-12) and a variable (`worstCaseDamageTaken`, comment "vs. worst-case enemy damage") MIS-stated this as a survival guarantee against "worst-case-for-them rolls" — a documented contract STRICTLY STRONGER than the code, implying a green run forbids a luck-loss. It does not — exactly the false guarantee every RPG tester intuits. **Made the contract honest:** rewrote the docstring to state the best-case-lower-bound semantics and that a deliberately luck-dependent fight is PERMITTED (not flagged); renamed `worstCaseDamageTaken` → `minDamageTaken` (it is the LEAST damage any run inflicts) with a corrected comment + accurate error-message figure. **No logic change** — the threshold, the bug_0097 best-reachable crediting, and the error text are untouched. The error MESSAGE was already correct ("even with best-case rolls"); only the docstring and the variable lied.
- **Why within the bar:** pure verifier documentation/naming correctness — no schema/runner/engine/content change, so **no pack's bytes move and no content hash re-pins**. In a project whose thesis is "the verifier is the bar," a verifier that mis-states its own guarantee is itself an integrity defect. The pinning test also forecloses the tempting WRONG "fix" (turning it into a real worst-case-roll test), which would false-positive BOTH shipped RPG packs (cold_forge atk6/def4 and sunken_barrow atk4/def4 both LOSE worst-case by design) and break the 0-warning bar against honestly-telegraphed intentional design.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 788 tests pass (144 files; +3 bug_0113 cases; 0 regressions)**; verify:integrity static **0/0** (no VERIFIER_TOUCHED); typecheck/lint/format:check clean; all SEVEN health-gated packs validate **0 error / 0 warning**. cold_forge content_hash unchanged (0684ada6…).
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0113_winnability_proof_contract_honest.yaml` + `tests/regression/rpg_combat_winnability_semantics.test.ts` (3 cases: a best-case-winnable-but-worst-case-lethal fight using cold_forge's reachable stats is NOT flagged; a fight impossible on the luckiest rolls IS flagged — the conservative error still fires; the `>=` boundary holds, minDamageTaken==HP flags and HP+1 clears). The existing bug_0097 suite stays green.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) The genuine player-experience gap the testers keep naming — an unlucky PREPARED player dies with no way to RECOVER HP (only to retreat, which neither RPG room heals) — is the standing open item. The sound shape (deferred, YAGNI today): an OPT-IN strong guarantee (a pack declaring its fight "fair", e.g. `meta.combat_guaranteed`, gets a worst-case-roll proof; gamble packs stay unflagged). (2) The standing **dormant-enemy engine feature** (woken Barrow-Lord opt-in boss, bug_0083 deferred) remains the top untouched structural lever. (3) Keep ROTATING the blind pass onto least-attended packs — cold_forge now freshly attended; next least-attended are **clockwork_heist** / **wreckers_light** (cyoa) by recency. All packs remain content-complete and at rest.

- Process: assessor ranks → blind LLM playtest for quality → one improvement → health + verify:integrity green → commit (trust-but-verify).

### Cycle result — OPT-IN combat fairness guarantee (bug_0114, validator + schema)
- **Mandated blind pass on watchtower_road** (the assessor's rank-1, cyoa). Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent, **NEW seed 53** (prior passes seed 7 / bug_0108, seed 19). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T10-03-51-892Z/playtest.md`. Played 3 routes to ending_truth + ending_captured (×2 strategies); **clarity 4/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional bugs** — gating both directions (show_papers only with the letter), reactive epilogue (beacon name-check), consumed-action retirement all verified. **Blind-clean across seeds 7/19/53.**
- **Why no content fix:** the tester's only note was the sealed letter's "break open and read" hint — but that was **already cashed last cycle (bug_0108)**, and "a knowing eye" idiomatically points at the hermit (whose show_letter breaks the seal). Rewording it a 2nd consecutive cycle would be content churn (memory: content-polish-backlog-exhausted). Confirmed the whole content+structure layer has **converged**: every parser pack is now branched (wreckers_light/bug_0099, alchemists_tower/bug_0111, **sealed_crypt already forks via bug_0105** victory↔plunder), and the dormant-boss lever **shipped** (bug_0083 → ending_woken, contrary to the stale "deferred" memory note). No linear pack remains to branch.
- **The fix (validator + schema, trust-but-verify):** built the **sound next-shape bug_0113 explicitly named** — an OPT-IN combat fairness guarantee that operationalizes the single most-recurring RPG finding (every cold_forge/sunken_barrow pass: "a prepared player can still die to luck"). (1) **Schema:** new optional `meta.combat_guaranteed?: boolean` (`RpgMetaSchema = ParserMetaSchema + field`), `.optional()` so absent stays absent ⇒ **every RPG pack compiles byte-identically, no content hash moves**. (2) **Validator:** when set, each fight must also clear the **UPPER bound** — best reachable stats but the player's WORST rolls (player min d6=1, enemy max d6=6), the exact mirror of the existing best-case math with rolls flipped (min player dmg MAXIMISES rounds-to-kill ⇒ enemy retaliates the MOST, player still first; the provable max total damage). If a best-prepared player still falls (`maxDamageTaken >= reachable HP`) → **ERROR `COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED`**; else the player survives EVERY sequence (sound). Mirrors src/rpg/combat.ts exactly (max(1, d6+atk-def), player strikes first, enemy retaliates only on survived rounds).
- **Soundness / no false positives:** strictly additive + opt-in. Packs without the flag are untouched — the shipped **gamble packs (cold_forge, sunken_barrow) stay unflagged**, so bug_0113's pinned "a gamble is permitted" semantics hold. New ERROR is a SEPARATE code, never the COMBAT_UNWINNABLE lower bound. No pack opts in yet → a **future-guarding bar exactly like bug_0110's** down-only var-gate check (teeth the moment any pack declares a fight fair). bug_0113's preferred shape, not its declined worst-case-by-default rewrite.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 796 tests pass (146 files; +5 bug_0114 cases; 0 regressions)**; verify:integrity static **0/0** (no VERIFIER_TOUCHED override); typecheck/lint/format:check clean; all SEVEN packs validate **0 error / 0 warning**. **cold_forge (0684ada6…) and sunken_barrow (80ab4154…) content hashes UNCHANGED** — no pack edited, no HASH_PIN re-pin.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0114_combat_guaranteed_optin.yaml` + `tests/regression/rpg_combat_guaranteed_optin.test.ts` (5 cases: the exact bug_0113 gamble stats NOT flagged without the flag / ARE flagged with it; a survivable-every-roll fight clean under the flag; the `>=` boundary HP27 flagged / HP28 clear; the shipped cold_forge pack has combat_guaranteed undefined and draws no COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED). bug_0113's suite stays green.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) The opt-in now EXISTS; a future RPG pack designed as a genuinely fair fight can adopt `combat_guaranteed: true` and have the bar enforce it (shipped packs intentionally stay gambles). (2) The standing player-experience lever — an unlucky prepared player has no HP RECOVERY (only retreat, which neither RPG room heals) — is a CONTENT/engine move for a future pack that wants a healing affordance; YAGNI until then. (3) Both var-bound consumers (deadline bug_0109, choice bug_0110) and now the combat contract (bug_0113/0114) are direction/contract-honest; the validator is at rest. (4) Keep ROTATING the blind pass — watchtower freshly attended at seed 53; clockwork_heist is the longest-unplayed cyoa. All packs content-complete, blind-clean, and at rest.

- Mandatory LLM playtest target this cycle: content/cyoa/pack/wreckers_light.yaml.
- Process: assessor ranks → blind LLM playtest for quality → one improvement → health + verify:integrity green → commit (trust-but-verify).

### Cycle result — PARSER exhaustive ending-reachability proof (bug_0122, test; structural pivot)
- **Mandated blind pass on wreckers_light** (the assessor's rank-1, cyoa). Fresh source-blind MCP-only general-purpose subagent on a **NEW seed 88** (prior dedicated passes seed 7 / 23 / 42 — this is the **fourth**, per the rotate-the-seed clause). Report: `ai-runs/2026-06-02T12-07-20-072Z/playtest.md`. Played 4 routes to **ALL FOUR endings** (saved/wrecker/mercy/drowned); **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 5/5, ZERO functional defects** — gating both directions, reactive knowledge-state prose, and inventory/scene-text updates all verified. The pack is now **blind-clean across four seeds (7/23/42/88)**. Content backlog remains exhausted (memory: content-polish-backlog-exhausted); no content fix warranted.
- **The fix (test, trust-but-verify):** executed the explicit **#1 next-step bug_0121 named in its own scope** ("the parser/RPG modes... are the natural next extension") — extended the exhaustive concrete ending-reachability solver from CYOA to the **PARSER** mode. The mode-agnostic BFS was **extracted to a shared helper** `tests/regression/support/exhaustive_endings.ts` (now used by both the CYOA suite, refactored onto it, and the new parser suite). For each shipped parser pack (auto-discovered from `content/parser/pack`, matching the health-covers-all-packs bar bug_0096) it steps the engine's own `rules.legalActions` set through `makeStep`, dedupes on a total state fingerprint, and asserts the endings ACTUALLY reached by play == the set declared in `pack.endings` (missing-ending fails, undeclared-ending fails, route-sever fails, cap-out fails — never a silent pass).
- **Two changes the larger verb×object space required (both sound):** (1) **Fingerprint completed** — the CYOA key (current|flags|inv|vars) collapsed distinct parser states and reached ZERO endings, because parser gates transitions on `visited` (win_conditions like `{visited: catacombs}`) and `objectState` (an opened/unlocked container). `stateKey` now also includes visited, objectState (open/locked/contents/location), questStage, and ended/endingId — every field a Condition/Effect reads — while still EXCLUDING `step` (monotonic; including it defeats all dedupe; nothing in the deterministic CYOA/parser modes depends on it) and `journal` (append-only narration). (2) **Monotone action restriction** — the parser space is dominated by REVERSIBLE/inert moves (DROP undoes TAKE, CLOSE undoes OPEN, LOOK/INVENTORY/READ/INSPECT change nothing) that explode the state count without ever being needed to win (an unrestricted exhaust of alchemists_tower **capped at 200k+ states / 17s WITHOUT finding the win**). The search steps only "progress" actions, skipping those six verb types. **Sound for a reachability proof:** restricting the action set is monotone — any ending still reached uses a real legal playthrough on a SUBSET of actions, hence genuinely reachable; a restriction can only HIDE an ending that truly needs a drop, which surfaces as a declared ending going unreached = a LOUD failure, never a silent pass. Audited safe: both parser packs gate every transition on has_item/visited/flags/is_unlocked; `not_item`/drop appear ONLY in reactive-prose `when:` variants, never on a route. **No-op for CYOA** (CHOOSE only) — the CYOA census stays COMPLETE and bug_0121's guarantee is unchanged.
- **Verified live (seed 7, 200k cap):** sealed_crypt **4568 states → BOTH** endings (victory via catacombs, plunder via the founder's coffin); alchemists_tower **2905 states → ALL FOUR** (cured/poisoned/master_poisoned/betrayal). CYOA on the shared helper: wreckers_light 441, clockwork_heist 1967, watchtower_road 82622 states, all endings. Every search exhausted under the cap; missing & undeclared sets empty for all five packs.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 835 tests pass (154 files; +3 parser-reachability cases + the shared-helper extract; 0 regressions)** (was 796 at bug_0114-era; intervening cycles added more); verify:integrity static **0/0** (no VERIFIER_TOUCHED — pure test addition); typecheck/lint/format:check clean; all SEVEN packs validate **0 error / 0 warning**. **NO content/ or src/ file touched → no content hash moved, no HASH_PIN re-pin.**
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0122_parser_exhaustive_solvability_proof.yaml` + `tests/regression/parser_all_endings_reachable.test.ts` + the shared `tests/regression/support/exhaustive_endings.ts`. The CYOA suite `cyoa_all_endings_reachable.test.ts` was refactored onto the shared helper (identical assertions; now fingerprints the full state) and stays green.
- **Next suggested focus:** (1) **RPG is the last solver extension** and the one genuinely hard case: its seeded combat/skill rolls are keyed on `state.step`, so a single fingerprint can transition many ways and a pure-fingerprint BFS cannot soundly exhaust it. RPG winnability stays proven by the dedicated combat-bound checks (COMBAT_UNWINNABLE / COMBAT_NOT_GUARANTEED, bug_0113/0114); a sound dynamic RPG reachability proof would need to branch over the dice range (best/worst rolls) rather than step a single draw — a real piece of work, audit value first. (2) The standing player-experience lever — an unlucky PREPARED RPG player has no HP RECOVERY — remains a content/engine move for a future pack (YAGNI). (3) Keep ROTATING the blind pass onto least-attended packs — wreckers_light now four-times attended; least-attended remaining are the parser packs (now also reachability-proven) and clockwork_heist. All packs content-complete, blind-clean, and at rest.

## AFK Cycle 2026-06-02T12-44-46-086Z — RPG exhaustive ending-reachability proof (the last solver extension)
- **Mandatory blind playtest (rank-1 target):** The Sealed Crypt (parser, seed 7). Fresh source-blind MCP-only subagent, two playthroughs → reached BOTH endings (ending_victory via the catacombs key-chain, ending_plunder via the founder's coffin), **clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO functional defects**. Report: ai-runs/2026-06-02T12-44-46-086Z/playtest.md. Confirms the pack is blind-clean (content-polish-backlog still exhausted); the only lever is structural.
- **Improvement (bug_0124 — engine_seam + test):** completed the exhaustive concrete every-declared-ending-reachable proof across ALL THREE modes. bug_0121 did CYOA, bug_0122 did parser, both naming RPG the last + genuinely-harder extension (its seeded d6/d20 combat & skill rolls defeat a single-Rules deterministic BFS). This cycle does RPG.
- **How (sound):** added a behaviour-preserving rng SEAM — `resolveAttack`/`resolveSkillCheck` (src/rpg/combat.ts) take an optional `rng: Rng` defaulting to the existing `rngForStep(seed,step)`; `buildRpgRules` (src/rpg/runner.ts) takes an optional `rngFor:(state)=>Rng` defaulting to the same. Production passes nothing ⇒ play BYTE-IDENTICAL, no content hash moves. The shared BFS gained `exhaustiveEndingsMulti(ruleSets,...)` (single-rules `exhaustiveEndings` now delegates to it, CYOA/parser unchanged): it steps every legal action under N rule sets and unions reachable states. The RPG test builds TWO — player-BEST rolls (strike d6=6 / damage-taken enemy d6=1 / skill d20=20) and player-WORST (1/6/1) — via a `fixedSeqRng`.
- **Soundness:** NO false positives (every successor is a real `makeStep` on a real legal die face). NO silent false negatives — the only routing-relevant consequence of a round/check is MONOTONE in the roll (enemy at 0 HP? player at 0 HP? d20+skill ≥ difficulty?), so the best/worst extremes bracket every middle-roll outcome. The single gap — an ending gating on a RAW HP value (a middle roll lands an HP the extremes skip) — is closed by a test ASSERTION that no pack condition reads an HP var (player `hp` or `__enemy_hp_*`), scanning var_gte/var_lte/var_eq nodes recursively; a violation fails LOUD ("extend the solver to branch HP"), never a silent pass. This proves ROUTE EXISTENCE (every declared ending, incl. the death ending, reachable under SOME play); the validator's combat-bound checks (bug_0113/0114) keep proving WORST-case winnability. Complementary.
- **Verified live + proof-has-teeth (seed 7, 200k cap):** cold_forge **19005 states → BOTH** endings (best-only reaches only ending_victory; worst-only only ending_fallen — the UNION is what proves both). sunken_barrow **1885 states → ALL THREE** (victory/woken via best, fallen via worst; neither regime alone covers all). Both exhausted under cap; missing & undeclared sets empty; HP-routing guard passes for both packs.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 844 tests pass (156 files; +3 RPG-reachability cases)** (was 835/154). verify:integrity static **0/0** (no VERIFIER_TOUCHED). typecheck/lint/format:check clean. All SEVEN packs validate **0 error / 0 warning**; cold_forge & sunken_barrow content hashes UNCHANGED (rng seam defaults preserve behaviour) → no HASH_PIN re-pin.
- **Locked by:** `traces/bugs/bug_0124_rpg_exhaustive_solvability_proof.yaml` + `tests/regression/rpg_all_endings_reachable.test.ts` + the extended shared `tests/regression/support/exhaustive_endings.ts`.
- **Milestone:** the exhaustive every-declared-ending-reachable proof now covers ALL SEVEN shipped packs (3 CYOA + 2 parser + 2 RPG) — the "last solver extension" the prior cycles deferred is done.
- **Next suggested focus:** the solver trilogy is complete; remaining levers are content/experience, not structural proof. (1) The standing player-experience gap — an unlucky PREPARED RPG player has no HP RECOVERY — is a content/engine move for a future pack (still YAGNI absent demand). (2) Keep ROTATING the blind pass onto least-attended packs (parser + clockwork_heist now well-attended; cold_forge/sunken_barrow are the least-recently blind-played and now also route-existence-proven). (3) If a future RPG pack ever HP-gates a route, the bug_0124 guard fires — extend the solver to branch the HP lattice (best/worst already bound it) rather than just the binary outcome. All packs content-complete, blind-clean, reachability-proven, and at rest.

## AFK Cycle 2026-06-02T13-02-56-343Z — Cold Forge death-ending render lock (bug_0125)
- **Mandatory blind playtest (rank-1 target):** The Cold Forge (RPG, seed 11). Fresh source-blind MCP-only subagent, two playthroughs → ending_victory 50/50 on BOTH a prepared run and a deliberately under-armed run (seed 11's combat variance was kind — the reckless run survived at exactly 1 HP). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5, ZERO mechanical defects.** Report: ai-runs/2026-06-02T13-02-56-343Z/playtest.md. Confirms cold_forge stays blind-clean (content backlog still exhausted).
- **The one actionable finding (§5):** because even the reckless run survived, the tester "can't confirm the death-ending text (ending_fallen) fires cleanly from actual play — only that the death:false victory does." That was the death ending's one genuinely-untested layer: bug_0124 proves it route-reachable (abstract BFS, no render); cold_forge_sentinel_prep_matters.test.ts case (3) drives an under-armed death but asserts only state.endingId + the pack's death boolean — it inspects GameState, never the player-facing observation. Nothing locked that a DYING player is actually SHOWN the death title/text + the score-closure tally.
- **Improvement (test layer — bug_0125):** added tests/regression/cold_forge_death_ending_render.test.ts. Drives the under-armed seed-1 route to the sentinel's killing blow, builds buildParserObservation at the ended state, and asserts the rendered death experience: ended + ending_id ending_fallen; ending block {id, title:"Cold on the Cinders", text, death:true}; the PLAYER-VISIBLE obs.title === "Cold on the Cinders" (not the room name) and obs.description carries the death text; and the renderer's honest score closure "Final score: 0 of 50." appended for a no-milestone death (with the canonical ending.text staying pure, no tally). Complements prep_matters case (3) (internal end-state) and bug_0124 (abstract reachability) with the rendered-experience layer the blind pass asked for.
- **Why this, not a content edit:** the pack is blind-clean — no defect to fix. The two other §5 notes are non-defects by design (the "under-armed warning over-promises" is bug_0101's intended probabilistic gamble — seed 11 fell in the lucky minority; the dialogue "~12 clicks for 4 topics" is standard tree navigation, and flattening it risks DIALOGUE_NONTERMINATING). The death-render gap was the one concrete, verifiable hole, and closing it is purely additive.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 847 tests pass (157 files; +3 cases)** (was 844/156). All SEVEN packs validate 0 error / 0 warning; cold_forge content_hash 8590fefd… UNCHANGED (no pack edit → no HASH_PIN move). verify:integrity / typecheck / lint / format all clean.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0125_cold_forge_death_ending_render.yaml + tests/regression/cold_forge_death_ending_render.test.ts.
- **Next suggested focus:** cold_forge's victory AND death endings are now both render-locked through the player observation; sunken_barrow's death ending (ending_fallen) has the same shape and the same gap — the rank-list will keep surfacing it, so the cheap next lock is to mirror this render assertion onto the barrow's death path. Beyond that the standing levers are unchanged: rotate the blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack, and the HP-recovery content gap stays YAGNI absent demand. All packs content-complete, blind-clean, reachability-proven, and at rest.

## AFK Cycle 2026-06-02T13-11-26-441Z — Sunken Barrow death-ending render lock (bug_0126)
- **Mandatory blind playtest (rank-1 target):** The Sunken Barrow (RPG, seed 42). Fresh source-blind MCP-only subagent, THREE playthroughs → reached ALL THREE endings: ending_victory (warded route, 50/50), ending_fallen (skip the shade, die to the wight, 0/50), and ending_woken (prise the sealed sarcophagus, 25/50). **Clarity 5/5, enjoyment 4/5 (4 only for brevity), ZERO mechanical defects** — no rejected actions, no loops, live dice seam observed (slab check rolled 16 / then 2→18 on different runs), reactive prose all correct. Report: ai-runs/2026-06-02T13-11-26-441Z/playtest.md. Confirms the pack stays blind-clean (content-polish-backlog still exhausted); no content defect to fix.
- **The actionable gap:** unlike cold_forge's seed-11 pass (every run survived), this pass actually REACHED both barrow death endings from real play — but only at the transcript level. Nothing in the suite locked the player-facing RENDERED death observation. The prior cycle's handoff (cycle 2026-06-02T13-02-56-343Z, bug_0125) named exactly this as the cheap next lock: "sunken_barrow's death ending (ending_fallen) has the same shape and the same gap … mirror this render assertion onto the barrow's death path."
- **Improvement (test layer — bug_0126):** added tests/regression/rpg_barrow_death_ending_render.test.ts, mirroring cold_forge bug_0125 onto BOTH barrow death forks. For each, drive the route and assert buildParserObservation at the ended state:
  - **ending_fallen** (under-armed seed-2 route, die to the wight): obs.ended + ending_id ending_fallen; ending block {id, title:"Another Niche Filled", text, death:true}; player-visible obs.title === "Another Niche Filled" (not the room name) and obs.description carries the death text + honest closure "Final score: 0 of 50." (no milestone fired — wight never slain, no +10); canonical ending.text stays pure.
  - **ending_woken** (canonical descent seed 1, then prise the sarcophagus): obs.ended + ending_id ending_woken; ending block {…, title:"What Sleeps Beneath", death:true}; obs.title === "What Sleeps Beneath"; obs.description carries the doom text + the PARTIAL closure "Final score: 25 of 50." (the doom never takes the crown so the circlet's +25 take_effect never fires — bug_0107; the partial tally reads "incomplete", distinct from the 50/50 win).
  Complements rpg_barrow_wight_prep_matters case (3) + rpg_barrow_lord_woken_fork (internal end-state) and bug_0124 (abstract reachability) with the rendered-experience layer the blind pass exercised but no test pinned.
- **Why this, not a content edit:** the pack is blind-clean — the playtester found zero defects across three runs and all three endings. The only critique (brevity, enjoyment 4/5) is the pack's standing identity, not a flaw; expanding it would churn a heavily-tuned pack with many pinned hashes for marginal gain. The death-render gap was the one concrete, verifiable, purely-additive hole, and it was the explicitly-handed-off next lock.
- **Evidence:** `npm run health` **exit 0; 852 tests pass (158 files; +5 cases)** (was 847/157). All SEVEN packs validate 0 error / 0 warning; sunken_barrow content_hash 7546c419… UNCHANGED (no pack edit → no HASH_PIN move). verify:integrity static 0/0; typecheck / lint / format:check clean.
- **Locked by:** traces/bugs/bug_0126_barrow_death_ending_render.yaml + tests/regression/rpg_barrow_death_ending_render.test.ts.
- **Next suggested focus:** both RPG packs (cold_forge + sunken_barrow) now have their victory AND death endings render-locked through the player observation — the death-render lever is exhausted. Remaining standing levers unchanged: (1) keep ROTATING the blind pass onto the least-recently-played pack (the CYOA/parser packs are now the least-recently blind-played; their non-death endings could get the same rendered-observation lock if a future blind pass surfaces a gap, though CYOA uses a different observation path); (2) the HP-recovery content gap for an unlucky prepared RPG player stays YAGNI absent demand; (3) if a future RPG pack HP-gates a route, the bug_0124 guard fires. All packs content-complete, blind-clean, reachability-proven, render-locked on RPG endings, and at rest.
